### alyson thiessen — 2016-04-16T21:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-192872>

Something I haven't talked about much on here is my mind. I am not an advanced student here. Not at all however something I am utmostly attuned to and vigilant about is....what I let into my mind.

My day starts with my body outside witnessing nature....sounds of birds....the beautiful sun.....frost on the grass...the lake breaking. This has been true now every day for 9 months. Prior to this I didn't "do" the outdoors.
This is what my mind begins it's day with.

Throughout the day I stop in on Facebook a few times. I pay attention to the posts that resonate. Those that are full of hate and bullshit...well these folks get deleted. Most of my feed are quantum folks

I don't read magazines...I don't watch network TV....I don't read newspapers.....I don't play stupid games on Facebook...I don't game (period). I don't read self help books. I haven't had cable for many years. I don't read flyers....

I have learned to quell the beast of 'want'. Everything I don't do...all the bullshit wants me to be something I am not and never will be. Nor want to be.

Today as I drove my guy to his 8 day retreat he commented on the sky and how it reflected his life. He talked about the various strains of clouds....the blue, grey and black...the rising sun......and the sky was completely beautiful and almost stormy. I told him "I'm not a blue sky girl"..... and he's not a blue sky boy because if we were we would live in the burbs with 3 tv's and all the devices and tons of debt and a very busy life getting little johnny too and from all his various activities and playdates....barf.

THERE IS TREMENDOUS BEAUTY AND POWER IN THE STORM.

### alyson thiessen — 2016-04-18T17:42:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-192974>

..... and..... the blue sky days with full Sun.....

TREMENDOUS POWER AND BEAUTY IN THE SUN!!!!!!!!

We have had UV of 4!!!!!!!! I have never appreciated the beauty of the sun until the past 6 months. Healing....
I have spent more time outside than ever. I love it. Grounding. Feeling the cool blades of grass on my feet. Connecting with first rays before 8am. I also notice how no one else is outside!!!! WTF!!! Oh right....the lie of the blue lit world. Which brings me to this:

I am noticing how disconnected I get with those that are blue lit and have a circadian mismatch. I noticed it first with a houseguest. She has a rhythm that sleeps until 9 or 10....is tired until noon...gets a wind of energy from noon until 3 and then sleeps until 5. Wakes for supper and then wants to go until 2am. We kept missing each other. The moment she wanted to 'do something' the ship had sailed. I had either already done it or was doing something else. When I was ready to receive her, she would be sleeping. This got me thinking....can circadian mismatch be tantamount to putting a potato and an onion together in a pantry? They both rot fast???

I am becoming very aware of those I resonate with. I believe we share the same rhythm. For example. My daughter and I live together. Our rhythms are similar. Connecting with here is easy. She's ready when I'm ready and vice versa. It's easy. Same with my fiancee. Our rhythm is identical. He gets up, I get up. He gets tired and I'm tired. No one is whining..."I just wanna go to bed" or...."just stay up another hour!".... we eat the same, sleep the same.....energy lifts and lulls in concert. A beautiful resonant symphony.

Went to bed at sundown last night around 920. Slept sound until 520. Got up, opened the drapes and stayed in bed and watched the sun come up while snoozing and let the little birdies sing to me until 7.

Oh...had another thought yesterday driving home from Calgary. Isn't quantum like a bank account? The blue lit world sees their bank account out by 1 dollar. So, they put a dollar in via exercise, diet and sups or drugs. Balanced right? Until the next day when they are out 100 dollars.... I see it like this. Oh....I'm out a dollar....I need to truly balance this account....and I go through everything and find the true error which was actually 10,000 and I fix that error. BOOM... balanced.

Beautiful warm day again here today. Hoping for a half day so I can have more UV later today. The lake is unfrozen however, I can't go in yet....I ended up with an acute UTI which hit my kidneys and a fever of 104 for 4 days. I have been using ice packs this week on my neck on on my bladder and kidneys. My body says...NO fucking way for the water....so...I am listening. HOWEVER....I will be out there next week for sure.

### alyson thiessen — 2016-06-20T18:58:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-196963>

I put on my blue linen pants today...it was a no go. I was about to fall into a sea of self loathing when I realized that this would just compound the matter...

I am asking...why.

Clearly I have entered a new phase of menopause or perimenopause. The hormone fluctuation has been apparent now for years....missing periods....accelerated periods...two in one month...then none for 2 months. I am 48 ... I get it. My tubes were tied when I was 44 after an unintended pregnancy which ended up as a miscarriage. I have breakouts on my face and chest. My skin is dry (amongst other things)....I am not so much bitchy as neutral...

I have been swimming twice a day now for well over a month....drinking 3-4 litres of water a day. 2 hours morning UV....2-3 hours afternoon/evening UV. And yet ....I am plumping...again.

I do want to approach this with some self compassion though. Self hatred will not solve this problem.

Leptin reset? Water Fast? Intermittent fasting?

I lost about 25 lbs last summer by eating a salad with sardines at lunch and then fish or oysters at supper. My budget won't allow oysters and sardines ... well lets just say I eat them for the effect and nothing else.

Food isn't everything...but it is something?

Oh...and the fatigue.

MY bp is 111/70. Blood sugar normal....thyroid normal. although...my voice is raspier

Sometimes I felt that the cold water is sending me backwards???? I would fucking hate that...but here is what happens after every swim....

Unless I get scorching UV post swim my drop is almost intolerable. Within 1-2 hours of swimming I get so incredible cold...like can't sleep cold. The lake isn't that cold...ranges from 60-70 right now depending on time of day. My fiancee who is much leaner than I gets cold/shivery right away and then is fine. I know this has to do with my high fat percentage.

And my legs....continue to swell...almost intolerably sometimes. Even with the many swims.

ENVIRONMENT???? Could I have actually moved to a worse environment???????? Fuck.

The mantra is self compassion...self compassion ... self compassion.....

I have no ability to move right now....

Ever since that damn cruise in November I went downhill....the acceleration came around March....

Comments...questions...I am open...

### Inger — 2016-06-20T19:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-196966>

Alyson, have you tested your environment well? How well? What have you tested and how?

I think food is important... especially if environment is not optimal. I do loads of raw wild oysters picked by me.. that is why I moved here because I know I am broke and cant afford to buy them so I need to live somewhere where I can get as many as I want for free... I need them!
I also get raw grassfed lambs balls for free from my boss... I make ball smoothie every so often.... and I get great grassfed lambs heart and liver and herring and mackerel.. all local... to tune my body into my local environment so it can make order out of chaos..... and the food is very cheap. I eat most of it raw to get as much viruses and bacteria as possible... because I feel it helps so much!
I am eating a great diet and I know it helps me. Because I still do not have a perfect environment even it is better than many have.

How is your diet now? Do you work, and how much? How is your work environment? Stress? Stress is real bad for women. I know... it affects me no good.. so I really try to limits stress in any form. Life has enough stressors anyways

If we have a not so optimal environment we need to do even more of the other things right! I also tan nude a lot. I live with and around the sun. Because here is not too much sun and it is not that strong, so I _use every bit I possibly can!
_
Are you having wine? I realize alcohol is not good for me. I know now, if I am in strong sun like at the mediterranean, wine does not affect me bad as it does here.

I do nicotine gums too. I like it a lot! Have you tried it?

Maybe 30 minutes of swimming would be enough - maybe sometimes it can be too much too soon? Maybe go slower?Maybe sit on the wet sand with your feet in the water instead.. or walk along the water edge... Everything is tied together.... You can figure out this
(((hugs)))

### endless — 2016-06-23T19:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-197150>

When you say your thyroid is \*normal\*, does that mean TSH within range, or have you had more extensive thyroid testing? That measurement alone is not enough. It doesn't really tell you much. It certainly SOUNDS like a thyroid issue! Down to the cold intolerance....not sure if Gretchen is on the forum anymore but get in touch with her on FB, she has had a lot of thyroid issues.

Other than that have you tried a strict AIP diet to see if something you are eating is causing inflammation? I am doing it now and I just dropped 16 pounds in 2 weeks...I was so puffed up I looked inflated. No booze, no grain, no sugar. I am amazed how inflamed I was. I am in the same environment, just reducing things I was reacting to (still not sure what those things are).

And yes, I would test your environment....can you swing a tri field meter or maybe source one to borrow?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-24T16:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyson-at-2800-feet.14602/post-197176>

Joe mentions something very subtle...............that things is more powerful that most realize. Learn to deepen your keel. When the winds of life blow you'll remain resolute in your journey even when you have to tack backward a bit from your target. This is the messiness of life; we all carry multitudes, so we must at times drift with the shifts beneath us. We are complicated creatures, and ultimately, the balance comes from understanding that sometimes you are like a boat and other time you actually are the water below. Life wants us to be more water like. Flowing, flexible, soft and chaotic. Subtly powerful yet open. Wild and serene. Able to accept all changes, yet still led by the pull of steady tides we face. Why do we need to be more like water and less like the boat? Water is the vagina for sunlight. It receives and memorizes the information in the sun. Factoid of the day: Limiting beliefs comes from an environment that gives our brain a low quantum yield of information. Low quantum yield means not enough sunlight. 'The sun is the most sophisticated information delivery service in existence'.........and you all need its messages to your eye and into your brain to teach you fundamentally what to do in just about any situation. When you listen to the messages your life becomes simple. You become like the hippo and don't have to be told what to eat of do because you already know. So the key is to do what you did Folks, step out into the light and bring as many with you as you can and watch how things improve without doing much at all.

# Thread: Quest for Health

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-21T15:19:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-164921>

So even though I try not to be on the computer much due to my super EMF sensitivity issues, I decided to start a journal anyway . . . I just don't know how often I'll update!

Here's my history:

I have been sick for 6 years now. I started developing random food intolerances with one after another and now can eat little more than meat and seafood. I have trouble digesting fats, carbs, fiber, etc, etc. The only thing I seem good with is protein. I have issues with oxalates and salicylates as well as histamine, amines, you name it - it's bad for me! This leaves me with virtually no veggies or fuits to eat.

I have been to 20+ doctors and no one can figure out what is going on. I have heard everything from Lyme (tests are negative) to IBS, depression, SIBO (tests negative), some strange infectious disease, etc, etc. I did have a blood microscopy test done that showed a dinoflagellate in my red blood cells. The only band positive on my Lyme test is also consistent with a dinoflagellate. I have yet to get any information on what this means from any doctor or the google searching I've done. Anyone familiar with this type of infection?!

I did start with getting sick a few months after I traveled to UT and got presumed food poisoning. I have had a few stool tests done that always show low good bacteria and minor bad bacterial overgrowths, but never anything major like parasites, yeast, fungus, etc. My limited diet concerns me about the low good bacteria - how can I increase the diversity of my gut bacteria when I can't get a good variety of foods in me?

I recently started adding in white rice - I know, BAD! - but I'm desperate to gain some weight and add something to my diet other than meat. I feel like my body desperately needs something else.

My issues include major GI problems - chronic constipation, stomach pain, frequent nausea, problems regulating my body temperature - I'm hot when I should be cold and cold when I should be hot. I don't sweat, have insomnia, headaches, my hands cramp/claw up, my feet swell and turn purple. Every time I try to add in a new food I binge like crazy on it.

I am doing CT with 15-20 minute cold baths 1-2 times per day. I wear blue blockers at night, no wifi, eat seafood 2 meals/day with ground beef my 3rd meal. I'd love to get off all the many supplements I'm on - taking all these pills is actually making me nauseous! I'll do another post at a later time with a list of what I'm currently taking.

Any insight from anyone so far on all of my issues? We have really cut out all EMF - I don't use a cell phone, no cordless phone in the house, like I said - no wifi. We're in a pretty rural area with no close cell towers. THe rest of my family is pretty healthy, thankfully. There's just something bizarre going on with me

I am so limited in my diet it's beyond frustrating. I've tried just plain iceberg lettuce and that is now causing me issues - it gives me major stomach pain and burning, which I'm chalking up to basically rubbing my gut lining raw.

Any suggestions at all on where to start with trying to get more veggies in?

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-27T18:06:43+0300

My cravings are going through the roof right now . . . and you'd think that means I want bad stuff like chocolate and chips. Nope! I just want to eat some freaking vegetables!! I think I'm going through an oxalate dump right now. Does anyone on here deal with oxalate issues?

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-27T19:29:00+0300

As of now, my breakfast is salmon/scallop pureed into a soup followed by 2 filets of Pompano (fresh from Florida). My lunch is about 4.5 oz of ground beef and my dinner is some pureed shrimp. I NEED something else. I've tried every veggie I can - from iceberg lettuce to rutabaga!

### Sue-UK — 2015-05-27T20:15:34+0300

It's spring here and I'm craving greens, big time. I think it's for the chlorophyll. I don't have a juicer to juice veg, I've today put spirulina powder in each glass of water, sort of drip feeding it in ..... Chlorella powder isn't right, (maybe for autumn?). If I didn't have some spirulina I might chew on some edible weeds to get some of the juice, and just spit out the fibre. I also use seaweed sprinkles on food but want it less now spring is here. But sprinkles, drops, powder, chewing the juice out.... Might be a way of starting with the tiniest amount with very little fibre, and work up .....??

### caroline — 2015-05-28T13:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-165661>

How do you do with a little rice? A lot of us don't eat much in the way of veg - depends on the season too. Remember this is your n=1 ....you need to find what works for you.

I do way better on raw meat - digest it way better than cooked ....have you tried that?

I just put a GF steak on a wooden board and put it in the fridge - DO NOT cover it.
I let it sit for a few days - but could eat it right away. As it dries a bit it seems more like salami to me!

Do you eat seafood sushi? raw salmon? avocados?

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-28T16:17:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-165673>

Thanks for the input from both of you. @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) - I love seaweed of all kinds and would absolutely love to get it back in my diet, but it is super high in salicylates and I just don't tolerate them at all  Most greens are very high salicylates, so they are pretty much all out. I used to eat TONS of them and I miss them so much!

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - I tried raw for a bit - lots of raw oysters, steaks, salmon, etc and it seeemd to really backfire on me. I got terrible heartburn and ended up with what I assumed to be SIBO (even though tests show it to be negative). Things got much, much worse for me when eating raw though. Histamine issues kicked up and I lost so much weight it was crazy. I don't know if I got some kind of infection or what, but I'm not willing to go that route again.

I would love to do avocados again too, but they are also very high in salicylates, so they are a "no" for me right now too

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-28T18:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-165693>

The key fix is repairing the circadian issue so the SCN works faster than the circadian clock genes in the LES to remove O2.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-29T14:57:56+0300

[http://www.tbiomed.com/content/pdf/s12976-015-0006-1.pdf](http://www.tbiomed.com/content/pdf/s12976-015-0006-1.pdf)

The problem is I don't seem to be able to handle veggies. Onions and garlic cause major IBS symptoms for me. Bok choy makes my ears and face turn bright red and hot - I think it's from salicylates. Brussels sprouts I can usually handle about 2 at a time, but they're only available in the Fall. The fiber in most veggies makes me even more constipated and always seems to cause inflammation that just makes my body overheat and cause me to feel like there's a fire right under my skin!

I also find that, even with veggies - or any new food, really - I have a tendency to binge. I don't know if it's because I'm finally getting a new flavor or texture or what, but I can't handle eating just a small amount. I always want MORE, MORE, MORE! I think more than anything I crave flavor because right now I don't even tolerate many herbs or spices - my food is just so bland.

For breakfast I usually have 2 filets of fish and I put on fresh lemon juice and salt and even that sometimes I can just easily overeat because it actually has some flavor and I go crazy for it!

I had a new dr appointment yesterday and this guy suggested I have a mast cell disorder. I came home and did some research and whattya know?! He may be right! Anyone else have this issue or have any experience with it? I'm guessing it's ultimately probably related to nnEMF as well, right? Of course my doctor didn't mention that at all. He said it's probably due to a leaky gut and "they" don't really know the cause.

This does make sense though as I often tell DH I feel like something builds up in me throughout the day since my evenings and nights are the worst - that's when my body always starts to heat up. Occasionally I'll get a burning red, flushing face right after eating something, but it's usually more of a gradual thing that really hits at the end of the day.

This doctor also mentioned the possibility of pyroluria, but said he really doesn't deal with that (or even the histamine issue really), so he's not sure how to proceed with any of it. If anyone has some insight, I'd love to hear it. I'm still researching to find all the connections here.

Zn Carnosine

At Bed
2 VSL #3 probiotics
Kavinace PM
SeaCure

Lunch is 3-4 oz pureed ground beef with fresh chives
Dinner is usually 4-5 oz pureed shrimp

I puree a lot of my food as it seems to help with my digestion. I've been trying to add in some veggies or white rice lately, but they're not working out too well. I get heartburn, burn up, my face turns red, hot and swells or I just get stomach pain.

Any thoughts on any of this?!

### Grmoline — 2015-07-15T19:12:16+0300

Well for starters, sunlight is free and a hell of a lot better than the Vit D pill for so many reasons that it is hard to know where to start..... unless you're in the southern hemisphere, you might start by jettisoning that one and getting your God-given solar panels into the sun. Supplements are just a patch. If it were me and resource was an issue, I would sink what resource I had into good sources of water and DHA (in its natural package) and then get yourself outdoors and grounded.

### SeaHorse — 2015-07-15T19:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-172022>

This is what stood out for me...read the last paragraph particularly.

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2004/05/01/glutamine.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2004/05/01/glutamine.aspx)

Why the Welchol?

SeaCure for me was very irritating and hydrolyzed protein can produce glutamate ...overall it seems like you are taking more supplements than food....have you ever just gone off all of them to see what you can tolerate ? If you have mast cell/histamine etc. I would stay away from anything that isn't super fresh or raw and that includes a lot of supplements....there are a lot of herbs in these formulas...do you know that each one is ok with your system?

When do you feel your best? Where are you and what are you doing?....start there and build up to the bigger stuff....I know you are planning on moving, but finding some relief in the meantime would be soothing.....

### SeaHorse — 2015-07-15T19:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-172025>

I know lots of people think SeaCure and l-glutamine are great, but you've been on them long enough that if you cut them out you will be able to tell if it's a loss or a benefit. Same with the Welchol...have you felt better taking it? It's not what people are saying or who is saying it....the whole dance is about what works for you.....experiment with more fat...less fat, more meat less meat...more fish, less fish....it feels like you are frozen in place and scared and that's a terrible feeling, but keep moving things around.
As for who to listen to.....it's yourself...trust yourself more and feel your way to what supports you knowing that your environment isn't giving you all that you need right now....

Firstly, allow me to apologize for the delayed response. i was out of town for a week. Luckily 4 of those days I was in the gulf of mexico. i would try just going down to a bare minimum. Maybe, keep the probiotics and the ox bile before your bigger meals and maybe some enzymes. I know Kruse recommended ubiquinol, , but I haven't read any of his stuff on it yet. With so many supplements you might have a hard time knowing what's working and what's not. Maybe you can try backing off slowly on the pureed food. Over time this is not good for GI tone. You might be worse off for a little after stopping the supplements. Try getting rid of one at a time. Be measured in your moves. Things like glutamine and DGL might work as temporary supplements, but not much of this is good long term. What are your digestive symptoms?

### lookingforanswers — 2015-07-22T16:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-172645>

Thanks so much @[Danny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12028/) ! Lucky you being in the Gulf of Mexico!

I have slowly been trying to cut back on supplements. I do worry about pureeing all of my food too, but it seems to be the only way I can really digest it right now.

As far as GI symptoms, the list runs all over the place:

-   PAIN! - upper and lower stomach, left and right side - pretty much all over
-   nausea
-   infrequent heartburn - as long as I don't eat veggies I can keep this to a rare minimum
-   chronic constipation unless I take Dr. Schulze's INtestinal Formula #1 - I will alternate with occasional diarrhea depending on what I've eaten
-   I have major oxalate issues, which I'm told is totally from gut problems
-   I also have salicylate sensitivities and histamine issues - again, I'm tol dthis is from gut problems
-   Slow motility
-   Stones in stool - no one has any idea what these are! Sometimes I'm told they are oxalate stones, other times parasite eggs, other times gallstones - no one knows!

Other issues are severe temperature problems - feeling hot and "on fire" when I should be cold and feeling cold when I should be hot. My neck is what usually burns up and you can literally feel heat coming off it if you put your hand about an inch or two away from it! I also get heart palpitations some, headaches, joint pain, weakness, fatigue, blurred vision, eye floaters, the list really just goes on and on.

### Danny — 2015-07-22T17:32:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-172650>

> [lookingforanswers said:](/goto/post?id=172645)
>
> Thanks so much @[Danny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12028/) ! Lucky you being in the Gulf of Mexico!
>
>
> I have slowly been trying to cut back on supplements. I do worry about pureeing all of my food too, but it seems to be the only way I can really digest it right now.
>
> As far as GI symptoms, the list runs all over the place:
>
>
> -   PAIN! - upper and lower stomach, left and right side - pretty much all over
> -   nausea
> -   infrequent heartburn - as long as I don't eat veggies I can keep this to a rare minimum
> -   chronic constipation unless I take Dr. Schulze's INtestinal Formula #1 - I will alternate with occasional diarrhea depending on what I've eaten
> -   I have major oxalate issues, which I'm told is totally from gut problems
> -   I also have salicylate sensitivities and histamine issues - again, I'm tol dthis is from gut problems
> -   Slow motility
> -   Stones in stool - no one has any idea what these are! Sometimes I'm told they are oxalate stones, other times parasite eggs, other times gallstones - no one knows!
>
> Other issues are severe temperature problems - feeling hot and "on fire" when I should be cold and feeling cold when I should be hot. My neck is what usually burns up and you can literally feel heat coming off it if you put your hand about an inch or two away from it! I also get heart palpitations some, headaches, joint pain, weakness, fatigue, blurred vision, eye floaters, the list really just goes on and on.

It's tough to know where to begin. I often wonder if I am doing the right things or if it's working. Keep trying to slowly wean and eliminate your supplements. I had the opposite problem… diarrhea. I felt I had to calm and soothe my intestines. Eliminating wi-fi, sunning, seafood, and the leptin Rx definitely helped w/ that. Then I realized bananas, papayas, chamomile, marshmallow root, soothed my GI lining as well. My motility has slowed down and my stools look normal on the bristol stool chart, but now that the transit time has slowed down I am experiencing lots of gas. Not sure if probiotics are my next step.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-07-25T17:37:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-172977>

So I finally got some test results back and it has been confirmed that I have a Mast Cell Activation Disorder. I guess I should have been thinking more along the lines of histamine all along considering some of my symptoms. Apparently I have too many active mast cells in my gut and that is why I react so terribly to pretty much all food!

Problem is they are telling me to avoid all the things that are supposed to be good for me! Fish, seafood, sun - I have been finding that I'm getting mroe and more "sensitive" to the sun - my face flushes, swells and turns bright red just like happens with food. I really have no idea where to turn from here . . .

My doctor is referring me to a specialist at Harvard Medical School - a 7-hour drive away and for what?! Just so I can be told there's nothing to be done!

### lookingforanswers — 2015-07-27T15:56:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-173211>

DH did not get the job in Orlando. He has another interview this week, so we're just trying to stay positive and hope that something will work out. This poor man is working so hard since I lost my job just to support us and working even harder to try to find a job so we can make this move happen. I'm beginning to worry about his health. I know he's under so much stress and pressure right now.

I guess I'm just asking for positive vibes that things will soon work out. We've been trying to make this happen for so long and I know I need to go, but with kids and a family to support we just can't up and go without a job! Our other option (though I have no idea how we'll afford it) is for me to go South over the winter while he stays here and takes care of the kids and continues to try to find a job. This breaks my heart as I don't want to leave my family - that certainly won't be good for my health and like I said I have no idea how we'll afford it  I started looking into Mexico as it's so much cheaper than FL for short-term rentals.

Does anyone know of any good areas in MX for winter rentals?

### zuzanac — 2015-12-02T21:40:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quest-for-health.14652/post-183087>

Did you ever find solution? I have similar problems.

# Thread: Hyperuricemia on keto
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-05-22T16:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165052>

Hi all, I recently had my blood checked, and despite I'm on paleo/keto and train frequently, my bloodwork is quite bad. My uric acid is very high, which might mean that my kidneys don't function properly.

Here are the results:
CRP = 2mg/L (should be 1, the standard here is below 5 though)
LDL= 166.7 mg/DL
HDL = 74.2 mg/DL
Total Cholesterol = 248.64 mg/DL
Triglycerides = 41.59 mg/DL (too low, should be 50-80)
Uric Acid = 6.7 mg/DL (too high)
Carbamide = 24 mg/DL (too high)

I know that the LDL profile is more important then the number, but social security doesn't allow me to do that test, so I need to find a private lab for that one.
About myself: I eat paleo for a while, but constantly paleo-keto since 3 months now. I eat 160-240g protein a day, 120-195g fat, and below 15g net carbs from vegetables mostly. My staple foods are chicken thighs, eggs, butter tea, green vegetables, ground beef, salmon, shrimps, avocadoes. I prefer my fats from meat (including fish) but since I was suggested to lower my protein intake and increase fat, I'm trying with more bacon, and coconut oil. No fruits at all, no cheat days, no grains, no starches. I eat nuts occasionally, but not daily. I work out 4 days a week, mostly fasted from 6AM to 7AM. I have some 100% beef protein isolate, but rarely use it.

I supplement with BCAA, Vitamine D3, Omega 3, Zinc, Magnesium Citrate, Chonondritin and MSM. Sometimes add some spirulina for some extra vitamins. I drink about 3L water a day.

I found some relevant information on wikipedia, but not sure how accurate it is "A [ketogenic diet](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketogenic_diet) impairs the ability of the kidney to excrete uric acid, due to competition for transport between uric acid and [ketones](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketone).[\[11\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperuricemia#cite_note-pmid488876-11)"

Could you guys please help me to suggest what might be the problem? What should I try to change in my diet? Does anyone else have this problem?

### nonchalant — 2015-05-22T23:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165072>

Hi Daniel. How often do you eat seafood?

### Daniel — 2015-05-29T19:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165851>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=165072)
>
> Hi Daniel. How often do you eat seafood?

Well not much, salmon once a week, shrips just a few times a month.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-30T00:25:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165862>

### Daniel — 2015-05-30T08:00:07+0300

Hi Jack,

Well, I supplement with Omega 3, since seafood is not widely available over here (Hungary), my circadian rythm is alright, I wake up at 5AM, and go to sleep around 9PM. At this time of the year, this is actually the time of sunset and sunrise. Have some salt lamps and red lightbulbs if I need light, but don't really use lighting at night. Computer has f.lux installed, iPhone banned, wifi plugged out for night.

Are you suggesting that more seafood (DHA) would solve the problem of high uric acid?

### Peertje80 — 2015-05-30T09:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165899>

Hungary? Love that country. Really nice folks there. Can't you get fish from Lake Balaton, maybe Eel from river or perhaps canned fish? Canned must be available. It is essential you get your DHA from fish not supplements for all sorts of reasons. But I think Jack is saying you should replace your protein with more fish.

### nonchalant — 2015-05-30T16:47:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperuricemia-on-keto.14673/post-165953>

> [Daniel said:](/goto/post?id=165892)
>
> Are you suggesting that more seafood (DHA) would solve the problem of high uric acid?

He's saying more seafood-sourced DHA would help solve the problem of low energy and the body not being able to repair itself. If your particular problem is high uric acid, yes, real DHA and water will fix that. But they also need information (grounding, circadian rhythm, Schumann resonance) and charge (sunshine, magnetism, IR, tensegrity, food).

# Thread: Bartek, Connecting the dots.. :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bartek-connecting-the-dots.14674/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bartek S — 2015-05-22T18:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bartek-connecting-the-dots.14674/post-165058>

Hey guys! (and ladies ) my name is Bartek I'm 27(probably biologically speaking a little older because of all the electronic usage lol) I live in Poland and I finally got to creating my Journal, I was a little hesitant and overwhelmed with the idea of creating it because there's so much I want to write and so much I dont understand but I just thought if I just start writing piece by piece and hopefuly some more knowledgable people will share some guidance I will be on my way to improving myself and those around me
With time I think I can find answers to my questions and connect them dots!

I thought I would provide some context to my environment, my lifestyle and how I'm doing things right now, I already came a long road from being a huge nerd sitting in front of computer like a rooted tree eating KFC and McDonalds I've tried many things and healthy lifestyle is one of my hobbies nowadays, something brought be here and Im pretty sure its not a coincidence because when I listened to one of Dr Kruses Blogs I got instant voice from my deep "infinite knowledge" and instinctively I knew this guy totally knows whats going on and it makes sense, I'm no physics or biochemical genius but on some level subconciously I think Im in the right place to dig deeper and I'm grateful that something led me here.

I guess I was about to provide context but somehow went for a little gratidute detour  no biggie
I live in Poland close to Berlin and Baltic Sea, so that would be northern/central Europe I live in my apartament on 5th floor, ground floor being 1st floor(not 0) dont know if that matters but its also highest floor in my building meaning I dont have anything above me(unless there are some vicious antenas etc)
as I was writing this feeling terror of all the potential radio emf devices I went on my balcony and looked at brother building nearby and luckily there are no devices of that sort, so Im safe from above
I wish I knew about all this before I spent so much money for the home Im living in right now but no biggie I think the area itself is quite calm, Im not in the center of my city I have some nice green "pastures" on one and some streets on the other with lots of living green things around.
I'm a professional poker player(I think its important to mention now that I do use a lot of computer for my work), previously I was a big nerd playing video games professionally since age 17 I think before that I played football and did regular kid activities I guess that helped a little  with time I gradually used less and less computer but I can safely say I use it not only for my work but for obtaining knowledge and leisure like watching movies, tv series etc

I love my work and its one my main hobbies in life, second big hobby is self development in general, I love good books, reading about health, biohacking, trying new things on myself and quantyfing results, I also meditate since more than 5 years now funnily enough just before I got to know Dr Kruse works(I just read Epi Paleo Rx Book) I bough hearthmath biofeedback device so I substracted a lot of my meditation practice to biofeedback "coherence training".

The last hobby of my trininty is BJJ (Brazilian Jiu Jitsu) its a martial art thats quite similar to chess and very scientific, when I go to train I do two trainings in a row 1hr +1,5hr = 2,5hr in one go, it has warmup technique and sparing sessions which are exacly like HIIT so very high intensity rounds against ressisting opponent I mention this because I think it will be relevant to a picture I want to paint, I start my training at 5pm and finish 7:30pm I often train fully fasted in ketosis (I get some raw honey and MCT oil into my training water) and immediatelly after my training I eat the food I prepared before I left home.

I started a little backwards with my daily routine I guess, when I wake up I adhere to bulletproof style from reading watching a lot of Dave Asprey, it made sense and it definitely felt good to wake up drink coffee(I buy his coffee and I feel its quality coffee) I add 1 full tea spoon of kerrygold butter and 2 spoons of his Octane Oil which is supposed to be 6x stronger version of MCT oil 1 spoon of raw honey I blend it manually and that is my breakfast at around 7-9am then I work and interestingely enough I follow this up with 2nd coffee with the same combination of "ingredients" at around 2pm the days I go training(fasted), post training I eat a full meal meat/fish + rice + fats + veggies and thats how I eat, the days I dont train I usually skip rice/carbs.
It's been working for me and Ive been feeling good but I have some symptoms that indicate it might not be perfect for me:
\-My libido while still there is not what it used to be (thyroid symptoms?)
\-I feel my extremities are cold, hands/feets (thyroid symptoms)
\-If I'm overweight its only slightly but I do have abdominal fat (I can provide pictures later)
\-I do my hardcore BJJ training twice a week because I subconciously feel like Im not recovering optimally from it, I feel like if I do 3 its going to be bad(this might be in my head but thats instinctive feeling that something is wrong) and also one month ago when I trained wednesday and immediatelly friday I got cold sore(HPV) on my lips two weeks in a row I thought this was a sign that my immune system was suppressed from overtraining or something(I'm really not sure here).
\-I have red eyes since some time and when Im sitting in front of computer a lot they feel irritated usually later in the day like my body is trying to tell me "dude stop looking at monitor" Im not sure but all this might've started when I got my 34'' inch monitor and my eye sight deteriorated slightly.
\-had some carpal/cubital tunnel syndrome and symptoms were strongest when I was living in Thailand(15months) with 3 other guys and we had huuge table and on it there were 4 computers and like 8 monitors, I didnt connect the dots at this time but now I think it was a huge EMF hell and it would make a lot of sense why I felt the way I did.

for now these are the symptoms that come to my mind

I have a practical question because I just started biohacking my water and I want to optimize this process, there are some things I want to understand, I wanted to buy 4th phase of water Book but getting books from amazon to Poland is really expensive and the aforementioned book is not cheap either (I still order sometimes but I just did recently and I have to watch my budget)
so I was pouring my tap water into one of those 2l "filtering" things and pour water from there but I understand these are no good so now I cherry picked a Spring Water with very low fluoride content and I order in bulk in big 22liter containers (I think thats like 5galons) I also ordered awesome Copper galon container in which I store the water before drinking, Dr Kruse suggested this and I would like to understand how that works, does it have to do with something about being really good at conducting and it creates EZ water when in it? if so is the effect immediate or does the water have to be in a container for some time? then I also use my infra red 230v 150W lamp and shine it at the water in this copper container I also am not sure what happens with it on physics level how long and how often I have to direct this light on the water I would love to get that magnetica sleep pad for sleeping and also for biohacking my water but its not available in europe (if any of you knows if there is European alternative or if I can construct this myself or at least I can buy some kind of magnet to put copper container on please let me know)

okay I think thats enough for first time, I'm happy I finally strated writing and sharing my story to understand universe a little better big thanks for reading and if you guys feel you can lend a helping hand I would appreciate it wholeheartedly

Best Regards, Bartek.

### Bartek S — 2015-05-22T18:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bartek-connecting-the-dots.14674/post-165059>

I just realized that my main reason I wrote is I was not sure if I needed Leptin Reset Rx, its much different from my current lifestyle and when I tried Leptin Reset for entire two days(yea I know) I felt sleepy after eating my big breakfast and kind of sluggish, that could be the sign that I needed it even more but then again I didnt know if I should go take a nap now because Im feeling tired or I should power through, I was also not sure if I should drop my BJJ training because thats a long HIIT excercise session(I would be sad to not do it for 1 month) so yea basically I wanted to know what do you guys think about it get some additional info before I do it the right way

I also never had any serious labs done but I suppose if Im doing them that would be good time to consider that? getting multiple panels done would be kind of expensive I think so I really wanna double triple check before I proceed with these things, if you can help, I thank you for feedback

### JanSz — 2015-05-22T23:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bartek-connecting-the-dots.14674/post-165073>

Czesc Bartek. Witam.

Post name of city you are in.
There is confusion about EZ water.
For a water to become EZ it needs to be in our cells, it needs confinement of at least xxx nanometers.
You may not worry about EZ water content in container, bottle or glass.
Just get spring water, if you like there is a number of Polish springs that have a water with large sulfur content. If not, mind your sulfur (garlic, onions, MSM).

There is not much sun in Poland.
Make sure that you look at the sky for 2-3 minutes (sunny or not) first thing when you get off the bed.
Preferably sleep in totally dark bedroom, for 7-9hrs within 10PM-8AM

1/eod-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)

4/day Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D

Consider using the brands above, specially vit D
My friend (in Poland) got some local junk and we wasted time figuring it out.

\============
Libido, cold hands
Measure your underarm temperature (five times), post average in Celsius.
You need Steroid and Thyroid hormone checkup and optimization.
If you want, I can give you my suggestions.
Preferably with minimal doctor's involvement.
But if you have open minded doctor, say so.
Some required blood tests you have locally available, need money only.
If you can travel to Geneva Switzerland to give your blood there, I know of very good test that saves lots of guessing (about $400).
\--------
If your test shows low cortisol and DHEAs (and you are likely low)
I will advice you to stop most of exercises until adjustment is made.
\--------

After sundown minimize your time with tv, computer, cell phone.
Print stuff for evening reading.
Use red light when reading.

...........................
Do widzenia.

.

# Thread: Need help...confused
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-confused.14689/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mo Badrawy — 2015-05-23T18:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-confused.14689/post-165124>

Okay..I really don't know where to start...but Ill get to the point where I'm having trouble with.

I'm mercury toxic due that I removed 2 amalgam fillings(1 with the classical drill and 1 with the proper procedure but poorly done).

I have several health issues...leaky gut,leaky brain,fibromyalgia, brain fog(24/7),fatigue,intolerance to stress, cognitive issues in general.....don't really need to tell more.

Now my question is about seafood..........Even though people advised me to stay away from seafood when I'm this toxic, but whats funny is....whenever I consume seafood(especially shrimp and oysters!), i get unexplained energy and my cognitive issues improve drastically...but the effects short live the next day and I return to the old symptoms I'm used to.

I did a hair analysis and found out that I'm severly deficient in Selenium.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My question is, is it absolutely safe to continue consuming seafood regardless of the hysteria surrounding the toxicity of seafood ? I feel better whenever I consume them but I'm also afraid that I'm not helping myself getting rid of the mercury in my body.

If it is safe....what kinds of seafood I can safely consume if fresh sources of oysters,shrimps aren't easily accessible everyday?

### Mo Badrawy — 2015-05-23T18:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-confused.14689/post-165125>

I didn't determine my toxicity levels from mercury from that hair analysis. I just did that test to check my mineral status.

### Mo Badrawy — 2015-05-23T18:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-confused.14689/post-165126>

Another thing....I consume regularly salmon and I don't get that same effect I get when I consume shrimps,oysters,snappers...

I buy my salmon from frozen packages in grocery stores. It claims that they are wild caught but anyhow, I don't get the same positive effects when I consume shrimps,oysters,snappers.

I consume shrimps,oysters,snappers from fresh sources(no frozen packages).

and to give an overview of my diet...I'm eating the healthiest I could possible be. Mostly vegetables + coconut oil + protein from fish(salmon)and chicken.

But I dont get any positive cognitive+energy effects from that diet unless I consume shrimps,oysters,fresh sources of fish.

### Matty_M — 2015-05-23T20:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-reaction.14690/post-165139>

This has happened to me for a while now, but I have failed to adress it.

Whenever I eat a large amount coconut fat, I get a small headache, I feel weak, I feel nauseous, and my mood might go down to sadness or something.

It is worse on an empty stomach, and it seems less present if the oil is cooked (say, in a stir fry). Also, I don't notice it too much if I eat even a half can of coconut milk (so a good amount of the fat), possibly because I'm already full, but I'm not sure.

I feel like it could have to do with the antimicrobial properties of CO and therefore "die off" but I'm not sure. Any ideas/insight is greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

\- Matt

I noticed it improved his mood. He was much more pleasant to work with but he didn't notice it. When he started acting like an idiot again I asked him "are you still taking the coconut oil?" He said "No". His "theory" was "My body is rejecting it so my body doesn't want it".
You can't argue with this guy. He is heading for "Dementiaville" big time. He has a terrible sense of logic but fiercely argues his point which usually makes no sense, completely disorganized, and he is constantly losing things, including MY tools!
He recently got diagnosed with lung cancer. I recommended Dr. Kruse's website but he doesn't listen.
He has six cellphone towers within two blocks!

### Sean Waters — 2021-11-25T03:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-reaction.14690/post-306645>

Epic also that DHA at 500uM inside the cell lowers Ubiquitination by half (50%). More seafood = less Ubiquitination = less need for Protein = INUIT Diet explained

[https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/)

**DHA Inhibits Protein Degradation More Efficiently than EPA by Regulating the PPARγ/NFκB Pathway in C2C12 Myotubes**

**Abstract**
This study was conducted to evaluate the mechanism by which n-3 PUFA regulated the protein degradation in C2C12 myotubes. Compared with the BSA control, EPA at concentrations from 400 to 600 µM decreased total protein degradation. However, the total protein degradation was decreased when the concentrations of DHA ranged from 300 µM to 700 µM. DHA (400 µM, 24 h) more efficiently decreased the I_κ_B_α_ phosphorylation and increased in the I_κ_B_α_ protein level than 400 µM EPA. Compared with BSA, 400 µM EPA and DHA resulted in a 47% or 68% induction of the NF_κ_B DNA binding activity, respectively.

Meanwhile, 400 µM EPA and DHA resulted in a 1.3-fold and 2.0-fold induction of the PPAR_γ_ expression, respectively. Interestingly, EPA and DHA both still decreased the total protein degradation, although PPAR_γ_ knockdown attenuated the suppressive effects of EPA and DHA on the total protein degradation. These results revealed that DHA inhibits protein degradation more efficiently than EPA by regulating the PPAR_γ_/NF-_κ_B pathway in C2C12 myotubes.

Oh my god.......... this blog, nails it ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ ...

_Eating tons of protein all day long increases mTOR pathways. There is nothing controversial here. The mTOR pathway is “downstream” from the Insulin/IGF-1 pathway. But here is where it gets interesting. The mTOR pathway completely “shuts off” autophagy and stimulates protein synthesis. What has this series been about? Ubiquination. Ubiquination = protein recycling and synthesis. The paleo diet causes up-regulation of mTOR and Ubiquitin marking of proteins. This would lead to the result the women of paleo seem to be getting. This is the primary “danger” of eating too much meat or protein (i.e. stimulating the mTOR pathway). The ancestral health and paleo community, still today, advocates these behaviors. They also advocate brisk exercise in this modern world. Look at the pictures from any paleo fx conference. Their generation and tribe, also happens to [use technology](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2976614/Is-satnav-harming-brain-Scientists-warn-use-modern-technology-linked-memory-loss-depression-later-life.html), in the same fashion to Mr. Moore.

When we eat more protein, the science says **we should exercise less**, not more, because mTOR will be stimulated further!!! [Hyperlink](http://caloriesproper.com/protein-requirements-carbs-and-nutrient-partitioning/). So, their advice on exercise is WRONG and not factual, based upon what we know about proper coupled cycles. The data on protein consumption and exercise is well established but completely overlooked by ancestral groups. The more you exercise, the more fat one should consider in your diet. Here Mr. Moore gets something correct, but for the wrong reasons. He does not realize the fats need to be augmented with massive amounts of DHA. We should eat less protein and more fat, otherwise you increase mTOR. This effect is magnified in a microwaved world. This is why the women of paleo have gotten the results they have. I predicted in Austin at the original Paleo Fx event this was coming. They scoffed and laughed. Who is laughing now?

So, this raises a point for us all, what should you eat post work out in a [microwaved world](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2976614/Is-satnav-harming-brain-Scientists-warn-use-modern-technology-linked-memory-loss-depression-later-life.html)? First, your exercise should be natural and connected to the environment. Erwan LeCorre, is one guy in ancestral health, who gets the exercise part down pat. The conventional advice from RD, nutritionists, chiropractors, functional medicine paleo practitioners, is post work out, carbs or protein are best. **Is it true?** I do not advocate that, have not advocated that, and would not advocate that. Why not? If we want to shut down mTor, we should fast or drink cold water post workout…….Why? Nothing to eat = Intermittent fasting (IF) = AMPK = autophagy. (See below why details matter)_

### Penny — 2021-11-25T19:38:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-reaction.14690/post-306665>

dude - you TOTALLY nailed it on this! I have been losing muscle mass:

Nuclear factor-kappa B (NF_κ_B) is one of the most important signaling pathways linked to the loss of skeletal muscle mass in normal physiological and pathophysiological conditions \[[1](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B1)\]. NF_κ_B expresses constitutively and exists in the cytosol as part of a heterotrimeric complex \[[2](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B2)\]. This complex typically comprises the DNA-binding proteins p50 and p65 plus the inhibitory protein I_κ_B_α_. Activation of NF_κ_B requires phosphorylation of I_κ_B_α_, followed by ubiquitin conjugation and proteolysis of I_κ_B_α_ by the 26S proteasome \[[3](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B3), [4](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B4)\]. The activated NF_κ_B dimer is then translocated to the cell nucleus, which is a feed-forward signal that leads to the upregulation of pathway components including ubiquitin, E2/E3 proteins, and proteasome subunits, subsequently increasing activity of the ubiquitin/proteasome pathway and enhancing skeletal muscle protein degradation \[[5](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B5)–[7](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/#B7)\].

I am shit canning ham and replacing it with seafood - not thinkin' my singular can of oysters at night is enough... I thought the progression was pretty funny actually - first, you can't eat anything fun, then you have to eat a bunch of fat/protein/seafood and then it turns out the best thing to do is not eat at all

### Penny — 2021-11-26T21:45:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-reaction.14690/post-306695>

I found this by JK about mtor and sarcopenia: EC coupling stands for excitation contraction coupling - ie.e muscle contraction:
[https://www.thehealthboard.com/what-is-excitation-contraction-coupling.htm](https://www.thehealthboard.com/what-is-excitation-contraction-coupling.htm)

so the sun mitigates the protein I guess... I always eat protein in the sun - especially high triptophan foods UV light mitigates tyrosine kinase -

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...openia-as-we-age-is-a-story-about-mtor.23865/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/avoiding-sarcopenia-as-we-age-is-a-story-about-mtor.23865/)
**Activation of mTORC1 signalling in rat skeletal muscle is independent of the EC-coupling sequence but dependent on tension per se in a dose-response relationship**
[Emil Rindom](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?ContribAuthorRaw=Rindom%2C+Emil), [Anders M. Kristensen](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?ContribAuthorRaw=Kristensen%2C+Anders+M), [Kristian Overgaard](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?ContribAuthorRaw=Overgaard%2C+Kristian), [Kristian Vissing](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?ContribAuthorRaw=Vissing%2C+Kristian), [Frank Vincenzo de Paoli](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?ContribAuthorRaw=Paoli%2C+Frank+Vincenzo)
First published: 24 June 2019
[https://doi.org/10.1111/apha.13336](https://doi.org/10.1111/apha.13336)
Citations: [7](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/apha.13336#citedby-section)
[Read the full text](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/apha.13336)
 [PDF](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/epdf/10.1111/apha.13336)
[Tools
Share](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/apha.13336#)
**Abstract**
**Aim**
mTORC1 is regarded as an important key regulator of protein synthesis and hypertrophy following mechanical stimuli in skeletal muscle. However, as excitation and tension development is tightly coupled in most experimental models, very little and largely indirect evidence exist for such a mechanosensitive pathway. Here, we sought to examine whether activation of mTORC1 signalling is dependent on tension per se in rat skeletal muscle.

**Methods**
To examine the mechanosensitivity of mTORC1, rat EDL muscles were exposed to either excitation-induced eccentric contractions (ECC), passive stretching (PAS) with identical peak tension (Tpeak) and Tension-Time-Integral (TTI), or ECC with addition of inhibitors of the myosin ATPases (IMA). To further explore the relationship between tension and mTORC1 signalling, rat EDL muscles were subjected to PAS of different magnitudes of Tpeak while standardizing TTI and vice versa.

**Results**
PAS and ECC with equal Tpeak and TTI produced similar responses in mTORC1 signalling despite different modes of tension development. When active tension during ECC was nearly abolished by addition of IMA, mTORC1 signalling was reduced to a level comparable to non-stimulated controls. In addition, when muscles were exposed to PAS of varying levels of Tpeak with standardized TTI, activation of mTORC1 signalling displayed a positive relationship with peak tension.

**Conclusions**
The current study directly links tension per se to activation of mTORC1 signalling, which is independent of an active EC-coupling sequence. Moreover, activation of mTORC1 signalling displays a positive dose-response relationship with peak tension.

**Citing Literature**

**Supporting Information**
[
Volume227, Issue3](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/toc/17481716/2019/227/3)

November 2019

e13336

-   [**Related**](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/apha.13336#pane-pcw-related)
-   [**Information**](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/apha.13336#pane-pcw-details)

I was in the same boat - I didn't eat enough fat - it's so hot here, all I want is carbs - you need 50 grams of carbs to make serotonin - so don't go to zero - also oregon grape root is fantastic for candida and it helps me go longer without eating - but honestly, if you can score some 2% CDS - GREAT! I ordered some 25% but it sounds like a nightmare to make it - I read some keto book or something by either Mercola or the Grain Brain guy - he says just fast and that is a great way to start it - for me, I think it is stress - it is literally killing me watching my family take these effing jabs - honey just took number 3 the day before T-day - then went and spread it to everyone there - stress jacks up blood sugar - the other thing you might have missed is chitosan - it helps your gut flora by feeding good bacteria - JK says it's very important - bone broth and coconut oil really help my stomach - what I really need to do is get off this computer The other interesting thing about oysters - at least the canned variety - is there is 11 grams of protein in the can and 200% the DV of zinc - zinc with B6 is required to make serotonin - you can pop a P5P which is activated B6 in case there is not enough zinc on board - Here is what snake juice is:

**The snake juice recipe is as follows:**

-   2 litres of water
-   2 gm of Himalayan pink salt
-   5 gm salt-free potassium chloride
-   2 gm edible Epsom salts

I actually like potassium chloride - in fact, it is too energizing for me to take in the evening -
But JK says salt will transform/mutate into potassium in the sun and my son who has done many fasts - swears that all you need to do is eat salt if you feel hungry - JK also sez you need salt to sleep - so I just keep a jar of salt in the bathroom, and when I get up - depending on sinful I was that day - just put a little salt on your tongue - he also sez to take some salt right when you get up in the morning - we dilute salt whenever we pee - I swear sometimes I would sweat and not even taste salt! Not good... Cheers!

# Thread: For those of you who think you have no time to do this:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-of-you-who-think-you-have-no-time-to-do-this.14692/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-24T00:38:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-of-you-who-think-you-have-no-time-to-do-this.14692/post-165147>

**THE SITUATION** : Do you see or observe? When you observe are your perceptive about the vision?**

In Washington , DC , at a Metro Station, on a cold January morning in 2007, this man with a violin played six Bach pieces for about 45 minutes. During that time, approximately 2,000 people went through the station, most of them on their way to work. After about 3 minutes, a middle-aged man noticed that there was a musician playing. He slowed his pace and stopped for a few seconds, and then he hurried on to meet his schedule.

About 4 minutes later:**

The violinist received his first dollar. A woman threw money in the hat and, without stopping, continued to walk.
**

At 6 minutes:**

A young man leaned against the wall to listen to him, then looked at his watch and started to walk again.
**

At 10 minutes:**

A 3-year old boy stopped, but his mother tugged him along hurriedly. The kid stopped to look at the violinist again, but the mother pushed hard and the child continued to walk, turning his head the whole time. This action was repeated by several other children, but every parent - without exception - forced their children to move on quickly.
**

At 45 minutes:**

The musician played continuously. Only 6 people stopped and listened for a short while. About 20 gave money but continued to walk at their normal pace. The man collected a total of $32.**

After 1 hour:**

He finished playing and silence took over. No one noticed and no one applauded. There was no recognition at all.

No one knew this, but the violinist was **Joshua Bell**, one of the greatest musicians in the world. He played one of the most intricate pieces ever written, with a violin worth $3.5 million dollars. Two days before, Joshua Bell sold-out a theater in Boston where the seats averaged $100 each to sit and listen to him play the same music.

This is a true story. Joshua Bell, playing incognito in the D.C. Metro Station, was organized by the Washington Post as part of a social experiment about **perception, taste and people's priorities**.**

This experiment raised several questions:****

\***In a common-place environment, at an inappropriate hour, do we perceive beauty?**

\***If so, do we stop to appreciate it?**

\***Do we recognize talent in an unexpected context?
**

One possible conclusion reached from this experiment could be this:****

If we do not have a moment to stop and listen to one of the best musicians in the world, playing some of the finest music ever written, with one of the most beautiful instruments ever made . . ..****

How many other things are we missing as we rush through life?**

Enjoy life NOW .. it has an expiration date

### Da-mo — 2015-05-26T07:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-of-you-who-think-you-have-no-time-to-do-this.14692/post-165379>

My daughter and I once had ocassion to witness perfection amidst the oblivious.

We were walking her horse past the local riding arena where several riders and horses were going about their daily training. In the middle of the arena was a Bavarian lady sitting astride one of the long in the tooth schoolmasters that spent his days enduring beginner riders bouncing on his back and pulling on his mouth. The lady was not giving a lesson to anyone and this is the first time we had ever seen her mounted.

Because we had learned by our own research some of the history of this lady (of which hardly anyone else was aware) we stopped to watch a little while. Over the course of 5 minutes, with 15-20 other people riding all around, we watched the combo ride back and forth on a straight line between 2 cones spaced 20m apart. With each successive traverse between the cones, the movements became more and more exquisite and finally culminated in 1 x tempi changes. At this point she praised the horse and dismounted and quietly left the arena, completely unnoticed by the other riders.

My daughter and I were left with our mouths hanging open not only at the perfection we had just witnessed but also because no-one else seemed to notice what had just taken place amongst them.

The rider had in fact been trained under one of the top 5 trainer/horsemen of the last century and the horse we discovered later had been a top Grand Prix horse in a former life.

This clip shows one tempi changes at the beginning where the horse appears to be skipping.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-24T03:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ice-cream-headaches.14694/post-165166>

A few weeks after I first started doing CT in the ocean, I started going underwater because of the benefits Jack covered in the Feb/15 webinar. I got one of those "ice cream headaches" almost immediately. But I noticed that the more I did it, the longer it took to feel that effect.

Now I have no headache when I go underwater but if I up the gradient, I feel it again.
For instance, if I have straight cold water on my forehead in the shower I will get it but even this is getting less and less.

Could "ice cream headaches" be a sure sign of brain inflammation?

**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RyanMSauce — 2015-05-25T00:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165243>

Hey everyone! I want to do more research in this area. I was thinking about making my own magnet pad with neodymium magnets. Can anyone take an estimated guess as to what the realistic field strength could be?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-25T01:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165245>

as high as you can afford. Neodymium is $$$$

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-25T01:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165246>

You need a human pic up too. New rule

### RyanMSauce — 2015-05-25T01:46:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165248>

Hey Jack. Ill do the pic. I was looking at bulk pricing online from china. I just need to figure out how many magnets and what size to get. I'm estimating real quick there are 500-1000 magnets in a sleep pad depending on size?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-25T07:33:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165269>

For a queen Magnetico 10 gauss there are 1248 magnets. For a 20 gauss there are 2496.
Magnetico uses high-grade, ceramic alloy, permanent magnets, with a gauss rating of 3 950 gauss at the core. The dimensions of each magnet are 3/8 x7/8 x1 7/8.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-25T07:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165270>

If larger magnets are cheaper you may be able to calculate the total mass of the magnetico magnets and use a metal plate to bridge the gaps. The metal will spread out the flux lines.

You will definitely need one of these analog gauss meters:

[http://ndtsupply.com/media/catalog/category/MT\_AN\_0005C\_4.jpg](http://ndtsupply.com/media/catalog/category/MT_AN_0005C_4.jpg)

### PaulG — 2015-05-25T07:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165271>

I looked into this a while back. Neodymium magnets can have very high surface gauss but that falls off rapidly with distance. If you were to put 1000s of Neodymium magnets into a mattress underlay/topper that was close to your body you would potentially get 1) too strong of a field and 2) issue with positive return fields that Dr. Bonlie talks about. If you used the same thing but put it under your 6-8" mattress then the field would be too weak compared to Ceramic magnets/magnetico.

See [https://www.fastmag.com.au/information\_gauss\_strength.htm](https://www.fastmag.com.au/information_gauss_strength.htm)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-25T08:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165273>

If you bridge the magnets with some type of ferrous metal, you avoid the return field as it becomes like one big magnet, but at a cost - the field becomes weaker.
I've experimented with this myself using the above gauss meter.
Also, if you listen to this months Q&A I asked Jack "How much is too much?" in regards to magnetism and he basically said "If Bonelie could make a 1000 G mattress and it wasn't crazy expensive I'd buy one".
But I do remember him saying in a previous Q&A something about "the dose makes the toxin" in regards to magnetism.
I also don't really understand the return field thing because when you lie on the mattress the field is moving in one direction through your body. When you roll over it's going the other way. Unless it has something to do with a cancelling of the earths field. Regardless, it's best to stay with natures polarity.
I'm also curious if the Magnetico would make us unable to sense subtle changes in the earth's native mag. field.
Or maybe it amplifies it (?)

### PaulG — 2015-05-25T08:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165274>

**Neodymium
Measures:** 1" diameter and is slightly less than 1/4" thick.
**Rated {12,300 gauss @ core}**
{1,250 gauss @ surface}
{325 gauss @ .5" from surface}
{86 gauss @ 1" from surface}
{15 gauss @ 2" from surface}
{5 gauss @ 3" from surface}
**Source: [http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5475082/5405575.htm](http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5475082/5405575.htm)**

**Ceramic _{1-7/8" x 7/8" x 3/8" thick}_
**100 gauss @ surface of pad
25 gauss @ 1" from surface
15 gauss @ 2"
13 gauss @ 4"
12 gauss @ 6"
11 gauss @ 8"**
****Source: [http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5456673/5395084.htm](http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5456673/5395084.htm)**
****
From Dr. Bonlie
"No matter how strong his magnets are, if they are 5" apart, there will be sizeable peaks of the "Return" Positive(+) field between the magnets, assuming he has the negative side up. This opposite direction of field slows down atoms rather than speeding them up like ours, and the slowing down will send an emergency message to the brain that all is not well in those areas. The brain, in turn, responds by sending an increased supply of energy to those spots....which, in time will be depleting their reserves of vitality. We all need reserves for true emergencies such as an auto accident, surgery, intense emotional upsets, illnesses, etc.

So, yes, he will probably be noticing more "effect" at the moment due to the stimulatory response he's getting. But, it's not good to do that long-term"

As to the gauss rating at distance, I don't know how it is derived or can be calculated!!?? See my other post [here](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/magnetic-mattress-sweet-spot.14496/#post-165118). This calculator is for a single magnet but when you have a load of magnets placed close together you get a 'unified' field. When you stack two magnets together you don't get twice the field more like 1.4 times the field, when you put them side by side I have no idea the cumulative increase. I will try and find out...

You could make a magnetic underlay using say 1000 ceramic magnets (single )that sits onto of your mattress then add a 2" mattress topper (to mitigate positive return field). This would give you 15 gauss field.

### RyanMSauce — 2015-05-25T23:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165331>

Thanks for the replies. I was reading somewhere on this forum saying that at about 60 Gauss is when you really start noticing enhanced sleep. If I could find bigger ceramic magnets this would be possible I think. That is interesting Paul I didn't even consider that there would be a difference in the drop off of field strength with different types of magnets. I want to figure this out.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-27T03:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165491>

When I experimented with the spacing of the magnets in the past there was definitely a return field in between the magnets but this was lessened the further above the magnets I measured. Placing a bar of steel in between the magnets eliminated the return field in between the magnets but the strength of the field was reduced, depending on the mass of the steel bar.
I will continue to do experiments as time permits and share the results so we can all brainstorm ideas and hopefully come up with some inexpensive ways to harness the health benefits

### PaulG — 2015-05-27T04:43:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165496>

Actually stacking two magnets does increase strength due to increased mass.

If you stack the magnets the way they want to stack you will have no change in strength.
But if you can push the magnets together like this -----→ 》NS《》SN《》NS《 ←-------------- Then you can increase the field and strength. but the field extends far in one direction only

I think this is how the [magnetico supersleep 20gauss](https://magneticosleep.com/products/super-sleep-system/queen-4/) works but not sure.
[http://www.kjmagnetics.com/FAQ.asp#stack](http://www.kjmagnetics.com/FAQ.asp#stack)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-27T05:08:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-165498>

I know this can be a bit confusing but I believe the magnets have their north pole on top and the south pole underneath like this: N\_N\_N\_N with north being negative and south positive. S S S S
The thing that had me stuck for a while is that the north field comes straight out of the earth from under us and then curves gradually southward, not north to south horizontally like I originally thought.
Then they create a flux pattern like the diagram that you posted in the other thread:

### RyanMSauce — 2015-06-03T22:11:40+0300

Does anyone know the way to calculate the field strength of a proposed sleep bed design? Say for example if I used 144 4''x 6''x1'' ceramic magnets spaced at about 1 inch apart for a queen size bed. How can we estimate how strong that would be 8 inches away?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-04T04:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-166488>

I believe that the more magnets you add horizontally, the strength doesn't increase but the strength will reach further above. If you stack the mags on top of each other strength will increase.

### RyanMSauce — 2015-06-04T05:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-166490>

Yea I found an online calculator. I think if the magnets are place pretty close you can measure the total magnet area and put that into the calculations. I also spoke to a technician at a magnet company and the person said that the field strength of Neodymium magnets does not drop quicker with distance than a ceramic magnet but she could be wrong i need to talk to some experts tomorrow.

### RyanMSauce — 2015-06-06T20:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-166850>

I think if the mass of the ceramic magnet and the neodymium magnets are the same the gauss level at any distance will be stronger with the neodymium. If the two magnets have the same rating at the surface, then the neodymium's magnetic field will become weaker quicker as the distance ins increased vs the ceramic magnet because the neodymium will have much less mass.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-06T22:12:01+0300
