If the two magnets have the same rating at the surface, then the neodymium's magnetic field will become weaker quicker as the distance ins increased vs the ceramic magnet because the neodymium will have much less mass.

hmmm... interesting
I have never tested a neodymium magnet but I have noticed that with the ceramic type the more mass, the greater the distance of field strength.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-06T22:49:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-166864>

You best be careful with neodymium magnets..........Magnetic field strength is also a lot more significant at close distances than gravitational fields where you will use these pads. Moreover, magnetic flux always forms closed loops in space. This means that it effectively does not follow the inverse square law, but, instead, an inverse cube law. Big deal when using neodymium. This makes magnetic field strength drop off much, much more quickly with distance. I have two large neodymium magnets that would utterly crush all the bones in your hand if you were to place them on either side of it, but at a distance of a couple of feet from each other there is no perceptible interaction. These pads could really hurt someone and kill them during installation.

This is why gravity dominates at cosmological distances. It follows the inverse square law and is unrestrained by any form of "antigravity" that would cancel it out.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-07T02:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-sleep-pad-with-neodymium-magnets.14697/post-166886>

This makes magnetic field strength drop off much, much more quickly with distance. I have two large neodymium magnets that would utterly crush all the bones in your hand if you were to place them on either side of it, but at a distance of a couple of feet from each other there is no perceptible interaction. These pads could really hurt someone and kill them during installation.
>
> This is why gravity dominates at cosmological distances. It follows the inverse square law and is unrestrained by any form of "antigravity" that would cancel it out.

^^^^This is absolutely true.

# Thread: DHA food intolerances and allergies

**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rlee314 — 2015-05-26T03:14:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-food-intolerances-and-allergies.14702/post-165355>

does a diet high in DHA-when mom is pregnant and nursing and post weaning for the child-'protect' against a child developing food allergies?

i'm in facebook groups w/moms of children who have issues nursing, reflux and food intolerances. suggesting eating fish-which is often poo poo'd b/c 'fish is highly allergenic'-does not go over well w/most. some say they supplement with fish oils, DHA oils etc. also..after reading the SIBO/GERD/IBS UBI blog i have come to the conclusion that these moms need to mitigate blue lights and EMFs...wear blueblockers etc.

thoughts? suggestions? anyone?

desperate moms trying to heal their babies...of course mom needs to heal herself to heal her baby and likely major gut healing needs to happen as well...thinking DHA should be numero uno...

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21332799](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21332799)

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19489765](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19489765)

**Fish oil supplementation in pregnancy and lactation may decrease the risk of infant allergy.**
[Furuhjelm C](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Furuhjelm%20C%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765)1, [Warstedt K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Warstedt%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765), [Larsson J](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Larsson%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765), [Fredriksson M](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Fredriksson%20M%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765), [Böttcher MF](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=B%C3%B6ttcher%20MF%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765), [Fälth-Magnusson K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=F%C3%A4lth-Magnusson%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765), [Duchén K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Duch%C3%A9n%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=19489765).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19489765#)**

**Abstract**
Maternal intake of omega-3 (omega-3) polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) during pregnancy has decreased, possibly contributing to a current increased risk of childhood allergy.

**AIM:**
To describe the effects of maternal omega-3 long-chain PUFA supplementation during pregnancy and lactation on the incidence of allergic disease in infancy.

**METHODS:**
One hundred and forty-five pregnant women, affected by allergy themselves or having a husband or previous child with allergies, were included in a randomized placebo-controlled trial. Daily maternal supplementation with either 1.6 g eicosapentaenoic acid and 1.1 g docosahexaenoic acid or placebo was given from the 25(th) gestational week to average 3-4 months of breastfeeding. Skin prick tests, detection of circulating specific immunoglobulin E (IgE) antibodies and clinical examinations of the infants were performed.

**RESULTS:**
The period prevalence of food allergy was lower in the omega-3 group (1/52, 2%) compared to the placebo group (10/65, 15%, p < 0.05) as well as the incidence of IgE-associated eczema (omega-3 group: 4/52, 8%; placebo group: 15/63, 24%, p < 0.05).

**CONCLUSION:**
Maternal omega-3 fatty acid supplementation may decrease the risk of food allergy and IgE-associated eczema during the first year of life in infants with a family history of allergic disease.

# Thread: Full immersion vs. vest
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### zoobdo — 2015-05-26T17:20:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-165427>

Hey guys!
Im a long time reader of the forum as well as the book.

Quick question:
I have the Cool Fat Burner and Gut Buster as a lot of you guys know about here, I know Eric the creator has posted here a couple times.

My question is, if I want to use CT for the muscle building benefits and nutrient partitioning to keep fat gain minimal while eating enough for muscle growth.
Would it be smarter for me to do full immersion in my pool for 15-30min (and increasing as I become able) or to use the cool fat burner vest and gut buster for 1-2hr a day?

Any help or input is very welcome!

### shah78 — 2015-05-26T23:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-165483>

Depends on the season. In summer the cold tub (50 degrees) seems five to ten times more effective. In the winter, if you wear the CFB+GB outside the equities come much closer. But I'm always partial to the cold tub. Of course the CFB+GB is portable. Bottom line. Become a master of both! Getting better adapted to one, will help dealing with the other.

### Butters — 2015-05-27T09:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-165518>

Here is what I do, because the Tap water is getting "warmer" here in germany while summer is coming:

I get an initial temperature of 55 degrees from the water so I get in. I stay there as long as I can or as it gets boring (never happend so far, max was 50min). Then when I get out I often shiver - when it evening or morning it is even better because outside temperatures are lower - and I put on the CFB. That really ramps the whole thing up and I noticed really intense shivering that would bring the water in the tub over the rims if I stayed in.
I tried the CFB alone in these temperatures and it wasn't enough.

My tub doesn't allow for full immersion so I have to choose between cooling the more of the lower or the upper body. The CFB is able to cool the carotis triangle more effective, so I could combinate lower body water CT with the CFB. This way both the femoral and carotis triangle could be cooled.

### Drew12 — 2015-07-01T18:35:19+0300

### jackware — 2015-07-15T05:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-171978>

### Sascha Heid — 2016-03-22T17:06:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-191293>

I just got my cool fat burner but honestly i'm not sure what to do with it now.
First i used it for a short run. It was 5°C and i ran 3 miles with it. The cooling effect was very minimal, maybe 2°C less. It's about the same when you take off your light gloves or cap. Running barefoot gives a much stronger cooling effect and the additional benefit of grounding.
I had to put the CFB below my shirt to reduce the bouncing. So, unfortunately it's not worth bothering with it for a run. Second attempt was cycling. There is no bouncing which is good, but even less cooling because it reduces the airstream hitting your body. Reducing clothes on the bicycle is cooling much better and is more comfortable.
I guess i could use the CFB when i am not exercising, in front of the computer and such. But why should i? A cold shower for 1 minute certainly exceeds the cooling effect of a whole hour with the CFB and i can spend the remainder of the time being outside and getting some fresh air and light.
To be honest i think it's a ripoff because they claim a 300% increase of metabolic rate. Why? Because Wim Hof gives the same number but he is referring to ice-baths.
But i blame myself for being naive, it's very simple:
Imagine a bath in cold water which covers 90% of your body and a very low thermal resistance between the water and your skin.
The CFB covers maybe 10% of your body but with a much worse/higher thermal resistance, so in the end you are left with maybe 1% of the cooling-power compared to cold water immersion. And you can even make the water much colder with ice.
Don't buy it. Just take a cold shower.

### Butters — 2016-03-22T17:22:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-191295>

I dont know what you are doing. But the CFB has tremendous effects on me. Although not comparable to a cold tub, but thats not a shocker. You cant expect to have a cold bath without a cold bath.
But for folks sitting in front of the computer working or doing something, putting it on will of course do something.
Maybe you have it a number to big, that was mine the first time. Also I did sit on a couch with the back pressed on the CFB. More contact area- more cold. Do you have the hybrid packs?
The device is made for still things like watching TV or as i say laptop work.

### Sascha Heid — 2016-03-22T17:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-191298>

Ok, your usage scenario is much different compared to mine. I don't watch TV or sit around, I don't even have a chair. Don't know which packs but the softer ones and the size is alright (size M 104cm chest)

### Eric — 2016-03-23T18:07:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-191354>

> [Sascha Heid said:](/goto/post?id=191293)
>
> I just got my cool fat burner but honestly i'm not sure what to do with it now.
> First i used it for a short run. It was 5°C and i ran 3 miles with it.

Why would you wear it while exercising? All our literature explicitly says NOT to do that.

> [Sascha Heid said:](/goto/post?id=191293)
>
> A cold shower for 1 minute certainly exceeds the cooling effect of a whole hour with the CFB

Absolute nonsense. A cold shower won't touch what the CFB/CGB has already been proven to do several times now, in university labs using indirect calorimetry, where it's been proven to burn hundreds of calories an hour. You think you're burning hundreds of calories in a 1 minute shower? You won't do that after a 30 min shower.

> [Sascha Heid said:](/goto/post?id=191293)
>
> To be honest i think it's a ripoff because they claim a 300% increase of metabolic rate. Why? Because Wim Hof gives the same number but he is referring to ice-baths.

Have you even looked at our website or watched our videos? We have 3 times now demonstrated, at independent 3rd party university locations, that the Cool Fat Burner / Cool Gut Buster can boost metabolism upwards of 300%. In fact the current randomized group study is duplicating this effect over a dozen times.

> [Sascha Heid said:](/goto/post?id=191293)
>
> The CFB covers maybe 10% of your body but with a much worse/higher thermal resistance, so in the end you are left with maybe 1% of the cooling-power compared to cold water immersion. And you can even make the water much colder with ice.
> Don't buy it. Just take a cold shower.

Again, completely untrue and inaccurate. A cold shower won't touch what the CFB/CGB can do. And the surface area of the two vests ranges from 480 square inches to 720 square inches. It effectively covers your entire torso. Hence part of the reason why we're the only cooling vest shown to activate BAT, boost metabolism upwards of 300%, increase both irisin and adiponectin, increase glycogen depletion and inducing ketosis, and increasing RMR.

> [Drew12 said:](/goto/post?id=170232)
>
> How long does the vest stay effectively cold? Since the Ultra-Combo comes with one extra set of packs, you would need to purchase another extra set to cover an extended session for both the 'fat burner' and the 'gut burner'?

Here is a link to an interesting review someone just did the other day; notice where they compare our packs to several other packs (it's towards the bottom of the thread):

[https://www.crsociety.org/topic/114...-increased-health-longevity/page-6#entry15548](https://www.crsociety.org/topic/11488-cold-exposure-other-mild-stressors-for-increased-health-longevity/page-6#entry15548)

### Eric — 2016-03-23T18:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-immersion-vs-vest.14735/post-191355>

> [zoobdo said:](/goto/post?id=165427)
>
> Hey guys!
> Im a long time reader of the forum as well as the book.
>
> Quick question:
> I have the Cool Fat Burner and Gut Buster as a lot of you guys know about here, I know Eric the creator has posted here a couple times.
>
> My question is, if I want to use CT for the muscle building benefits and nutrient partitioning to keep fat gain minimal while eating enough for muscle growth.
> Would it be smarter for me to do full immersion in my pool for 15-30min (and increasing as I become able) or to use the cool fat burner vest and gut buster for 1-2hr a day?
>
> Any help or input is very welcome!

The main ways that CT can help with clean bulking (gaining muscle w/ minimal to little fat gain) are:
1\. increasing irisin
2\. increasing adiponectin
3\. glycogen depletion, allowing for carb refeeds / glycogen supercompensation
4\. increased insulin sensitivity (kinda similar to # 3)

All of those, especially 1 & 2 (hormonal responses) have the largest response, from shivering. Probably glycogen depletion does as well.

Another variable here is the timing of it all, as well as what to eat w/ what meals, etc...

Meal timing and composition,
resistance training (many, many variables there),
cold thermogenesis (timing, intensity, duration)

All of that will factor in, and can change muscle growth and physical performance.

I'm hoping to get "[www.CoolFatBurnerGuides.com](http://www.CoolFatBurnerGuides.com)" up and running very soon, and it will address a lot of those specifics. It sounds complicated, but it's really just a bunch of simple concepts layered on top of one another.

If you haven't seen them, here are two sources I did that contains a lot of info on using CT to help with body recomposition and muscle building:

1\. [Ben Greenfield article](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/12/day-life-ultimate-fat-burning-weapon-inventor/)

2\. [Body Recomposition video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JkzhyVOgfb4)

THE GROUP'S PURPOSE AND CRITICAL THINKING
1\. Our goal is to help you teach yourself → [http://on.fb.me/1cEfwfl](http://on.fb.me/1cEfwfl)
2\. Why curiousity helps with learning and retention →[http://bit.ly/1RQbhOo](http://bit.ly/1RQbhOo)

4\. 9 rules that will help you detect B.S. → [http://bit.ly/1JQZKbW](http://bit.ly/1JQZKbW)
5\. What you should consider before trying any biohack →[http://on.fb.me/1FBxRDL](http://on.fb.me/1FBxRDL)
6\. Want to get a friend in the group or have a general question about the group? → Tag the creator and admin, Jason Lauritzen, in your post or comment.

CIRCADIAN RHYTHMS, LIGHT AND UBIQUITINATION
1\. What is the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) and why is it important? →[http://bit.ly/1FNjKNh](http://bit.ly/1FNjKNh)

5\. Screw up your circadian rhythm and memory and learning suffer →[http://on.fb.me/1FUPZfh](http://on.fb.me/1FUPZfh)

>
> COLD THERMOGENESIS (CT)
> 1\. Read Jack’s complete CT series (13 in all) as your starting point →[http://bit.ly/1EcBb4e](http://bit.ly/1EcBb4e)
> 2\. Ready to start CT? Read this overview of the protocol →[http://bit.ly/1IFyAaI](http://bit.ly/1IFyAaI)
> 3\. The CT easy-start guide → [http://bit.ly/1e8GbBZ](http://bit.ly/1e8GbBZ)
> 4.Don’t have access to a tub you can fill with ice for CT? An alternative is to use the Cool Fat Buster → [http://bit.ly/1IJEHJJ](http://bit.ly/1IJEHJJ). (Bonus: Eric Grove, the creator, is in the group.)
> 5\. CT converts white adipose tissue (WAT) to brown adipose tissue (BAT) → [http://on.fb.me/1JR1emG](http://on.fb.me/1JR1emG) [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-exposure-cold-reveals-formation-brown.html?fbclid=IwAR1Atj-S-AYpN6eC8mB3yd4QORn3zD4UQUH9xoOvpOsp756ohQCTJEBkkyc](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-exposure-cold-reveals-formation-brown.html?fbclid=IwAR1Atj-S-AYpN6eC8mB3yd4QORn3zD4UQUH9xoOvpOsp756ohQCTJEBkkyc)
> 6\. How CT activates different pathways to generate body heat →[http://on.fb.me/1PrpXor](http://on.fb.me/1PrpXor) [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141110110104.htm?fbclid=IwAR1LGWWpL0m8koE6T0Jc\_r5QqrWRCCjz4MlGdCITRR8PlLwmuxRlMoztqec](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141110110104.htm?fbclid=IwAR1LGWWpL0m8koE6T0Jc_r5QqrWRCCjz4MlGdCITRR8PlLwmuxRlMoztqec)
> 7\. Cold air exposure increases BAT, T3 synthesis and glucose utilization → [http://on.fb.me/1FlOyEu](http://on.fb.me/1FlOyEu)
> 8\. Why you should ignore articles about pills that convert WAT to BAT →[http://on.fb.me/1Ke2Av5](http://on.fb.me/1Ke2Av5)

> 10\. Shivering from CT promotes irisin which helps with cancer cell apoptosis → [http://on.fb.me/1B5mDmW](http://on.fb.me/1B5mDmW) [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015...bYgGGJBP4gZgeHDrLE09XHkRcZ5RUizfFmdNyjXSCcl9I](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-02-irisin-aggressive-cancer-cells-negatively.html?fbclid=IwAR2xG9c6biWCWabYgGGJBP4gZgeHDrLE09XHkRcZ5RUizfFmdNyjXSCcl9I)
> 11\. CT helps remove superoxide → [http://on.fb.me/1ET0MAL](http://on.fb.me/1ET0MAL) More on CT and hydrogen sulfide and removing superoxide by eliminating T cells that generate massive amounts of superoxide. [http://ow.ly/LwUux](http://ow.ly/LwUux?fbclid=IwAR3bedgRdB9IehlUNyXreedoLEbvWz19piQ192_lzkqZhiO4Sh931ol2RwM)
> Cold allows leptin easier access to your brain → [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-brain-bodies-fat.html?fbclid=IwAR3rapgZmELf7RaamA2dz1mxq3L2DjkV3utG95GofWrYYEAJQyQBdrj2g\_k](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-brain-bodies-fat.html?fbclid=IwAR3rapgZmELf7RaamA2dz1mxq3L2DjkV3utG95GofWrYYEAJQyQBdrj2g_k)

# Thread: What to consider going from high north to a bit south of the equator
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-consider-going-from-high-north-to-a-bit-south-of-the-equator.14739/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Peertje80 — 2015-05-26T22:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-consider-going-from-high-north-to-a-bit-south-of-the-equator.14739/post-165472>

In November I'll be visiting Brasil for 2 weeks. In preparation I'm of course going to read Jet leg rx. I'm sort of used to jet leg, flying to Japan forth and back. But I've never went across the equator. Messing up not only time but season as well. 2 months ago I would not have considered asking but...

I'm wondering what I should prepare for? How much time would it take to get in sink with light and season? For instance should I stick to my high fat low carb winter diet? Or can I eat local? And with respect to light... would it be safe to be out in the sun at midday for me (red head, albino skinned) or should I use sunscreen again? What times are safe? Can I exercises or would this mess me up even worse? Is there maybe something I'm forgetting in my preparation that has to do being on the south side? Does somebody have experience going from far north to south?

Much appreciated

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-26T22:57:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-consider-going-from-high-north-to-a-bit-south-of-the-equator.14739/post-165473>

more water and beware of the decrease in the time needed in the sun. DHA in tissues will extend sunning abilities

# Thread: How much Sun to get? Melanin and DHA?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RyanMSauce — 2015-05-27T06:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165501>

I have been reading through the blogs and what I got today is that DHA in the tissue increases the electric charge potential and that it can also create/store energy from the sun.

So we add energy into our bodies by getting sunlight? How much sunlight against the bare skin is optimal in a day if you are in a place like phoenix or los angeles? Just enough not to get burned?

Since melanin captures the energy of the sun is it involved in the process of dha capturing energy from the sun?

Is having more melanin a good thing or does it depend on how much light you get? I have been taking the Melontan 2 peptide a few times a week while I go out in the sun and I can get really tan quickly from it if I wanted to.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-27T07:29:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165503>

As much sun as possible . All other questions answered here: -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-to-do-if-i-get-sunburns.13815/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-to-do-if-i-get-sunburns.13815/)

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-05-27T10:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165526>

my belief on melanin is that it upregulates as vitamin D/sun exposure becomes optimal

this is based on observation of northern polulations gettingw hiter as they moved to places with less photonic power/duration
it appears that the absence of melanin increases the ability to "grab" the sun
as melanin increases it would be a sign that this "need" is diminishing, possibly debunking the aussie mass messaging by the anti cancer council which states "theres nothing healthy about a tan"

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-27T10:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165530>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=165526)
>
> my belief on melanin is that it upregulates as vitamin D/sun exposure becomes optimal
>
> this is based on observation of northern polulations gettingw hiter as they moved to places with less photonic power/duration
> it appears that the absence of melanin increases the ability to "grab" the sun
> as melanin increases it would be a sign that this "need" is diminishing, possibly debunking the aussie mass messaging by the anti cancer council which states "theres nothing healthy about a tan"

I would argue that the more melanin you have, the more of the Sun's energy you can make use of. Melanin absorbs UV radiation well, and then tapers off until you hit an absorption low at 700nm -- [http://www.cl.cam.ac.uk/~jgd1000/melanin.html](http://www.cl.cam.ac.uk/~jgd1000/melanin.html) . This also likely implies that the absorbed light from melanin gets re-emitted at wavelengths above the 500nm range, which is getting closer to red light that is again usable by the body.

Melanin production itself is triggered by UV light exposure. Too much UV light screws up DNA signalling. Incoming UV light needs to be regulated \-- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/neurons-protons-and-lactate.14517/#post-164393](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/neurons-protons-and-lactate.14517/#post-164393) . Too much red light doesn't screw up signalling, and I even suspect that the more sun-derived red light the better.

So with more melanin, you can literally absorb excess UV light and turn it to red light, while continuing to absorb the rest of the (less regulated) spectrum of sunlight.

....

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-05-27T10:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165531>

good call...why then did humans get white af when heading north...my theory is debunked, however i have never really read about it so who am i to have a theory lol

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-27T11:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-165535>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=165531)
>
> good call...why then did humans get white af when heading north...my theory is debunked, however i have never really read about it so who am i to have a theory lol

Probably because there wasn't enough UV light for signalling at the extreme latitudes given our present solar environment. So you drop pigmentation both in skin and eyes to allow for as much UV signalling as possible. Hence white skin and blue eyes.

If you believe some of the DNA evidence out there, blue eyes didn't appear until around 10,000-8,000 years ago, with white skin following after. It would make sense that eye colour changes faster, since the light coming into the eye is far more important for cryptochrome and opsin mediated circadian signalling, and of course, the eye is a smaller area, where less pigmentation changes needs to occur for change in colour.

Also note that this fits the Proto-Saturn as original Star theory. That model predicts electrical capture of Saturn by our current Sun about >10,000 years ago. During the reign of Saturn, the model predicts a uniform distribution of UV and IR radiation globally (enveloped by the Saturn heliosphere). There would be no reason to evolve blue eyes or white skin in that context. After Saturn was lost however, then we get the current differing spectrum of solar radiation, which demands white eye and less-pigmented blue eyes at the extreme latitudes.

....

### lohd2015 — 2016-02-15T01:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-189048>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=165530)
>
> I would argue that the more melanin you have, the more of the Sun's energy you can make use of. Melanin absorbs UV radiation well, and then tapers off until you hit an absorption low at 700nm -- [http://www.cl.cam.ac.uk/~jgd1000/melanin.html](http://www.cl.cam.ac.uk/~jgd1000/melanin.html) . This also likely implies that the absorbed light from melanin gets re-emitted at wavelengths above the 500nm range, which is getting closer to red light that is again usable by the body.
>
> Melanin production itself is triggered by UV light exposure. Too much UV light screws up DNA signalling. Incoming UV light needs to be regulated \-- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/neurons-protons-and-lactate.14517/#post-164393](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/neurons-protons-and-lactate.14517/#post-164393) . Too much red light doesn't screw up signalling, and I even suspect that the more sun-derived red light the better.
>
> So with more melanin, you can literally absorb excess UV light and turn it to red light, while continuing to absorb the rest of the (less regulated) spectrum of sunlight.
>
> ....

Several questions, please:

1\. I understand that once the skin has turned dark from melanin, it shows that the body can produce melanin. But does that mean you now need even more sunlight to achieve the same results since your skin is darker?

2\. Is the melanin pigmentation on the skin still active or are their 'storage sites' filled to capacity, and the body must produce new melanin in order to capture more sunlight (hence the skin will get even darker?)

3\. If that's the case, the darker you can get the better? Is that different from someone who is born with dark skin (who needs more sunlight than a fair skinned person)?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-15T01:39:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/post-189049>

Not exactly.......think the next few blogs will tease that out.

# Thread: Could Apple earphones cause iEczema?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-apple-earphones-cause-ieczema.14776/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-05-27T19:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-apple-earphones-cause-ieczema.14776/post-165570>

I think I may have tracked down the cause of the eczema I was asking for advice about on these boards last year.

It has occurred periodically inside my ears - and only there.

I went to my doctor - late last summer, I think - and she said something about no one knowing the reason for that kind of thing, and perhaps it was a change in the weather. I asked her if it could be related to diet or alcohol consumption or anything like that, and she said no. That was as far as it went.

Here's the thing. It went away and I haven't been troubled with it for months.

But it just came back. I realised its reappearance has coincided with my buying a new Apple iPod. Now I just remember that around June last year I replaced the earphones on my last iPod and at that time I actually got the genuine Apple ones - earphones will break fairly often (my carelessness, I guess) and so I've used various replacements from different manufacturers over time.

Now the new iPod, of course, came with Apple earphones. And guess what: the eczema in my ears, which has been absent for months, came back. I put two and two together and was immediately suspicious. Anyway, a few days ago I caught the earphone lead in something - a door catch - and it stretched and stopped working. For about the last three days I've been using a old pair of JVC headphones I had and, over the same time period, the eczema has slowly cleared up and is now almost gone.

I have two explanations. Either: Apple might be using some nasty hardener in its plastic and it tends to migrate out of the plastic and into one's skin in use and can cause problems; or this is an EMF effect. If the latter, perhaps Apple headphones produce a stronger field than many other makes do. Interesting …

I've never owned an iPhone. I always use my mobile phone on speakerphone. I've not thought much about an iPod - as opposed to iPhone - presenting any sort of potential EMF problem. But perhaps it does.

On the same note - I suppose watch batteries can't produce much of a field ... or can they? I have come across a couple of statements to the effect that while it's a small one its effects might not be negligible. I did hear a comment that a watch would be "right over an important acupuncture meridian". Now I'm not over-credulous of "alternative health" but meridians certainly do seem to exist - didn't Robert O. Becker show that? I'm beginning to wonder whether I should have given my old-fashioned automatic, and actually rather nice Swiss-movement, watch away now. I was getting fed up with the cost of having it cleaned every few years - as you should really do - and thought a battery-powered watch would be a better option - cheaper maintenance costs!

Thoughts????

### fitness@home — 2015-05-27T20:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-apple-earphones-cause-ieczema.14776/post-165573>

> I have two explanations. Either: Apple might be using some nasty hardener in its plastic and it tends to migrate out of the plastic and into one's skin in use and can cause problems; or this is an EMF effect. If the latter, perhaps Apple headphones produce a stronger field than many other makes do. Interesting …

Sounds like some testing is in order!

The accupuncture merideans are where the bacteria in the teeth - think: root canal - travel to infect organs : Jerry Tennant's facebook page contains a picture of the merideans and which teeth affect which organs - his book "Healing is Voltage" is chalk full of great info I have not found anywhere else - it only takes 3 milligausse to produce an effect BTW... remember "the square of the distance" and "measure measure measure" -

Becker, M.D., and biophysicist Maria Reichmanis were able to prove that electrical currents did flow along the ancient Chinese meridians and that 25% of the acupuncture points existed along those scientifically measurable lines. ### Gagnrad — 2015-05-27T21:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-apple-earphones-cause-ieczema.14776/post-165578>

Thank you, Penny, for that long and detailed answer.

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=165575)

> Skip the watch - any watch will contain metal - this makes you an antenna - this goes for all metal jewellery -

Really? I haven't been around for months. Does Dr. K advise that? He seems to have a metal pendant round his neck in this - very interesting - interview:

> "In the 1970s, Robert O. Becker, M.D., and biophysicist Maria Reichmanis were able to prove that electrical currents did flow along the ancient Chinese meridians and that 25% of the acupuncture points existed along those scientifically measurable lines. They reasoned that these points acted as amplifiers to boost the minute electrical signals as they traveled along the body and that the insertion of a needle could interfere with the flow and block the stimulus of pain."

Yes. I had one of Becker's books - though where it is now I don't know. I probably lent it to someone who neither looked at it nor gave it back …

I can recall a lot in different bodywork circles a few decades ago - people saying that the Indian and Chinese and Japanese stuff makes a kind of sense, but you don't have to believe in prana/chi/ki you can just regard it as a metaphor. It seems much more likely to me that this "life-force" or whatever you want to call it does exist. And I guess Becker showed that. I find people who are always looking to the Orient faintly risible - it's a form of superstition: believing something is significant simply because it's strange to oneself. But I think these "energetic" views are not so much "Oriental" as something that was probably once universal and that, in any case, probably do capture something of how things are.

I think it's interesting that the "Iceman" - the Copper Age frozen body found in the glacier not the Dutch guy - has tattoos over what are known to be acupuncture points:

[http://www.iceman.it/en/tattoos](http://www.iceman.it/en/tattoos)

No one expected anything like that.

That fits in with what seems to be said in these references. I'm still left with the question: "Why are the Appel ones specifically bad?" I think it may be because those white Apple earphones are shaped to fit very closely into the ear - they're sort of pointed and slide in. Most earphones are round rather than shaped to totally fill the cavity.

On quartz watches and EMF - I found this:

I thought this was quite interesting, too:

> The magnetic field from an analogue Quartz watch is in the region of a few milligauss, i.e. spikes of a couple of Milligauss every second, with nothing in between. Digital quartz watches emit a negligible if detectable magnetic field, with no pulsation detected. A digital quartz watch will only emit a significant magnetic field when the backlight is switched on or sounds are made, which is in excess of the every-second field spike from an analogue quartz watch. The spikes from an analogue quartz watch are greater than the constant magnetic field from a set of HiFi speakers at a reasonable volume (if sat a couple of metres away).

[http://www.medicalinsider.com/electromagnetic.html](http://www.medicalinsider.com/electromagnetic.html)

Hey everyone. I stood in the sun for about 15-20 with my feet in the dirt this morning at about 10am and then I lying in the sun for about 20 min at about 1pm. After the 1 pm session I felt drained and tired. This has happened before. I also feel like this if I do my far infrared sauna for a while. What could be going on here?

### Peertje80 — 2015-05-28T00:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-tired-from-the-sun.14777/post-165588>

In the morning I can tolerate 1 or 2 hours of sun, but between noon and 3PM even 15 minutes will still fry me. Also when I lay down on the earth, I become really sleepy and just want to continue hugging the earth. Pretty weird.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-01T08:34:07+0300

### LisaLearning — 2015-08-22T23:45:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-tired-from-the-sun.14777/post-175398>

I wonder if the siesta or riposo has more to do with morning sun exposure than a large midday meal (as I have always heard).

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T01:13:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-tired-from-the-sun.14777/post-175402>

Getting tired from the sun is a reaction to what the sun can do to a trashed hormone panel of a lifetime of mismatches cause by calcium efflux. What are the human photosynthetic chemicals that sun light makes? Vit D3, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups of cholesterol in the skin I mentioned in Tensegrity 7 blog post on my website........[https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/)

What does it destroy naturally? UV light lowers adrenalin the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system. When it drops in someone who is chronically stressed sleep can be induced. This is a sign of how bad your environment really is. UV and IR AM light also lowers aberrant steroids (CT 7) and nucleic acids without any liberation of heat on the skin's surface. This helps preventing aberrant optical scattering of cell signaling below (non linear optics). In this way it serves as a supreme example of how the surface photochemistry controls the biochemistry of your cells below in fat cells and in your organs. Biochemistry is not deterministic as most believe (think about the weather today while you lived, does it affect you? Its a surface effect too on your biology!).......it is determined by what happens to the surfaces in our eye, skin, and gut. This is why circadian biology are incredibly important. Natural harmony requires diurnal and seasonal destruction and renewal. Without renewal we cannot sustain. We have to come apart so we don't fall apart.

Nature presents us with a series of great opportunities brilliantly disguised as impossible situations. We are forced to take our atoms and collide them against the light we face in our daily lives. If that light i man made you get a facade of a life.

I told you all long ago the KT event was key to understanding Jack Kruse's perspective on things. This answer has that shot of truth in it........WHY?

This is why I get so pissed with providers who deal with genetic/SNP's data from 23andme.......they think, and make those with SNP's believe, they can change the inside of their genome with supplements.....and they can't. This is akin to the story of the KT event and why geologist did not believe the asteroid impact story for so long. When Alvarez postulated the sequence of events that occurred 65 million years ago do you know why geologist thought he was crazy? Because prior to 1990, no one had ever thought that an asteroid from space (outside Earth's realm) could change Earth fundamentally. They just could not see how an outside force from space could change the energy flows to reverse photosynthesis in a short period of time. That is what happened. ........they just thought climate weathering was the key to surface erosion. Modern humans are making the same error in judgment. None of them can fathom ho blue light or non EMF can change their cells biochemistry deep in their heads and organs.
Alvarez's ideas made them realize they had to look further outside. I am doing the same thing. I am telling people that genes and SNP's are not tied to endogenous breakdown in your nucleic acids or the way you work. It is tied to the environment you allow and via ubiquitination rates they become expressed.........that is when they are a problem. Genomic/SNP's therapies requires an environmental clean up first and foremost and not a pill or surgery. The Rx should not be prescribing to do things the other way around trying to solve the SNP presence. It is very similar to the KT event perspective of Alvarez. Do you see it yet? It is an environmental epigenetic issue tied to an altered environment increasing ubiquitin rates. SNP's just slow down epigenetic changes and this is why in environments with higher exogenous energy flows they get sicker faster than other people.

A sphere within a sphere can create electrical tension. Schumann taught us this in 1951, and yet we still don't realize he was teaching us about how energy can or can't flow from outside in or inside out. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the ionosphere. That tension becomes a pulse wave for thing that live within both spheres. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling changes between things in a cell. Blue light and nn EMF change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things that live upon its tectonic plates.

Awareness, alone doesn't enlist change. You all have known this........you have to understand why you must change your environment. Then wellness will visit you.

Learn about the sun........and your skin..........you have a lot yet to learn about the photo electric effect and biology. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=141&v=1zBBmh8xQhM&app=desktop](https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=141&v=1zBBmh8xQhM&app=desktop)

### LisaLearning — 2015-08-23T21:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-tired-from-the-sun.14777/post-175456>

Thanks for this, Jack. I get that being mismatched to light signals and being around lots of nnEMF causes a lot of stress, and then I can see how sunlight destroying the resulting adrenaline (I didn't know that) could cause this sleepiness reaction to sun exposure. I don't get why you told Ryan that this happening was a good sign. Is it a good sign because it means there was some sun exposure, or am I missing something?

It made me think back to being a kid (the 70s) when I played a lot of sports and I recall one of my coaches insisting that we not swim in outdoor pools or sunbathe on days that we had to play matches against other teams - he argued that sun exposure made us tired. Of course, in the 70s, we children played out in the sun all day (during the summer, anyway) and were very light-yoked, and there was so much less EMF, but it's interesting that people noticed this sort of effect of sunlight back then. I'm not sure if that means this has been happening for a much longer time than I think, or what it means, apart from something about sun being tiring is not really recent, although perhaps more common.

But I have a side question about the histamine. I googled around a bit bc I didn't recall you saying anything about histamine production in the skin in response to sunlight (my memory is not superlative as it once was), and when I didn't get anywhere with that, I went back and read Tensegrity 7 again, and now I am thinking you are not saying that optimal humans produce histamine in response to sunlight, but perhaps that humans who are deficient in sulfated D3 due the stressors mentioned above, these people who have responded to the sun as though it were a toxin, they are the ones with higher levels of vitamin A in tissues, and this somehow creates some histamine response (that I am all too familiar with, with my photo sensitivity). Is that what you were saying when you mentioned histamine in your response above?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-15T03:13:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-tired-from-the-sun.14777/post-196587>

Look at your mtDNA haplotypes and then re watch the Wallace video. Y'all will be brilliant soon enough.

# Thread: Biohacking Creativity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-05-28T16:01:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/post-165672>

Hi People from the Krusian Planet.
I've been having ideas about how to get cool ideas from the void of your mind. (what I call being creative) We all know they come when they want to come, but they are often so necessary and useful. It always means putting your mind in a certain state (put a certain mindset, give a problem to your mind). I noticed a lots of things help:
\-NOT BEING STRESSED. Being really relaxed. Really important. Lots of good thoughts come from meditation.
\-Great sleep. Sleep like shit, you're gonna have shitty thoughts. So to me sleep is the most fundamental thing.
\-Being in the sun exposed, being outdoors, moving and enjoying nature. Especially the beach.
\-Eating seafood, tons of it.

Doesn't it seems like ideas you see here on jackkruse.com?
You've got ideas, things that work for you? Share.. I am sure if Jack comes around here he will give pages of complicated biochemical terms lol.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-28T19:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/post-165704>

The higher my redox goes the more creative I get. If you want to remain calm and peaceful as you go through life, you have to have high intention and low attachment. Creativity manifests. You must do everything you can to create your desired outcomes, and then you let it go. Potential is what is within us to make each of us potent. But true potency is only determined when it is delivered. Potential energy has to be transmuted to kinetic energy. The more you do the better creativity becomes.

### caroline — 2015-05-29T03:35:21+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-29T04:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/post-165753>

weakly held and meta stable to all possibilities = creativity.

### NeilBB — 2015-05-29T05:11:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/post-165754>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=165750)
>
> still not sure I get it ^^^^^

You have to always be willing to let go of whatever no longer serves the purpose of your life...
Ideas. Jobs. Houses. Countries. Money. Toys. Family. Friends. The past. Doesn't matter.
No unconditional loyalties. No sacred cows. No free passes. No blank checks.
Loyalties must be earned and renewed regularly. And it isn't always "fair."
To believe otherwise is to shackle one's own soul. A soul needs freedom to be creative.

> An inventor is a man who asks 'Why?' of the universe and lets nothing stand between the answer and his mind.

If you don't like my answer, consider Will-I-Am's, which is pretty good too:

### Peertje80 — 2015-05-29T14:38:21+0300

### prAna303 — 2015-05-29T17:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-creativity.14819/post-165838>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=165672)
>
> \-NOT BEING STRESSED. Being really relaxed. Really important. Lots of good thoughts come from meditation.

Stress for me is vital, same as conflict. Stress makes me take on new paths and i tend to love being in it. When i did work like most normal humans do i loved business with high stress and big chances to make money, why? In these places you can not rely on old actions, old laws and workflows. The flow is me not a guideline...

I hate it when a work goes into, soft and mellow. This is when i must make some actions and i often do not care about others, it is me, myself and i. So at a place where we should eat cake on friday and hold hands i tend to be the guy with shitty attitude.

Life must be based on tons of choices not limited by rules, here i see myself as a modern world outlaw.

Relaxed in what i do is because i think we can change how time dictates our choices. When being in a state where energy can penetrate you in everykind of way, thru skin, thru nose, thru hearing, thru connection with other living things you can again become creative.

As i have been for so long now, critical to modern technology and how i felt in my senses i can only feel a chill with how the big herd is adding it to their own being.

# Thread: Salicylates

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-28T16:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salicylates.14820/post-165674>

Ok, I'm about ready to throw in the towel. I can't believe this is my life right now. I have no idea where to go from here. I posted about my major food reactions - oxalates, salicylates, histamine, amines, etc, etc. I thought I would have a breakthrough this summer with getting in the pool and doing some major CT. We just spent the $ to convert it to saltwater, so I was super stoked.

I've been getting in the last few days and have not been doing well - face burning up in the evenings, ears turning red, feeling near suicidal - not sure what was happening. Now I find out chlorine is super high salicylate and even saltwater systems get converted to chlorine! I'm supposed to be taking Biocidin and all these other broad-spectrum herbs and I find out they are all high salicylate and high oxalate! Everything I'm supposed to do to help me seems to always backfire on me!

I don't know how much more of this I can take. I stay positive for so long, but I just don't feel like I can handle all of this anymore. I'm eating so much seafood that I'm literally going broke. My food bills are outrageous. We just spent the $ to convert the pool and now I find I shouldn't even be using it. Ican't be on my computer to research as much as I want to because my hand literally goes ice cold, gets numb and painful at the same time and I'm having trouble gripping things.

I'll still keep up with the cold baths with hydrogen peroxide as much as I can, but I'm just so frustrated. I want to turn this frustration into something positive and fix this once and for all, but I fear my gut is just so trashed nothing is working. It really feels like everything I do just backfires on me.

I'm taking l-glutamine, gelatin, aloe, and SeaCure to heal my gut - have been for months now and none of it seems to be working.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-28T17:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salicylates.14820/post-165682>

Aspirin increases the EZ in water..........This thread tells me your redox is bad because your needle is slow to move.......your choice if you stop charging your battery but I have a sense if you do it is not going to be a wise choice.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-28T17:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salicylates.14820/post-165686>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - Here's where I get so worried: I question whether the reaction to salicylates is doing more damage than the good of being in the pool. So you're saying I should just "push through" the reaction and stick with the pool? That's usually what my husband tells me to do too, but I always worry I'm making things worse by having the reaction because isn't that a sign of inflammation?

WHen I have these reactions I end up with the back of my neck literally feeling like there is a fire inside it all night long. That can't be good, can it?! I've always said I'll deal with ANYTHING as long as I know it's helping. I'm just always afraid of making things worse.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-28T18:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salicylates.14820/post-165689>

What I am saying is that your struggle sounds like your redox when you began was very bad........youre getting frustrated because your ascension is not happening on your time table. This is when labs tell you how bad the redox really was........worrying about it will make it worse. Test these things with your doc or forget about it.......and continue to do the things that will help you while paying attention to your changes as time elapses.

I haven't really been exercising barely at all since I've starting following Jack's recommendations because he doesn't really advocate it that much when you are sick and it seemed like it would increase the chances of getting a headache.

### nicld — 2015-06-08T16:14:54+0300

Gotcha...Then CT it is for me too. Thanks for clearing that up for me. I was actually going to start a thread (CT vs. exercise) but I don't need to now. I definitely have inflammation too; migraines, fatigue, mood problems etc.
CT after work in a nearby mountain creek is just awesome! I'm so fortunate to have that so close to home as well as the ocean
I used to weight train pretty heavily for almost 10 years. At the same time I would go to the bar on the weekend, drink and stay out really late, sometimes until 5 AM! I was also on an SSRI during this entire period so I think my 'mammalian battery' will take a while to recharge.

### nicld — 2015-06-09T16:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-during-the-day.14858/post-167225>

I am hoping to start some strength training later this summer and get out on my bike again. For me, biking is just enjoyable and does not seem like a workout as long as I don't set a minimum distance or time or calories burned.

I used to be hard core, insane amount of working out all in the effort to achieve the "ideal" body and tracked EVERYTHING. I used to get up by 4:30 am to be on a trail by 5:30 to go for a 20 mile bike ride before work and then go hiking after work (talk about sleep deprivation). When I switched jobs and could no longer go to the trail I was using, I started getting up and running at home in the morning before work, then seeing a trainer after work who worked me hard. By the end of my time with him I felt I was losing strength and stamina, not gaining. I also gained weight while training for my half marathon, hello inflammation!!!!

When we know better we do better so CT away!!!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-10T05:50:27+0300

>

You got it! Sounds like we had similar goals. I was looking for the perfect body too.
I wanted to be a 'chick magnet' (lol!) like the rock stars of 'hair metal' in the '80's: a fantasy a lot of us guys who grew up in the '80's had.
Later down the road I would find that I would get tendonitis really easily, especially in my shoulders and wrists so I had to back off heavy lifting.
I'm tempted to hit the weights again because my legs are starting to look 'elderly' (thin). but I really need to get my mood and energy back. I've had this issue for so long that it's hard to imagine feeling 'normal'. I used to be very social (a looong time ago) but now I avoid people (even my wife!) because for me just having a conversation is exhausting. I think a lot of it is because I have to become 'fake' - I have to fake being interested in whatever we are talking about and I have to focus harder because of the lack of interest.

### nicld — 2015-06-11T02:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-during-the-day.14858/post-167483>

My thought is that I no longer really need to attract anyone. I have a great husband and we have been together for over 26 years now (yes we started dating when i was really young) and he loves me no matter what I look like. > Global incidence rates for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) have gradually risen over the past 20 years. Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) have identified over 160 genetic loci associated with IBD; however, inherited factors only account for a partial contribution to the disease risk. We have recently shown that urban airborne particulate matter (PM) ingested via contaminated food can alter gut microbiome and immune function under normal and inflammatory conditions. In this addendum, we will discuss how PM can modify the gut microbial form and function, provide evidence on changes seen in intestinal barrier, and suggest a working hypothesis of how pollutants affect the gastrointestinal tract. The significance of the work presented could lead to identifying airborne pollutants as potential risk factors and thus provide better patient care management.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4063847/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4063847/)

### Josh — 2015-05-30T01:24:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-15-is-live.14864/post-165866>

> In recent years, nitric oxide (NO), a gas previously considered to be a potentially toxic chemical, has been established as a diffusible universal messenger that mediates cell–cell communication throughout the body. Constitutive and inducible NO production regulate numerous essential functions of the gastrointestinal mucosa, such as maintenance of adequate perfusion, regulation of microvascular and epithelial permeability, and regulation of the immune response. Up-regulation of the production of NO via expression of inducible nitric oxide synthase (iNOS) represents part of a prompt intestinal antibacterial response; however, NO has also been associated with the initiation and maintenance of inflammation in human inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Recent studies on animal models of experimental IBD have shown that constitutive and inducible NO production seems to be beneficial during acute colitis, but sustained up-regulation of NO is detrimental. This fact is also supported by studies on mice genetically deficient in various NOS isoforms. However, the mechanism by which NO proceeds from being an indispensable homeostatic regulator to a harmful destructor remains unknown. Furthermore, extrapolation of data from animal colitis models to human IBD is questionable. The purpose of this review is to update our knowledge about the role of this universal mediator and the enzymes that generate it in the pathogenesis of IBD.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1782592/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1782592/)

### Josh — 2015-05-30T01:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-15-is-live.14864/post-165868>

> The biological nitrogen cycle involves step-wise reduction of nitrogen oxides to ammonium salts and oxidation of ammonia back to nitrites and nitrates by plants and bacteria. Neither process has been thought to have relevance to mammalian physiology; however in recent years the salivary bacterial reduction of nitrate to nitrite has been recognized as an important metabolic conversion in humans. Several enteric bacteria have also shown the ability of catalytic reduction of nitrate to ammonia via nitrite during dissimilatory respiration; however, the importance of this pathway in bacterial species colonizing the human intestine has been little studied. We measured nitrite, nitric oxide (NO) and ammonia formation in cultures of Escherichia coli, Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium species grown at different sodium nitrate concentrations and oxygen levels. We found that the presence of 5 mM nitrate provided a growth benefit and induced both nitrite and ammonia generation in E.coli and L. plantarum bacteria grown at oxygen concentrations compatible with the content in the gastrointestinal tract. Nitrite and ammonia accumulated in the growth medium when at least 2.5 mM nitrate was present. Time-course curves suggest that nitrate is first converted to nitrite and subsequently to ammonia. Strains of L.rhamnosus, L.acidophilus and B.longum infantis grown with nitrate produced minor changes in nitrite or ammonia levels in the cultures. However, when supplied with exogenous nitrite, NO gas was readily produced independently of added nitrate. Bacterial production of lactic acid causes medium acidification that in turn generates NO by non-enzymatic nitrite reduction. In contrast, nitrite was converted to NO by E.coli cultures even at neutral pH. We suggest that the bacterial nitrate reduction to ammonia, as well as the related NO formation in the gut, could be an important aspect of the overall mammalian nitrate/nitrite/NO metabolism and is yet another way in which the microbiome links diet and health.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4372352/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4372352/)

_**Figure 79.2.**— Urea cycle and associated pathways: (1) carbamoyl phosphate synthetase I activating system and (2) orotate forming system. The blue line separates two compartments: (1) mitochondrial and (2) cytoplasmatic. Enzymes found inside the mitochondria: NAGS: N- Acetylglutamate synthetase (carbamoyl phosphate synthetase I activating system); CPS Ii: carbamoyl phosphate synthetase I inactivated; CPS Ia: carbamoyl phosphate synthetase I activated; OTC: ornithine transcarbamylase. Enzymes found in the cytoplasm: CPS II: carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II (orotate forming system); AS: arginosuccinate synthetase; AL: arginosuccinate lyase._

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-30T07:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-15-is-live.14864/post-165888>

Ubiquitin needs Sulfur (cysteine) to be activated (E1).
Then needs more Sulfur (cysteine) to be conjugated (E2).
Then needs to reduce Nitrogen to be ligated (through an E3, resulting in bonding to a Lysine Residue forming an isopeptide bond, or to an Amine group) .

Reminder: The wavelength of absorbance for a peptide bond is 190–230 nm. (same with isopeptide bond)

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-31T16:07:05+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-31T16:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-15-is-live.14864/post-166024>

I always "felt" off on pollution peak days, so I suspected there was a neurological aspect to it too.
I was the odd one. My non asthmatic colleagues didn't feel anything special but they would agree that there was an increase of consults during the peaks.

When I was offered to participate in a Public Health panel to revise air pollution alert guidelines, I was happy to help.

Then I told them about the neurologic effects , and the Experts chuckled…

I knew I couldn't tell them I "felt" it or that my mood was low or that I had compulsive binge eatings on pollution days…
So I told them "TNF alpha , UFP mechanistic models , Milano UFP vitiated air rat studies …."
Confirmation bias?
They said "bring the studies" ( mind you they were Ze Experts..)
So I did.
And then we left the Beautiful City of Light.

### Paleodocteur — 2015-05-31T16:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-15-is-live.14864/post-166026>

I had brains to protect.
Two years later , despite the fears and hurdles, I still believe it was the right thing to do.

PLoS Med. 2015 Mar; 12(3): e1001792.
Published online 2015 Mar 3. doi: [10.1371/journal.pmed.1001792](http://dx.doi.org/10.1371%2Fjournal.pmed.1001792)
PMCID: PMC4348510
**Association between Traffic-Related Air Pollution in Schools and Cognitive Development in Primary School Children: A Prospective Cohort Study**

**Abstract**
**Background**
Air pollution is a suspected developmental neurotoxicant. Many schools are located in close proximity to busy roads, and traffic air pollution peaks when children are at school. We aimed to assess whether exposure of children in primary school to traffic-related air pollutants is associated with impaired cognitive development.

**Methods and Findings**
We conducted a prospective study of children (_n_ = 2,715, aged 7 to 10 y) from 39 schools in Barcelona (Catalonia, Spain) exposed to high and low traffic-related air pollution, paired by school socioeconomic index; children were tested four times (i.e., to assess the 12-mo developmental trajectories) via computerized tests (_n_ = 10,112). Chronic traffic air pollution (elemental carbon \[EC\], nitrogen dioxide \[NO2\], and ultrafine particle number \[UFP; 10–700 nm\]) was measured twice during 1-wk campaigns both in the courtyard (outdoor) and inside the classroom (indoor) simultaneously in each school pair. Cognitive development was assessed with the _n_\-back and the attentional network tests, in particular, working memory (two-back detectability), superior working memory (three-back detectability), and inattentiveness (hit reaction time standard error). Linear mixed effects models were adjusted for age, sex, maternal education, socioeconomic status, and air pollution exposure at home.

Children from highly polluted schools had a smaller growth in cognitive development than children from the paired lowly polluted schools, both in crude and adjusted models (e.g., 7.4% \[95% CI 5.6%–8.8%\] versus 11.5% \[95% CI 8.9%–12.5%\] improvement in working memory, _p_ = 0.0024). Cogently, children attending schools with higher levels of EC, NO2, and UFP both indoors and outdoors experienced substantially smaller growth in all the cognitive measurements; for example, a change from the first to the fourth quartile in indoor EC reduced the gain in working memory by 13.0% (95% CI 4.2%–23.1%). Residual confounding for social class could not be discarded completely; however, the associations remained in stratified analyses (e.g., for type of school or high-/low-polluted area) and after additional adjustments (e.g., for commuting, educational quality, or smoking at home), contradicting a potential residual confounding explanation.

**Conclusions**
Children attending schools with higher traffic-related air pollution had a smaller improvement in cognitive development.

# Thread: Male Continence (celibacy)?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-continence-celibacy.14916/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### badger — 2015-05-31T17:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-continence-celibacy.14916/post-166035>

Sex is almost universally promoted these days. You hear the advice given by some doctors that Men should be masturbating to avoid prostate cancer, there's a constant preoccupation with how to improve one's sex life, have more sex, have more orgasms, etc. I think we can all agree that commercial advertizing and the media seek to keep people in a sexual mode. Schools now teach sex education, I went through it not too long ago. I'm not fully informed on Dr. K's take on it, but in my semi-ignorance I can say I know he encourages the production of Oxytocin.

Nature is a delicate balance of substances and energies. Nothing in nature happens without reason, without a discreet purpose, because the production of anything costs something. The rigors of evolution has ensured that all natural processes are coupled to each other to ensure maximum robustness and super-efficient functioning. Those organisms who cease to be efficient or robust for whatever reason die. Small changes to those environmental factors we're coupled to (light, water, magnetism) has huge effects.

Am I basically on base with this? This site really drives this point home.

When we intentionally lose semen/sperm thru the orgasm, are we not taxing the body for the production of more sexual materials? So therefore would there be any detriment to the constant taxing of the body in this way, as is so common among Men these days? Is there any potential value to retaining the sexual fluids at least for a longer period of time than one is accustomed to? At least the body wouldn't have to put energy and resources into the production of more. And surely the sexual hormones/fluids are more refined and of higher cost than the blood (seeing as the body produces less of it), and it's not controversial to say that intentional, chronic bloodloss would pose a health risk. So why does intentional, chronic semen loss get promoted as a health wonder?

The simple questions of a child is all this is intended to be. If we're interested in looking into our blind spots, why not look into all of them?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-31T19:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-continence-celibacy.14916/post-166039>

pregnenolone steal syndrome on this site addresses this. When life is under attack it preserves the cortisol pathway and forsakes the sex steroid pathways. The system always chose fight or flight over sex.

### JanSz — 2015-05-31T21:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-continence-celibacy.14916/post-166049>

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-03T14:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ct-alone-outweigh-other-factors.14918/post-166428>

Aerose, CT in the ocean at dusk sounds great …. But if 30 seconds in the water is too much, could you try walking in and walking straight out again, warming up in the rays of the setting sun, and repeat if you can ….?. When I first started lower temperature CT, I couldn’t drink cold water before, during or straight after the session, I needed it warmer. I could drink cold water, and I could CT, but not together within a certain timeframe. So you may find that you do better with a warmer drink before going in and/or after you first come out of the water, until your tolerance improves. Might that help you to transition to taking full advantage of a low hanging fruit such as ocean CT?

I find belly grounding much better to recharge my battery than my feet … might be worth a try, perhaps resting your forehead on the sand too …. ?
Are you doing anything else at other times in the day using cold to reduce inflammation without causing stress? We could then perhaps add a few more ideas we’ve tried that have helped.

Hey dude, our stories share many similarities so I decided to go back though your old posts. I went back to your first post and wasn't surprised to find out that your problems started from Benzodiazepines. Everything you're describing can simply be down to benzo withdrawal. Protracted withdrawal is only termed if symptoms are not gone within 5 years of ceasing the medication, so you may even be in what's considered the normal range of healing.

Here's some info:

[www.benzo.org.uk/manual/index.htm](http://www.benzo.org.uk/manual/index.htm)

Ive been a member of benzobuddies, very supportive and helpful community.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-01T19:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-more-dha-you-eat-the-more-gets-into-your-tissues.14962/post-166132>

and the faster you can reverse a disease.

More data on why Epi-paleo Rx tops any SAD or any paleo solution.[http://ow.ly/NIPtF](http://ow.ly/NIPtF) Why? the more DHA you eat the more you put into your tissues. Grass fed cows can never provide more DHA than the marine seafood chain.

So in my recent webinar series I taught you about how the loss of negative feedback control in coupled biologic systems is the sentinel event for aging and disease generation. Moreover, I showed you what happens when you lose it in one side of the coupled event. There I used predator or prey to make the point. If you alter the balance of predator or prey the result is always the EXTINCTION of both animals. I have told you that in aging and neolithic disease generation that NAD+ becomes altered in relationship to NADH. The chronic loss of NAD+ is the critical sign of a loss of negative feedback control of the ubiquitin cycle. Now for how this scales to your molecular circadian clock and your peripheral clock genes (CCG's). The current model of the mammalian circadian clock includes two interlocking transcription-translation feedback loops comprised of several so-called “clock” genes and their protein products, which ultimately regulate the transcription of “clock-controlled” genes. These feedback loops consist of positive and negative components. The positive components include the basic helix-loop-helix-PAS domain transcription factors, CLOCK and BMAL1. These transcription factors heterodimerize, translocate from the cytosol to the nucleus, and bind to circadian E-box promoter elements that enhance the transcription of genes encoding the negative components PERIOD 1 & 2 and CRYPTOCHROME 1 & 2. The CRYPTOCHROME and PERIOD proteins feedback inhibit the transcription of the Cryptochrome and Period genes by blocking CLOCK/BMAL1-mediated trans-activation. The second feedback loop involves the trans-activation of the Rev-Erbα and Rora genes by CLOCK/BMAL1. The protein products of these genes compete for binding to RRE elements in the Bmal1 promoter, driving a daily rhythm of Bmal1 transcription and closing the second feedback loop. Rhythmic expression of these clock gene products produces circadian clock outputs by regulating transcription of clock-controlled genes (CCGs). At least some of these CCGs, including aanat, the gene encoding the penultimate enzyme in the melatonin biosynthetic pathway, contain circadian E boxes, which have a core nucleotide sequence of CACGTG and are activated rhythmically by CLOCK/BMAL1. Post-translational regulation, including phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitinatation, sumoylation and proteasomal degradation are also important in the regulatory mechanisms generating the circadian oscillation. Once you SCN goes haywire it is a matter of time before your circadian clock genes in tissues the SCN controls goes haywire. What will be the ultimate result? EXTINCTION of both sides of the circadian coupling and cancer is that result. My PSA for the day is done.

1\. Herzog ED. Neurons and networks in daily rhythms. Nat Rev Neurosci. 2007;8:790–802.
2\. Yamazaki S, Numano R, Abe M, Hida A, Takahashi R, et al. Resetting central and peripheral circadian oscillators in transgenic rats. Science. 2000;288:682–685.
3\. Ko CH, Takahashi JC. Molecular components of the mammalian circadian clock. Hum Mol Genet. 2006;2:R271–277.
4\. Munoz E, Baler R. The circadian E-box: when perfect is not good enough. Chronobiol Int. 2003;20:371–88.
5\. Gatfield D, Schibler U. Proteasomes keep the circadian clock ticking. Science. 2007;316:1135–1136.

Eukaryotes spend 80% of their total energy budget on protein synthesis. That process is controlled by ubiquitination. This is why you need to understand ubiquitin. Once you master ubiquitin you can use that recovered energy to reverse illnesses. Each peptide bond requires 5 ATP to seal the bond. That amount is 5 times as much that is needed to polymerize nucleotides into DNA!! Each protein is reproduced in thousands of copies, which are continuously turned over by ubiquitin to repair wear and tear.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-02T04:07:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-more-dha-you-eat-the-more-gets-into-your-tissues.14962/post-166187>

Yew read the beginning paragraph of BG 5 about VDR, RXR, and DHA.

**Geeks:** The implications of [Brain Gut 4](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-4-what-was-homos-solution/) mean that to develop a brain seafood is required part of the program to maintain it. Nutrients and many other environmental factors have also been found to influence epigenetic programing of our DNA either directly or indirectly via metabolic sensors. Peroxisomal proliferator-activated receptors (PPAR’s), the vitamin D receptors, and the retinoid X receptors (RXR), and the retinoic acid receptors (RAR) are all examples of the nuclear receptors that interact with the brain cell membranes to control inflammation and metabolism all over our bodies. It turns out that PPAR’s are the receptors that are at the crossroads of where inflammation and metabolism actually cross. These are specialized lipid sensors that pay attention to our balance of omega 6 and 3 levels. 3%-10% of the human genome is directly or indirectly controlled by the endocrine functions of the Vitamin D system. This shows you how environmental signals are intertwined into the expression of DNA in humans. The [Epi-Paleo Rx](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/) controls [inflammation at a hormonal level in the brain](http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/106/3/289.full) better than the more famous paleo diet.

**Non Geeks:** What we eat is really damn important when you have 3 lbs of lipid in your head called a brain. If you eat outside our design you get sick.
