Heh, to be honest, I don't have the time to write down the list of things that DHA has a positive impact on, and all the studies that support those. There's too much stuff.

Control over PPARs, control over BMAL, better Melanopsin signalling, improved Cryptochrome signalling, improved RBC zeta potential, rhodopsin and G-coupled protein signalling, improved protein synthesis rates, better gene expression, etc ...

\----

**Sidenote**: I think the paper '**A novel hypothesis for atherosclerosis as a cholesterol sulfate deficiency syndrome**' is incredibly forward looking -- [http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract](http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract)

My excerpts here -- [http://qhwiki.com/excerpts:cholesterol-sulfate-artherosclerosis](http://qhwiki.com/excerpts:cholesterol-sulfate-artherosclerosis)

However, I think that they fail to mention anything about DHA, which plays directly in everything the authors talk about. The electrokinetic vascular streaming potential (ESVP) model which they support also supports a big role for DHA.
Example quote:

The experimental results presented in \[76\] virtually prove, by process of elimination, that structured water in the interphase of the endogenous biofilm of the endothelial glycocalyx is the “target” of the EVSP.

a) the **amphiphilic Ch-S** in endothelial and erythrocyte membranes and blood plasma,
b) a high (negative) ZP, and
c) high interfacial water tension, effectively facilitates the electrokinetic pulling of deformable RBCs through the microvasculature
​

Heh, basically you need electromagnetic fields for everything, including blood flow and RBC-mediated oxygen delivery. While sulfates are clearly needed, DHA is also needed to maintain the membrane potential needed for high Zeta Potential, as well as to maintain.

DHA also seems to sensitise various cells to the action of sulfates -- [http://journals.cambridge.org/actio...ge=online&aid=921276&fileId=S0007114506000043](http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract?fromPage=online&aid=921276&fileId=S0007114506000043)

DHA also affects all the liver pathways needed for circadian response to food, which in turn is an entrainment signal that ultimately controls actual cholesterol synthesis.

The sulfurylation of cholesterol by SULT2B1b is probably an independent factor though, which is why a source of sulfate is concurrently needed on top of DHA.

\----

**Sidenote again**: After reading that paper, I feel like one of the functions of positively charged Exclusion Zones in serum is to amplifying the Electrokinetic Pull at sites of inflammation.

DHA that is not in the sn-2 position is simply used as a lipid for fuel.

Make sure you get a whole food source. Raw is best. Eat cooked if you need to. **Quantity** trumps Quality so long as it's a whole food seafood source.

Our thinking process is divided into two compartments, the conscious mind and the unconscious mind. There are two guards constantly guarding the city of life and they are called inhalation and exhalation. This is such an important link, but see what the scientists are doing. Otherwise no one knows much about breath. “We are all breathing,” they say, “what is there to be learnt?” I say you are breathing, but you are not breathing properly, you are not breathing accurately. There are five main pranas and five secondary pranas in your body. Some of them supply energy to your body and there are others that cleanse your body.

If a fool engages in sex, he comes out from his bedroom still a fool. If he sleeps, he wakes up as a fool. But if he goes to meditation he’ll come out as a sage. How to meditate? ### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T06:14:22+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T06:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-more-dha-you-eat-the-more-gets-into-your-tissues.14962/post-166375>

The retina of the eye is technically part of the brain. The brain is the key part of the CNS. The CNS contains very low levels of EPA but very high levels of DHA. Therefore, EPA may compensate DHA roles outside the CNS. DHA-phospholipids support transmembrane receptor function in the CNS, thus resulting in impaired receptor function when DHA bioavailability is decreased. Phosphatidylserine (PS), a DHA-rich phospholipid, modulates AKT signaling by recruiting cytosolic proteins, Raf-1, and protein kinase C to discrete membrane locations, in turn promoting cell survival. This action blocks apoptosis to promotes cell survival and optimal health. **This is why DHA has shown health benefits in most studies where it is found**.

DHA-phospholipids support transmembrane receptor function in the CNS, thus resulting in impaired receptor function when DHA bioavailability is decreased. Phosphatidylserine (PS), a DHA-rich phospholipid, modulates AKT signaling by recruiting cytosolic proteins, Raf-1, and protein kinase C to discrete membrane locations, in turn promoting cell survival. This action blocks apoptosis to promotes cell survival and optimal health. **This is why DHA has shown health benefits in most studies where it is found**.

Interesting..
I remember several years ago EPA was considered to be the main player of the two according to some European studies.
But by trying to 'fine tune' what gives the benefits we miss out on the "30,000 ft view" of what really going on...we need the entire biological package.

We didn't evolve taking fish oil. We evolved eating fish.

DHA-phospholipids support transmembrane receptor function in the CNS, thus resulting in impaired receptor function when DHA bioavailability is decreased. Phosphatidylserine (PS), a DHA-rich phospholipid, modulates AKT signaling by recruiting cytosolic proteins, Raf-1, and protein kinase C to discrete membrane locations, in turn promoting cell survival. Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by it.
1\. Look at the sun every morning and get 30-90 minutes outside every day
2\. Be grounded.........100% of the time
3\. Drink good water.
4\. Avoid blue light and nnEMF.
5\. eat seasonally for your latitude longitude and altitude, add seafood based upon how much you disconnect from nature.

How hard is that?

### lilly antic — 2016-03-21T15:18:26+0200

> [Ben777 said:](/goto/post?id=191218)
>
> OK, no DHA supplements - I will follow that. Still, I ask: Any ballpark number/range that one should aspire to, for daily or weekly mcg intake of DHA from sea-food? (I do understand the qualitative issues that it’s dependent also on UV, nnEMF, and other factors, yet I seek a guide-line-number). Thanks.

To answer this question will be a little difficult, Ben....you never know exactly how much DHA the fish in question has.
Also you have to consider that DHA is paramagnetic. So in order to get into cell membranes etc. it needs a good magnetic field provided by the mitochondria. As this varies in every person due to individual context, redox potential, environment etc, the amount of ingested DHA reaching the target tissue will also differ.

### Penny — 2016-03-21T21:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-more-dha-you-eat-the-more-gets-into-your-tissues.14962/post-191260>

All I know is when I eat a can of oysters at dinnertime - hell a few days ago I ate one at 10:30 due to a screw-up of eating too many strawberries that day - I was starving, knew the night would be screwed so I remembered somewhere that Dr. K ate a crap-ton of oysters one night and slept like a log - worked like a charm... in fact, it works so well, I have been eating a can of them at dinnertime for the last week - I think it has tons of bioavailable zinc plus electrons (maybe cause the oyster doesn't move so it keeps more electrons?) plus DHA - I would \*so\* love to know how an oyster works - like its mito transport chain and its photosynthesis - and why does it have more zinc than anything in the ocean? Like, what's the diff between a clam and an oyster? Except an oyster looks more prehistoric...  It has so much more zinc - how does an oyster use zinc - yet another transition metal - does it use it like magnesium in plants? I read the blogs over and over and I still don't get this part... maybe if I could fathom how an oyster worked I could fathom how we do...

Penny, can you tell us what brand of canned oysters do you eat? The ones we get here in Texas are all from China. I've tried 'fresh' oysters in see through containers in the refrigerated section from Washington State. My system doesn't like them. I nearly fainted after eating some a few months ago. (I had no carbs/protein that day, only coconut oil) Nutrients too dense and body can't handle it?
Then again, I have had no trouble eating raw oysters with no carbs/protein in the system. Really can't figure that one out.

Same thing happened to me in a restaurant two weeks ago. I had fried frogs legs which were previously frozen. Almost passed out on the floor. (And I even had pickled herrings several hours prior to that meal). I knew better and asked for some table sugar. Solved the problem instantly. Something about processed 'fresh' seafood my body can't handle. I can eat canned sardines, canned mackerel without problems.
Wonder if there are chemicals involved in the processing of fresh seafood.

I get the canned oysters at Trader Joes - here they are on Amazon:
[http://www.amazon.com/Crown-Prince-...e=UTF8&qid=1458608137&sr=1-1&keywords=oysters](http://www.amazon.com/Crown-Prince-Natural-Oysters-3-Ounce/dp/B00GJ0WW24/ref=sr_1_1_s_it?s=grocery&ie=UTF8&qid=1458608137&sr=1-1&keywords=oysters)

They are in a BPA can... maybe that's part of it? BPA - I think - are estrogenic - if that means they bind to the receptors preventing estrogen from doing so or giving you more estrogen - beats the hell out of me

Oysters are a weird bird - they have to be harvested at the correct time of year or they can be contaminated and make you ill - I talked at length to a raw oyster farmer at our local beach and I forget when they harvest them but they said they have a very low likelihood of being contaminated - so, maybe your "bottled oysters" were simply picked at the wrong time:
[http://www.foodsafetynews.com/2010/10/florida-warns-residents-of-eating-raw-oysters/](http://www.foodsafetynews.com/2010/10/florida-warns-residents-of-eating-raw-oysters/)

Not sure it's wise to just eat coconut oil all day?

As far as frogs go, there are a lot fewer of them and thus they may harbor more toxins:

[http://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/2009/aug/07/frogs-legs-extinction](http://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/2009/aug/07/frogs-legs-extinction)

"But those days are long gone. As elsewhere in the world, the amphibians' habitat in [France](http://www.theguardian.com/world/france) – where frogs' legs have been a recognised and much remarked-upon part of the national diet for the best part of 1,000 years – is increasingly at risk, from pollution, pesticides and other man-made ills. Ponds have been drained and replaced with crops and cattle-troughs. Diseases have taken their toll, and the insects that frogs feed on are disappearing too. Alarmed by a rapid and dramatic fall in frog numbers, the French ministry of agriculture and fisheries began taking measures to protect the country's species in 1976; by 1980, commercial frog harvesting was banned.

These days, a few regional authorities in France still allow the capture of limited numbers of frogs, strictly for personal consumption and provided they are broiled, fried or barbecued and consumed on the spot (a heresy not even Boeuf is prepared to contemplate). There are poachers who defy the ban; two years ago a court in Vesoul in the Haute-Saone convicted four men of harvesting vast numbers of frogs from the Mille-Etangs or Thousand Lakes area of the Vosges. The ringleader admitted to personally catching at least 10,000, which he sold to restaurants for 32 cents apiece."

As elsewhere in the world, the amphibians' habitat in [France](http://www.theguardian.com/world/france) – where frogs' legs have been a recognised and much remarked-upon part of the national diet for the best part of 1,000 years – is increasingly at risk, from pollution, pesticides and other man-made ills. Ponds have been drained and replaced with crops and cattle-troughs. Diseases have taken their toll, and the insects that frogs feed on are disappearing too. Alarmed by a rapid and dramatic fall in frog numbers, the French ministry of agriculture and fisheries began taking measures to protect the country's species in 1976; by 1980, commercial frog harvesting was banned.
>
> These days, a few regional authorities in France still allow the capture of limited numbers of frogs, strictly for personal consumption and provided they are broiled, fried or barbecued and consumed on the spot (a heresy not even Boeuf is prepared to contemplate). There are poachers who defy the ban; two years ago a court in Vesoul in the Haute-Saone convicted four men of harvesting vast numbers of frogs from the Mille-Etangs or Thousand Lakes area of the Vosges. The ringleader admitted to personally catching at least 10,000, which he sold to restaurants for 32 cents apiece."

Penny, thank you sincerely for your thoughts, link to oysters and the fascinating and informative article on frogs.

You are absolutely correct to be concerned about eating coconut oil all day. For that, I must apologize for not explaining in detail. Several months ago (before I knew better) I was doing keto plus IF. I would eat about 3 tablespoons of coconut oil around hour 15, then have my main meal 2 hours later. The oysters was the main meal that day. I don't think they were contaminated because my husband ate even more oysters from the same jar, and the oysters were cooked in organic heavy cream. So right now, the only thing I can come up with is both the oysters and frog's legs had some mild toxins that the body can normally handle but with my trashed mitochondria, I needed extra glucose to deal with it. The frog's legs were probably more toxic because I had eaten a healthy portion of pickled herrings about 2 hours before the meal, and should have enough glucose to deal with the situation.

I am post menopause, and actually don't know how BPA and the estrogenic effects you described may affect me. Normally, I don't feel anything from eating canned foods that has BPA (not that I eat canned foods regularly). There is just so much to learn! Life is just a miracle.

I am truly grateful for Doc and his patient guidance and endless supply of INCREDIBLE knowledge, and the support, encouragement and help from Forum members like yourself. Thank you again.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-01T21:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool-vs-stimulants.14963/post-166138>

I was diagnosed with adult ADHD about 4 years ago and have been prescribed a hefty dose of Vyvanse (an amphetamine based stimulant) which has more cons than pros. That being said, I'm finding it very hard to quit because it is so good at abolishing boredom. Without it I would probably only get through one paragraph of Jack's blog!
Does anybody know to what extent stimulants like this one and caffeine impacts our ability to benefit from the three legged stool?
Is it even possible to improve your redox potential while taking such medications?

### Josh — 2015-06-01T22:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool-vs-stimulants.14963/post-166141>

Would fit under the issues at the end of this blog, like procaffeination....

[http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-4-ubiquitins-connection-to-light/](http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-4-ubiquitins-connection-to-light/)

Thanks Josh for the link!
I know for a fact that stimulants are contributing to my problem of chronic fatigue and lack of motivation but the reason it is so hard to quit is that it makes me think much more positively and drives me to learn as much as I can about this very condition. I get somewhat excited when I hear Jack's webinars because this information becomes so interesting, especially because no one else (but us) really knows this stuff.
Ideally I would like to support my own endogenous neurotransmitters so I can titrate my meds down and eventually have no need or desire for them. But I wonder if that is even possible with the dehydration and side effects they cause.

I really need to start doing some testing, but here in Canada we don't really have the luxury of doing our own labs unless we drive over the line and get the draw done there. (which hinders my motivation).
But just discussing it helps with that.

By the way Josh.. thanks for the link to Direct Labs. I wasn't sure if they dealt with Canadians but they do.

# Thread: Low serotonin OR low redox? That is the question....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-serotonin-or-low-redox-that-is-the-question.14965/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-02T00:29:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-serotonin-or-low-redox-that-is-the-question.14965/post-166157>

SSRI's are prescribed for many conditions now, not just depression. The theory is that when serotonin is low in the synaptic cleft, we get these conditions. The serotonin molecule takes the message across the gap and then gets recycled.

SSRI's slow that recycling down.

I've always thought (since I am an electrician), how the hell does that molecule move across that gap so fast? Electricity moves at near the speed of light. I don't think a molecule can move that fast. It makes more sense to me that serotonin (or any other neurotransmitter) are more like 'stepping stones' for the current to cross the gap.

If the voltage across that gap was stronger, less 'stepping stones' would be required for the signal to cross over.
Maybe we don't need the serotonin as much as we need the voltage. My guess is that the real reason is a combination of both - hence oysters, Magnetico and CT.

Any thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-02T00:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-serotonin-or-low-redox-that-is-the-question.14965/post-166167>

electricity = DHA.........but ADHD is a story about protonicity =

make sure you listen to the last ten minutes.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-02T01:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-serotonin-or-low-redox-that-is-the-question.14965/post-166169>

# Thread: Ketosis with a brain tumor: A story to follow.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-with-a-brain-tumor-a-story-to-follow.14968/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-02T04:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-with-a-brain-tumor-a-story-to-follow.14968/post-166184>

[http://zerocarbzen.com/2015/05/31/h...ro-carb-ketogenic-diet-by-andrew-scarborough/](http://zerocarbzen.com/2015/05/31/healing-brain-cancer-with-a-zero-carb-ketogenic-diet-by-andrew-scarborough/)

Without a lot more DHA as the major fat I will predict that Andrew will have some longer term issues. The fact that he can't handle salicylates is a big deal already. The fact that he was a trainer......not so good for redox. The fact that he seems to like technology is also no bueno. He seems to believe this is a food happy story. As the Ubi series marches on you'll be able to understand why I don't share his beliefs. His, is story to follow. If you do you will learn something about ubiqutin marking.

Anaplastic Astrocytoma is a grade 3 glioma that tends to de-differentiate. Being around nn EMF and blue light is a real trigger. The fact that he already can't tolerate sunlight is a big clue to Tensegrity 7 is missing in him. Not having a lot of DHA with this finding will also be a problem.

This story should be a short term one.........Median survival is 24 % at five years. His age is his best survival tool. Ketosis with DHA is next best, but he has to change his environment he got ill within.............I saw nothing about this in his story and that is why I dont feel he has it all dialed in. If he eats a lot more seafood and avoids technology I could see him getting to 10-15 yrs because of his age. I also think he needs to seriously consider moving. The key risk with technology will be the de-differentiation to GBM. And it likely will be a very malignant version because his AA was highly vascular.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-02T04:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-with-a-brain-tumor-a-story-to-follow.14968/post-166188>

Read the beginning paragraph of BG 5 about VDR, RXR, and DHA.

**Geeks:** The implications of [Brain Gut 4](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-4-what-was-homos-solution/) mean that to develop a brain seafood is required part of the program to maintain it. Nutrients and many other environmental factors have also been found to influence epigenetic programing of our DNA either directly or indirectly via metabolic sensors. Peroxisomal proliferator-activated receptors (PPAR’s), the vitamin D receptors, and the retinoid X receptors (RXR), and the retinoic acid receptors (RAR) are all examples of the nuclear receptors that interact with the brain cell membranes to control inflammation and metabolism all over our bodies. It turns out that PPAR’s are the receptors that are at the crossroads of where inflammation and metabolism actually cross. These are specialized lipid sensors that pay attention to our balance of omega 6 and 3 levels. 3%-10% of the human genome is directly or indirectly controlled by the endocrine functions of the Vitamin D system. This shows you how environmental signals are intertwined into the expression of DNA in humans. The [Epi-Paleo Rx](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/) controls [inflammation at a hormonal level in the brain](http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/106/3/289.full) better than the more famous paleo diet.

**Non Geeks:** What we eat is really damn important when you have 3 lbs of lipid in your head called a brain. If you eat outside our design you get sick.

[Brain Cancer causes more deaths in children in Australia than any other disease](https://www.curebraincancer.org.au/news/1092/why-does-brain-cancer-kill-more-children-than-any-other-disease) - WHY? - Not enough research??!!

"Survival rates for brain cancer have hardly changed in 30 years and remain far too low, with only 2 in 10 people surviving for 5 years. The reason for this - and by association, the reason why brain cancer kills more children than any other disease in Australia - is largely that not enough money has been invested in research. Cure Brain Cancer is doing things differently to improve outcomes for patients and one of things that makes us different is our approach and outlook. Our question is not ‘why?’ but ‘why not?’ and - more importantly - ‘how can we?' Perhaps this is the more pertinent question here; not why brain cancer kills these children, but how are we going to change this?"

"The new research also showed that women working with chlorinated solvents, such as trichloroethylene, at any time before the child’s birth, increased their risk of having their baby develop brain cancer. "

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T03:36:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-with-a-brain-tumor-a-story-to-follow.14968/post-166363>

Andrew thinks his doctors have thought this all out, but I beg to differ big time because of how ubiquitin, light, and ketosis all work in quantum fashion.............He needs to understand how these things affect time scales of the relationship of NAD+ and SIRT 1. They are critical in understanding how acute and chronic inflammation affect DNA expression. All inflammation is positively charged due to the protons (type is big deal). Chronic inflammation has a much lower pH (pronton bio-physics alert) and more sustained pseudohypoxia distal to the ATPase. Acute inflammation usually leads to neolithic diseases that do not kill. Chronic inflammation leads to diseases like oncogenesis that extinguish life via higher grade and malignant tumors. The more deuterium the worse the disease grade.

In acute inflammation, chromatin works as it is designed by evolution. Chromatin should departs from, and returns to, equilibrium on DNA in an orderly sequence to control protein synthesis and ubiquitin marking. This temporal sequence depends on proper circadian shifts in NAD+ availability for SIRT1 activation and deacetylation of signaling proteins, which support orderly gene reprogramming during acute inflammation by switching between euchromatin and heterochromatin. In contrast, in chronic inflammation states where the redox potential is low, oncogenesis is much more likely. The lower the redox or the more blue light one faces the more malignant the outcome is. This is due to the limited availability of NAD+, in concert, with the reduced expression of SIRT1 in the retina and in the gut, so this becomes able to sustain aberrant chromatin structure and functions. SIRT1 also influences inflammation and cancer by directly de-acetylating targets like NFκB, p65, and p53 in the genome.

Chronic inflammation sustains pro-inflammatory chromatin. With chronic inflammation, several things occur connected with SIRT1. A key point, is a high fat diet cleaves SIRT1 and lowers NAD+ levels to reduce SIRT1 synthesis. The net result of a high fat diet and lower NAD+ levels is a loss of SIRT1 function. This is the predator/prey relationship that I mentioned in the Ubi series that describes the loss of negative feedback control in cells. This results in hyperacetylation of the p65 subunit of NF-kB, which produces pro-inflammatory products. These proinflammatory mediators have a “positive feedback” on the formation of obesity, diabetes, cancer, and aging.

>
> Because I am known locally, every once in awhile a very sick person gets dropped on my doorstep. Because there are a lot of expats living in Phuket, there are lots of retiring folks with no family support system around. When someone is dying of cancer, for some reason, friends will pull up in the driveway and simply drop them off for me to take care of. This is not a responsibility I am really comfortable with. I usually simply refer them to this blog and look up the threads for them and their doctors.
>
> Last Saturday, a man was brought here by his friends who has this very aggressive brain tumor, a glioma brain tumor.
>
> [http://www.mayfieldclinic.com/PE-Glioma.htm#.Vg3n2BOqqkp](http://www.mayfieldclinic.com/PE-Glioma.htm#.Vg3n2BOqqkp)
>
> I called his doctor and asked him what he wanted me to do. His oncologist told me to put him on a raw vegan ketogenic diet. He sent me the recipes to cook.
> This is the website of the clinic taking care of him.
> [http://www.anoasisofhealing.com/](http://www.anoasisofhealing.com/)
>
> I wanted to recommend the patient get out and do the morning sun and evening sun as we are all doing here but there is no DHA in this diet. I need to find the right articles to send to his doctor to explain why I want to add DHA to the diet and of course sunlight.
>
> Off the top of your head, which blog post would be perfect for me to send to his doctor to get him up to speed as to why I want to add DHA in the diet?
>
> The man is dying in front of my eyes. I called his family in Australia to come and help and I requested they move to a quiet beach nearby so I could get him into the sun. Before I recommend he get the sun, I would like to get the DHA into him quickly.
>
> I am delivering his meals to his house daily. The whole family is dependent on me though I am not qualified to take care of him. The only tool I have is this blog which I will use responsibly by sending to his doctor, who will then give me orders.
>
> Thanks for anyone who can help me to keep this poor man alive. He is young, in his earl 40's and really is fighting for his life. I really feel the keys to his survival are in this blog. I am sure of it.

>

# Thread: Advanced Adrenal Fatigue Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-adrenal-fatigue-help.15014/>

### krw2586 — 2015-06-02T21:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-adrenal-fatigue-help.15014/post-166302>

Hi all!

I just joined as a gold member and am going to try to hopefully get a consult with Dr Jack, but I'm waiting for some issues with my username / password to be resolved. So I thought I might as well start here! I'm a 28 year old female with quite a history of health issues

I've read through much of the website, but my brain is really struggling. My history includes Lyme disease, severe gut dysbiosis and paralysis (resulting in a partial obstruction), multiple chemical sensitivities with neurological presentations (possibly porphyria), adrenal fatigue, autoimmune thyroid disease, thyroid medication resistance, food sensitivities, etc. This past January I experienced a sudden crash. It was after trying a new thyroid medication (WP Thyroid) and doing a ketogenic diet experiment to help with neurological symptoms. Ever since then, my adrenal issues have become severe, I've gained almost 20 pounds, have an orange-ish/bronze color, can't sleep, get severe hypoglycemia even after eating only protein and fat (I've passed out from it), my thyroid medication feels completely ineffective, but my thyroid is fully suppressed so I can't stop taking it, my gut is very paralyzed again and I need to take 6,000 mg of magnesium a day just to have a bowel movement. I'm sweaty all the time and it smells so strongly like ammonia.

My saliva cortisol levels are very low, but I have paradoxical reactions to everything I've tried - everything, without fail, makes it much worse and I don't recover from the experiment. This includes vitamin C, pantothenic acid, glandulars, pregnenolone, and progesterone. But my progesterone is low, estrogen is high. And ironically my aldosterone is high, which makes no sense. I'm trying my best to follow the lifestyle changes, like eating a BAB, sleeping in complete dark, trying very small amounts of melatonin, no lights/screens at night, etc. But even those changes are not being accepted.

Has anyone been in a similar place? Any suggestions? I even have issues with the BAB - it makes me extremely fatigued. I've been fighting for my health for about 5 years and have seen many, many excellent holistic/functional medicine doctors, but even they are frustrated. I'd love anyone's feedback!

### seanb4 — 2015-06-02T23:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-adrenal-fatigue-help.15014/post-166327>

I don't have much good answers for you but just some thoughts. Maybe look into methylation as if you smell of ammonia that could mean you have trouble getting rid of it / converting it. High protein is one thing to cause ammonia levels to raise...

I have bad fatigue as well but the things that really helped me were CT, Magneico, enzymes/betaine, getting as much mineral water as possible and other things.

I would stick at eliminating blue light at night and bright light / sun in day as when your body gets used to it, it should be very benefitial for your hormone cycles and overal body timing.

I personally try to limit protein though it's hard when doing vlc. Maybe 40g in morning and 20g at tea for a ttal of 60g?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-03T01:44:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-adrenal-fatigue-help.15014/post-166348>

No human being itself should be considered impaired innately, instead there are environmental short comings that cause the impairment. Thus, it is incumbent on the on the clinician to **recommend treatment of the environment their patient is in**. People _react to an inferior environment,_ way before their genome is altered. That is what the science of epigenetics and ubiquitin rates are telegraphing us, but the modern paradigm is not listening. You must.

### krw2586 — 2015-06-03T02:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-adrenal-fatigue-help.15014/post-166359>

Thanks for all the input!

I have read a bit about CT and will try to incorporate that once I get a little further with the basic epi diet. And I will put up a picture asap!

Caroline, thanks for the suggestion to post in the Journal section - I will do that as soon as I get a chance.

And Dr K, I briefly looked through the Redox post. Some of those labs fit (low vitamin d, low cholesterol, long-term hormonal imbalances), but some are fine like BUN/Creatinine ratio and potassium. Actually the most recent CMP I had done, my potassium was much higher than normal at 5.0. I do have a heterozygous MTHFR mutation on the C677T gene. I have tried simple things like taking vitamin d3 and I react very badly even to that. Vit d completely paralyzes my gut and causes major water retention. Even after taking it for just 3 days. Maybe it's raising calcium way too high. I think a huge piece is my gut, but it's has been quite resistant to healing thus far, no matter how many supplements I've taken. I've been seeing Dr Klinghardt for Lyme / neurotoxin issues and was told to drink ASEA water to help with redox stuff. Are you familiar with ASEA and if it's any good?

I love believing that our bodies are not innately broken. That's a powerful picture. My environmental sensitivities have made me very aware of toxins and other unhealthy external influences and I've done much to minimize those. Unfortunately, it doesn't matter how clean the environment is, my body learns to react to it over time. It's definitely a learned response, which is why I think this brain re-wiring would be so helpful!

# Thread: Nice to meet you all...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nice-to-meet-you-all.15068/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tracy Guild — 2015-06-03T19:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nice-to-meet-you-all.15068/post-166442>

Age: 46 Sex: F Joined JackKruse.com: 06/03/2015

Location: Corona, CA USA (1000 miles above sea level)

Dx: chronic hip pain from age 18, recently diagnosed with congenital right hip dysplasia (I have doubts) and labral tear (all this has improved greatly since leptin rx and anti-inflammation diet/ CT Therapy begun 5 weeks ago), weight/hunger issues (controlled since recent changes), blood pressure was creeping up

Surgeries: n/a although this spring, a Kaiser doctor injected cortisone into my hip joint (I will never do that again)

Current Rx: none

Current Therapies / Protocols: epi-paleo + anti-inflammation diet, CT, magnetic sleeping pad, starting to make changes with light/electrical fields, slow/light exercises and stretching I am doing to rehabilitate and stabilize my atrophied hip muscles.

Health WINS: I have less pain! woo hoo The pain I have now is more related to the rehabilitation. I can tell the difference between inflammation pain and other kinds of pain. Lost 12 lbs (5'2", 134). Better sleep - I feel as though (even though CT makes me generate more heat), my body's ability to control/tolerate my temperature is better. I used to wake up hot every night (even in the winter, even when I was much younger) but now I sleep with ice packs, and I sleep like the dead. Sleep also improved because of less hip pain. Normal blood pressure.

Health Goals: I want to get my hip fully rehabilitated so that I can freely move without pain for long periods of time. I want to optimize my health and my kids (16 and 19 year old boys) health in all ways.

My Optimal Journal:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/)

I am a community college teacher and a perpetual student.

# Thread: Optimal Journal for Tracy Guild
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tracy Guild — 2015-06-03T19:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-166443>

Began Reset end of April, 2015.
Diet changes very successful but CT was my breakthrough. I turn the shower to cold when I am done and stay in as long as I can, but my favorite is to sleep with the cold packs. I love that. It has changed my life. I also use the cold packs whenever my hip hurts or after exercise.
Recently began eating seafood for breakfast because I was getting tired of eggs. This has been great - I am now hungry for seafood in the morning. My kids like it too - they are getting up earlier and asking me, "what is for breakfast?"
My next step is to figure out how to get the labs done. I really hate going to the doctor.
O yea, recent health win - I went to my dentist the other day and she said, "whatever you are doing, keep doing that because you have no plaque!" instead of her usual scolding of me to brush better and floss and come to see her more than every 2 years. (I remember reading about this in Dr. K's book).

**Updates 07/04/2015:** 20 lbs lost since I started. Feeling great, sleeping great. Following all the protocols - sun light, rythm/timing of meals and light, CT, minimizing nnemf and artificial light, sleeping on a magnetic sleep pad (not a magnetico I could not afford that one), tons of seafood, no snacking, BAB, lots of coconut oil in my coffee every day, minmal carbs/sugars, supplementing K2, iodine, iron, mg, multi, b complex, selenium, dhea. I stopped D because started having trouble reading at night. Have kept up walking and mild rehabilitative exercises for hip. Just started HIT last week with a focus on upper body and core. I am 46 so there may be hormonal issues, but no symptoms yet. I will make sure to ask for this in my labs.

Biggest problem that I know of is that my hip still hurts, so I thought I would give a more detailed history about this:

No hip issues as a kid - VERY active all my childhood, skiier, gymnast, softball. I was very strong and fast, coordinated and hardy, kind of a big risk taker. Never had any problems with my joints except my wrists hurt when I overburdened them in gymnastics.

At age 18, in my first year of college, I went to the uni doc for hip pain. They xrayed and said nothing is wrong with your hip, you need an mri which I never went back for.

At age 30 something, I went to a specialist and same thing. Doc said nothing wrong with my hip, no arthritis or anything. He said it was most likely soft tissue and sent me to physical therapy which helped very midly.

Jan 2015 I went to a hip specialist at Kaiser and he xrayed me like 10 times and then mri, said I have congenital hip dysplasia and that was what was causing my labrum to tear. There was also 6 or 7 small cysts in the bone which he said was from inflammation along with an impingement. He said surgical repair would not help except a full replacement. In the meantime, he said to come in every 4 months for a cortisone shot into the joint. I did one of these and it was terrible with minimal relief. I asked my doc how I could have been born with hip dysplasia and it never showed up in an xray before and never a dislocation in my life. He did not know. I feel doubtful that he can help me and I am truly frightned of a hip replacement. I think he is wrong, and I would like to fix this from a quantum health perspective if possible. I am going to start my labs next week to try.

The Kruse protocols have helped a lot. There is very little pain while sitting or sleeping, but I still experience quite a lot of pain when I walk or stand for a while. The pain is in 2 places mostly - deep in the groin what feels like the top of the ball of the hip joint along with pain in the hip flexors in front of the hip and the muscles directly behind it. I occcasional go to a chiropractor for adjustment and ART for these muscles and it helps a little bit. CT helps as well but I am still stuck with it for now. I want to be free.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-03T20:00:01+0300

### Tracy Guild — 2015-07-07T00:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-171189>

update - my doctor will not order my labs so I will have to do this myself. Signed up with Gretchen's website for insurance 101 and will be changing insurance.

### Tracy Guild — 2015-07-08T03:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-171311>

I read the blog about Lyme, FB, etc and took the Contrast Sensitivity Test. I failed it, and it got me to thinking about the possibility that my hip pain is stemming from neurotoxins. I do not have brain fog, headaches or depression, but I really suffer at times with restless legs and hip pain that flares up and goes away at times.

"Radiographs of affected joints are typically normal. Joint effusions, periarticular soft tissue swelling, and osteopenia of soft tissue calcifications are frequently observed. In advanced stages of involvement, symmetric loss of joint space, Chondrocalcinosis, bony erosions, and subchondral cysts may be evident." (Lawrence, S. J., M.D. (1992). LYME DISEASE: AN ORTHOPEDIC PERSPECTIVE. _Orthopedics, 15_(11), 1331-1335. Retrieved from [http://search.proquest.com/docview/962450558?accountid=12611](http://search.proquest.com/docview/962450558?accountid=12611))

These are similar to the results my Kaiser specialist explained were present in my hip - cycsts, lost of space in the joint, and I think he said an impingement which I think is a bony erosion. My other hip is completely normal (none of those things).

I am not sure if I am overthinking this, but i would like to know - is this something to consider?

### Tracy Guild — 2015-08-22T22:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-175394>

After listening to the doctors/hormones/replacements, I realize that I have really dry skin which is a symptom of hormone problems. Perhaps this is contributing to inflammation. No other hormonal symptoms, but they are just around the corner if I don't fix it. Also, at 46 it is just a matter of time I think. Until i figure it out, I will start taking DHEA supplement.

updates:
1\. Weight stable at 129, I expect to lose some in the winter when I stop eating carbs - eating some fruits, plantain chips and a little dark chocolate on occasion - I recently ate some potatoes and regretted it (increased hip pain) so I will not eat anymore of those at all.
2\. Sleep, mood, energy, immune function all good.
3\. Hip pain is still flaring up pretty bad. I went to a chiropractor/functional medicine doc from primalhealthdocs.com and I was dissappointed. He is essentially a homeopathic doc. On the phone, he said he was familiar with Kruse protocols and biohacks, but clearly he is not. I will continue to save my money and look for a new doc. Side note - the pain seems to be just in front of the hip joint. It feels hot and when I put ice on it, I can't get the skin cold enough. I can melt through an ice pack in a short time. The pain and heat radiate down my leg. The pain is worse when I am on it walking or standing and not terrible when i am sitting or laying down. No pain at all in other hip. Muscles in bad side are weak. All my joints are pretty flexible but bad hip has some stiffness.
4\. Optimal Goals - I have been hesitant to run labs when I am not working with a doctor who can help me figure out what is needed and how to make sense of it all. Still looking for a doc in my vicinty that I can afford to pay cash (no insurance for this).

### Tracy Guild — 2016-01-29T21:42:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-187644>

update 01/29/2016
I went to see a functional movement specialist ( [http://drmarkcheng.com/](http://drmarkcheng.com/)) this week about my hip pain. It went very well. He said after spending considerable time talking with me, watching me move/stand/sit, manipulating my muscles/joint/soft tissue that he thinks my problem is that my muscles above and below my hip are so tight and bunched up they are holding my hip too tight like a bunch of rubber bands. He said we have to release these muscles, retrain my nervous system to move right and strengthen the right muslces. So he used a technique (Chinese word I forgot) to work on the muscles, tested my range of motion, worked some more, retested etc. I am assigned to use the deep tissue foam roller every day until I see him again in two weeks along with heat on the area to encourage their release. It feels better, although i noticed after the rolling I get shin splits feelings in my right lower fron shin which is good because it means something is changing. I feel very hopefull. I want to be able to move freely and enjoy my life. I want to be free of advil or alleve to get through my day.

### Tracy Guild — 2017-01-19T00:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-207072>

Struggling through the last 6 months health wise. Weight gain (15 lbs) and high blood pressure problems. I think this has been due to an excess of stress this year. Hip still painful, but on a positive note, I found a chiropractor and a sadistic massage therapist that have been helping a great deal. The movement specialist was good, but too far and too expensive for me to go enough sessions for real change. I need to exercise more, but pain has been keeping me avoiding anything that will really make a difference. I am hoping to change that soon with help from the therapeutic massage sessions which include an ungodly amount of stretching and active release (pain, but good pain).

I listened to the heal your hormones bootcamp and basically realized that there will be no way to heal my hormones without labs, but it is daunting to figure out which labs to run, what to prioritize etc. I believe my problems are due to excessive stress this year, too much cortisol release and adrenal stress. I started taking Hawthorne just to get the blood pressure down and AdrenaCalm as mentioned by Dr. Jackson in the bootcamp to calm down my stress reaction. Of course, all other quantum health protocols still in full force. One exception, I have backed off on CT until my bp is normal. I really don't want to go to a normal doctor and be told I need to take bp meds.

On another positive note, some of my external stress is resolving and I am hopeful for a good 2017.

So, I am looking for recommendations on deciding if I should try to do these labs myself or get the help of a doc. I am ready to pay for the labs that I can out of pocket.

### fitness@home — 2017-01-19T03:38:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-207083>

Genova Diagnostics Complete Hormone test cost me $169 out-of-pocket for the Patient Pay amount with CIGNA medical insurance.

The DUTCH hormone test paid for with my FSA. Was about $300 (I think)

I did both of these in October, collecting samples the same weekend. The DUTCH included the cortisol/melatonin panel.

Both need a medical provider to order. My BHRT doc uses Genova and a physician friend ordered the DUTCH for ne.

My reason for doing both was that I was curious to see the differences in reporting between the two tests.

### Tamás — 2017-01-19T18:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-207104>

> [Tracy Guild said:](/goto/post?id=207072)

> Struggling through the last 6 months health wise. Weight gain (15 lbs) and high blood pressure problems. I think this has been due to an excess of stress this year. I need to exercise more, but pain has been keeping me avoiding anything that will really make a difference. I am hoping to change that soon with help from the therapeutic massage sessions which include an ungodly amount of stretching and active release (pain, but good pain).
>
> I listened to the heal your hormones bootcamp and basically realized that there will be no way to heal my hormones without labs, but it is daunting to figure out which labs to run, what to prioritize etc. I believe my problems are due to excessive stress this year, too much cortisol release and adrenal stress. I started taking Hawthorne just to get the blood pressure down and AdrenaCalm as mentioned by Dr. Jackson in the bootcamp to calm down my stress reaction. Of course, all other quantum health protocols still in full force. One exception, I have backed off on CT until my bp is normal. I really don't want to go to a normal doctor and be told I need to take bp meds.
>
> On another positive note, some of my external stress is resolving and I am hopeful for a good 2017.
>
> So, I am looking for recommendations on deciding if I should try to do these labs myself or get the help of a doc. I am ready to pay for the labs that I can out of pocket.

Tracy, regarding the hip pain I suggest checking the Egoscue method. I am also dealing with joint pain but finally I realized that the source of pain is the bad posture (compensations/asymmetries) that we developed by too much sitting and lack of movement and the solution is the posture exercises done DAILY. When I found this [http://breakingmuscle.com/video/straightening-up-progressive-posture-alignment-part-1](http://breakingmuscle.com/video/straightening-up-progressive-posture-alignment-part-1) article I gave a try to this 4-week program, and I felt the benefits at the 3rd day. Since then I am starting my every day with a 20-30 minute posture exercise session, placed a few of those exercises into my warm-up and cool-down of my workouts. Also, doing low-impact posture exercises between the sets. The key is to do them every day! If I skip a day or two I start to feel the discomfort in my hip, neck, back.
The best would be to have a few session with the posture specialist, but I cannot afford that, but maybe you can check the girl who wrote the article - she does Skype consultation - or an Egoscue clinic.
Best Wishes

### Tracy Guild — 2017-01-30T01:19:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-207552>

Thank you Fitness. I am hoping to find a doc soon to order these tests for me. My last reg doc said no to any lab testing, and since I have changed insurance, I have not gone to see a doc (not hopeful that route will be beneficial anyway).

Thank you Tamas for the link. I will take a look at that and give it a try. Posture is important.

Thank you Dr. K for the link. I am guessing you are thinking my hip pain is due to dehydration and inability of the muscle and fascia to deliver electrons? Ok, the fix is to do all the things I am already doing - daily sun, grounding, seafood, emf/blue light blocking (2+ years now). My question about this, though is why would it not be more wide spread? I have no problems like in my hip anywhere else. I also don't have low energy or any of the other symptoms, except I have always been very highly sensitive to noises since childhood.

Health improvements to date: Two years ago, my hip x-ray showed arthritis and impingement in the right hip. Current xray shows no arthritis or impingement at all in my hip. The only problem now is lack of synovial space. My chiro also commented that my hips are very straight and my spine looks terrific. I no longer have metal sensitivity (at all). I am no longer lactose intolerant. Reduction in melasma, no new white spots.

Regarding recent spike in bp - my biohacks have been: reduce caffeine by 3/4, on my way to elimination of caffeine. Weekly massage (the first part is not relaxing, it is therapeutic for my hip but the last 15 min are utter bliss). Increased sun, Hawthorne and Adrenacalm. BP is back to normal, so that is good. Hormones still questionable.

I have avoided blood/lab tests and I really need to get on that.

### Tracy Guild — 2017-03-21T05:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tracy-guild.15069/post-210824>

Monday my magnetico (20 gaus) came in. Feeling pretty good. I did not experience flu like symptoms except a bad burning feeling in my underarms. I thought I might have been in the sun too long this weekend, but that area is not sunburned and I am not sunburned anywhere else. It feels like a rope burn. Hip pain very light. I feel very good. Blood pressure good.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-list.15071/post-166479>

As you might imagine, this summer vacation I will have a lot of free time. I want to read a lot and really enhance my knowledge and understanding. I couldn't find one on the site or forums, so I think making a compilation of books relevant or helpful (maybe even vital) to understanding many of the topics discussed on the blog, could be helpful to everyone.

So far I've seen

Jack Kruse- Epi-Paleo Rx (of course)

O. Becker- The Body Electric, Cross Currents, Electromagnetism and Life

Andrew Marino- Going Somewhere: The Truth About a Life In Science

Robin Sharma- The Monk Who Sold His Ferarri

Gilbert Ling- Life at the Cell and Below-Cell Level

Elizabeth Kolbert- The Sixth Extinction

any additions would be phenomenal. I think this would be really helpful to reference for me and hopefully others too.

\- Matt

# Thread: Chasing Clarity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alexia — 2015-06-04T20:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166570>

Hi all, this is my first post. I'm very excited to have found myself among others who are truly interested in getting to the root of their health issues. Like many, I have been searching for answers for as long as I can remember, and I feel like I'm finally getting close.

I wanted to make this post brief but, since context is key... I've lived in Minnesota my whole life. I grew up on the Mississippi river, swimming in the water in the summer and playing in the snow in the winter. House with well water half the time, apartment with fluoridated city water the other half. As far an environment goes, not too bad. However, I also grew up with the internet, have had a cell phone for 15 years, spent countless hours in school or work on computers with wifi and flourescent lighting, consumed a ton of soda and refined sugar through early adulthood, slept horribly (if at all), and had a childhood characterized by extreme family tension and conflict (divorced parents).

Overall I seem generally healthy, except that my brain seems to be functioning at roughly 37% (just a guess). I started having headaches at age 4, just after my parents separated. I have had a headache, muscle tension and brain fog every day since then. Doctor after doctor did scans told me my brain was normal. We tried medications. Supplements. Elimination diets. “Stress management.” Chiropractic. Acupuncture. Vision correction. Vitamin B-2. Sinus surgery. By 2006 my life revolved around managing 10-15 debilitating migraines a month. Finally I found a neurologist who put me on a multi-faceted treatment program. It took 10 months and five experts to get off of medication. I started reading ancestral health books and finally began to understand that while the root cause was psychosomatic, I was exacerbating the symptoms with poor sleep and sugar. I worked very hard on the stress and tension (now my only stress is really just my two year old ). Reading ancestral health books led me to Dr. Kruse, which led me to beginning to understand how my environment was a key factor.

I've been very aware of how my diet has influenced my brain and body; I used to work in fitness and have been researching as much as I can on my own. I went primal and gluten free for 10 months (which helped alleviate some of the fog but not the pain--I did have an amazing marathon though due to being fat adapted), then I did keto for 21 days (felt amazing after two weeks but no energy) before getting derailed by bad "carb cycling" advice. Since then I've had a very hard time getting back on the wagon.

My biggest struggle right now, is wanting to devour/understand/implement the RIGHT information to fix my brain. I want the big picture to become clear to me, and it's getting there. I know it's a journey, I just have a hard time understanding and simplifying and applying it to my life so that I can begin taking those steps. I'm drinking spring water and chaga, grounding, using blue blockers and turning off my cell phone at night, and working on my diet. We eat a lot of seafood and fats, but I need to wean myself off of dairy and sugars again. I'm reading blogs, forum posts, and have read Epi-Paleo Rx. Starting CT has been a struggle; I've been avoiding cold despite growing up a swimmer on the river in Minnesota. My husband and daughter are perfectly healthy, which I'm very grateful for, but my husband thinks I'm crazy and doesn't want to turn off our wifi or get amber lightbulbs for the house. I just want to wake up ONE day feeling like I actually slept, without a headache, and feel some semblance of clarity. I'm very optimistic. Baby steps........

### nonchalant — 2015-06-04T22:10:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166582>

Ruben is wearing The Jacket???
Hi Alexia, welcome!
You say the root cause is psychosomatic...I'm not so sure about that. If we feel depressed or anxious, we often think back to what could be the psychological cause. Lost pet? Sure, that must be the reason I'm depressed. It's just all psychocomatic.

But could the real reason for the anxiety/depression/whatever be physical? And we just attach whatever recent emotional label that seems to fit? What if our cells themselves are anxious, because of a bad environment?

We can experience trauma, grief, anger, etc as acute distress. But our prolonged reactions to it are determined by health.

### Alexia — 2015-06-05T04:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166636>

Thank you Caroline! Love your profile pic  I'm just getting started on EMF and have not tested our house yet. I think we do have a smart meter, but I'm not sure how to tell without calling the company.

As for the bedroom, I can flip the switch every night. Is there a timer/dead switch I can install for that? We have begun to put our phones on airplane mode at night as a start at least... I told my husband that the bedroom is for two things only, and that doesn't include tv!

I am home with my two year old who loves to be outside and hug trees (literally), so yes we do a lot of that this time of year

Yes to oxy !

### Alexia — 2015-06-05T04:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166637>

Nonchalant, I just saw the rest of your post. You may be right; after counseling as a kid and years of doctors telling me my pain was (ironically) "all in my head," I assumed it must be my reaction. My reaction to stress has always been to pull my shoulders up toward my ears, and eventually it seemed like I was creating the headaches to escape from the stress. After years of doing the emotional work and creating a very happy life, I still have the headaches. Go figure

I have learned over the years that the pain cycle is much more complex than the sufferer understands, despite living it....A notion that is made worse by the sufferer's desire for quick fixes. I've always known there had to be more to it, and that it had to be fixable. I'm so glad my eyes are being opened to the role environment plays.

### Penny — 2015-06-05T05:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166640>

Welcome Alexia

I would go with the dehydration theory of everything on this one myself - it takes 28 molecules of magnesium to break down one molecule of sugar - my husband - even though he still has Pellegrino soda, got rid of his caffeine addiction - many Pepsis per day - and while sometimes it seems like a migraine is coming, it never arrives... ice cold, non flouridated water - seafood and eat your protein and any carbs early in the day - you must detox blue light which can be actual light from a screen or any EMF in your environment - even an alarm clock within 3 feet of your head can emit enough EMF to produce a biological effect - I would be taking Epsom Salt baths and taking 8 magnesium malate pills per day - I would also add chromium and some bitter melon for the sugar spike which blue light/EMF produces - cold dark and boring at night - I find reading at night with a red incandescent lightbulb purchased at a big box knocks me out at night - high fat dinner chased with the cold will help sleep and demolish hunger - like shrimp with bacon, a salad and an avocado - nothing after 6:00 - and think of it as an adventure - ask questions -

For the EMF, get rid of the smart meter, make sure there is no metal on your bed - like a box spring - go to [www.antennaesearch.com](http://www.antennaesearch.com) to check out how close you are to cell phone towers, unplug the wireless router (replace it with a wired one - go to [www.lessemf.com](http://www.lessemf.com) - they'll have an Amazon link to a wired one - which will be faster anyway - put any laptops/ipads/tablets/cell phones on airplane mode or they will still transmit - unplug the base station of your cordless (DECT) phone as that is like having a cell phone tower in your house - no electronics near your head - get a meter - you can order one from these guys -it's on sale $139.00 plus there is a great how to measure video there:

[http://takebackyourpower.net/product/cornet-ed78s-rf-lf-electrosmog-meter/](http://takebackyourpower.net/product/cornet-ed78s-rf-lf-electrosmog-meter/)

Watch "Take Back Your Power" - it's free on Netflix -

Pay attention to the light - yoke food/activity/sunshine with daylight and no food/darkness with sunset and your clock will start to work again - of course you will need seafood for your eyes to run the "clock" in your head so the signalling happens at the proper times - read Cold Thermogenesis 7 to see what proper signalling should look like -

People on this site like to help

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-05T07:43:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-166658>

Medical science will continue to find that mitochondrial function, and not genomic expression, will be the core deficit in the human condition that causes disease. Higher ubiquitin marking rates of proteins will always cause higher mitochondrial mutations to increase and disease to manifest. These two issues lead you to the inconvenient truth for modern humans: The science of how light and timing, fundamentally uncouple ubiquination from circadian signals. Disrupting the SCN causes a "circadian arrhythmia" and an inability to entrain to light. Once this arrhythmia occurs food is no longer a tool that can "fix you". This idea is currently, very “un-paleo”. No human being itself should be considered impaired innately, instead there are environmental short comings that cause the impairment. Thus, it is incumbent on the on the clinician to recommend treatment of the environment their patient is in. People react to an inferior environment, way before their genome is altered. That is what the science of epigenetics and ubiquitin rates are telegraphing us, but the modern paradigm is not listening. You must.

### Alexia — 2015-06-09T06:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chasing-clarity.15113/post-167164>

It was a combination of things. In 2007 I went through treatment with a neurologist, pain psychologist, physical therapist, occupational therapist, and nurse (medication adjustment). I had been taking OTC and RX pain killers constantly, so the goal was to get that under control first (I was having to give myself dihydroergotamine injections). I got down to just topamax and decided to stop taking it. I still had occasional migraines but fewer debilitating ones. The pain withdrawal phase was not fun.

In 2011 I met my husband, who values sleep, and started getting regular sleep. (A huge change because I grew up with night owls.) I read Nora Gedgaudas' "Primal Body, Primal Mind" and started eating a primal template and taking cod liver oil and greens. I ate absolutely no gluten for the better part of a year to find out where my food sensitivities might be, and it turns out I have a limit of how much gluten, dairy and sugar I can have before my headache turns to a migraine. I quit diet soda and started drinking more water. I learned the fine art of emotional detachment and lowered my stress. I got out of retail management, which was making me incredibly sick. I moved out of my city apartment into a house in a more rural area. I found chiropractic. I started being more mindful, praying, doing guided imagery, etc.

Many things that have been helpful, have been intuitive, and are things that Jack recommends--but now I'm finally understanding the "why," what the big picture is and what the critical last pieces are. I am 30 years old, so the time I started having headaches (~1990) coincides with Jack's timeline for rise in nnEMF and is also when my father got his first mobile phone... I've been on a mission for years to fix my head and I'm getting close. I just watched "Take Back Your Power" and will be calling my utility co tomorrow...

# Thread: Jeremy Thomley's success story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeremy-thomleys-success-story.15124/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-05T07:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeremy-thomleys-success-story.15124/post-166656>

Most of you may not know Jeremy. Read the blog I wrote for him called Tensegrity 1.

Tonight I had the pleasure of see a movie called "Anthem" made about Jeremy and MC who share something rather amazing that not too many others could fathom.

I went off to see [Jeremy Thomley](https://www.facebook.com/jeremy.thomley) movie tonight being shown in its premier...........I thought it should be ass kicking. Sold out. Ass kicking was an injustice. I drove home stunned with so many things in my head.

-   Post viewing raw thoughts: What happens to you when you see slivers of someone's true soul and sense their light? I waited to see what "Anthem" had, but the expectations of what I thought it would be never quite captured the visions that I saw tonight. I had a smile on my face the entire movie. I wish I had a digital recording of all the things that went through my mind as I watched it. I was upset with myself, really as I drove home. It's discouraging to think how many people are shocked by honesty and how few by deceitful living. When you see two people living their life in nature and doing what they love to do with passion, it is a remarkable thing. Many believe poor character is the only reason people do wrong things. That is short sighted. Actually, it turns out circumstances cause people to act a certain key critical ways that define them. It's from those circumstances that a person's attitude is affected followed by a strengthening or weakening of character. It is not the reverse path in which most think this occurs. If we had no issues, faults, or failures of our own, we would not take any pleasure in noticing those things in others. It would allow us to stop judging our lives and other lives as black or white, good or bad, different or indifferent. We all live our lives in shades of gray. We are directors of our own film and we control how it is edited. Life should never be led by a silent majority. MC's open quotes in this flick stunned me. They were on breath being light. MC diving back into the water and saying that the water was how she reconnected with her breath also stunned me and made big time sense. Finding out later that MC and [Jeremy Thomley](https://www.facebook.com/jeremy.thomley?hc_location=ufi) met under water for the first time to make it safe was a joy to hear. friends and family are good for many things, but sometimes life requires you to figure out the truth. You can’t figure out the truth, your truth, when you’re asking people around you that are emotionally or socially invested in you to be brutally honest. Sometimes you need a MC to come in and open doors that you just sealed shut. You learn in the toughest circumstances in life. Aim to put yourself in those spots more than you have. Expose your flaws and let life pick your scabs. I see so many people flushing life down the drain daily. It is breath of fresh air to see people exhale air into the environment of others and bring them to life. Instead of living a life "circling the drain" with biased speculation, we should all go out often and get twenty unbiased people, that have nothing to gain or lose if they speak their mind, and then ask them what they think about life and what we think and what we are doing with it. Carefully listen and observe for those people’s perspectives. I think we should consider accepting their answers and opinions, because you’re not going to ever know the real truth when the person you love lies to you. Someone who sees the circumstances you have, brings reality to you in a new way. A new idea or truth emerges. Watching MC and Jeremy in a forrest, climbing and sharing was an amazing random walk through life through someone else's eyes, circumstance, and thoughts. I can't stand movies. Tonight, at a movie I experienced several moments where I saw that love is not about losing or winning circumstances. It is just a few moments in time, followed by an eternity of situations to grow from.

-   I hope people can see this flick in other venues someday.................there is a lesson about life in it we all need to experience.

    { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

    Visit [http://www.mohawksteelco.com/shop/anthem/](http://www.mohawksteelco.com/shop/anthem/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-05T07:32:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeremy-thomleys-success-story.15124/post-166657>

I love my glass aqua lung even more after seeing Anthem tonight. I saw where Anthem pieces came from tonight. The naked truth is small wisdoms make big impacts in life. Tonight you did that for me. The things that really change the world, are the smallest things. A butterfly flaps its wings on a screen in Mississippi, and subsequently a vortex of possibilities may ravages the other side of the globe soon. Small changes in your energy, can lead to massive alterations in results.
We all must learn to push hard to expect great results in this life, Jeremy. If we believe our ability is small, then our efforts will be shallow and our push lame. Your push on a rock, within a sculpture, or within a movie is not small. Your punch, your effect, is deft and sleight in its delivery. It is like the flapping wings of a butterfly disturbing the air around others. With each flap of your wings, the air becomes disturbed across great distances in our souls and minds and can span timescales in many other creatures; it will affect lives unknown to your generous spirit. Few may even know who was the source of this good echo. Your gifts have now been set free and will continue to pass on to others. It will magnify with time because your simple courtesy will become an act of selfless courage for many others with CF. It really is a “CF thing now”, for many others who feel that flap of air later, will see your breath and fell your touch. If we think we are not capable, we won’t try with effort and energy. You tried. Without strong conviction, we won’t seek the knowledge required to achieve our goals. You acted. We will not launch out into the deep or push against obstacles with the force’s breath from us to bring us confidence. Your breath is stronger than the wind of any hurricane. We manufacture our own breath to manifest destiny. You define this. Give fate a push or pull of air, to gain wisdom to breath life into your masterpiece. You create masterpieces. The world needs to see your “Anthem” my friend. Your art and your truth are now butterfly oscillations vibrating across oceans……….I can’t wait to hear those echo’s in others thoughts and see the touch of your breath on their soul’s.

### kovita — 2015-06-05T09:12:52+0300

great thoughts, Jack. Everytime I think about what is happening with my life I am more and more sure, it was the hardship of physical pain and loss of confident and powerfull body what made all the important difference to get happy again. It breaks you and throws you to the deepest mud so you can rise again from scratch.

Random thought of the week that should stun you: Half the bestselling drugs in the US target the product of a circadian gene yet pharma ignores chronobiology...........share it your fellow MD's, famil, friends and your patients.

-   For example take the best selling drugs, the statins, do they target circadian cycles?

-   Yep. How?
-   They increase the distance, in angstroms, between the respiratory proteins...........for every increase of 1 Angstrom the rate of quantum tunneling of electrons drops ten fold. Do you think that drop has no effect on bio-energenics in a cell? This is why statins cause cognitive delay and metabolic syndrome. They increase the distance electrons must travel via tunneling. Tiny shifts in your thinking, and even smaller changes in your energy, can lead to massive alterations of your results. This is why this happen biophysically. People need to know this data. They also need to know that supplementing ubiquinol is highly beneficial for statin patients because it shortens the distance between the same proteins to lower the energy barrier for quantum tunneling of electrons.
-   The next disease? NSAIDS and Degenerative arthritis..................[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20042581](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20042581)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T21:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-you-think-circadian-biology-is-not-important.15131/post-170936>

Why are summer time high fat diets not a great thing to do chronically across all seasons? High fat diets force the use of mitochondria because they are oxidative powerhouses. But what if your mitochondria functionally suck? If that is the case you must first optimize the things a mitochondria uses to recycle. That is O2 and UV light collection to maintain NAD+ levels at cytochrome 1. What does that mean if you have real bad mito-nuclear coaptation and a poor redox? What does it mean for good coaptation? High carbohydrate diets provide more energy by fermentation, without causing humans actually needing to use their badly functioning mitochondria. Bacteria tend to like this situation. Where are bacteria in you? How to do bacteria and your mitonuclear coapatation work together? Here is another clue: those with mitochondrial diseases who have "adopted, used, and advocated" long term ketogenic diets have been known to collapse into coma because their damaged mitochondria can't provide enough energy needed for a conscious state. They actually cannot function neurologically without help from fermentation. Environmentally broken Paleo advocates use and advocate carbs for that reason. Environmentally broken LCHF folks just get sicker as time evolves because they cannot replace DHA fast enough to offset their environment they allow their mitochondria to face. Some might begin to advocate temporary nicotine use when they are completely stalled and spinning their wheels.........Why might nicotine work? What other cofactors would make nicotine work even better? Nicotine works best with good O2 consumption and strong natural sunlight. Most nicotine users smoke so they never see the optimized effect of nicotine because of co-morbid lung disease from smoking. Most people who are sick rarely go out in the sun because their doctors or their beliefs keep them ignorant of basic physics. So how might nicotine work when O2 and UV light are optimized? Nicotine slightly increases glucose levels to give a small superoxide pulse to make new mitochondria and dig yourself out of the hole called mitochondria senescence. You cannot get the superoxide pulse without the O2 or the UV exposure. Did you know that DHA in your RBC's in skin arterioles 7-10 AM allows you to absorb even more UV light? If you're anemic, hypoxic, or diabetic, your fighting an uphill battle but you can reverse it if you get the details correct. Few people have that discipline. Some of us do. That is why we share what we have learned. All people as they age, they acquire this same kind of mito-nuclear coaptation dysfunction.......the major difference in an older person is the timescale of the decline is slower and results in diseases of aging instead of the neolithic disease today's modern humans get 20-40 years old. My PSA today

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-05T21:54:28+0300

Summertime carbs offer high energy photons to aid mitogenesis and turnover. When energies are high ubiquitination and turnover are possible. This is the role of summer. It is a growth season to prep the system for harder times. The oxidation is offset by higher vitamin D levels at this time (UVB).

Pincussen found in 1937 that carbohydrate metabolism is greatly influenced by ultra-violet and infrared sunlight in combination. The effect is lost when the two forms of electromagnetic energy is delivered separately by artificial means. These forms of sunlight are both coupled in natural sunlight and act to lower blood sugar naturally. This points out why strong sunlight in spring and summer helps offset the blood sugar raising effects of foods that grow during these seasons. Nature couples these effects of sunlight on food to limit RBC and WBC glycation. Today these things are uncoupled in modern humans by our choices. RBC and WBC glycation make these cells less paramagnetic and more dimagnetic. When this occurs they cannot deliver their payload of DHA and oxygen as they do normally. The affect of glycation alters the iron oxidation state making the hemoglobin more dimagnetic. Glycation/dimagnetism affects how much UV light can be stored and carried in RBC mass.

### Sami — 2015-06-05T16:04:03+0300
