# Thread: Hi Everyone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.15208/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daedalus351 — 2015-06-08T10:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.15208/post-167042>

Hello,

I'm Chris from just outside Brisbane, Australia. I've been battling some health problems for nearly two years now and I came upon Dr Kruse's site, I think, late last year sometime. I found the content extremely interesting and it covered much of what I was/am dealing with, and the parts that I could understand made sense to me. I think at the time I wasn't feeling well enough to make too many changes to what I was doing to try to and get better but I found a new Dr about the same time and she has helped me move in the right direction.

When I say I've been unwell for two years, that's not quite accurate. I've been unwell enough to not be able to function well and have been actively trying to get better for two years. I think the problems started back in my mid teens or even earlier. The problems I'm trying to recover from are: leaky gut, adrenal fatigue (I really dislike that name), and the things that go along with them, food intolerance (eggs, dairy, salicylates), low hormone levels, anxiety, depression, irritability, low energy, etc.

Early last year I started cutting out, on the advice of a naturopath, a lot of the foods I was eating. I thought I was eating pretty well - I ate a lot of meat, salad, vegetables and fruits - but I also ate a lot of junk as well. Right now I eat a piece of steak and a small bowl of rice mixed with ghee for breakfast, roast port, oysters, and some more rice for lunch and then meat and salad or vegetables for dinner. I only drink filtered water at the moment. The oysters for lunch is an addition I made last week and the steak and rice for breakfast is only something I've been doing for a bit over two weeks. Prior to that I was eating a bowl of porridge for breakfast.

Thanks to those who have read this short summary. I plan on posting more details elsewhere, plus various questions I have from reading the various blog posts.

### Daedalus351 — 2015-06-09T07:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.15208/post-167174>

Thanks everyone

I will be starting a journal when I get a moment to put all the information together.

I'm a software developer. I work in an office building in the Brisbane CBD.

I've read through about half of the easy start guides so far but I will read the rest. I've read a heap of the blog posts as well - many I plan on forwarding to family members because they explain so well what their issues are.

I haven't listened to any of the podcasts yet but I travel an hour a day to and from work so I will be able to during those trips.

nnEMF is around me quite a bit, especially at work with many Wi-Fi networks and mobile phone towers around. At home it's not as bad. There is a phone tower about 400m from the house but it's on the other side of a hill so the signal isn't as strong as it might be. Other than our own Wi-Fi network there is one other that I can detect. I've configured ours to turn off at midnight and come back on at 6am. I also reduced the transmission power The only electronic device in the bedroom is a mobile phone in flight mode used as an alarm.

I've ordered some blue blocker glasses. I'm hoping they will be delivered early next week.

Since early last year I've made sure to go out and spend some time in the sun every day, especially on work days. On the weekends I walk around outside barefoot but not as often as I could/should. Unfortunately I rarely get into the ocean any more. I grew up in a small coastal town so was in the water all the time as a kid but only go to the beach a couple of times a year now.

The chevice (ceviche?) sounds really nice and I pretty much love all seafood but I'd probably have to leave out a couple of the ingredients for a while (based on the recipe I found by Googling, anyway) due to the intolerances I seem to have.

### b612 — 2015-06-09T11:56:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.15208/post-167205>

You are a software developer... Your profession might be making you ill. I spent three years working with software developers in the same office. I had A LOT of health problems back then. Actually, no one in the office looked healthy. We had massive servers just behind the door... My advice for you is to drink a lot of water. What I noticed is that programming makes me really thirsty, lol. Much more than just reading news online. It has someting to do with intensive typing, thinking and problem solving.
Hydrate well and spend lots of time outside!

# Thread: june webinar and the direction of time

### lilly antic — 2015-06-08T18:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167098>

Listening (and re-listening) to the june webinar , I was almost knocked off my feet, as it might give an explanation to one of the weirdest experiences I have ever had.
Some 15 years ago I had a very spiritual phase, spending 1-2 hours daily meditating.I was practising a technique called Kriya Yoga, which was supposed to increase the energy flows of the spine and the like.
( Back then, I didn't spend much thought about the effects- I just did it, ..).
One day, sitting on my newly arrived sofa in my new flat, relaxing, the following super-bizarre situation occurred: I could foretell IN DETAIL the happenings of the next hours. Not like a momentary dejà-vu, but like a film, clear and detailed. These were happenings, which were not very likely to occur, like a long lost friend calling with spectacular news, my sister entering the room with a surprise present( I saw that she did and what the present was) etc...
It all took place exactly like my "foreseeing".
This in itself is mind-blowing and amazing, but the real shocker is that it was NOT a foretelling in the FUTURE, as in this direction ------>
but a MEMORY, as in this direction <---------------.
It was as clear a memory as thinking of what one did the previous day. Only in the other direction.

If I understood correctly in the webinar, in a star and in the mitochondria(only on a different scale) light can be slowed down by strong EMF, and time can be uncoupled from space. Time needs light to be moving in order for it to emerge and to acquire a direction. Time emerges from the collision of light with atoms.
What if the meditation technique somehow really increased the currents in the spine/ cells/ wherever , so the light slowed down somehow or was altered and time's direction was changed for a moment??????????
Can that be??????

A member who recently left (or was banned?) the Quantum Health facebook group sent me this a couple days ago; I feel as though it is rather related (as are all this guys videos):

Lil summary/excerpt: "**Could the future be an emergent interactive property with 'time' formed by the spontaneous absorption and emission of light photon energy?** A continuous process of energy exchange that forms the ever changing world of our everyday life. The Universe is a continuum with the future coming into existence photon by photon with each new **photon electron coupling or dipole moment.** This forms the movement of positive and negative charge with the continuous flow of electromagnetic fields. Consciousness in the form of electrical activity in the brain is the most advanced part of this process and can therefore comprehend this process as 'time'. With a past that has gone forever and a future that is always uncertain in the form of a probability function or quantum wave particle function that is explained mathematically by Schrödinger's wave equation Ψ. Therefore each individual is in the centre of their own reference frame as an interactive part of this process being able to look back in time in all directions at the beauty of the stars! It is this personalization of the brain being in ‘the moment of now’ in the center of its own reference frame that gives us the concept of ‘mind’ with each one of us having our own personal view of the beauty and uncertainty of life. At the smallest scale of this process this uncertainty is seen as Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle ∆×∆p×≥h/4π with the Planck constant ħ=h/2π being a constant of action in the dynamics of space and time!"

I prefer Hameroff and Penrose's approach -- [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1571064513001188](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1571064513001188)

Some of my incomplete notes (didn't do anything before Section 3) -- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/orch-or/](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/orch-or/)

Like I wrote in my notes, I think the dependence on the big-G gravitational constant that Hameroff uses is probably not correctly assumed to be a constant. I personally think that G needs to be calculated relative to the electrostatic moment of the displaced mass. (no proof of this yet, just an intuition)

The Orch-OR model predicts consciousness as being a first-order qualia of light-matter interactions (and so is time). It predicts that light-matter interactions in the presence of gravity cause objective reduction of quantum superpositions into what we deem as classical phenomena.

I think that Hameroff does not give enough speculation into the role of EZ water surrounding microtuble lattices. If anything, I think that the water is there to serve as: **(a)** a means of information transfer, and **(b)** a way to isolate certain parts of the the microtubule syncytium from external entanglement effects. I will need to give this more thought.

I think that everything Jack said in the June 2015 webinar is compatible with the Orch-OR model.

.....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-09T05:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167154>

G is never constant........that is a standard model failure. The same thing is true of radioactive decay.......and the result is seen in how neutrino from different source react differently because of their oscillations.......hence no way radioactive decay is stable or constant. In my view, everything is built to be metastable and far from equilibrium. Physics supports symmetry. I don't. Biology supports and has an equilibrium bias........I reject it.

My blog paradox: I’m a quantum alchemist that craves to be understood. Ironically, alchemy desires the mystery of enigma. In QED, certainty is replaced by probability, which rises and falls like a wave. Biology hates "guessing" and "surfing" and this is why I doubt biology at the most fundamental levels. Waves for two particles having the same energy, but slightly different masses, will have marginally different wavelengths. Which brings up a another enigma, what about electrons that carry the same mass, but carry different energies from the photons they carry by their seasonal variations? Remember all food is stripped to electrons in electron chain transport.
What Nature doesn’t forbid, will happen. Time emerges because of these interactions.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-09T06:36:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167169>

For the sake of clarity for myself and other readers, let's break down this reply a little

​

\----

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=167154)
>
> G is never constant........that is a standard model failure.

Agreed

\----

> In my view, everything is built to be metastable and far from equilibrium

To be clear about the implications, "Far from equilibrium" means that there must always be a lot of energy stored in the system.

The only way to be "metastable", while being able to make use of all that stored energy is to have cycles of energy transformation => these cycles are inherently coupled, hence the whole importance on accuracy with timing (circadian biology)

(book ref: 'Rainbow and the Worm')

\----

> Physics supports symmetry ... Biology hates "guessing" and "surfing" and this is why I doubt biology at the most fundamental levels

One of the things that Hameroff notes is that the brain becomes consciously aware of a stimulus about 30ms after the stimulus, but then only after about 400-500ms does the full awareness of decision making arise.

In essence, the brain is looking back into the past via the CPT invariant laws of Quantum Physics, and then using the best computational path to get things done.

IMO this is the only explanation for how exciton transport is solved in photosynthesis, to achieve a near 100% efficiency of electron transfer away from reaction centres (book ref: 'Life on the Edge').

Biologic systems routinely (and I daresay deterministically) solve the Travelling Salesman Problem, which a Computer Science guy like me know is still a Turing incomputable NP-hard problem. **Translation**: a million existing supercomputers can't create 100% efficient electron transfer even once, yet a plant does it billions and billions of times every second.

Path-Dependent Computation is critical in Biology, and seems to require backwards time reference through Quantum Mechanical laws. "Path-Dependent" just means that an action that happens in the present requires all the available information of the previously taken paths to be readily available, in order to make a good decision.

\----

> The same thing is true of radioactive decay.......and the result is seen in how neutrino from different source react differently because of their oscillations.......hence no way radioactive decay is stable or constant

> Waves for two particles having the same energy, but slightly different masses, will have marginally different wavelengths. Which brings up a another enigma, what about electrons that carry the same mass, but carry different energies from the photons they carry by their seasonal variations?

This is where things get confusing for me .

Wallace Thornhill has also put forth the idea of a plenum of Neutrinos that act as the aether for light transmission. He has offered no proof of this yet, and it still remains a speculative model ([http://qhwiki.com/video:eu-nov-2014-wal-thornhill#toc5](http://qhwiki.com/video:eu-nov-2014-wal-thornhill#toc5))

Neutrino energies also vary. If the "plenum on neutrinos" model is true, then it could be plausible that when comparing a blue-light excited electron and a red-light excited electron, they both carry the same mass, but the blue light electron "distributes its mass/energy" to the neutrino lattice, but resonates / remains entangled with that energy, so that upon objective realisation of the energy, the blue light electron can emit a high-energy photon.

Speculative thoughts for sure

.....

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-10T16:54:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167398>

Hi Lilly

When I was about 17 I was walking along a road one early winter evening (Safe, lots of people around etc). I heard footsteps running up to me from behind, and without turning round I saw instantly what was about to happen. He could have just been going to run past me, or catch up to speak because he knew me etc, but without turning, I put both hands on my woolly hat and held on tight, just before the stranger, a young man, tried to grab it off my head. He asked me how I had known he was about to do that …. Perhaps I entangled with the young man’s intention (to take my hat, a future event) and brought the information back to the visual camera of my present. Perhaps it was not reversing the arrow of time, but an information arrow from the probable future to my present.

As a pulse or wave begins and gains momentum, time might speed up, then start slowing again as it reaches its peak, then there might be a moment where time stands still because it has stopped moving, before it speeds up again moving to the next off position, a bit like a pendulum coming to a stop before it swings back the other way. At the 2 potential switched off time positions, perhaps consciousness/the observer can expand in time and space in any direction, bringing back information when the light starts moving again. The movie stops, and the observer pops off to view a later clip, the movie starts again …. The observer has returned knowing what is coming later …..

Perhaps the better the transmitter/receiver unit of the brain (SCN, water, DHA etc) together with meditation makes a more efficient system. Perhaps if the SCN isn’t running faster than the body clocks it doesn’t work properly. What happens if the body knows something the mind doesn’t…?

By creating the right environment, a meditator might have a distinct advantage. Maybe the more in sync (efficient) our brain waves/light emissions are, the more information we can obtain at the moments of rest, and pay better attention to, and the further our consciousness can expand to get even more information, with more advance notice.

The interesting thing was that I saw what was going to happen, and I changed the outcome. Had I had the information earlier I could have changed the outcome by going home a different way. Although my brain seemed to foresee the event, and instructed my arms and hands to act, it would have been better decision to assess the risk properly, even if that meant losing my hat ….. Not eating enough seafood at 17 ?……..Ho hum …. My grandmother had dementia, my mother has dementia, and at one time I thought that would be the way I was likely to go ….. But reflecting on the incident of the hat encourages me that that yes that is where I might end up ….. But that with the right information and enough time to implement it, it is not a certainty, I can completely change the outcome …..

If my musings are complete rubbish, in my defence I'm probably talking from under the wrong hat

From WIKI:
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Family\_Constellations](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Family_Constellations)
**Family Constellations** is an [alternative](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alternative_medicine) therapeutic method which draws on elements of family systems therapy, [existential phenomenology](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Existential_phenomenology) and [Zulu](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zulu_people) attitudes to family.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Family_Constellations#cite_note-1) In a single session, a Family Constellation supposedly attempts to reveal a previously unrecognized systemic dynamic that spans multiple generations in a given family and to resolve the deleterious effects of that dynamic by encouraging the subject to accept the factual reality of the past.

Once I went there, not knowing what to believe . I was put in the role of a person I didn't know anything about, and the whole family was represented by strangers and each member placed in a certain position in relation to the others. I didn't have any thoughts or feelings in the position they placed me, my knees just kept bending, turning me in a direction way from the other people. This movement went on as long, as it took to face me into the opposite direction of the rest of the family. All by itself! I could not resist the urge to turn. The body knew what my mind didn't even perceive as an idea. And it was not even **my life** we were trying to place.
There is so much knowledge stored and we are all connected to it through .... Light????

### DavidL — 2015-06-11T18:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167631>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=167600)
>
> Btw--I'm aware that I reported a "vision" which links more to the brain, and at the same time when I reflect on it, it was an experience in my body that led to a visual representation of that feeling, and then to my mind making meaning out of it. Hope this makes sense.

Interesting. Here's how I became intrigued by "time".

Years ago I had "a vision" about the place where I work blowing up...I was training on the hydrogen unit when i had this stream of consciousness....called wife and dad to tell them (dad worked there too at the time). I couldn't grab a "time stamp" for the vision but it was too powerful/strong to ignore that something was about to happen. I went home that night thinking I had totally lost it.

My "vision" of my sense of future events were confirmed when the next day, a line carrying a mixture of hydrogen and diesel burst and blew up a couple units at the refinery. Massive cloud of smoke and fireball. [LINK to ARTICLE](http://www.cjme.com/story/videophotos-co-op-refinery-explosion-fire-regina/26387) At the time of the explosion I was sitting with my head beside a 4160 kv switchgear in a substation in the hydrogen unit (massive nnEMF)...

I was one of the first operators on the scene, fighting the fire and blocking in pumps in the compressor building where the explosion occurred. I remember as we hosed down the units that "time" had been completely altered. The trauma and shockwave of the explosion in my sense had altered time...it was if things were happening so slow...

From this day forward I began to wonder about "time". How did I have access to time? Had the pipe been x-rayed say earlier that day we would have found the thin line and shut the plant down and repair it. It was true that the pipe was going to burst at somepoint...it was a certain future occurrence but without looking, no one would know when it would happen or that it would happen. Yet I did.

My questions have always been how do we access future occurrences? Where is that information coming from? Why did I pick up that information? What in my biology picks up that information? What is time? What it our purpose of being "stuck in the now" with limited conscious access to the future events?

My next experience that made me wonder about time was a past life regression. I watched a movie in mind of what I sensed to be myself in a previous "time". Vivid. Moving. Very personal and fascinating. Access to "past time".....where in our biology/space time allows us access to this? The past life regression was no different than any childhood memories that I have in this life. Seeing a past life is like remembering riding your favorite bike, details may be not clear/possibly embellished/tied to emotion....

Then DHA happened. "Dha slows time". SO I ate more Dha. And what happened? My sense of time in this life began to change.....hours are not the same hours as I had 5 years ago.....

sorry if this is too woo

> My "vision" of my sense of future events were confirmed when the next day, a line carrying a mixture of hydrogen and diesel burst and blew up a couple units at the refinery. Massive cloud of smoke and fireball. [LINK to ARTICLE](http://www.cjme.com/story/videophotos-co-op-refinery-explosion-fire-regina/26387) At the time of the explosion I was sitting with my head beside a 4160 kv switchgear in a substation in the hydrogen unit (massive nnEMF)...
>
> I was one of the first operators on the scene, fighting the fire and blocking in pumps in the compressor building where the explosion occurred. I remember as we hosed down the units that "time" had been completely altered. The trauma and shockwave of the explosion in my sense had altered time...it was if things were happening so slow...
>
> From this day forward I began to wonder about "time". How did I have access to time? Had the pipe been x-rayed say earlier that day we would have found the thin line and shut the plant down and repair it. It was true that the pipe was going to burst at somepoint...it was a certain future occurrence but without looking, no one would know when it would happen or that it would happen. Yet I did.
>
> My questions have always been how do we access future occurrences? Where is that information coming from? Why did I pick up that information? What in my biology picks up that information? What is time? What it our purpose of being "stuck in the now" with limited conscious access to the future events?
>
> My next experience that made me wonder about time was a past life regression. I watched a movie in mind of what I sensed to be myself in a previous "time". Vivid. Moving. Very personal and fascinating. Access to "past time".....where in our biology/space time allows us access to this? The past life regression was no different than any childhood memories that I have in this life. Seeing a past life is like remembering riding your favorite bike, details may be not clear/possibly embellished/tied to emotion....
>
> Then DHA happened. "Dha slows time". SO I ate more Dha. And what happened? My sense of time in this life began to change.....hours are not the same hours as I had 5 years ago.....
>
> sorry if this is too woo

No woo, but muy bueno

Reading all this I am more convinced than ever that time is nothing but a thin veil covering up the real thing. Thank you ,Jack for bringing up the june webinar . I am curious where all this is heading.
UBI 17 is fantastic! One of my favourites so far.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-06-13T03:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167883>

Well, what a quantum coincidence. I was just thinking about Einstein's Mass Equivalence, then looked up some information on retrocausality and wanted to ask about it here, only to find you thinking about similar things

E2 = p2c2 + m2c4 (those numbers are exponents) being a quadratic has two solutions - one positive and one negative. The positive -> energy from a cause. The negative -> "backward in time" energy from a cause, in other words, effect before cause.

This relates back to a lot of what Dr. Ray Peat described in the 70's about time as both an emergent, interactive property, and also as a source of "energy". The "backward in time" energy from a cause can --> increase in differentiation and complexity, reduction in entropy, increase in order and information.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-06-13T03:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167884>

"In 1941 the mathematician Luigi Fantappiè (1901–1956), while working on the properties of the equations that combine quantum mechanics with special relativity, realized that the forward-in-time solution describes energy and matter that tends towards a homogeneous and random distribution. When heat radiates from a heater, it tends to spread out homogeneously in the environment; this is the law of entropy, which is also known as heat death. Fantappiè showed that whereas the forward-in-time solution is governed by the law of entropy (from the Greek en = diverging, tropos = tendency), the backward-in-time solution is governed by a symmetric law that Fantappiè named syntropy (from the Greek syn = converging, tropos = tendency). The forward-in-time solution describes energy that diverges from a cause, and requires that causes be in the past. But the backward-in-time solution looks—to us moving forward in time—like the energy is concentrating, rather than dissipating. The mathematical properties of syntropy are energy concentration, an increase in differentiation and complexity, a reduction of entropy, the formation of structures, and an increase in order and information. These are also the main properties that biologists observe in life and which cannot be explained in the classical (time forward) way. This realization led Fantappiè to suggest, in The Unitary Theory of the Physical and Biological World, published in 1942, that life is caused by the future."

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-13T07:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-167909>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/), @[cinnamon](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5619/), and @[bio-fractal-soul-self](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12496/)

Time to put my God Complex to work and start speculating

\-----

I don't like trying to use Math as a basis . Truth should be obtained by finding empirical observations, formulating a plausible mechanism based on those observations, and then maybe using Math to describe the mechanism after the fact.

If time is emergent from interaction between light and matter, then having the notion of "a future" would make the system completely unstable. There must be some global notion of the present if a coupled system can be stable. If there wasn't such thing as global time, components could not coordinate cause and effect, and the whole system falls apart.

"global" refers to the bounds of the system; it is possible to have another system that is either uncoupled from our Universe, or only loosely coupled, such that it has minimal interaction with the laws of our Universe​

It is more accurate to say that given a particular state of energy and matter of a particular system, there are a finite set of possible states that it can progress to. ie: There are constraints set on what the future states can be. This does not imply that the future influences the present.

Backward time reference is perfectly compatible with Quantum Mechanics and all observations made --> The states a system can progress to is influenced by the states it has transitioned through in the past.

\----

**Celeritas (c) = 299,792,458 ms^-1**

I currently think that time is more accurately described as:

The quanta of energy transferred between 2 CMOs​

This is a "tick" in the fundamental compute cycle of the Universe, and is defined in energy units. Ticks can only happen when there is momentum (see below for definition).

(0) CMO is short for "Condensed Momentum Object", and refers to a piece of matter with the lowest possible momentum for a particular light interaction => this is the most condensed state of matter capable of interacting with light. There is thus also is the smallest possible spacing between each CMO.

(1) Define momentum in the quantum mechanical sense, which is a function that generates the momentum eigenvalue. An easier way to visualise this is that the CMO has a "hole" in all dimensions around it (3 dimensions as assumed in (1)). Light can fill these holes. A hole is distorted in the direction of all the possible energetic transfers which the CMO can undergo. Hence, momentum represents the possible vector space to which kinetic energy can be potentially transferred. This necessarily sets a lower bound on how far apart any 2 CMOs can be (hence why this is the most condensed form of matter possible for a particular light interaction).

(2) Assume that space is filled with CMOs in 3 dimensions. (this is an assumption to which I have no proof ). The other underlying assumption is that minimum distance is limited only by the minimum momentum that a CMO may have. Each CMO must have some momentum, so the distance between 2 CMOs cannot be zero.

The transfer of energy (light) from one CMO to the next nearest CMO, is termed a unit of time. Time is now defined as a function of energy and momentum (where momentum can be uncertain).

The "speed **limit** of light" is then a result of the lower bound on distance between CMOs. The time (energy) it takes for light to travel a distance of meter is then best defined as the number of "CMO hops" that need to happen before the meter distance is reached.

\----

For this model to work, photons of different energies must be transferred "just as easily" (at the same rate / speed).

Photon energy has been observed to be described by: E = hc / _λ_ = hv

where:
\- E is the energy of the photon
\- h is the Planck Constant
\- v is the frequency of the photon
\- c is the speed of light in vacuum
\- _λ_ is the wavelength of the photon​

Photon momentum has been observed to be described by: p = ħk

where:
\- p is photon momentum
\- ħ is the reduced Planck constant: ħ = _h_/2π
\- k is the wave vector (related to the wave number by |k| = 2π/_λ_)​

I think both equations are fairly accurate, however, the Planck Constant h has been measured to various degrees of certainty using different methods -- [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Planck\_constant#Determination](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Planck_constant#Determination) . We have reason to suspect that this may not actually be a true constant, but may vary according to the fundamental momentum a CMO has.

Sidenote: there seem to be larger uncertainties in the Planck Constant when there is significant magnetic fields, or when crystal properties are introduced (birefringence at a fundamental level?).​

If momentum of a piece of matter corresponds to the "size of the energetic hole" that it can accommodate, then this variance in the Planck Constant is natural, since the momentum that each CMO will have to accommodate is going to be altered by the light that it interacts with.

We may also have a fundamental explanation for the phrase: "Red light slows time down, while Blue light speeds it up"

Compare a red light photon going from point A to point B, which has a lower energy than a Blue light photon. The Red light photon will actually result in less energetic transfer between each CMO in order to get to point B. The signal still reaches point B, but with less energy transitions => time is directly proportionate to how much energy is transferred from one momentum-containing particle to another.

\----

That's enough speculation for now ....

....

Then (perhaps when consciousness diminishes below a critical mass), time starts to go backwards chronically. I think certain behaviours considered odd are attempts to generate charge, or reset the clock, to get time running forward again.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-09T06:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-171447>

> [lilly antic said:](/goto/post?id=167123)
>
> It would be fantastic to have a blog about this webinar. It is so complex and a quite difficult topic, and NOBODY is talking about _**this.**_ But it makes so much sense and I feel there is the key to many processes( Might also explain how you yourself manage to do all the things you do; work, family, blogs, talks....probably your arrow of time also goes in a different direction, like physics has shown in experiments.

Well it is done............very detailed. Here is a preview of the next series.........TIME: Visualizing a civilizations recovery plan what did Feynman say was the key point to leave them?

Imagine a major planetary catastrophe. It could be a global nuclear war, a CME, a devastating global pandemic, or rapid climate change due to interruption of photosynthesis or the thermohaline current. It could be a viper computer virus, wiping out all digital memory banks on servers. In any of these scenarios, we would anticipate an economic and environmental collapse. The catastrophe would directly and indirectly involve a massive die-off of human populations. What did RF say was the key?

Light and atoms. The earth receives about one billionth of the suns energy, and that humans utilize about one millionth of that. So we consume about one million billionth of the suns total energy. We have a lot to learn according to that basic math. There is nothing in the laws of physics which prevents building armies of molecular-sized machines that give us quantum control of most things we can experience. We can do that with something called a quantum dot.

In the 1960's Feynman was asked to think about what might be the most important scientific “sound bite” to pass onto future civilizations faced with catastrophe. Feynman opted to give future peoples the idea that "all things are made of atoms" This shocked many. I think it was brilliant because 50 years later we still have not opened that box of Pandora to get the essence of this gift. Atoms are little particles that move around in perpetual motion, attracting each other when they are a little distance apart, but repelling upon being squeezed into one another. How light interacts with with matter is key...............

In fact, when Feynman he saw atoms for the first time with a scanning tunneling microscope he called it a religious experience.

My bet is he would have thought he died and went to heaven if he knew about atomic force microscopy. We can only guess today that atomic force microscopy which extends our ability to see beyond the scanning tunneling microscope. This method of observation allows us to probe the atomic forces between atoms and light that have been found so elemental to scientific understanding. That interaction is what RF said was most important so now 50 years later we are just getting the ability to fully assess that interaction.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-10T03:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-171513>

The universe is expanding; interstellar distances are becoming greater and greater. How does the relationship between space, time, and the speed of light compensate for this? Changes in interstellar distances are measurable. The speed of light is constant and is measurable but it the weight of time variable? If it is as its weight changes it would drag through Einstein's space time because gravity would slow it down because of gravitational lensing. But what about time itself, can changes to time be measured? Or is that a paradox within itself? How can we measure a potential change in Time itself if it is changing while we are trying to measure it?

For example, hypothetically speaking, if you are trying to measure how much a yard stick is decreasing in length against a second yard stick that is also decreasing in length at the same rate as the first one and if everything else around you (including yourself) was shrinking at the same rate than you would never confirm that such a change actually did occur. So if we look at the relationship between space, time, and the speed of light; if space is measureably expanding and the speed of light is still constant, can Time be speeding up?

You can measure it if light gains weight.
So How Heavy is Light and how does its weight change?

Drop a stone from a bridge, and it will accelerate, but shine a laser beam down, and its propagation speed is fixed. Still, Einstein's relativity theory predicts that the light beam will _gain energy as it drops,_ not by speeding up, **_but by slightly increasing in frequency_**, which is the same as a tiny shift in color. The shift was too small to detect until 1959, when physicists discovered an extremely precise technique for measuring the frequency of short-wavelength light called gamma rays. Robert Pound and Glen Rebka of Harvard University set up a gamma ray source at the top of a tower in a Harvard physics building and a detector in the basement, 74 feet below. They detected a frequency shift of just a few parts in 10 to 15th power and verified the so-called gravitational _redshift_ predicted by Einstein to within ten percent of the expected value.

No wonder they say, “the older you get, the faster time goes by”, if they only new. How it happens is _illuminating_. Humanity continually strives to make its vehicles, technologies, and processes faster yet we never seem to have enough time or wish we had more time, doesn’t that make you wonder that humanity might be speeding up time by its use of light? If this is true might the change be measured or seen in other forces in nature the are linked to light's action? The Schumann resonance should be altered if time is speeding up. Is it? There is some foreign data to support it.

Schumann discovered in 1951 that the earth gives off a pulse, it has a heart beat. This frequency of this beat has been stable at approximately 7.83 Hertz (cycles per second) for thousands of years. However,no credible source geomagnetic source has reported this change. There is evidence that the Earth's magnetic field is very complex and chaotic now and that alone could alter timing on Earth.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-17T15:47:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-184335>

Einstein received the Nobel Prize for explaining the photoelectric effect. In my humble opinion this effect is still not well appreciated by physicists. This effect was described initially in metals. Its greatest effect is on biologic topologic insulators. It occurs if a metal electrode is treated with photons, which liberate electrons if their quantum energy is high enough. In simple words, photons can make ions thicker or thinner, depending on absorption or emission activity. The other key that people forget is that this effect includes momentum buried in it.......even though the math equation for it does not have it in the open. This is what makes it so misunderstood.

Mitochondrial surfaces have 30 million volt potential which is akin to a bolt of lightening. Plasma is a form of powerful light. Lightening is a form of plasma. Can plasma affect a water surface photoelectrically? Plasma-activated water: less exotic than it sounds. After solid, liquid and gas comes plasma, the fourth state of matter. When lightening hits water is makes defects in water's surfaces and coherent domains to allow natural magnetic monopoles to appears on a fleeting basis as long as the plasma affects the water. Lightning is one of Nature’s ways of making plasma, and scientists can replicate it in the lab by running electricity through a pair of electrodes; the strong electrical field ionizes air molecules and turns them into plasma. Scientists have been experimenting with creating plasmas since the late 19th century; sometime in the mid-20th century they discovered that when they exposed water to a plasma, the water picked up molecules such as nitrogen oxide and hydrogen peroxide from the air that made it more acidic and capable of fending off microbes. “Spray the water through the lightning,and you create a natural antimicrobial spray.

### PaulG — 2015-12-18T05:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-184412>

Well, JK said it was a frequency - so 1523 puts it in the Near Infrared C band.

During the day the planet receives near-ultraviolet spectrum (250nm-380nm), visible spectrum (380nm-720nm) and **near-infrared spectrum (720nm-1500nm)**.
Humans use 1550nm for optical fibre communications. See [this post](https://www.researchgate.net/post/Why_is_1550_nm_the_most_widely_used_wavelength_in_optical_communication_systems2)

1523 or thereabouts is used in [Near Infrared Spectroscopy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Near-infrared_spectroscopy) hence my post about Aquaphotimics in the Quantum Facebook page which jumped off the page when I first read it and set off my spider senses...

So, my guess is that 1523nm is the particular frequency of monochromatic light that is emitted from the mitochondria. The sweet spot between light and heat. Doping efficiency of the collagen semiconductors is dependent on absorption and scattering. At 1523nm water absorption is high, scattering is low therefore the attenuation is low. See this article on [Water Absorption Spectrum](http://www1.lsbu.ac.uk/water/water_vibrational_spectrum.html) by Martin Chaplin and See [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electromagnetic\_absorption\_by\_water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electromagnetic_absorption_by_water)

The C22H32O2 structure of DHA is quite unique and Crawford talks about it in his paper [A quantum theory for the irreplaceable role of docosahexaenoic acid in neural cell signalling throughout evolution](http://www.ars.usda.gov/SP2UserFiles/person/4986/set4/Final%20DHA%202012.pdf) in relation to the photoelectric effect. See also his earlier paper [The role of docosahexaenioc and arachidonic acids as determinants of evolution and hominid brain development](http://www.ars.usda.gov/SP2UserFiles/person/4986/set4/Final%20DHA%202012.pdf) so I suspect 1523nm is the key to the DHA Doorway for JEDI-like electron tunneling to occur. "May the force be with you.."

See also [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dielectric\_spectroscopy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dielectric_spectroscopy)

### PaulG — 2015-12-18T05:59:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-webinar-and-the-direction-of-time.15227/post-184413>

[https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/)

"_The hydrogen bonding network in water is a quantum measuring device because its motions and action provides a record or a memory of the state of entangled pair of electrons in free radicals made in mitochondria._ This is why mitochondria use free radicals to signal. They also release _monochromatic infrared light_ because you can only **entangle electrons** and protons with light at **one specific frequency**. The double slit experiment proving quantum entanglement only works if light is monochromatic. This is why your skin has to begin the filtering process of sunlight a long time before your mitochondria can process the electromagnetic wave of light. You should be reminded that water absorbs electromagnetic radiation best at 270 nm directly in the ultaviolet range. Everything is coupled by quantum design when you understand her plan."

"During sleep is when we are most effective in using electrons as waves, while having a small distance to tunnel. During awake hours when the sun is out exercise is the other way in which we can tunnel electrons while keeping the distance to tunneling lower than 14 Angstroms. This is why exercise is associated with free radical generation, sweating, and heat release from your mitochondria. All of this ties back to the edge where the quantum mechanisms of electrons and protons meets the statistical laws of thermodynamics in mass equivalence.
Most people understand that when water cools it expands when it is ice. What people forget to realize is that when liquid water is heated by infrared light it shrinks naturally. In liquid water in cells, low density water clusters predominate, and hence water volume shrinks when heated by infrared light released from mitochondria. I cannot stress how critical these relationships are to one another. This shrinks cell volumes and mitochondrial volumes just using heat and water’s interaction. This is why ice expands when water cools. It also points out why cold thermogenesis works fundamentally because cold increase heat release from mitochondria naturally. This makes you more energy efficient. This makes water the ideal substance for life, because of Einstein’s mass equivalence equation. Anything that condenses carries more energy thermodynamically.
Ultimately, this is a mass equivalence effect; remember anything that loses energy gets larger in the world we observe. This is why the heart gets bigger in heart failure and why the brain swells in pseudotumor cerebri. Both of these disease are associated with bad mitochondria. It is also why people get fat, and stay fat when they eat nothing but fat or protein. Size and shapes change in key areas of the cytochromes are where these changes occur. Since science can’t see well in these small spaces, they act as if it is not there. But our observations of cold thermogenesis uncoupling and IR heat release during exercise show these effects are present in mitochondria."

"Your mitochondria releases light in **specific frequencies** from the cytochromes because entanglement fundamentally requires a monochromatic light. Mitochondria makes infra red light, normally. So how is this process the same in us and the sun? How does it differ specifically, to lead to different sources of light?

This will be the topic of the next blog."...

[https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-10/](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-10/)

"Do you want to know why this is a big deal? The experiment that defines quantum weirdness, is called the double slit experiment. It shows that subatomic particles can be in two places at once. To make the double slit experiment work, the beam of light must be monochromatic. If you leak excess light with other frequencies of energy, you can’t reproduce quantum tunneling. If you can’t tunnel you can’t entangle electrons in your mitochondria so you lose the best way for a eukaryote to remain healthy by being able to utilize the quantum mechanisms that come with your mitochondria during sleep. **So if a mitochondria tunnels electrons or protons the light associated with the “ quantum weirdness” must have a unique wavelength**. This is why metabolic syndrome is disease tied to light and not to food fundamentally. When you can’t fully utilize sleep you live in the classical world of calories in and calories out and you get ill and you think food and exercise are the ‘be all end all’ of wellness when they are not"

"Everything on this planet is bound and exists because of the fundamentals of sunlight. Understanding its quantum dance helps you understand how the laws of quantum mechanics joined to organize life and create the metastability used to find some semblance of order for molecules to begin to organize around. The cosmic creator was light………and remains light. Light is the key signaling metric in cells and in the human brain. This is why new research is published daily that shows us that certain frequencies of light have specific and sensitive effects in cells. Optogenetics research shows how light shown into our retina can alter our hormone panels. The last few blogs have shown you how the light released in mitochondria changes the hydrogen bonding networks and subatomic particles and waves. Protein polymers can take light signals, they can be polarized and to form new frequencies of light to change signaling. The size and shape of the atomic array of the atoms that make up these protein polymers has the ability to change light into something new…….or different and this change can signal a cell to do something different. When light met DHA for the first time, light could be changed to a DC electric current. Prokaryotes cannot do this. Eukaryotes can. This is fundamentally why Prokaryotes release more light than we do. Eukaryotes use this light to their advantage because of their **cell membrane’s construction"**

### fitness@home — 2015-06-08T21:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonjour-from-france.15229/post-167109>

Hi b612 and welcome.

I haven't had to forgive a parent, but have had to forgive others that were an integral part of my life at one time or another. Honestly, I feel like I have had multiple lives within my current one. Those people I needed to forgive were in various stages of it. Carrying anger, hurt and fear within me was causing my body/mind too much stress.

It's took 20 years to finally move past the anger I felt toward my first husband for not being a father to our children and/or supporting them as he should. He now realizes he gave up meaningful relationships with his kids. Those were his choices. We can have a conversation now and I don't feel that anger or bitterness any longer. I actually feel sad for all he's missed and know he won't be around long due to some severe health issues that were self-inflicted.

In another situation, I sought out help in the form of counseling and also utilized EFT to help deal with the downstream symptoms from anger-fear. Horrible IBS, sleep was a mess, high stress level, anxiety. Knew I had to make the changes myself.

I know this may not help you with your own situation, but at least you know you're not alone.

p.s. I also love a cup of coffee at sunrise

### Lahelada — 2015-06-09T12:25:03+0300

Welcome B612 . This thought just occured to me out of the blue. To love your body your home you need to love the day. A home is just a succession of days you love . Don't make plans for happiness tomorrow. What could you do today ? Something unusual,something you never do ? A good thing would be to sunbathe in the nude. if you have no privacy maybe you can sunbathe in a room by the open window (not behind glass or nets though) It is a lovely free moodboost.
Do have a look around for anything fish, tuna in cans sardines in cans are perfectly ok.
I miss France . I lived there for 3 months. The cheese,oh the cheese,the foie gras you may keep though

### Flora — 2015-06-10T18:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonjour-from-france.15229/post-167426>

Hi there b612
I'm in France too - in the Lot, which is pretty rural and remote from the sea. But there is still a fresh fish stall at the weekly market in the local town - I buy 2 dozen oysters every week and eat them over 4 or 5 days, and there's always a huge selection of seafood on sale. Also all the supermarkets have fresh fish stalls - even if it's a bit of a trek to your nearest, you could stock up and freeze some. - and the range of canned fish is enormous and cheap! I know you want to avoid BPA (as do we all) but I think getting the DHA you need is more important and is going to make that matter less anyway (and I don't think it's an issue with fish canned in France).
Good luck on your journey.

### JanSz — 2015-06-10T19:16:24+0300

I know that people are trying to help here but I'm just sick of dieting and detoxing all the time. Food makes me stressed and I want to overcome that. I eat Weston Price diet and I think I should stick to that. I'm gonna add some fish too but it's the last thing I'm changing. My vitiligo gets worse after nights of insomnia and panic attacks. This is the first problem I need to solve...\[ /quote\]

Cortisol ( blood, evening) 554,20 nmol/l =20.06µg/dL
1 nmol/L = 0.0362 µg/dL

cortisol(AM) (6.2-19.4)ug/dL
cortisol (PM) (2.3-11.9)ug/dL

You have high evening cortisol.

Trying to figure out if you are girl or boy.
Going by the hgb range, I think you are girl. Please confirm.

You had some estrogen problems, details please.

Why you had tested only evening cortisol (which is high), what about morning?

You have to check for mold or lyme (back to that book above and finding right local doctor).
In the mean time spend max day time outside, max amount of skin exposed.
No glasses.
Does not have to be sunny days only, any weather is helpfull.
On sunny days look at sun eyes closed (no glasses).
On cloudy days look at sky, eyes open (no glasses).
Sleep in totally dark room, when you get out of the bed, immediately look at the sun (or clouds on the sky), no glass between you and sun.

May want to try Phosphatidyl-Serine (PS). Tends to move high cortisol toward morning where it belongs.
If it helps, another proof that you need good control over your fatty acids (by the numbers) and possibly intravenous phospholipids and latter phospholipids in pills.

Good idea to do:
24hr urine cortisol
4 or better 8 samples of saliva cortisol.

high evening cortisol

describe your need for love life, need it or do not care, orgasms?
do you use birth control pills (should not)
condition of your hair, strong, weak

cortisol AM/PM, pregnenolone, progesterone, estradiol, estrone, total and free testosterone, SHBG
measure your underarm temperature in mid day (it should be 36.6C)

# Thread: Can ice packs be used on someone weighing 560 lbs, intubated and not clearing CO?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-06-09T02:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/post-167139>

Hi doc - my nephew is on his last leg - he went to the hospital because he couldn't breathe when he had a cold - they immediately intubated him - he's on propinol - has a 50/50 chance of making it - has - maybe - picked up MRSA from the tube and -maybe- has pneumonia - all I can think is \*\*\*hypoxia\*\*\* - so naturally I thought of you, the Ice Man, John Muir who went to the mountains, layed down on the ground and nearly froze to death, but he lived another 20 years when they told him his infection was "incurable" - naturally, I don't want to interfere with a doctor, but I'm not sure they're getting anywhere - they have him on diaretics and he can only clear 20% of whatever it is the lungs clear - so a machine is breathing for him - how would one approach a doctor about trying the cold and would it even be a good idea? Do doctors use it at all in hospitals?

I read in your book it destroys IL-6, raises alpha-MSH - that would be good for a bacterial infection me thinks-

If you don't answer, I totally understand why - I just thought I'd give it a shot - he's 40 something years old - take care and thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-09T05:10:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/post-167155>

are they a eukaryote? Are they a mammal? If yes.......of course. Why would you even question this?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-09T05:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/post-167156>

Sounds like he needs HBO too but he may not fit in.

### Penny — 2015-06-09T07:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/post-167175>

Thanks Doc

I would not question it, but when I stick my nose where I am not an expert, I always seek confirmation from one who is

I'm assuming it's called therapeutic hypothermia? Like, would they know how to do it - would they pack him in ice or do the cold saline thing I read about... ? I'll tell my sister-in-law

### Penny — 2015-06-09T17:00:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-ice-packs-be-used-on-someone-weighing-560-lbs-intubated-and-not-clearing-co.15230/post-167230>

### Penny — 2015-06-12T02:13:23+0300

Does anyone use either of these products outside of coffee....I don't drink coffee so I'm just wondering how else they might be incorporated into other meals....just drizzle on top I guess?

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=167243)
>
> anyone using MCT oil or brain octane from the bulletproof range? I'm just wondering if they're worthwhile using or is it better to stick the coconut oil in its whole state? Does anyone use either of these products outside of coffee....I don't drink coffee so I'm just wondering how else they might be incorporated into other meals....just drizzle on top I guess?

I use it (Brain Octane) and have for the past few months. However, I'm looking to use up what I've got and switch to just coconut oil because it is the "biological package". I've gotten more benefit from oysters, water and sunshine, and I'd rather put my money toward more of the first two.

As for using it in other things, I have used it in salad dressings and mixed with tuna and avocado. It's flavorless so it is easy to mix into many things if you decide to get it.

### sfast — 2016-12-04T07:58:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-oil-brain-octane.15268/post-204749>

I don't consider any disintegrated food as healthy. There are to many second order effects that are opaque to our understanding. This is one lesson I learned from Dr. Kruse. I consider the main lesson as the following: Connect with nature. Light, cold grounding, sea food. Everything serves the connectivity. MCT is fuel for people who can't generate energy efficiently in my opinion. It is performance with costs. Example: It is energy which doesn't come with the secondary factors food is packed with (vitamins, minerals, xenohormetic properties). It is the same thing as eating protein powder and supplementing with fish oil, B12, Selenium etc. It doesn't work.

Therefore, I only would eat coconuts and sparingly use coconut oil and never use MCT.

I like avocado and salt too but if you need that extra flavour try coconut aminos. You might need to dilute it a bit though as it's thicker than soy sauce.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-10T05:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167329>

Valerie the key to the blog is understanding that the NAD/NADH ratio = your redox potential. The ratio is NOT determined by dietary intake, your supplements or any fake IV bullshit. It is enzymatically controlled. All enzymes work via proton tunneling. That enzyme is called “**_NAD(P)H dehydrogenase, quinone 1._** It is activated by light. **_Cancer up regulates that gene and aging reduces it._**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-10T06:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167336>

Photodynamic therapy (PDT) is a clinically approved and rapidly developing cancer treatment. PDT involves the administration of photosensitizer (PS) followed by local illumination with visible light of specific wavelength. In the presence of oxygen molecules, the light illumination of PS can lead to a series of photochemical reactions and consequently the generation of cytotoxic reactive oxygen species (ROS). PS, like bok choy can increase NAD+ by increasing **_NAD(P)H dehydrogenase (quinone 1)_**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-10T06:22:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167340>

DCA can be sued to increase NAD+. Human use is interesting in the heart........Dichloroactetic acid, a simple, cheap chemical that is well absorbed orally, induces a “metabolic switching” of cardiomyocytes from the utilization of fatty acids to the utilization of glucose. _This glucose utilization is NOT aerobic glycolysis (i.e. the_ _Warburg_ _effect), but instead is true “glucose oxidation” in the mitochondria._ This increases the amount of ATP that can be generated and reduces the workload of the heart to generate ATP. The dose is relatively high (50 mg/kg body weight in humans). This means for an average person, they would need to take 3-4 grams of DCA.

Key point in UBI 17: When the respiratory proteins do a poor job of this, low NAD+ and SIRT 1 ratio’s are the result in cells. How does ketosis fit into this scenario? If you read my book, The Epi-Paleo Rx, you will see that ketosis is a small critical part of reversing most diseases. If you don’t understand this blog you will think I have just contradicted myself. I have not. Ketosis only augments the flow. It cannot recapitulate the quantum speed of TUNNELING electrons !!!!! ### NeilBB — 2015-06-10T18:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167417>

I have often thought that obesity must be somewhat protective to some degree in many cases. I've long noticed that it's when people reach what seems to be their maximum obtainable girth that the bigger problems like DM and heart disease, etc, tend to kick in. The people that don't have the "ability" to get fat obtain the same diseases at lower BMI's.

In this way bariatric surgery is exactly analogous to cataract surgery in that it fixes a "problem" which was actually a compensation without addressing the underlying cause, thereby worsening overall health in both cases.

### NeilBB — 2015-06-10T23:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167468>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=167421)
>
> absolute spot on.........but obesity has another hidden benefit..........this is where we store our stem cells while our mitonuclear connection waits for a better environment so we can improve the precision fit.

Makes sense. So "adiposity" buys time in multiple ways. Lowers blood sugar by converting excess glucose to fat mass-delaying onset of metabolic syndrome and DM. Stores concentrated energy that is unable to be dissipated at present due to environmental/metabolic conditions- for lipolysis and electron liberation at some point in future. Stores stem cells to be epigenetically programmed later when environmental conditions become more favorable for life. So getting fat can be like a pause switch or holding pattern, a way for life to "slow time down" in a bad environment until things improve. The only problem for most today is that things don't ever improve. So the potential benefits can never be realized.

### NeilBB — 2015-06-11T00:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167469>

> [angie-s said:](/goto/post?id=167465)
>
> Why not just supplement with NAD+ to stop ubiquination. I have seen NAD+ tablets at the local Vitamin Shoppe.

I believe what they sell is either the reduced form, actually NADH, or a newer very expensive precursor to the oxidized form NAD+, called nicotinomide riboside, the one Sinclair is selling. You have to be very careful about which form you are using. Increasing NADH will actually make things worse by damaging the ratio of NAD+/NADH, if you understand the redox signaling.

You see, this is why if you're going to mess around with supplements you better make sure you know exactly what you are doing. Few do.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-11T03:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167486>

@[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/)

Gets back to Physics

From Chapter 5 of Mae-Wan Ho's 'Rainbow and the Worm'

It is of interest to compare the thermodynamic concept of 'free energy' with the concept of 'stored energy'

'Stored energy', originally defined by McClare with respect to a characteristic time interval, can readily be extended, in addition, to a characteristic spatial domain. As such, **stored energy is explicitly dependent on the space-time structure of the system, hence it is a precise concept which can be defined on the space and time domain of the processes involved.**

Perhaps what we really need, in order to understand the living system, is a thermodynamics of organized complexity which describes processes in terms of stored energy instead of free energy

The living system operates simultaneously at isothermal equilibrium and far from equilibrium regimes, as well as in the molecular quantum domain.

The crucial difference between living and non-living systems lies in how energies are stored, channeled, and directed.

The secret of the organism is that it can mobilise the whole spectrum of energies for work, from the translational to the electronic, making scant distinction between 'bound' and 'free' energy.

'Negative entropy' in the sense of the organism .... has not so much to do with free energy, but with the way energy is trapped, stored, and mobilised in the living system. Energy is trapped directly at the electronic level. It is stored not only as vibrational and electronic bond energies, but also in the structure of the system: in gradients, fields and flow patterns, compartments, organelles, cells and tissues.​

We've heard the phrase: Living organisms are designed to live far from equilibrium, as a dissipative system.

Being "Dissipative" is important, because the only way you can create order in an organism is to give disorder back to the environment. This usually takes the form of IR light (something that even a biochemist like Nick Lane can appreciate -- respiration is fundamentally Exergonic).

IMO, "obesity" is a result of either:

**(a)** inability to dissipate energy from coupled cycles => while these are designed to be near zero entropy systems once established ([http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/)), they are never 100% efficient in taking on inputs or generating output (eg: Complex I electron leakiness) -- the balance needs to be dissipated.

**(b)** breaking feedback loops in the far-from-equilibrium cycles (no longer close to zero entropy) => I speculate that obesity results when net flux in coupled cycles is too fast, leading to accumulation of un-dissipated, free energy (as entropy). I'm not 100% sure of this point

Yeah, we can explain obesity in terms of systemic insulin resistance (preventing glucose overload of a cell), increase in inflammatory (positively charged) compounds like NF-kb. I like the Physics perspective because it scales from the mitochondria to the entire human body.

.....

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-11T03:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167488>

For those who feel like they're not getting anywhere despite seemingly doing everything right, there's a quote from the blog post:

Quantum mechanics can predict the existence of energy levels inside atomic lattices (the SCN is such a lattice), whereby, **any electron in that energy level can be effectively spread across the whole structure.** **_That is called coherent control of signaling._**​

This sucks for those trying to biohack their environment, because it is these precise and very weak lattice interactions that allow components like mitochondria to function.

A single photon can activate the SCN (just search for "single photon detection human eye" on the internet and look at how many studies pop up). The body is very very very sensitive to weak signals.

A measurement of 0.01mW/cm^2 on an EMF meter is a massive signal to your mitochondria. This signal gets propagated through the atomic optical lattices of the SCN, and gets interpreted. What happens next is unknown ......  , and will depend on how well the rest of the machinery in the body can handle the signal.

....

### NeilBB — 2015-06-11T23:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167694>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=167486)
>
> @[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/)
>
> Gets back to Physics
>
> From Chapter 5 of Mae-Wan Ho's 'Rainbow and the Worm'
>
> It is of interest to compare the thermodynamic concept of 'free energy' with the concept of 'stored energy'
>
> 'Stored energy', originally defined by McClare with respect to a characteristic time interval, can readily be extended, in addition, to a characteristic spatial domain. As such, stored energy is explicitly dependent on the space-time structure of the system, hence it is a precise concept which can be defined on the space and time domain of the processes involved.
>
> Perhaps what we really need, in order to understand the living system, is a thermodynamics of organized complexity which describes processes in terms of stored energy instead of free energy
>
> The living system operates simultaneously at isothermal equilibrium and far from equilibrium regimes, as well as in the molecular quantum domain.
>
> The crucial difference between living and non-living systems lies in how energies are stored, channeled, and directed.
>
> The secret of the organism is that it can mobilise the whole spectrum of energies for work, from the translational to the electronic, making scant distinction between 'bound' and 'free' energy.
>
> 'Negative entropy' in the sense of the organism .... has not so much to do with free energy, but with the way energy is trapped, stored, and mobilised in the living system. Energy is trapped directly at the electronic level. It is stored not only as vibrational and electronic bond energies, but also in the structure of the system: in gradients, fields and flow patterns, compartments, organelles, cells and tissues.
>
> We've heard the phrase: Living organisms are designed to live far from equilibrium, as a dissipative system.
>
> Being "Dissipative" is important, because the only way you can create order in an organism is to give disorder back to the environment. This usually takes the form of IR light (something that even a biochemist like Nick Lane can appreciate -- respiration is fundamentally Exergonic).
>
> IMO, "obesity" is a result of either:
>
> (a) inability to dissipate energy from coupled cycles => while these are designed to be near zero entropy systems once established ([http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/)), they are never 100% efficient in taking on inputs or generating output (eg: Complex I electron leakiness) -- the balance needs to be dissipated.
>
> (b) breaking feedback loops in the far-from-equilibrium cycles (no longer close to zero entropy) => I speculate that obesity results when net flux in coupled cycles is too fast, leading to accumulation of un-dissipated, free energy (as entropy). I'm not 100% sure of this point
>
> Yeah, we can explain obesity in terms of systemic insulin resistance (preventing glucose overload of a cell), increase in inflammatory (positively charged) compounds like NF-kb. I like the Physics perspective because it scales from the mitochondria to the entire human body.
>
> .....

I would say that obesity is one compensatory strategy mammals in particular can utilize to manage the physical state in which energy cannot be adequately dissipated. Essentially, a capacitor, or secondary battery of sorts.

But the point is that the inability to dissipate energy and return entropy to the environment is primary. The adioposity is reactive and largely adaptive. Human obesity is surely an extension of mammalian winter hibernation across a longer timeframe. Focus on weight loss, or even fat los

As such, stored energy is explicitly dependent on the space-time structure of the system, hence it is a precise concept which can be defined on the space and time domain of the processes involved.

Energy is trapped directly at the electronic level. It is stored not only as vibrational and electronic bond energies, but also in the structure of the system: in gradients, fields and flow patterns, compartments, organelles, cells and tissues.
>

>
> IMO, "obesity" is a result of either:
>
> (a) inability to dissipate energy from coupled cycles => while these are designed to be near zero entropy systems once established ([http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-1-the-zero-entropy-system/)), they are never 100% efficient in taking on inputs or generating output (eg: Complex I electron leakiness) -- the balance needs to be dissipated.
>
> (b) breaking feedback loops in the far-from-equilibrium cycles (no longer close to zero entropy) => I speculate that obesity results when net flux in coupled cycles is too fast, leading to accumulation of un-dissipated, free energy (as entropy). I'm not 100% sure of this point
>
> Yeah, we can explain obesity in terms of systemic insulin resistance (preventing glucose overload of a cell), increase in inflammatory (positively charged) compounds like NF-kb. I like the Physics perspective because it scales from the mitochondria to the entire human body.

I would say that obesity is one compensatory strategy to manage the physical state in which energy cannot be adequately dissipated. Essentially, a capacitor, or secondary battery of sorts.

But the point is that the inability to dissipate energy and return entropy to the environment is primary. The adioposity is reactive and largely adaptive. Human obesity is surely an extension of mammalian winter hibernation across longer time. Focus on weight loss, or even fat loss as a primary goal is clearly misguided. Fat loss will follow from energy efficiency of the organism. And it will be constantly resisted without it. Period.

The metabolic pathways, insulin, thyroxin, leptin, etc, are just details, the biochemical machinery of this physical process of energy flow that living systems have to manage. And they are there for a reason. The reason is found in physics.

It's also why diets and weight loss strategies fail. The body's compensatory metabolic response to an energetically unfavorable physical environment is what actually drives the fat storage. If this compensatory mechanism is "subverted" by any means (other than actual recoupling of energy dissipation), the result of "weight loss" will be hunger, lean tissue loss, and other diseases, as we see and nobody in medicine can really explain today. Ketosis is the only real shortcut because it does address the mitochondrial energy efficiency issue, even if in an incomplete way. But, as Jack has explained, it also has its limits.

I have seen all of this in patients and experienced it in my own body. At one time I weighed almost 100 pounds more than I do now. And I did for years. But now, I never gain any weight. Zero "effort" is required for me to maintain approximate 180 pounds, no matter how much I eat or how little I exercise. That is the truth. It is this concept that I needed to understand fully that led me to Jack's stuff in the first place.
