> But the point is that the inability to dissipate energy and return entropy to the environment is primary. The adioposity is reactive and largely adaptive. Human obesity is surely an extension of mammalian winter hibernation across a longer timeframe. Focus on weight loss, or even fat los
>
> I would say that obesity is one compensatory strategy to manage the physical state in which energy cannot be adequately dissipated. Essentially, a capacitor, or secondary battery of sorts.
>
> But the point is that the inability to dissipate energy and return entropy to the environment is primary. The adioposity is reactive and largely adaptive. Human obesity is surely an extension of mammalian winter hibernation across longer time. Focus on weight loss, or even fat loss as a primary goal is clearly misguided. Fat loss will follow from energy efficiency of the organism. And it will be constantly resisted without it. Period.
>
> The metabolic pathways, insulin, thyroxin, leptin, etc, are just details, the biochemical machinery of this physical process of energy flow that living systems have to manage. And they are there for a reason. The reason is found in physics.
>
> It's also why diets and weight loss strategies fail. The body's compensatory metabolic response to an energetically unfavorable physical environment is what actually drives the fat storage. If this compensatory mechanism is "subverted" by any means (other than actual recoupling of energy dissipation), the result of "weight loss" will be hunger, lean tissue loss, and other diseases, as we see and nobody in medicine can really explain today. Ketosis is the only real shortcut because it does address the mitochondrial energy efficiency issue, even if in an incomplete way. But, as Jack has explained, it also has its limits.
>
> I have seen all of this in patients and experienced it in my own body. At one time I weighed almost 100 pounds more than I do now. And I did for years. But now, I never gain any weight. Zero "effort" is required for me to maintain approximate 180 pounds, no matter how much I eat or how little I exercise. That is the truth. It is this concept that I needed to understand fully that led me to Jack's stuff in the first place.

Awesome summary, Neil. I have often thought of my quick response to Jack's question about my goals during our first consult. 'Lose weight' has sounded so ... shortsighted, misguided, and simplistic for quite a while now.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-12T02:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-167709>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=167659)
>
> I'm also seeing what my body's done to heal itself and why my body said Fuck you Gretchen and loosing weight, we've got more important stuff to fix, such as fixing my retina, so it could then fix my CLOCK. 3/4 of the way through @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and these are the BOMBS that keep dropping:
>
> Leakiness at cytochrome 1, increases as SIRT1 & NAD+ uncouple; which results in disease. BOOM!
>
> free radicals = ROS/RNS = bipohoton release ELF UV (270nm) to generate cellular/mitochondria repair of proteins. What does 270nm do to water ? increase EZ. When coupled with DHA you fix things that conventional medicine says can't be fixed (IE retinal tear) BOOM!

To be clear, what Pollack et. al. found was that EZ water absorbs strongly at 270nm. This is mostly used as a test to distinguish EZ water from bulk water. It doesn't necessarily mean that the size of the EZ grows larger upon exposure to 270nm light. Nor does it mean that bulk water will charge-separate when exposed to 270nm light.

Sidenote: the most effective method of growing EZ is exposure to far infrared light at 3,100nm. Mae-Wan Ho speculates that this is due to the OH stretch resonance which is excited by 3100nm light, and allows dissociation of this bond from H2O, into the hexagonal EZ lattice (when a nucleating substance is available) -- [http://www.i-sis.org.uk/WaterElectric.php](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/WaterElectric.php)​

What does 270nm do to **EZ water**? Probably not much. What EZ water probably does is allow for efficient transmission of this light to various proteins.

270nm light is relatively rare in nature. Light came first, and then biology organised itself around the constraints of light. Thus, the 270nm wavelength was used as a signalling frequency to signal for mitosis (cell division).

Implication: presence of 270nm light is not always a good thing. It is likely designed to act as a trigger to force cell division (when the amount of 270nm light hits a certain threshold). If the cell has enough resources to divide, you get 2 healthy cells to do good things in the body (eg: re-myelination). If the cell is already energy starved, you can get 2 sick cells.

Sidenote: while free radicals do encode UV biophotons, they can also capture many other wavelengths of light. Their signalling effects extend beyond the UV range.​

\-----

The wavelength of light that Fritz Popp found to repair DNA is 380nm light, but only in very low intensity.

High intensity 380nm light causes protein over-expression and eventually apoptosis.

Scrambling 380nm input to a cell causes cancer (all carcinogenic compounds do this). This is likely due to lack of negative feedback (a protein gets made, but there is no signal back to the nucleus that it has been made ... so a stronger growth stimulus continues) ... more on this below

If we think in terms of deterministic switches, then 380nm could just be another switch that biology organised around. Exposure to 380nm light is a trigger to "express proteins" (aka: grow).

This growth is likely regulated by tight feedback loops. Making proteins is an energy-consuming process, and each peptide bond has quite a large (Gibbs) free energy barrier to climb before formation (peptide bond formation is endergonic). Formation of peptide bonds therefore requires energy transfer from the surrounding cytoplasmic matrix to the newly formed peptide bond. It is then this "decreased cytoplasmic energy" signal that tells the nucleus to stop expressing proteins.

If the description in the previous paragraph is accurate, it would also make sense why carcinogens act like they do. A carcinogen absorbs 380nm light, and then re-emits it at various wavelengths. Re-emission of this light into the cytoplasmic matrix erases the "decreased cytoplasmic energy" which is meant to stop protein expression. So protein expression continues.

Sidenote: another more fundamental way of describing this is that light acts to un-condense any form of matter by making it more massive. Peptide formation should act to re-condense matter, but carcinogens shut this signal off by emitting random frequencies of light.​

....

> Makes sense. So "adiposity" buys time in multiple ways. Lowers blood sugar by converting excess glucose to fat mass-delaying onset of metabolic syndrome and DM. Stores concentrated energy that is unable to be dissipated at present due to environmental/metabolic conditions- for lipolysis and electron liberation at some point in future. Stores stem cells to be epigenetically programmed later when environmental conditions become more favorable for life. So getting fat can be like a pause switch or holding pattern, a way for life to "slow time down" in a bad environment until things improve. The only problem for most today is that things don't ever improve. So the potential benefits can never be realized.

and medicine keeps saying "obesity is a risk factor for X disease" lol - i always though that was so stupid. it is a sign of a deeper process, not a problem in and of itself.

### NeilBB — 2015-06-12T05:06:25+0300

> and medicine keeps saying "obesity is a risk factor for X disease" lol - i always though that was so stupid. it is a sign of a deeper process, not a problem in and of itself.

A last word on this topic regarding the stem cells in adipose tissue...

It seems to me that if weight is lost too fast or inappropriately (starvation, bariatric surgery) then the pluripotent stem cells contained therein would probably be more likely to be lost rather than utilized or conserved. They may even become inflammed or unstable (oncogenic potential?)

But if fat is lost "rationally," as a response to the organism increasing energy efficiency throughout the whole system, not only would stem cells be more likely to be effectively utilized in the process, but there may even be some natural dedifferentiation of adipocytes and other hypertrophied tissue back into more multipotent cell lines, effectively restoring a degree of youthfulness. (If plastic surgeons can use fat tissue to make you younger, I'm betting it can be done naturally too.)

### Penny — 2015-06-14T04:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-168026>

**Berberine-induced AMPK activation inhibits the metastatic potential of melanoma cells via reduction of ERK activity and COX-2 protein expression.**
[Kim HS](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Kim%20HS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676)1, [Kim MJ](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Kim%20MJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676), [Kim EJ](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Kim%20EJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676), [Yang Y](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Yang%20Y%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676), [Lee MS](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Lee%20MS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676), [Lim JS](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Lim%20JS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22120676).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22120676#)**

**Abstract**
Berberine is clinically important natural isoquinoline alkaloid that affects various biological functions, such as cell proliferation, migration and survival. The activation of AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK) regulates tumor cell migration. However, the specific role of AMPK on the metastatic potential of cancer cells remains largely unknown. The present study investigated whether berberine induces AMPK activation and whether this induction directly affects mouse melanoma cell migration, adhesion and invasion. Berberine strongly increased AMPK phosphorylation via reactive oxygen species (ROS) production. 5-Aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide-1-β-D-ribofuranoside (AICAR), a well-known AMPK activator, also inhibited tumor cell adhesion and invasion and reduced the expression of epithelial to mesenchymal transition (EMT)-related genes. Knockdown of AMPKα subunits using siRNAs significantly abated the berberine-induced inhibition of tumor cell invasion. Furthermore, berberine inhibited the metastatic potential of melanoma cells through a decrease in ERK activity and protein levels of cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) by a berberine-induced AMPK activation. These data were confirmed using specific MEK inhibitor, PD98059, and a COX-2 inhibitor, celecoxib. Berberine- and AICAR-treated groups demonstrated significantly decreased lung metastases in the pulmonary metastasis model in vivo. Treatment with berberine also decreased the metastatic potential of A375 human melanoma cells. Collectively, our results suggest that berberine-induced AMPK activation inhibits the metastatic potential of tumor cells through a reduction in the activity of the ERK signaling pathway and COX-2 protein levels.

### Penny — 2015-06-14T04:25:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-168027>

In fact, Fermented Papaya is all the rage on the Parkinson's boards... it downregulates ERK, AKT, P38...

[http://www.researchgate.net/profile...and\_cancer/links/0deec53778eb278fe6000000.pdf](http://www.researchgate.net/profile/Jhoti_Somanah2/publication/261839347_Diabetes_as_a_risk_factor_to_cancer_Functional_role_of_fermented_papaya_preparation_as_phytonutraceutical_adjunct_in_the_treatment_of_diabetes_and_cancer/links/0deec53778eb278fe6000000.pdf)

The use of complementary medicines, such as plant extracts, in therapy varies according to the different cultural traditions. However, the antioxidant and neuroprotective actions of some phytochemicals and plant extracts, such as gingko biloba, have been demonstrated in the treatment and prevention of dementia pathologies, particularly Alzheimer’s disease \[[116](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/anemia/2012/563251/#B116)\]. Also fermented papaya preparation (FPP) (a product of yeast fermentation of Carica papaya Linn) has been tested in chronic and degenerative disease conditions (such as thalassemia, cirrhosis, diabetes, and aging) and performance sports showing the ability to favorably modulate immunological, hematological, inflammatory, vascular, and oxidative stress damage parameters. Neuroprotective potential evaluated in an Alzheimer’s disease cell model showed that the toxicity of the β-amyloid could be significantly modulated by FPP. FPP modulated the oxidative stress-induced apoptosis through the downregulation of the ERK, Akt, and p38 activation mediated by H2O2. FPP reduces the extent of the H2O2-induced DNA damage, an outcome corroborated by similar effects obtained in the benzo\[a\]pyrene treated cells \[[117](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/anemia/2012/563251/#B117)\].

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/anemia/2012/563251/](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/anemia/2012/563251/)

Most solid tumors are also characterized by a lipogenic phenotype. Cancer cells often require that fatty acids be generated de novo, regardless of their exogenous levels. Most people do not even KNOW this basic stuff.

This requirement in cancer cells is so critical because its needed in tumors to maintain proliferation and viability. As it happens massive ELF-UV is released and mitosis is stimulated in the neighboring cells. As a result, the metabolism of fatty acids, in particular their biosynthesis becomes a critical step. ### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-28T04:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-17-is-live-and-speaks-volumes.15269/post-193731>

While most glucose consumed in glycolysis is ultimately metabolized into lactate, some of the pyruvate generated during this reaction will be further metabolized into the fatty acid synthesis pathway. In order to do this, pyruvate is transported into the mitochondria and converted to acetyl-CoA through the activity of enzymes, including pyruvate deydrogenase . Acetyl-CoA can also be produced in the mitochondria from ketone bodies, such as 3-hydroxybutyric acid. Since fatty acid synthesis takes place in the cytosol and the mitochondrial membrane is impermeable to acetyl-CoA, it must first be converted into citrate. This is accomplished when the two carbon acetyl group from acetyl-CoA is condensed onto oxaloacetate by citrate synthase in the TCA pathway. It should be noted that transamination of aspartate can also serve as a source for oxaloacetate and citrate production. In non-proliferating cells, the TCA cycle generally serves to synthesize ATP from oxidizable substrates, such as amino acids. However, in rapidly dividing cells, like tumors, many of the intermediates, particularly citrate, that are generated during the cycle are effluxed from the pathway in a process termed cataplerosis. This stuff is well known but no one is connecting the dots on why some of the shit being spewed is completely wrong.

Once citrate is shuttled into the cytosol, it is cleaved by ATP-citrate lyase into acetyl-CoA and oxaloacetate, comprising the first step of fatty acid synthesis in our cells. It's critical that the carbons utilized for the production of acetyl-CoA are derived from glucose. This is why Warburg cells use glucose. It is also why Nora Volkow found nnEMF from cell phones were associated with unregulated glucose metabolism and calcium efflux with calcium flowing into mitochondria like mad. Once within the cytosol of the cell, the acetyl-CoA is converted to malonyl-CoA via carboxylation by acetyl-CoA carboxylase. This is the rate-limiting enzyme in the fatty acid synthesis pathway in humans. The coda: fatty acid synthase catalyzes the ultimate steps of fatty acid synthesis in these solid tumor cells, yielding the 16-carbon fatty acid palmitate.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2015-06-10T04:49:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/osteoporosis.15273/post-167322>

Hello everyone!
My mom was just diagnosed with osteoporosis and the MD wants to give her a shot of prolia. She has stage 3 kidney disease. I am not a fan of this so was wondering if anyone could give input as to what would be a good way to increase bone density besides weight lifting, vit d, vit k2, and progesterone therapy. She is already doing the first 3. She just increased k2m7 to 200 mcg. She is in her mid 70s and a long term breast cancer survivor. Her cancer had started after she was placed on hormone replacement estrogen after a hysterectomy back in the late 80s. Is it safe for her to start progesterone therapy and not do estrogen? I didnt know how her past medical history with an estrogen fed cancer affected the treatment now of low bone density. She also had adrenal fatigue and takes 50 mcg of t3.
Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-10T05:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/osteoporosis.15273/post-167326>

DHA, water and sunlight is the fastest way..........with avoidance of blue light, excessive carbs. Read the 4 part osteoporosis on the site. Lots of data.

AF= brainstem disease = a bad environment. Brain Gut 16 is that blog.

Do not make singular guesses. There is small chance to do multitude of them right.
For you, nearest place to draw blood for Micronutrient test at Spectracell is in Geneva Switzerland.
Do this test 2x/year until stabilized, then once/year or less.
[http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/](http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/)
Micronutrient Test

On a slide #12 there is a list of measured items:
[http://www.spectracell.com/media/up...itamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/s/0e2059361_slides-functional-vitamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf)

Based on test results compile you list of supplements, revise that list after each test.
\------------------
Also optimize your steroid hormone panel.
Start with
DHEAs
Pregnenolone
Progesterone
Cortisol
\==================
First goals:
DHEAs, serum (Women 275-400 μg/dL)
progesterone,serum~1.5 ng/mL (day 2-4 of menstrual cycle)
cortisol,serum (7:30AM)~20ug/dL

\--------------
do not eat birth control pills
do not eat fish/kril oils

### JanSz — 2015-06-20T20:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-169063>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169058)
>
> Yesterday I had an adventure... I ate some ice cream! I used to react so badly to it before (bad heartburn for the of the day). Nothing happened yesterday. I believe my stomach is getting stronger and I have more acid to digest all the sh\*t and to absorb the nutrients.

Heartburn often is caused by low stomach acid.

Are you getting heartburn eating lard or butter by tablespoons?

Try some Betaine HCL.
[http://www.amazon.com/Betaine-Pepsin-Country-Life-Tabs/dp/B00068IY0O/ref=sr\_1\_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1434822623&sr=8-5&keywords=betaine hcl with pepsin](http://www.amazon.com/Betaine-Pepsin-Country-Life-Tabs/dp/B00068IY0O/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1434822623&sr=8-5&keywords=betaine+hcl+with+pepsin)

Use them with meals.

Start with one tab.
Increase every meal by one tab.
When belly feels warm, it was too much. (Get something greasy to use up extra acid, next time use less).
8-10 tabs per meal is likely top dose.

//

Now I'm having a rough time getting off of it because of the same reason as caffeine -It makes me feel good and for longer too.
Since following Jack's protocol I have been able to reduce it to 75mg. (The 'max' is 70mg).

### chilly willy — 2015-06-22T17:28:04+0300

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169243)
>
> Slept only for two hours. Now it's almost 12 pm and my heart is still bouncing. I need to sleep but every time I fall asleep my lungs stop working and I don't inhale. One minute later I jump out of the bed gasping for air.

This sounds like sleep apnea. Do you ever snore? You should have a sleep study to determine the cause, and then it can be addressed. Read both of these links.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep\_apnea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep_apnea)
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep\_apnea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep_apnea)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-22T21:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-169272>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169243)
>
> Yesterday:
> Supper : Veggie soup, oysters and mackerel filet cooked in olive oil.
> I was feeling pretty well until I went out with my friends. There was a big music event happening in the city. Lots of loud music, drunk people and cigarette smoke everywhere.
> I was trying to do my best and hang out with my friends but I couldn't...
> Came back home completely knackered. Went straight to bed, put an ice pack around my neck and tried to sleep... had another panic attack, of course. How could I do without it, huh?
> Slept only for two hours. Now it's almost 12 pm and my heart is still bouncing. I need to sleep but every time I fall asleep my lungs stop working and I don't inhale. One minute later I jump out of the bed gasping for air.
>
> I hate this city, I hate my parents, I hate my boyfriend, I hate my body, I hate everything around. I feel like an animal trapped in a cage. I WANNA GET OUT.

I know exactly how you feel (except for the breathing problem).
It's really tough when you have a condition that nobody can see or even understand. Even if they do, they forget. There is no visual clue to them that reminds them.
If we were in wheelchairs it would be completely different. That's probably why you 'hate' everyone. they are probably treating you like you're well.
I understand the music festival thing. I am a guitar player who normally loves to play high voltage rock n roll and the guys in the band are very frustrated and don't get it.

Sometimes I just feel like packing up my truck and just leaving this life behind me. When you're brain doesn't function well life becomes complicated and very stressful. Even my 'therapy' becomes a source of stress. I forget to write my diet down for my naturopath partially because Dr. Kruse says it is not that important, I forget my supplements, I leave work early because I get migraines which are caused almost entirely from my stimulant medication.But without the stimulant I feel 'lifeless'. My wife is frustrated with me because I'm doing 'weird' stuff like wearing blue blockers at night, eating raw oysters, getting rid of the cordless phones etc. I try to explain to her why and she understands, but only for a minute or two then she forgets as well.
and yeah, I feel trapped too..trapped financially and emotionally. I am just all around unable to perform well and it frustrates everyone around me who depend on me some way or another.
I took a week off of work to really try and focus on getting myself off of this stimulant. It's tough because when I'm on it I'm a very positive person even though it causes so many problems and when I'm off of it I'm so negative but I have less problems
Its really backward and hard to understand.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-23T13:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-169341>

Oh ,but you could make it fun for him . Do you know that the doc said that he is needed twice a day to do his "thang'" ? That part of the prescription makes them sit up and be interested usually. Oh and before you say anything the doc also says it is not a matter of whether you want to . You gotta...
As far as moments go,life is a chain of moments. Often we fail to recognise the moment and look for others to make a moment for us. Moments are when you are not looking. Have you ever laughed with a stranger about an absurd conversation you started ? On a chilly morning at the bus stop say glad I did not book that Mallorca trip . I would have missed this heatwave with a wink in your eye. Now the other has a choice . They can ignore you ( boohoo but not your fault) or you might get the answer oh yes I waslooking at the tank top in the closet and almost threw it on .Instead I am wearing this boring two piece skirt suit. Now you have something else to work with. Standup cpomedians call it working the room and it takes practice but when you pull it off everyone gets to start whistling on their way to work.

### chilly willy — 2015-06-23T17:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-169391>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169261)
>
> Hey. No, I never snore. I hope it's related to anxiety... It doesn't happen every night, only when I'm really out of myself.

Snoring is not sleep apnea and sleep apnea is not snoring. You would have known that if you had read the first paragraph of the first link.

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169261)
>
> every time I fall asleep my lungs stop working and I don't inhale.

**"Sleep apnea** (or **sleep apnoea** in British English; [/æpˈniːə/](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA_for_English)) is a [sleep disorder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep_disorder) characterized by pauses in [breathing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Breath) or instances of shallow or infrequent breathing during [sleep](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep). Each pause in breathing, called an [apnea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apnea), can last for several seconds to several minutes...."

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169261)
>
> One minute later I jump out of the bed gasping for air.

"When breathing is paused, carbon dioxide builds up in the bloodstream. Chemoreceptors in the blood stream note the high carbon dioxide levels. The brain is signaled to wake the person sleeping and breathe in air. Breathing normally will restore oxygen levels and the person will fall asleep again...."

Why do you hope it's related to anxiety? And what does "really out of myself" mean?

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-25T23:28:59+0300

Well... frogs and cats were to advanced for me... as a kid I had an obsession with rain worms. If you cut 1 in half, you've got 2 crawling about. And then I would eat them. Oh remembering that... I wonder if that had some special benefit nutrition wise? Anyway... haven't we all done something similar. Just to explore the borders of evil and righteousness. You're just fine.

What is holding you back with the screaming? You can scream really loud everyday. Forest is not required. Screw the neighbours. Screaming is good breathing exercise and helps to let go of those deep f\*#^k upped things.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-27T07:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-169711>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169659)
>
> You made me remember a two year old girl that I saw in a park once. She took a rainworm, put it in her mouth and started to chew it. Her dad freaked out, he was in panic
> Anyways, it's a good source of protein I think.
> Once I saw my sister eating a big spider and I didn't stop her.

Ha!! That's funny!
Change of subject...and an idea you can try if you want..
First I'll tell you what happened...
I went camping (by myself..I'm too tired to have someone to talk to) for two nights but I came home after one. I had a headache that lasted the ENTIRE DAY even though I spent half of it in the creek, grounded and CT'ing. And that creek is COLD,( 5 degrees celcius..I brought an aquarium thermometer).
Anyway....In the late afternoon I packed up and started driving home, still with a headache. My mood was awful so I just could not enjoy myself at all I couldn't believe how depressed I felt!
If I feel really bad I can usually find a dietary or circadian reason for it. There were many reasons this time but I still should not have felt as bad as I did.
It's then I realized that I hadn't had any coconut oil in a while. (I tend to avoid it when it's hot because it melts and is just messy.) Anyway, I pulled my truck over, hopped into my camper and had two huge tablespoons of coconut oil.

Within minutes I felt MUCH better!! My mood was much more stable!

I thought of you not long after that and I thought I'd pass on the info

Give it a try..it might not fix your problem entirely but I'm sure it would help take the edge off. It certainly did for me! It gives your brain a more stable source of fuel.
Hope this helps you a bit.

### thomas — 2015-06-30T16:16:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-170080>

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-18T21:08:29+0300

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=170072)
>
> I just went to the bathroom and saw that my entire right armpit is WHITE.
> I'm trying not to panic. Ok it's just another vitiligo spot. I have lots of them, why do I want to cry when a new one appears?
> I need some support people

I think my mom had vitiligo in her 50's. She was getting it on her face, She was so paranoid about it being permanent but it eventually went away on its own.
It will with you too. You are young and very pro active with your health. There are advantages to having health problems when you are young (believe it or not) and that is that you LEARN about health long before its too late. People who don't have any problems eventually get them, go to the 'doctor' who doesn't help them and they spend the rest of their life on drugs that don't fix the problem. They don't understand that the conventional medical system has very little resources to fix their problems. It took me a VERY long time to figure this out. I thank the internet.
If everybody had good foundational knowledge about health, the system would become more accountable. But since everybody kind of assumes that the doctor 'knows what he is doing' he can cover himself by throwing a bunch of complicated words at you.
You're gonna do okay

### Inger — 2015-07-23T16:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-172758>

You speak Norwegian? How fun....  we could speak

I am living that girl every day  it is amazing
people think I am crazy but I do not care one bit lol. Gives many interesting situations for sure
Funny enough so many want to visit me..... I feel like running out in the woods and hide some times, ha!
I tell about everything I do.. and they think it is funny! So it gives lost of laughter...  I like that!

The other day at work I talked to 2 guests and they asked me so many questions at the bar after their dinner... and I answered of course, honestly. They almost passed out and said they are scared of me now... they said when I served them at their table a few hours before they thought I was so sweet and they could have never ever imagined that this girl in that white summer dress looking so innocent is digging out brains from sheep heads and eating it raw.. and making ram ball smoothies! Oh man they thought I was so crazy...lol

I said I am scared to open the skulls too but I do it anyways, I just think about Jack how he operates on live human brains.... and then I cant be such a pussy! So I just do it!

One of them is a hunter... I asked if I can get the heads and balls and hearts and he said yes, for free! But I must keep the head whole... I cant do that I only am able to get the brain out cracking the scalp... so my boss (the Chef and owner of the restaurant) was there too listening to the conversation and he said he will help me get the brain out without cracking the head!!! He is soooo sweet.
He feeds me raw meat in the restaurant kitchen..lol and opens up oysters for me! He is a star chef... he really can cook! He does not think I am that crazy, just a little bit..lol He likes brains him self but cooked, and he loves raw liver and raw fresh blood

Inger,
I noticed that. I adore your journal. It makes me happy every time I read it. Looks like you don't have problems in life because you are so positive.
I spoke Norwegian when I was little. My friend was half Norwegian and she tought me a lot of things. Then I stopped and now I can't say a word... but I can understand it and I can read it. It's a very strange feeling.

I want to learn Norwegian again but it's very hard here in France. You can find teachers only in Paris.
When I remember my childhood, I think of this song:
Nisser og griser og alle andre sma skal vaere inne na.
Kose oss i bingen og ingen skal ha no vondt a tenke pa.
Takk for denne dagen..... blalblablabla LOL. I don't know if it's famous in Norway but we used to listed to it with my friend

I used to think this was really funny:

I would find a big banana slug which would withdraw its eyes right away and curl up. I would put it out on the street . A car would appear about a mile away. The slug would start unwinding. The car is getting closer. The eyes would start to come out. The car is getting really close. The slug is cruising as fast as he can in a long straight line yelling "NOOOOO!!"....... SPLAASH!! Like a tube of toothpaste!

My buddy looks at me and said "I feel so sorry for him. He was trying so hard to run!"

Yeah we all did stuff like that but now I have much more respect for life.
It was kind of funny, but kind of mean too.

### Peertje80 — 2015-08-22T23:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-175397>

I can very much relate to your nightly horrors. I experienced pretty much the same thing for over 6 years or so. It started with cramping calf's and ended with cramps and spasm every were: hands, jaws, respiratory muscles. Sweating like crazy, feeling way too hot at night. I did sleep tests and tried several drugs out of desperation, even anti epileptic drugs. But nothing worked and nobody knew what is was. Then I moved to a sea side village... and it just went a way. I think being in the sea a lot and out of the city helped me tremendously. I hope you find your solution too. This condition (or what ever it is) really sucks.

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-24T21:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-177795>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=177787)
>
> I agree with you. He has the right voice for it. But I don't like this song though. Isn't it too sweet?

It is when Cat Stevens sings it. This is because he doesn't understand it. (If he had he wouldn't have sung it because it's a thoroughly Christian hymn.)

Notice among other things that he sings "recreation" (as in rest and recreation) rather than "re - creation". And all the difference is in there.

"The Word" is an ancient Christian term for the Second Person of the Trinity. "In the beginning was the Word …"

[http://www.kingjamesbibleonline.org/book.php?book=John&chapter=1&verse=&t=1](http://www.kingjamesbibleonline.org/book.php?book=John&chapter=1&verse=&t=1)

What the hymn-writer wants you to do is to think of Adam walking in the Garden, but also to think of Christ walking in another garden:

> Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.

[http://www.kingjamesbibleonline.org/John-20-15/](http://www.kingjamesbibleonline.org/John-20-15/)

The thought in the hymn is that everything has been made perfect _because of_ the resurrection.

And, well … you know what comes in between. The crucifixion. Nothing over-sweet about that.

### Gagnrad — 2015-09-24T22:01:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-swim-sometimes.15265/post-177801>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=177800)
>
> You want to turn me into ginger.

Who's Ginger? And I was just explaining to you what the writer had meant. You don't have to like it.

It went over Cat Stevens' head, but it is kind of interesting, nonetheless. If you're going to look at something, some work of art, you have to _start_ by knowing what its author had in mind. Otherwise, you simply don't know what's going on.

### Erik — 2015-06-10T19:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update.15310/post-167435>

Three days ago started haveing a night problem. I wake up at 3 or 4 anda cannot sleep again. Yesterday y turned off all the lights at 6 pm and stayed in bed till 7 in the morning. The sleeping problem improved but it still was there. The same day my sleep worsened i ate bread toasted with olive oli( terrible mistake) the sleep problems + omega6 + wheat, was a horrbile combo that worsened my brain fog. Yesterday and today its contiuous. It never was this way. These days there is no sun here. Since two weeks ago the sun is cimpletely gone A month ago i dropped CT becasue it was so cold oustide here. Today i started again. I NEED my focus these days because the day after tomorrow i have my second physics exam: AC circuits, maxwell equations, wave interference, doppler effect, geometric optics,..... its hard to study the last parts with the fog lol. I just want to say that the following days (starting today) i will try these:

\-reestart CT (already did)
\-olive leaf extract
\-prepare sourkrout( and have my acidophilus pills)
\-coconut oil ( not virgin thoug)
\-cats claw tea
\-2 garlic cloves each meal

PD: I cant afford seafood. I cant get extra virgin coconut oil. I cant get sun because these weeks are extremely cloudy. I cant get a magnetico. I still dont have blue light blocking glasses. My family wont listen to me when i ask them to change white lights at night. Im living a nightmare.

But i will do what i can, just tell me if my protocol seems right. (Ive read from people who cured brain fog with olive leaf extract and cats claw tea)...

Thanks

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2015-06-11T07:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/causes-of-hair-loss.15332/post-167555>

Hi guys
I am having a lot of hair loss...just coming out of the roots.
I am on 75 mcg of t3, I have low cortisol so I take 2 adrenal cortex in am and one in afternoon. My iron is optimal and so is thyroid for now. Not sure what else would cause this hair loss. Maybe still low cortisol? I sometimes have a very fast and pounding heart rate at night when I have a dream win which I am in a fight or flight situation in the dream itself . The fast heart rate goes back to normal as soon as I wake up. Adrenaline rushes I guess due to low cortisol? I would like to get a 2nd opinion before I start HC again as I have been on it in the past but had weaned off using the circadian method. This is my saliva cortisol test from last year. I know it can change quickly but just posting it to give a reference point.
This was the cortisol test from last year..just a reference.
Cortisol (saliva) 5.3 ng/mL 3.7-9.5 (morning)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.5 ng/mL 1.2-3.0 (noon)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.6 ng/mL 0.6-1.9 (evening)
Cortisol (saliva) 0.3 L ng/mL 0.4-1.0 (night)
Therapies
20mg topical Progesterone (OTC) (8 Hours Last used)
Its low 3 out of the 4 times I undertand
Also here are the results of hormones as tested last year on day 19 as well

SHBG 69 (24.6-122)
progesterone 8 ( (luteal 1.7-27)
17-oh progesterone 100 (35-290 luteal)
pregnenolone 106 ( it just says adults <151 as the ref range??)
estrogen total 170 (11 to 120 days ref range is 122-437)
estradiol 107.8 luteal (43.8-211)
free testosterone .2 (0-2.2)
total testosterone 33 (8-48)
LH 7.1 (1-11.4)
FSH 5.3 (1.7-7.7)
prolactin 22.1 (4.8 to 23.3) usually highish
I'm 41
I am supplementing with 20 mg of topical progesterone day 12 to 26. Is estrogen lowish? My cholesterol is always on the lower side even though I eat a lot of good fats (157). I am gluten intolerant and have been off gluten for 5 yrs now. Here are recent blood tests:

Glucose 104 ( this is a whole another issue..I feel high BG is related to low cortisol)
TIBC 274 (250-450)
UIBC 136 136 (150-375)
serum iron 138 (35-155)
ferritin 101 (15-150)
percent saturation (50%) (This has come down from 57% from 3 months ago) I havent supplemented iron for 4 months now
vit b12 1824 I am on 1000 mg of sublingual methycobalamin
folate >19.9 ( on a B complex that has 400 mcg of folate) I am hetero for 1298 C
RBC 4.14 (3.77-5.28)
hemoglobin 12.1 (11.1-15.9) (no idea why this is on low side while percent saturation is high)
hematocrit 35.5 (34-46.6)
mcv 86 (79-97)
immature granulocytes 0
immature grans 0
reticulocyte count 0.7 (.6-2.6)
sodium 139
potassium 4.3
free t3 4.8 (2-4.4)
All I can think of as the cause of hair loss is low cortisol, low P or E. Can anyone point me in the right direction PLEASE?

Hi ladies

No definitely not spam! I am a real person with real issues.

I eat a low carb paleo type diet with protein and lots of good fats like avocado , coconut oil, coconut milk, and olive oil. I need to start eating more seafood but I am not able to get local seafood so been a bit hesitant about seafood that has been coming in from China. Plus not too skilled in seafood recipes. I know this is important but I've not had a lot of support in this. Ive been spinning my wheels for 6 yrs now researching and biohacking but I'm hitting a block with the adrenal fatigue. Initially I thought it was the gluten and I should bounce back after I went gluten free but that wasnt the case.
Normally eat pasture raised eggs for bfast but I am getting kind of tired of the eggs every day. Some type of protein either salmon, chicken, or turkey with cauliflower and broccoli, big salad for dinner with olive oil , apple cider vinegar, and celtic salt. Plus a bit of same as what was for lunch. I have been gluten free for 5 yrs now. I've been struggling with adrenal fatigue for 6 + years. So my issues of concern are a high fasting BG (118-130) even with 1000 mg of ER metformin, low cortisol 3 out of the 4 times per day,low progesterone (which I havent been able to raise even with 20 mg of Progesterone cream for a year now) and last but not least lots of hair shedding. This intrigued me, and while I am a woman with thick hair, my new haircut made it easy for me to increase my use scalp brush in the shower and actual brushing of the scalp. FWIW

### JanSz — 2015-06-23T00:54:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/causes-of-hair-loss.15332/post-169281>

\=======

Good SHBG for you would be under 40.
If your cortisol is low you should not push thyroid hormone.
RT3 is a safety valve. That is how body is able to get rid of T4 that it can't use.

Say you have 20 amp fuse on your electrical circuit, but it blows often because hair dryer is connected to circuit but it actually sits in water.
You got sick of not being able to use that circuit, and you replaced 20Amp fuse with 100amp fuse.
That is not going to end well, you will likely burn your house.

Same thing you are doing by eating T3, not little bit
but
to the excess.

You not only do not have cortisol, but are eating excessive amounts of T3 (removed weak fuse and replaced with solid wire).

Back out slowly.
You are full of unprocessed thyroid hormones.
Make sure to not eat hydrocortisone or other cortisol during this process,
otherwise you may learn what thyroid storm is.

\===============================================================================
You may increase your DHEAs by eating (sufficient amount) of DHEA pills.

Low cortisol, likely pregnenolone and progesterone are low and LDL cholesterol high.
Missing all kind of vitamins, minerals and other micronutrients.
Fatty acids likely need help.

To raise cortisol you may use
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology.

desired values (serum)(aim at top values):

Cortisol(AM) (6.2-19.4)ug/dL
Cortisol (PM) (2.3-11.9)ug/dL
DHEAs Women 275-400 μg/dL
progesterone (day 2-4 of cycle) 1.5 ng/mL

All other Circadian Rhythm and light/EMF management advice apply

### LisaLearning — 2015-07-22T10:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/causes-of-hair-loss.15332/post-172619>

Too little thyroid hormone can cause hair loss, and too much can cause hair loss. Anecdotally, I see a very well known endocrinology expert, and when my T3 was slightly elevated, as yours is, I was also having some symptoms of too much T3 (very similar to yours - a few mins of heart pounding/palpitations at bedtime), the doc wanted me to reduce, but whenever I reduced I had horrible leg cramps at night, and having tried magnesium with no result, I was convinced that I did not have enough thyroid hormone bc nothing else helped the cramps (and the thyroid reduced them, but didn't eliminate). I had also was losing some hair.

I found Jack recommending B6 for muscle cramps, and that has almost completely sorted the leg cramps, so I reduced the dessicated thyroid bc I could now see the doc was right about the levels. My hair loss reduced quite a bit.

I find the other thing that affects my hair loss is keeping my scalp very clean. I wonder if this is tied to my slightly high blood glucose readings (98-106), because having more glucose tends to encourage more bacterial and fungal growth (amongst other things).

### LisaLearning — 2015-07-22T10:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/causes-of-hair-loss.15332/post-172620>

Oh, a comment about you lying in bed at 10 pm after having turned everything off hours before and still not being able to sleep. I find it makes a big difference if I eat first thing, as jack suggests, and make sure your last meal is four hours before bed - so for you that would mean finished eating by 6. Otherwise I find it really can interfere with sleep.

# Thread: What are your most memorable Peak Experiences/Flow States?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-06-11T12:42:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167582>

I am interested in [peak experiences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peak_experience) or flow states but not that aware of the work of [Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi](http://lateralaction.com/articles/mihaly-csikszentmihalyi/)
and [Steve Kotler](http://www.flowgenomeproject.co/) ([The Rise of Superman](http://riseofsuperman.com/))

[Maslow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maslow%27s_hierarchy_of_needs) found that as his test subjects recalled their peak experiences this actually increased their ability to experience new ones.

My most memorable peak experience was half an hour spent kicking a football (soccer ball!) into a goal! But a bit of background to this story. Growing up as a kid I was mad about football, my typical day was school then outside playing football with friends or often alone till the sun went down, watching TV programs such as [Monkey Magic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OTxPlUnDQ1U), [The Water Margin](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A-T_IfIBij4) or [Grange Hill](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LRlOD_wBOfQ) and then playing pool or pinball or video games (Space invaders!) in my parents pub in my dressing gown and going to sleep late due to the street lighting and noise from bar below my bedroom. Ah...good times.

So I was a pretty decent football player. My big opportunity came when our team were asked to play against Chelsea youth team but unfortunately for me I developed a terrible cold/fever and performed poorly.

Fast forward to University in my twenties and I aspired to be the cool dude who could smoke, drink and party all Saturday night and then play football Sunday morning. Suffice to say I was not in the first team and was a pale shadow of my former glory.

_O_ne eventful day I went out to kick a ball with my friend in goal and I entered into a flow state in which there was no separation between my mind and body. As a kid I had dreamed and imagined scoring perfect goals, top corner, ripping the net but reality is far removed but during this half an hour every single shot I imagined in my head I was able to execute. Shots flew pass my friend, volleys, lobs, chips, curlers into corners, left foot, right foot, inside/outside of foot - it didn't matter. I remember one shot I was twenty yards out and I saw the gap between the goalies legs and imagined scoring through his legs and I did. Time seemed to stand-still, there was no fear or trepidation, no negativity or doubt, just pure joy. I was alive and all was well with the world.

Why did this happen? The answer.... I was in Love. Much of my time at University my friend and I bemoaned our inability to find a girl (we were both painfully shy) but I had met my soul mate the previous few days and was euphoric - years of frustration and lack of self belief rolled away and now happiness. We have been married now 20+ years.

Have tagged the following after cursary search of terms 'peak experience' and 'flow states'
@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) @[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/) @[prAna303](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1363/) @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) @[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) @[Barry](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/717/) @[Shijin13](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/969/) @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) @[kovita](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9805/)
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) @[Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/) @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) @[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/) @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) @[nicld](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6689/)

### prAna303 — 2015-06-11T16:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167607>

I think of many different ones.

Today i would say that i found a state where almost every single day is flow. Some days are different but always in a way that can be used to find another angle...

Main reason is the life of no demands of systems. I no longer live to work, work is in my way of seeing life removed and replaced by something else, i create my own dream. Because i live by a simple idea and have done it for the last nine years now, fine tuning it each day. Everything is possible, just not predictable. Just in a second this beauty will go away if i try to find the right keys to the secret, locking it into some theory instantly kills it and i can only loose track again.

When i run i always do it in the pure nature, cold or warm, summer and winter and always letting nature get to me. Here time is not what machine time is, here it goes into another kind, changing each second. My trails are never the same even if an GPS would say so but if i would do this in a manmade world... Nothing can feed me anymore. This is flow versus modern life in my opinion.

Doing breath training i always find a state where i sink in my mind. My body is on one plane and my brain flows out into each cell, i can feel local spots in my body but also a bigger me, my mind changes the movie i see behind my closed eyes, together with my body which is connected to the environment. This way i cured myself i think, in a weird world.

Watching a dance performance, since they and me are likens we can be synced. What they feel and do is directly played in my body and mind, the light they cast from the stage always do kick my systems to life. After a great one i can barely walk, i am so used that i must melt the sweet pains i feel.

Many say i live in a dream world, yes i do. Many say that i should work like they do, no i do not. It has become very clear thru the last couple of years that the normal is not made for me, sure i tried it but... I must gather the pieces of each day and build what i feel in that special moment, otherwise i will go crazy... In a bad way!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-11T17:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167619>

For me flow is a state idealize mitonuclear communication. It manifests in my brain's output.

### Josh — 2015-06-11T18:24:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167629>

From my N=1 experience, when someone develops something that we might call a "meditation practice" questions of "flow state" become irrelevant. The absence of "flow" is an absence of "meditation" or whatever one wants to call it....children were reputedly taught this before anything else once upon a time, the modern world has decided to skip this step and look what has happened...

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-06-12T03:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167724>

err...^

i have had probably 3 memorable flow states

1) as a youngster through to my mid-late twenties i played (ice) hockey
one memorable moment was in a finals series against our teams nemesis "the jets" in senior A which is australia's highest amateur hockey before the AIHL
finals were a series of 3 games, we were in game 3, 3rd period and down by two goals
i vividly remember the huddle during a time out, and the level of intensity everyone felt..it was total coherence of energy
the plays that ensued coming out of defense (i played L D, alongside a good friend who started the same day as me back when we were 11) were magic, and pure flow.
we scored 4 goals in about 6 minutes and held the other team out of our blue line the rest of the game...something clicked and it seemed effortless, pure coherence between all teammates

2)

DJing this gig in sydney, as well ads this one --> [http://www.inthemix.com.au/forum/showthread.php?t=227550](http://www.inthemix.com.au/forum/showthread.php?t=227550)

everything flowed perfectly and all the artists buzzed off the energy of the crowd...pure vibes from the foundational days of exploration in music (before dubstep turned crap)

recording of the void set here (levels drop when the bass comes in from compression at the desk: [http://dubstep.com.au/mixes/Spherix@Index-Sydney\_April\_17\_2009.mp3](http://dubstep.com.au/mixes/Spherix@Index-Sydney_April_17_2009.mp3)

3) 5th years university technique exams. shitting myself that i would get over nervous and make a mistake, the examiner said he hadnt seen a pair of hands that good all day

Any time I'm under the waves (in the ocean)

Any time I'm surfing tumblr  (5 minutes have passed, but it feels like an hour). This generalises to any instance of artistic bombardment

Many instances of avoiding punches and incoming basketballs to my head

Any time I discover some fundamental connection of the Universe:

Recently, it's been how the vau-calculi and f-exprs seem to require different forms of substitution, but yet can generate all forms of computation. But they are fundamentally built upon a single type of operation, namely Substitution.

Computation is just a description of a state machine transition from one state to another, and this seemed too similar to how objects in the Universe (and possibly the Universe itself) transitions from one quantised state to another, with a single fundamental operation, namely electromagnetic interaction with matter.

Moreover, the distinction made by vau-calculus between Symbols and Variables seemed too akin to the Light and Matter distinction used by the Universe. Hmmm .... this spurred a whole bunch of thoughts about how I could potentially view the laws of Physics through the lens of Computer Science
​

When I feel the scent of such a connection, the research and thoughts that follow are definitely in a flow state.

...

### PaulG — 2015-06-12T11:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167792>

Yesterday, I went for my semi-regular barefoot 3hr hike - before this I downed a 2litre cocktail of structured spring water + liquid chlorophyll + potassium iodide + magnesium citrate + 6 spirulina tablets + 6 Chlorella tablets + some nootropics and a strong coffee and 6mg of nicotine gum

This time it took only 2hr because I was moving so freely and efficiently, the terrain is hill forest trail with many rocks and sharp stone hidden underneath a blanket of leaves and twigs. Typically I make a few 'mistakes' - toe stubs, landing sharp stones, losing balance, slipping but for the first hour I move gracefully with confidence and purpose with my body knowing where to go and my mind vaguely thinking about how to word this forum post and in a creative ideas generating mode. On the way back as the effect of the caffeine wore off I started to thing more consciously and negatively and worrying about where to place my feet each step - the consequence of which was increase of 'mistakes'. Felt great afterwards nonetheless but made the error of another coffee at 12pm which basically induced high level anxiety and very poor sleep e.g. wired until 12pm when normally I'm out by 10pm. Not sure what this says about my redox but know my body and mind craves morning light and I suspect I also need to get more pm healing light 4-6pm and knock off the coffee.

### Shijin13 — 2015-06-12T16:44:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167822>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=167808)
>
> during headaches time perception is altered too.

I've experienced this with migraines. but I've also been able to channel it when I was competitively swimming. Every night before bed I would visualize what my next personal record swim would be. What times I'd take out the first 100m, and subsequent 100m if I was swimming 200m breast or 200m fly, and I'd do the visualization practice while listening to Pink Floyd's Learning to fly and Shine on Crazy Diamond. I've actually gone back to meditating and thinking about tomorrow before going off to bed. I'll read for a bit. then laydown and begin to breath deeply and focus on a mindlight and what I want to accomplish tomorrow. First thing upon waking I lay in bed and think of actualizing what I meditated on the night before...

When I had my best swims - the were all effortless. all the visualization allowed me to trigger the events w/o thinking during the actual swim.

as an adult I've also used flow to manage deployment/receptions operations and avoid major incidents on the runway and ramp loading/off loading multiple aircraft and load. Also through out schooling - I've been able to tap into it to conduct research and write 20page term papers in Spanish in under 5 hours - and end up with an A on the paper.

One of my favorite flows - though was learning how to water ski. I remember starting on Ticks and popping up and easily popping 180's and 360's and quickly transition to single skis at the age of 10!

I have to echo @[David Limacher](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11045/) sentiments of hitting some steeps, deeps, with lots of bumps through the trees. the stillness in fresh fallen snow is an amazing way to pull in the snow.

I also remember rock climbing at Garden of the Gods my Senior Year at the Academy.... totally awesome feeling... as was free climbing up the edge of a water fall while Hashing on Guam...

### Josh — 2015-06-12T19:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167845>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=167629)
>
> From my N=1 experience, when someone develops something that we might call a "meditation practice" questions of "flow state" become irrelevant. The absence of "flow" is an absence of "meditation" or whatever one wants to call it....children were reputedly taught this before anything else once upon a time, the modern world has decided to skip this step and look what has happened...

I know I sound arrogant here which is a personal weakness. My first "experiences" were lying in bed sick as a kid. I had several extended periods of being bedridden pre-adolescence. Then there were experiences related to physical activity, music, various drugs, etc.. Meditation started in spite of me at the end of a martial arts class where we did zazen. It was my first time on a zen bench so I could sit straight and comfortably (I could never sit cross-legged). I simply disappeared for an hour. Afterwards, I knew I had experienced something different with no "effort" and for the first time I clearly recognized something different than "sleep" or what I had previously associated with "awake". I slowly found ways to cultivate this and it took about 5 years of practice in various and sundry ways for it to become grounded in my being. Since then (about age 30) it has become as essential as eating, drinking and sleeping. Paradoxically, I have experienced more through just sitting and letting go than any of the methods I had used before to cultivate any particular "state". In my research to understand such things, I discovered that everything I have come across is well documented and described in detail in multiple places/traditions. It seems our so-called "culture" is at best adolescent as it is just coming upon these things and recognizing their value. Focusing on the transcendent is a bad revenue model and bad for the mind control business.

### Josh — 2015-06-12T19:46:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167846>

Every tradition I have explored has emphasized that the "experiences" are not the point and that one ought to be grateful for them and to move on without attachment. "Flow" is actually "Normal" when we are healthy....this is my understanding. I have met 4 people in this life who embody this, so I know it is possible and far from common.

### Da-mo — 2015-06-13T02:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-167880>

This one is probably not what you are expecting . . . Its one I've never been able to work out but it was definitely a flow state . . . .

When I was 11 years old a group of 4 or 5 of my friends and I were chatting in the classroom. During the conversation (I have no recollection what we were talking about but I don't think it was anything significant) I drifted into that staring into space state where you are not thinking of anything. I came out of that state when I noticed everyone had stopped talking and they were all looking at me. One of the boys, the one who I last remember had been talking, was holding his hand over his cheek with a shocked look on his face.

I then had an inkling of what had happened, just the faintest of memories, but wasn't sure so asked, "Shit Harry, did I just hit you?" Turns out I had slapped him in mid sentence - for what reason I have no idea. Harry was one of the nicest people you could ever meet - not a mean bone in his body and never said anything bad about anyone. All of the others were just the same - a group of nicer people would be difficult to find. I too was like them - unlikely to get in trouble and not predisposed to malice.

It was as if my hand just flew without me realizing it, without any conscious inkling of it - it still puzzles me to this day . . . Of course I apologized, but yet it did not feel wrong. It just felt as if it was meant to stop him from talking further.

I'd like to think that unconsciously I picked up on something unsaid, or maybe prevented Harry from saying something regretful. It just occurred to me that subconsciously I may have perceived him as a rival/threat in the group dynamic - social acceptance and all that. I don't think I'll ever know the answer. In any case, we all remained friends for many many years afterwards.

Hmm interesting... I experience flow when kite boarding. Being very focused on finding the perfect spot for take off. I can then somehow slow time. Bringing speed, angle and force together and push off for a jump, feeling the ascent flying through the air, until you're not rising any more, steering back the kite, spot landing, twist body for safe landing and ride on. The duration of a jump is some seconds at best but it feels like minutes.

### sjoshua — 2015-06-17T21:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-168622>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=167724)
>
> err...^
>
>
> i have had probably 3 memorable flow states
>
> 1) as a youngster through to my mid-late twenties i played (ice) hockey
> one memorable moment was in a finals series against our teams nemesis "the jets" in senior A which is australia's highest amateur hockey before the AIHL
> finals were a series of 3 games, we were in game 3, 3rd period and down by two goals
> i vividly remember the huddle during a time out, and the level of intensity everyone felt..it was total coherence of energy
> the plays that ensued coming out of defense (i played L D, alongside a good friend who started the same day as me back when we were 11) were magic, and pure flow.
> we scored 4 goals in about 6 minutes and held the other team out of our blue line the rest of the game...something clicked and it seemed effortless, pure coherence between all teammates

Holy shit, this was some serious flow, and still lingering strongly in your mind friend. I just got head to toe chills reading this one.

I've had a few unique experiences on and off drugs, performing in ways that weren't 'possible' if you restrict your thinking. Here's the most memorable:

I ingested cannabis and felt suddenly lifted, as if intended and energized by something beyond me. I consecutively felt a drive to assist my SO (who, at the time - 10:21 PM, I recall it like I can still grab the clock in front of me) had already gone to bed. She had not participated in mind-altering that day, due to not feeling well. All of the following up until I indicate below felt as if it were happening in a state of flow, which I would describe as a state in which my fleshpod is functioning exactly as it should without conscious direction. Upon entering the bedroom, I felt the desire to massage her - she was just drifting off to sleep and no longer fully conscious after a quick hi. While touching her it was as if I could push the positive and negative energy around inside of her, it appeared as hues of red/dark purple respectively in her skin tone. I could run my hand along it and change its nature, like tuning with a magnet. Additionally, as I was massaging along her spine and neck and felt that I was releasing and re-aligning her muscles and bones in unfathomable ways, as if they were free-moving building blocks and I were a child playing with toys. There were distinct popping noises that I can recall, similar to that of cracking someone's back.
There came a moment where the flow ended as I suddenly attempted to consciously observe what was happening. The following all happened in the same instant:
\- When I looked down at myself, it was as if my view zoomed through my chest, just below the sturnum, and then up through myself until I arrived back at my eyes. It was like looking in a circle at myself... difficult to describe
\- When I looked down at her, her backbones up through her neck bones appeared to be significantly longer than typical by 6-12"
\- Anxiety plagued me momentarily due to what I was perceiving.
\- My hand that had at the moment been massaging her neck suddenly felt as if it were completely stuck, and I had the feeling that I was in some way holding her in a limbo state.
\- I closed my eyes and knew that all is good, and all is love, and when I re-opened them everything had shifted back, I could move my hand from her neck and she was again in a normal state.
After this, I was still a little thrown as it was the first time I'd ever experienced anything beyond the typical laws of this reality/illusion, so I felt it best to leave her be and go hang out with the others again. Upon leaving the bedroom, it was as if I was EITHER orchestrating everything that was going to happen next, OR was somehow able to know what was going to happen next before it occurred. For example,
\- I look at my roommate and knew a moment later that his stereo was going to skip. He switched songs, and I knew that one was going to skip too.
\- I knew exactly what another friend was going to say before he said it, as if the conversation had been scripted in my mind.
\- I went outside, and felt an overwhelming desire to go 'up', i.e. into space, sorta I guess, although I felt no inkling towards any specific destination. I knew it was not yet time for such things as I was to help more, I had the urge to dance radically to burn some energy and/or to keep that energy flowing properly.
\- I looked at my dog, and she had the craziest puppy eyes and a look on her face I'd never seen before. Without words, with only a thought on my part she came running to me.
This continued for around 5 minutes, until a point where I felt a sort of jolting across my heart, and then all returned to 'normal'.

### DavidL — 2015-06-19T01:59:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-168825>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=168656)
>
> Just reading this on gravitational time dilation and how mass/gravity supresses quantum behaviour.
>
> I wonder if this has a link to how flow states (quantum in nature?) are accompanied by a feeling of lightness and lack of inertia.

My best flow states where time appears to stand still are when going 100km/h on my board....the edge of death is where I like it...I feel that the flow state is triggered by the need for survival in some cases.

A few years ago in Vail I hit well over 100km/h....at the end of the run I came to how dangerous that really was as the run was packed with skiiers. Vail is speed freak paradise and there are many pretty girls in fur...but I digress....I don't remember there being so many people on the run. One wrong move and shit would get real quick and would likely have altered the next 6 months of my life.

Aother flow state was acheived a few years back on a longboard skateboard. However the result was not good. My wife and I were driving on the way to our cabin on a lake in the valley. The road down into the valley is long and curves....perfect for a longboard...I had never done it before but I thought I had it in me.

My wife stopped the CRV and let me out 1/5 of the way down....I was wearing a t-shirt and shorts and skate shoes. It was 35\*Celcius so I knew the asphalt would be a little bit sticky, likely slowing down my ride.

I began down the hill with a few turns to speek check but then two cars came up the hill. I had to go straight so as not to get hit by the oncoming cars. Erin followed behind in the CRV.

Well after the cars past I was going roughly 55km/h....In the zone, feeling the imortance of being in the zone I was confident that all was well. After 55km/h I noticed a speed wobble- the equipment could handle the mechanical stress that was on my board....as I got going faster I remember the wobble becoming unsustainable....

I was going down. I knew it. Knowing that the ride would end in the most utterly awful way, I began to reason....It was like I had all the time in the world to make a decision...I was falling...so the decision became laying it down on asphalt or running it into the steep ditch with trees....

My wife said she was following at 67km/h when I pulled it into the ditch....I cut hard and hoped for the best. I did roughly 3 cart wheels uncontrolled down the slope and landed in the bush. I was cut, bleeding from numerous places, shirt ripped up, but I was alive.

I didn't hurt till later. It was a rush.

Is there a survival mechanism that can speed/slow/alter time to keep us alive? I think so.

### Da-mo — 2015-06-19T03:43:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-most-memorable-peak-experiences-flow-states.15351/post-168853>

> [David Limacher said:](/goto/post?id=168825)
>
> Is there a survival mechanism that can speed/slow/alter time to keep us alive? I think so.

Recently we were put through a new safety training program where a large part of it was about mindset. They talked about the RAS or reticular activating system. [http://www.innovateus.net/health/what-function-reticular-activating-system](http://www.innovateus.net/health/what-function-reticular-activating-system)

So apparently this system sorts the info and prioritizes it in this order;
Dangerous
Important
Pleasurable
Interesting

I would guess that on the long board all input was filtered to dangerous and all resources diverted to processing that situation.

On working with horses we find that they really only have one rule - to survive. When threatened they are not scared of getting hurt, they are scared of getting killed and so will run through fences etc. if they percieve a survival threat.

I believe we have an override built in as well that will choose survival over injury.

The really interesting thing is that sometimes an animal will choose the survival of another over their own survival - so maybe it is also built in to prioritize survival of species or genetic line over self preservation.

In any case, it appears that these flow states and survival mechanisms are built into the architecture/design - inherent or ancient. Thus it is only experience and environment that works to separate us from this way of being.
