# Thread: Does cancer have a function?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-11T22:54:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167687>

Here's an article by MIT researcher Stephanie Seneff I found at the WAPF site.

There's a phrase "intimidatingly clever" - I find Mrs Seneff a bit like that. She often seems to have something really unusual but very well thought out to say.

For example, I find her argument that by taking high oral doses of vitamin D people may be "digging themselves deeper into a hole" quite intriguing. I'm not claiming to know she's right. I don't mind admitting: it's above my head. But what if she is right? What if the primary role of sunlight is to make cholesterol sulphate in the skin? Then there would be all these people taking anything up to 10,000 IU of D3 a day and patting themselves on the back, and all the time it would be a too easy "solution" to a problem they hadn't understood, and _really_ they needed to get out in the sun. Even Cordain, who's spent some time pointing to what the Vitamin D Council has been saying, would have come unstuck.

So here she argues that cancer may be the body's attempt to do what it can in a difficult situation. (And, again, she thinks at the root of it may be a sulphur (and cholesterol) deficiency.)

> Why would cancer cells do this? I have a very simple explanation: the tumor is clearing the excess glucose from the blood and replacing it with an abundance of lactate to provide usable fuel for the critical organs like the heart and the brain. This is one reason why the tumor is part of the solution instead of part of the problem. The service it performs is essential to allow the heart and the brain, in their compromised state of severe cholesterol and sulfate deficiency, to continue to function by utilizing lactate as a source of fuel instead of glucose. But the tumor suffers from glycation damage and acidification as a consequence, so it struggles to survive under such harsh conditions.

[http://www.westonaprice.org/modern-diseases/cancer-to-the-rescue/](http://www.westonaprice.org/modern-diseases/cancer-to-the-rescue/)

Again: I can't judge. But what if?

And, this does seem to make a kind of intuitive sense to me. What I mean is this: I mean the current model seems to be that "your body is out to get you" as Zoe Harcombe remarked. It just does random, dangerous stuff. I think Zoe was talking about the diet-heart hypothesis and statins, but I think you could probably apply that to any number of medical "problems" and their "solutions".

IIRC she said this on air speaking to that very nice and friendly PT Sam Feltham. I have a vague memory of her saying, "Do you really think your body is out to get you? Or, if you believe in God, do you think God would be out to get you?" And, actually, the latter addition develops the thought in an interesting way: I mean not from any theological point of view. It's a way of saying: "Is reality really like that?" Well, now you say …

I think so much of what's currently done seems to revolve around assuming that stuff just randomly goes wrong with us for no particular reason—because our bodies, and Nature, and the whole process of evolution are stupid, I guess.. And then we're offered quick fixes: it might be a statin, or perhaps a D3 pill (yes, I've taken them: I just wonder, that's all), or a blood-pressure lowering medication ... and all the time it's not as simple as that. And if you think in terms of lowering (or raising) some number—perhaps merely because you _can_ measure it—then there will be bright people at a pharmaceutical company who can, and will, look into disrupting some pathway to do that. Everyone's happy … yet perhaps that might be worst thing to do. What the Zen people might call "monkey mind" at work—_mere_ cleverness.

I don't know. Just some thoughts. But might cancer be some kind of adaptive response?

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-06-11T23:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167689>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=167687)
>
> Here's an article by MIT researcher Stephanie Seneff I found at the WAPF site.
>
> There's a phrase "intimidatingly clever" - I find Mrs Seneff a bit like that. She often seems to have something really unusual but very well thought out to say.
>
> For example, I find her argument that by taking high oral doses of vitamin D people may be "digging themselves deeper into a hole" quite intriguing. I'm not claiming to know she's right. I don't mind admitting: it's above my head. But what if she is right? What if the primary role of sunlight is to make cholesterol sulphate in the skin? Then there would be all these people taking anything up to 10,000 IU of D3 a day and patting themselves on the back, and all the time it would be a too easy "solution" to a problem they hadn't understood, and _really_ they needed to get out in the sun. Even Cordain, who's spent some time pointing to what the Vitamin D Council has been saying, would have come unstuck.
>
> So here she argues that cancer may be the body's attempt to do what it can in a difficult situation. (And, again, she thinks at the root of it may be a sulphur (and cholesterol) deficiency.)
>
>
>
> [http://www.westonaprice.org/modern-diseases/cancer-to-the-rescue/](http://www.westonaprice.org/modern-diseases/cancer-to-the-rescue/)
>
> Again: I can't judge. But what if?
>
> And, this does seem to make a kind of intuitive sense to me. What I mean is this: I mean the current model seems to be that "your body is out to get you" as Zoe Harcombe remarked. It just does random, dangerous stuff. I think Zoe was talking about the diet-heart hypothesis and statins, but I think you could probably apply that to any number of medical "problems" and their "solutions".
>
> IIRC she said this on air speaking to that very nice and friendly PT Sam Feltham. I have a vague memory of her saying, "Do you really think your body is out to get you? Or, if you believe in God, do you think God would be out to get you?" And, actually, the latter addition develops the thought in an interesting way: I mean not from any theological point of view. It's a way of saying: "Is reality really like that?" Well, now you say …
>
> I think so much of what's currently done seems to revolve around assuming that stuff just randomly goes wrong with us for no particular reason—because our bodies, and Nature, and the whole process of evolution are stupid, I guess.. And then we're offered quick fixes: it might be a statin, or perhaps a D3 pill (yes, I've taken them: I just wonder, that's all), or a blood-pressure lowering medication ... and all the time it's not as simple as that. And if you think in terms of lowering (or raising) some number—perhaps merely because you _can_ measure it—then there will be bright people at a pharmaceutical company who can, and will, look into disrupting some pathway to do that. Everyone's happy … yet perhaps that might be worst thing to do. What the Zen people might call "monkey mind" at work—_mere_ cleverness.
>
> I don't know. Just some thoughts. But might cancer be some kind of adaptive response?

Yes Gagnrad, this thing of your 'body is out to get you' reminds me also of the whole idea aobut viruses. They are out to get us also, the will wheedle their way into our cells and propagate themselves at our expense. It's also a very 'military' metaphor which the USA instinctively relates to. The world is a battlefield and the strong one wins

I am more and more of the idea that there might not be ANY harmful viruses. And the whole rationalization behind vaccines would largely go away. Bacteria may be another matter but even there I would not think vaccines are the way to go. It is interesting how really 'broad' ways of thinking or metaphors really can skew 'science' in a particular direction and often quite badly IMHO

Viruses I think are just the way we exchange useful bits of genetic code with others and other species and within out own body also. Colds and flu's are 'good' for us they serve a very useful purpose and the idea of 'vaccinating' against them is silly again IMHO

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T00:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167695>

Seneff ideas are very similar to what I wrote in the Tensegrity 7 blog on my site. And yes I do think cancer is an epigenetic disease with an evolutionary purpose. I wrote a bit about that in the CPC evolutionary friend or foe blog on my site as well.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T00:04:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167696>

Viri are our spare parts and they change optical signaling in us to force genomic change when mitonuclear precision is a bad fit. This is why metastatic cancer travels in my opinion........to seek out mitochondria in places with a better match.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-12T08:17:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167774>

> [patgrif@hotmail.com said:](/goto/post?id=167689)
>
> Yes Gagnrad, this thing of your 'body is out to get you' reminds me also of the whole idea aobut viruses. They are out to get us also, the will wheedle their way into our cells and propagate themselves at our expense. It's also a very 'military' metaphor which the USA instinctively relates to. The world is a battlefield and the strong one wins

The way people instinctively think here in England, too. Possibly strongly imbedded in specifically modern European ways of thinking and possibly growing from some early-modern English ways of thinking, I shouldn't wonder. (Francis Bacon might have moved us in that way—and have been expressing something that was developing in culture, anyway. But I'm thinking aloud, and don't know intellectual history well enough.)

Interesting that the near-hysteria about raw milk seems to be limited to the _English-speaking_ countries (US, UK, Canada, Australia and New Zealand specifically). Apparently, there's just not the same attitude in France, or Italy, or anywhere else. And I know there are also economic reasons for anti-raw-milk: pasteurisation suits large dairy concerns. But I wonder whether it goes deeper. Wow! this is something _alive_ … can't have that.

When Ive thought about it, it seems very much to me that contemporary large-scale farming is like a form of warfare. I've heard other people make the same observation. I don't even just mean drenching crops in pesticides—a war on any organism that might take even a little. And doesn't work in the long run, because the farmer also poisons what would have functioned as the microbiome of the crops, so that they can never be really healthy.

Among other things I've read a description of sterilised grain, some kind of boiled-up mash, being delivered to animals, and they're petrified of infection coming in with the feed—which _is_ a real problem, because there's too many animals, too closely packed in there, and eating a deficient diet. But it only take a speck of the wrong type of yeast to blow in there and—boom!—it grows like wildfire and can put aflatoxin all through the feed. That will end up in the milk, and certainly pasteurising milk can't get rid of aflatoxins in it. At the end of the day, you just can't do it by "cleanliness" (really sterilisation) and what's ultimately a form of force not a working with nature. It's a broken model.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-12T08:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-cancer-have-a-function.15354/post-167775>

# Thread: EPA, DHA, Delta-6-Desaturase, and Mood
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rabu — 2015-06-12T03:14:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167717>

Over the last couple years I've been experimenting with Jack's ideas. I've experimented with a lot of dietary protocols and changing my light exposure cycles has made more of a difference for me than radically different diets.

But my question is about increasing DHA/EPA. I've noticed a huge change in mood (in the negative direction) after just a few days of supplementing with DHA/EPA. I thought it may be the supplemental form so I dropped it and gradually improved in a few days. Next I tried whole food sources and ended up with the exact same mood shift.

After a bit of research I've stumbled upon some info on delta-6-desaturase and how if someone isn't producing sufficient d-6-d fish oil competes for what little there is and causes the symptoms I experienced.

Has anyone else experienced this?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T04:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167731>

The first casualty in supplement marketing is usually the truth. Most people think they understand DHA on a physiologic level.........they dont because its biophysical abilities are what is key. I remarked once to a best selling author, if DHA was so limited why has it not been replaced once in 600 million yrs since eukaryotes showed up..........???? Crickets. Barry Sears was speechless when I handed him data.

He sells pills called supplements. That is why. I walked away without ever introducing myself. I learned all I needed to in that chance encounter.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T04:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167733>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20413162](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20413162)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T04:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167734>

you need a human pic up.........stat. Or this will be the last response FYI. New rule.

### Rabu — 2015-06-12T05:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167743>

I'm pretty much sold on DHA being critical. That's one of the reasons why the results of increasing it were so unexpected.

I dropped the supplement thinking maybe it was the problem, but had same results on fish/oysters.

I've repeated the results several times now and I'm fairly perplexed.

Looking around I've found a few clues that led back to D6D and "pyroluria" for people that respond negatively to fish oil.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T06:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167744>

Fish oil does not equal seafood.........the reason why is how it enters the brain and tissues. It is not unique in mammals because of mitochondria

### Rabu — 2015-06-12T06:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167750>

Agreed. Should have been more specific.

The only clues I have found that show negative results from increasing seafood or supplemental DHA were with people who had a D6D deficiency. Since DHA is such a big piece of the puzzle I'm wondering if anyone has experienced this and figured out a way to address it.

Since I've noticed the same issue on seafood without the supplement, I'm leaning towards thinking it's a problem in processing somewhere.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T15:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epa-dha-delta-6-desaturase-and-mood.15358/post-167810>

Most people perceive seafood or DHA as a negative because of the connotation people like Kresser and alternative practitioner's have given it..........When my patients use it things change and none of them attribute it to the DHA they seem to want to believe it was the surgery I did on them. I just smile.

# Thread: Alex's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arborescence — 2015-06-12T16:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/post-167819>

Hi guys,

I think that most of you know my story now but for those that don't I will try to make it short:

My name is Alex, I am french (so sorry if you don't understand me, I am not fluent in english, don't hesitate to ask me to reformulate) and 25 years old, I have discovered Dr. Kruse almost 2 months ago now and started applying his principles as much as I can (eating lot of seafoods, epi-paleo diet, doing a leptin reset (not ended yet), going out a lot in the sun and grouding, taking mitochondria supplements, mitigating nnEMF and blue lights, meditation and cardiac coherence/coherent breathing).

I've always had a bit of a weak constitution, with lot of deformities and thin bones (despite being tall, 6'5), constipation, GERD and bad ADHD (so it is hard to read Dr. Kruse blogs and understand the whole picture even if little by little it's starting to make more sense thanks to your kindness and patience guys), low libido and maybe a bit of a OCD, schizoid personality disorder and/or asperger syndrom.
5 years ago I was working hard (8 hours/day and sometimes more without days of rest) to become a professional drum player and it was working very well, but unfortunately one night after my working session my world got upside down, my heart started to beat very fast and bp to rise very high over the roof and my body became cold as ice, it was really creepy and I ended up in the emergency room, they found nothing and it calmed after 2 hours so I went back to my home. I forgot to mention that at the time I was smoking weed often to soothe my muscles (but stopped since 4 years because it triggers the same symptoms) and help with a feeling of anhedonia I was feeling since I was 13 years old.
I figured out later that I had a tetany seizure this night, and that I had a lot of signs that something was not going well before (tired, etc). My lymph nodes where very swollen and hard like rock and it has not changed since then.
I stayed during 1 year with these seizures almost h24 and very tired, with very cold hands and red crust on my hands. After 1 year of saying that everything was okay, the doctors discovered that I had Lyme disease so I took 1 year of antibiotics as I was not improving at all. It helped just a bit and the tetany seizures, but destroyed my digestion (and I still have hard constipation issues, addicted to enemas everyday). Then I stayed one year like that without being able to do anything and quite spaced out and depressed.
I started to do a lot of research seeing that the MDs where not taking my case seriously and that this science was in fact a lot less advanced that what I thought (and controlled by ego and money problems), and eventually I stopped gluten, tried eating low IG foods. It improved a bit some symptoms like my tetany/spasmophily and I took a lot of supplements that people on the health field are advising in France, it helped me feel a bit less tired but eventually my liver started to have a lot of spasms in reaction to fat (fish oils and oils) and to become unable to digest fats and I became more and more tired/apathic.
Then after some time I discovered Raw vegan foods and tried it for 1 year and half, at first it improved some of my symptoms (maybe because I stopped glycation, grains, bad fats and had a lot of antioxydants and coherent water) but after some times my brain power dropped a lot (it was already not that high) and I was left in total apathy with no energy.
And now I have discovered Dr. Kruse and stopped raw vegan foods and I am doing what I told you.
You can read more here: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...s-following-dr-kruse-method-in-england.13802/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hello-any-practitioner-that-is-following-dr-kruse-method-in-england.13802/)
By the way I am not in England anymore but back in France, will start and IT school next year, not the best choice I know but it was the only option I had.

So let's recap my actual symptoms:

\- Deformities and craking and joints. Spasms in the solar plexus/stomach (and always bloated with constipation, despite my stools are quite normal on the bristol chart stool) and it is difficult to breathe (my VO2 max is very low and always was). I feel sometimes that I have some spasms near the heart area or that is not working very well.
\- Not able to do sport or play drums because I feel bad afterwards (my BP rises and I hear "motor sounds" when it happens, my dark circles under the eyes are getting darker) and I am not flexible enough for that.
\- I was emaciated on the raw food thing but now I am almost a bit chubby (storring fat in the belly and around the waist and the but) despite the fact that I am on a leptin rx and taking curcuma, carnitine and resveratrol.
\- Spider veins, it is quite a shame for me at my age and I really wish to get rid of these (and they are in embarrassing locations). Very dark/purple circles under the eyes mixed with some spider veins and purple veins.
\- Swollen and hard lymph nodes (since the beginning of my issues).
\- ADHD and anhedonia. Bad memory, difficulties to remember my dreams.

Supplements I'm taking:

\- Resveratrol
\- Acetyl L-carnitine
\- Magnesium malate
\- PQQ
\- Ubiquinol
\- Align probiotic

A typical day:

\- Getting up at 8am (sometimes a bit later, rarely earlier, I don't know wether that good and if I should get up with the sun), taking my supplements, eating 8 or 9 eggs and taking the sun from 9 to 11am
\- Eating at 12am
\- Eating at 4 or 5 pm
\- Going to bed at 10:30-11pm

I'm eating a lot of proteins because I don't really love the fat (coconut oil, duck fat, ...), sometimes around 140g a day of proteins. It is hard to get some good quality wild seafood and meat but I am trying to do my best. I am bit concerned to not eat enough vegetables, I am eating an avocado almost everyday and a bit of cucumber and sometimes salad but that is all, some cucumber and sometimes juicing (celeri juice with cucumber, parsley and turmeric/ginger).

On the Facebook some people told me that my deformities and some other issues (like the spasms and heart problems) might be a Marfan syndrome, a genetic disease so I will try to get tested for as soon as possible. I don't have a lot of money as I am not working so it is a bit hard to afford a lot of testing (but I am ready to do as much as possible) and I don't know the equivalents in France. I did a 23andme test and will get the results soon, will post it here.

So, I have several questions and need advices and help for the things I will work on the following months:

\- I have a feeling that my ADHD, apathy and anhedonia are related to low calchecolamine, testosterone, endorphins (or bad endorphin sensitivity) and nitric oxyde (low libido) because when I eat a lot of onions and black pepper I feel slightly better. I believe that I have endorphin problems too because I don't feel orgasms at all.
\- I am a bit affraid of getting defficiencies (potassium, calcium, vitamin C) with all the meat (phosphorus) I am eating, it is hard to get whole fish with bones for the calcium so I don't know.
\- I need advices on blood test and devices to get (leds, ionizer, magnetic pad, ...), I am in an area with some nnEMF and will start an IT school next year, I don't have any other choice for now and want to organize myself to be in the better shape possible and continue to improve my condition...

So far I have already started to have a bit of improvements:

\- Normal perspiration again.
\- Feeling hungry again (I was eating machinalement) and no more undigested food in my stools.
\- Sleeping patterns really improved and starting to dream a bit again (mostly very cloudy ones).
\- Attention is slightly better, especially when I am eating onions and black pepper.

I am higly motivated to get myself out of that crap and even attain optimal health even if it takes ages, I am ready to build it step by step.
Sorry for the big post and thanks for the reading!
Thank you guys and see you on my journal for optimal !

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T17:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/post-167832>

DHA........and avoid bad light environments drink lots of water and read more and take less supplements.
Examine the past, take from it, leave it there. The real work in life's currents is to break the rules we rigidly establish for ourselves and subtract the dogma from nature's rules.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T17:32:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/post-167833>

Modern science is not what people claim it to be. It is top down, and authoritarian. Go too far off the beaten path, and you get slapped.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T17:38:36+0300

### b612 — 2015-06-12T18:26:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/post-167838>

Man... I feel your pain. My condition is very similar to yours.
My GERD (after 13 years of torture) disappeared after I started squat pooping  , sleeping on firm surface and lots of squatting in general during the day. Google squat heartburn and constipation  Plus, I eat a lot of gelatin and bone broth and I take HCI betaine to increase stomach acidity. I used to think I was allergic to gluten and milk but I wasn't... Correct spine and muscle alignment is even more important (at least in my case).
Try to eat lots of nutritious foods like liver, brain and heart meats.
Regarding your deformities, read Weston Price " Nutrition and physical degeneration". There is a free PDF version online. You may find the answer there...
Did you wear braces?
Where in France are you?

### Arborescence — 2015-06-15T14:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexs-journal.15396/post-168245>

Thank you Jack for taking the time to answer to everyone, you are so kind and I know it must be hard to cope with being a surgeon and doing so much work, read all the negative stuff about people loosing their health.
I need to become stronger and independant with all the informations you give, find my n=1, I am really trying to learn a lot of things from your blog, forum and FB group and the books you advise, it's hard with the ADHD but I am giving at least 6 hours a day of my time learning these stuff and understanding it, I will keep you all updated in this journal with my journey to optimal .

@[b612](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/15915/) Oh thank you for all these informations . I sent a PM to you!

# Thread: Holy shit it's hot

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-06-12T19:34:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-shit-its-hot.15399/post-167844>

It's only 22C outside and I'm already dying. I'm so fucking hot I think I'll pass out. My forearms are hot, yet when I touch it with my palm it feels normal. My face is burning, but if I touch it it feels normal (well I guess my palms are same temp as other bodyparts...). My head is pounding and feel really lethargic. But I'm not sweating a lot which is weird. Is that supposed to happen? Should I not do CT because it will boost my metabolism and make me create this much heat I can't stand a hot weather without doing more CT? It's not even that hot yet and I feel like I'm in a sauna.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-12T23:24:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-shit-its-hot.15399/post-167861>

Is this for real?

-   CT all day every day shrinks the respiratory proteins to improve tunneling. This also affects the MINOS which is the sea around the respirtory proteins to make a larger EZ. Larger EZ = more electrons flowing and it also keeps the charge high on the the ECT chain to allow chemiosmosis to work to exclude protons. When you exclude protons in the matrix you general 30 million volts on the ECT.

-   That is what keeps you well

    Put up real human pic or this will likely be your last post here. New rule. And I think this post is horseshit but I will give the benefit of the doubt for now.

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-06-13T11:14:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-shit-its-hot.15399/post-167944>

Well first of all, yes it's real. I stand to gain nothing by just making it up. Sorry that I didn't have a human picture, didn't ever plan to post here, I just lurk around because I don't really understand anything you talk about so I figured I'd never have anything to pitch in.

But yeah, yesterday was weird as hell, I felt straight up ill once it was bedtime, and only thing I could think of was that I was creating so much heat cause of the CT. Woke up this morning and everything is 100% normal now.

Anyway, thanks for the reply, I have no idea what respiratory proteins, tunneling, minos, EZ or ECT chain are _(do you really assume that I do, after asking such a "horseshit" question? lol)_, but I take it my kinder garden-level hypothesis is incorrect and CT is not only not the cause, but should actually help withstand more heat(?).

the heat issue can be directly related to the way the two genomes in you connect. You get your mitochondrial DNA from mom only. You get your nuclear DNA form mom and Dad. They need to connect properly like two gears and when they cannot........you get unusual temperature and sweating patterns.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T18:21:52+0300

What is not esoteric in the deep discussions of biology? Natural truth bombs. FFA's release what during cold? Protons from fat, is that answer. CT controls CO2. Did you know that? CO2 is another signaling compound that gets electrons to the place where they need to go. CO2 has no protons in it, but yet is it acid. Why? Because its presence controls what protons (pH) can and can’t do by controlling electrons within your atomic lattice. Science is astounding when you get it.

Fascinating stuff science can be.....real science usually is. The stuff we used to think is true just need to be refurbished. The examples are in journals books and video’s, but the key is making sure the ideas in you mind also keep up with that pace of discovery. More often than not beliefs lag behind the leading edge of science and in that lag is where paradigms become established. Most people beliefs about their world chain them to paradigms.

Is it worth the trouble or just stick to a CT session or 2.

# Thread: Hey there, everybody
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-there-everybody.15402/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### oliverg — 2015-06-12T23:57:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-there-everybody.15402/post-167864>

Hey guys,

My name's Oliver. I was born, raised, and live today in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. I'm excited to be a part of this community and look forward to learning as much as I can to correct my, and my loved ones' health.
I don't know if it's appropriate to post my story here, or ask questions, but I'm going to go ahead and do so. I hope to also learn how to act correctly on this forum. I have not read all the Dr Kruse's blog posts, and I am getting on with that, as quickly as I can. I'm a very busy guy, so it's not easy at the moment. I have listened to all the podcasts Jack has been on, loved the material.

Ok, so... I'm 21, and have had eczema ever since I was a few months old. For most of my life, it was very intense, around my inner elbows and ears, especially. I have always been super fatigued (lazy), and had regular headaches and migraines, allergies, etc. I was somewhat overweight, nothing shocking, but far from pleasant. I couldn't do much else than read, draw, watch movies, and eat. I've always had a never ending appetite. I will eat until I feel like I'm going to burst. It was rather stressful socially.
I never ate especially bad. My parents always tried to feed me mostly just good meats, veggies, fish, rice, etc, but I was obsessed with food and always managed to get fries, ice cream, candy, chocolate, dairy, etc. It was only when I was around 15 that I realized how depressed and unhealthy I felt, mostly because I noticed how difficult it was to stay attentive and present around people in highschool. My attention was mostly taken up by the physical pain I was in basically 24/7.
I started trying to exercise, and read up on diet. The first thing, after eating Eat Right 4 Your Type (blood type diet), I cut out nightshades, bread and dairy, which weren't easy to part with, but I got quite used to it. I lost a lot of weight, but I was skinny fat, felt disproportionate, and even though my eczema etc lessened, they remained and stayed pretty bad. I continued to binge eat no matter how much willpower I could muster, and despite everything, I know I have powerful determination if I really want something. This my biology taking over.
Around 17 I went vegetarian, then vegan, then fruitarian. I was happy at first, starting in summer, probably because I could eat near infinite amounts of sweet fruits and look like i was still in shape. But the eczema started getting worse and worse. It took two years, by the second winter, to realize something wasnt right. My girlfriend at the time couldnt stand me being up all night scratching myself. I was wearing bandages around my arms to reduce the irritation.
I did a lot of reading and ended up in the paleo sphere. I didn't trust meat, but then I read about raw meat eating. May sound extreme, but I was ready for anything at this point. I had some raw salmon and immediately felt better. I started reducing fruits and saw that my symptoms would reduce.
Anyways, now I can basically not tolerate any kind of cooked foods, and anything plant based (pretty much the only plant foods I do eat occasionally are some lettuce, coconut oil, spices), they make me feel tired, lethargic, itchy (especially my scalp), and generally uncomfortable in my body. I basically just eat raw beef fat and marrow, which are the only things that fill me up, and small amounts of fish, shellfish, organ meats, and muscle meats, within the limit of 70 grams of protein/day. I exercise most days, things like pushups, chinups, planks, grip exercises, handstands, for 30 seconds on/1.5-2 minutes off, for 30-60 minutes. I work from home on the computer, at a standing desk most of the time, sometimes sitting, where I can do some stretches, sometimes I juggle a bit. I get up around 6 or 7 am, and get to bed around 9 30 - 10 30. When I can, I go for long walks, usually barefoot in the grass.
I'm so much better than I've ever been, I have good muscle mass, and something like 11-13% body fat, which is nice (5'9.5" tall, 160 pounds), but most of my fat collects around my butt and thighs, which I don't think is a good sign for a man, and I still feel like something is wrong. I don't feel as lively and strong as I feel like I should, a lot of the time, I know there's a lot more to do, and I have so many questions, still.
For a while now, I've been trying to follow Stephanie Person, from Youtube's, advice, but whenever I eat anything cooked, and to a lesser degree just any kind of plant food, I start getting itchy, get binge cravings, etc, etc.
So, of course, I'm working on eventually having a home outside of the city, and eventually purchasing all kinds of EMF protective gear, like the magnetico pad. Also, I drink all my water out of a Berkey filter. I turn the lights down at least an hour before bed and try to do a little breath work. I'll be living in the country for a few months starting this july, which is exciting for my health.
My goals include: living a long, healthy, active life (I love moving when have the energy, especially climbing, surfing, drumming, etc..), put on as much muscle as possible, having the least amount of body fat, stay mentally sharp, happy, and build a good life for me and those in it, etc, etc. I would also love to be able to tolerate more foods, because I do miss eating a lot, but maybe that's just an unhealthy craving.
So, I will try to limit my questions, because I know I will probably get some answers when I read the blogs, but that will take a while, and after 21 years of frustration, I really want as many answers as quick as possible. Btw, I hope to contribute to the forums, not only ask questions. I've developed my own insights on health, and though they may not be complete, we all have to share, not only take.
Ok, here: at the moment, I've been trying to eat mostly beef fat and bone marrow, something like 300g of fat and any marrow I can get, to get enough calories in, and about 150 grams of wild fish, usually cod, mackerel, sardine or salmon. I heard that there is some correlation between more fish meaning we should eat less saturated fats, but if I eat less fats, I'm left hungry and tired, due to a lack of calories, and if i eat a whole lot more fish to compensate, the protein becomes excessive, which I react badly to, and I hear is tough on us, especially over 120 grams a day (me, its more like 65/70grams). I'm sure there's plenty I don't understand yet, and I know I don't exist in an optimal environment, but along with my work, and music, its a major priority, and I'm also working towards moving to the best possible environment there is.
If anyone has any insights, thoughts, ideas, I'll take them all. All those who know me will concur that I am open to all kinds of ideas, and will test them extensively before even developing a shadow of an opinion. I hope I can help others the best I can as well.

Thanks, everybody!
lots of love

### Vinje — 2015-06-13T03:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vinjes-journal.15403/post-167886>

Finally my optimal journal starts. I've been putting this off for a long time but here I am. A little about me and why I want to start a journal, I'm 23 and have been battling depression and sugar addiction for at least the past 3 years. I've been fed sugar since i was shot out of my mother and I realized i was depressed a few years ago and I can't stop eating sugar. Ive tried BAB and have gone sugar free for about 3 weeks but then I decide to reward myself and I spiral out of control. Food is constantly on my mind, whether I'm eating epi-paleo or stuffing my face with donuts, candy, or whatever else.

A little context about my environment until now, I grew up in a suburb of minneapolis of about 60,000 people before going to college in duluth, minnesota. While in duluth, I lived in 3 houses with mold problems and more than 1 smart meter on each house. I never noticed any acute symptoms but I've felt a decline in my health and depressive symptoms over the past few years. It was during my junior year when I admitted to myself that I was depressed and I thought food was the cause of it so I tried to eat as clean as I could but I would have a cheat day at least once a week. Since I thought food was the problem, my consumption of bad foods kept increasing since I'd keep telling myself that I’d quit cold turkey tomorrow. Well, tomorrow hasn't come for more than a few weeks at a time before I go off the deep end and I find myself visiting the local gas station everyday to grab whatever carb source i can find.

I'm to a point now where I understand that I'm a sugar addict and I can control the cravings during the day but when 5pm hits, I cave in almost everytime. I eat a big breakfast and fish almost everyday so most of my day I eat quality food. I've done the most intense Ct you can do (30 minutes in 33 degree water) so cold adaption isn’t a problem.

Currently I live in a suburb of Minneapolis and I’m not moving until I go to brazil for a few months starting in august. We have wifi, a smart meter, tvs on at night. My depression hasn’t been getting worse at all but the thing that sent me over the edge and forced me to write this is I’ve been waking up with numb hands every morning for the past month. I’m guessing this is a EMF issue. I need help and I’m asking for any tips I can use until I go to brazil to live in the middle of nowhere for a month. I know that’s where most of my healing will take place but I need some advice on what to start doing before I leave. I’m afraid the depression, sugar addiction, and numb hands are going to lead to my death so I need any help I can get. Sorry if everything sounds sporadic. Let me know if you need any more details. Thanks guys, you all kick fucking ass!

I have my labs from over a year ago attached

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vinjes-journal.15403/post-194274>

Its been a long time and there's a lot to write but ill keep it short. I traveled in brazil for 75 days and ALL my ailments went away. I was in the sun barefoot constantly plus i did a ton of mediation as well as the wim hof method(which works wonders) and ate a ton of seafood and fresh fruit/veggies. Quitting sugar instantly became easy as soon as I got there. Must be all the UV. I was a success story! Well now I'm back in the states, living in colorado springs, annnnndddd all my ailments came back. Depression, Binging on food, numb hands and feet. This transition was all most instant. As soon as I landed back in the states I spiraled out of control. Now I'm back to where I was but I just purchased a ticket to mexico and I'm staying indefinitely (hopefully at least 2 months). I know wha tneeds to be done to get well again. TONS of sun, seafood, the ocean, good water and good vibes. I will to be a success story again. And tips or recommendations would be greatly appreciated

### Vinje — 2016-07-19T21:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vinjes-journal.15403/post-198622>

alright i'm back. I made it to mexico in a small surf town called San Blas and WOW! Instantly my craving for any kind of processed, sugary food is gone immediately. Literally as soon as I land there is zero cravings. I went to the beach to detox and jump in the ocean and a vendor brought me raw oysters and a coconut. I continued this for the next 12 days, eating a ton of seafood, some fresh fruits and fruit juices, tons of sun (especially in the morning), wim hof method every morning, grounding most of the day, being around great human beings, and just loving life. Loving life is great after dealing with my gray existence of depression and addiction. Both are gone. Buuutt then a nuke was dropped on my reality, my dad passed away on june 18th. I instantly flew back to minnesota to be with my family and to bury my dad. Man, one thing I really learned during this time is that love, supports, and especially hugs are very therapeutic. I can feel people sending their energy to me as a gift and it really is a powerful medicine. I had to stay in minnesota, in a place where I knew my depression would come back and it did, but I had things to take care of. Other issues flared back up like occasional sugar craving, brain fog, and the lack of emotions. Now I'm back in San blas Mexico and i'm continuing right where I left off. I've only been here for four days and I still have a little bit of depression but no carb cravings. I'll be here for a month and i expect massive improvements. I'm staying in a hut 20 feet away from the ocean with not a single emf reading in the entire cabin unless i turn the fan on but its a very small reading and only extends out six inches. I jump in the ocean first thing upon waking, do the wim hof method, and stare into the sun for a few minutes. I will beat this awful disease and I will empower people to heal their own ailments. Any suggestions or recommendation would be extremely helpful. I'm on a good path but there's always more to learn and implement

You have already got lots of good advice and experiences.
Mostly you already know what you need.

This is my review of your blood test.
\--------------------

Cortisol is not bad.
If sample taken in the early morning, looks not bad, could be better.
Good first step in checking Circadian Rhythm
\-------------------------------------
Insulin, HgA1c ok
Gucose lowish (if you feel need, eating sugar is ok)

\-----------------------------------------
low testosterone, LH, FSH, low IGF-1
pituitary is not responsive
have you been hit in the head?
If no head trauma, good idea to get pituitary MRI

Your avatar shows martial art.
Lots of opportunity for a head trauma.
\-------------------------------------
Low EPA
High DHA
very low AA

You may already have some of the neolitic diseases that @[Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/) it talking about
Get better testing in this area.
\-------------------------------------
Good vit D
it is ok to get more
\-------------------------------------
T3 and FreeT3 are low

supplemnt with selenium and Lugol's solution
recheck after few months
if still low may need NTH Natural Thyroid Hormone supplementation.
\----
ReverseT3 high
that means that your body thinks you are making too much T4

Your thyroid system looks suspect,
lets hope that selnium and Lugol's will do the trick.

If RT3 insists at being high, rather than NTH you may want to use straight T3.

\--------------------------------------
Homocysteine highish.
Possibly you are missing on micronutrients.
Good idea to do Spectracell micronutrient analysis

# Thread: Daina's New Life Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daina — 2015-06-13T08:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-167919>

Hi Everyone,
**Early Life**
My name is Daina and I have started my journey to a new type of life. I have been on a journey for the last 10 years it seems to find out who the person is in the mirror that I don't recognize and why my body does not do what it should anymore. I am many things. I am a wife, but I am on this lifestyle journey solo. I am a mom of 5, and step-mom to four. Of the five that I am a mom to, I have birthed 2 and took into my heart 3. Their ages are: 33, 28, 24, 17, and 16. Only the 16 year old is still at home. The step children are also grown and gone. I was diagnosed as an arthritic in high school. I am black, 5'3", pear shaped, heavy thighs, tummy, back fat, neck rolls. I last weighed 172 on my Wii in April.

I am the 3rd of 5 children. My mama has FM, PN, COPD, spinal stenosis with surgery in early 2014 and poor health condition. My dad recently had a stroke (last year), has a defib, used to walk 5-8 miles per day, 4 days per week, prior to the stroke. Both were previous smokers. I can remember their bedroom looking like a smoke filled bar. I have suspected he is a celiac. Now has blindness in one eye and weakness on one side. Walks with a walker and is really deteriorating. My oldest sister, who would have been 5 years older than me, has already passed away from ovarian cancer. She may have also had bipolar issues. History of fibroid tumors, childless, tilted uterus, miscarriages. She passed away in 2004. My oldest brother is 3 years older than me. He is self medicating with marijuana, bi-polar and unable to hold a permanent job. A little bit of a vagrant with a big heart. Then there is me. I was a baby that had her days and nights mixed up. Mama says her and her sisters had to take turns rocking me; i was dropped a few times, . I was colicky. Back then they gave you paragoric. I have a younger brother 2 years younger than me, and another one 9 years younger than me.

I was a small baby and called 'dainty'. Turned into skinny. I was always skinny. Not a big eater. And a bit picky on the eating. Mama didn't let us know that we were considered poor. We had a hot meal for dinner everyday, usually small piece of meat, vegetables as much as you wanted, and rice. We had so much rice i thought we were asian. Not a lot of sweets. There were 5 kids and usually 2 adults so a cake or dessert on Sunday was gone by Tuesday of that week. Breakfast was usually oatmeal or cereal. Lactose intolerant so killed cereal and breakfast early. We were allowed to make eggs for ourselves too. I can remember bologna sandwiches (good times), and butter and sugar sandwiches (bad times). We didn't get a lot of pasta, that was for rich folk. Not much of potatoes either, unless it was in potato salad. Lots of oranges and grapefruit, as I am from Orlando, Florida. Watermelons in summer. I can remember early on, maybe 7 or 8 years old, being bent over in the bathroom cluthing my stomach after a meal in pain. Intermittent. I never figured out what triggered it. I just thought i was the 'sickly' child that everyone picked on. Homelife was not always fun or peaceful.

We played outside in the sunshine. I was a girl scout that went to away camp for two weeks for at least 3 years. Loved it when I would get there. Outside was magical, even with the mosquitoes. Didn't always fit in, as I didn't have a regular girl scout troop. I think it was an outreach for inner city girls; somehow my mom helped me get in. I have never been able to float in water. I can tread water, and I can swim some to keep from drowning, but i cannot float. Lower body just sinks.

I took one year of piano lessons in second grade and still play to this day. Mom sacrificed so that the girls could have music lessons and the boys played little league baseball. I started french horn in second grade and occasionally will play it; i play trumpet, which are similar fingering for french horn, just using the other hand for keys. I am not a virtuoso, but I do enjoy communing with God with piano music and playing my horn with other musicians. I played tennis in jr high and high school. I was in the marching and symphonic band from elementary school through high school. I went to FL A&M University and marched in the Marching 100 for 2 1/2 years. When I left college with my degree in Information Technology, I weighed 102-105 pounds. I had begun to eat like other college students and really introduced junk then. Still was having bouts of stomach pain. Thought it was gastroenteritis. Could never pinpoint it. Had soft porous teeth as a child, no dental insurance. Ended up with many cavities and needed root canals by college. Was afraid of the dentist due to a botched tooth extraction at about 10 years old. Took me until my first professional job to really spend time at a dentist for cleanings and repair work. Used to get gas for cleanings i was so afraid.

I took in my first child, a daughter who was 3, a friend of the family, when i was 22 years old. Our family just seems to do this for the good of some child. My arthritis really kicked up my first year of post college. Walked with a cane. Got a job at a good local company. Was on the swing shift running computer reel tapes, then the graveyard shift running reel tapes. Did that for about a year. Got my first salaried job in data management. Got married and on doctor's recommendation tried to have my first birth child, in case my condition got worse and i may not be able to carry. Apparently under a whole lot more stress than I realized. The marriage lasted about a year and a half. Had my first birth child at 24. July baby, 29 hours of labor, given pytocin to progress labor. Baby girl born 6 lbs 9 ounces. Healthy and beautiful, but turned colicky and gassy for 3 months. My heaviest weight during first pregnancy: 129. Breast fed her for six-nine months. Went back to work after maternity leave of 8 weeks.

Eager to read more

### Lahelada — 2015-06-13T15:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-167955>

Hello Daina ,
welcome and great introduction . We grew up in different surroundings but we have things in common . I never leaned to swim at school .They gave me a belt for floating and the belt staid on the surface. I just folded up like a pocket knife.Picky eater too but I did have fads. I loved vinegar even at the tender age of 5 . Did you have any thgat stick out? I also was a starch eater - potatoes of course in Germany. grandma was always so afraid that they would not last( the potatoes sold in wintertime were deemed subpar) that she bunkered 600 lbs of potatoes in the cellar . We were not poor but always acted as if we were as my Gran had lived through the war and had never forgotten shortages so our cellar always looked like a grocery store.

Welcome ! looking forward to hearing more of your story

### Daina — 2015-06-13T16:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-167960>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=167925)
>
> Welcome Daina! Daina means "a song" in Lithuanian language! Beautiful name.

Well! I am already learning something new! I never knew that! Thank you! My mom had a thing for unique names for her children. She liked Dana Andrews, but wanted a different spelling. I can't wait to tell her what she stumbled on, as she was a classically trained pianist! lol! She will get a kick out of that I am sure. Thank you Lahelada! I loved vinegar on my canned spinach, even when young, never knew why. And on fries (in the summer she would stock up on big bags of fries for the 'fry magic' machine. we were somewhat modern, lol) My grandpa lived through the depression and supported his brothers and sisters even when he was very young (like 10), and his property had a room that was always stocked with canned goods, especially sardines, which i detested the smell of, but having had 7 children, had prepared for the eventuality that he may have to feed them and their families in another depression. Had at least 2 freezers of meat. His children, if they ever fell on hard times would go to his complex and he would just give them food.

### Daina — 2015-06-13T17:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-167974>

**Young Adult Life**
I had accomplished graduation from college as the first child in my family to do so. My mom had gone back to college when i was in high school and finished her degree in early childhood education; we both graduated during the year of 1979. Me from high school and her from college. My mom was a great teacher, I can remember that she always had us working hard in the house and around the house during school breaks, but also encouraging us to read the newspaper, draw, paint, and create. We put on plays and skits to entertain ourselves and family members and when she was a kid, they did the same. I tried to do the same for my kids, but wasn't nearly as good at it as she was, lol.

Getting my first salaried job got me out of the freezing computer room(looking back that might have been a good thing, as well as working in freezers when I was a food server at Wendy's and a waitress at Perkins), but i was on my feet the entire shift, which aggravated the knees and joints. The reel tapes where carried looped onto the arm, as they had metal holes for the cores (metal conducts cold? duh?) But I didn't stay there long and never gave it a lot of thought. Just knew I needed to get out. During the first year of the salaried job, the marriage disintegrated, and we parted on amicable terms, somewhat. Eventually moved back home to regroup my life. I have never moved out of Orlando once returning from college in Tallahassee, so it was a cross town move for me and my girls. Forgot to mention, Dad was a functioning alcoholic. But who really functions with that, right? Stayed home for a year, regrouped, and started going to church at the family church. Was raised Lutheran. Mom was the organist, (dad never attended back then), but wanted gospel music included in the service to reflect our culture. I knew I needed the help of the underpinnings of structure, morality, and fellowship for my girls. And I needed to emotionally recover. I needed a Savior. Just my own personal story. My heart had already been bruised by a broken high school relationship, not so good college relationship, and now a broken marriage. I was going 90 miles an hour but not really resolving or repairing anything.

I became involved with the music and began helping with the choir and singing. Met my second husband there at church. He was a visitor to our small Lutheran church and began to attend. He was a professional musician, and it seemed we had a lot in common. My mom kind of pushed us together. Side note: Following this marriage, which produced my second birth child, a son, I determined that my mom's man picker was broken and I could no longer dutifully depend on her to have any relationship sense. My son was born when I as 29. He was 8 pounds 1 ounce and looked like a fat faced Winston Churchill, lol. April baby. Complications were, kept losing the heart rate on the fetal monitor. Turns out that while he was doing back flips in my tummy, he got himself tangled in his umbilical cord around his neck. Both kids had their merconium stool prior to getting out. He was removed with forceps that left red bruising under his jaws temporarily; he recovered fine. He was breast fed for a year. She (mom) was banned from ever trying to 'set me up' ever again. He wasn't a bad man; actually he was quite gifted musically, intellectually and spiritually. He was a good Dad to my girls prior to our marriage and even afterwards. But we just didn't make it. Two smart people with competing goals of life. He had a bit of a freer life before marriage and kids, and just depending on himself. Instant family changed that, and I think it was a bit too much. And I expected a partnership, where we all worked hard and towards goals that we would pick. Yeah, just didn't happen that way. We were married for 6 1/2 years before he started an affair. My son was 3 when the divorce was final. At this point, I began to wonder again if I was just picking the wrong people, was I the wrong person, or was I sabotaging my personal life. Jury is still out.

As a smaller kid, I developed quite a mouth and attitude to go along with it, to keep people off my back. In corporate america, I had to learn to tone that down or I would be out of a job. I have worked at the same company for 30 1/2 years, with the last 15 being in information security. I work from home, but know that like any good thing, that can not last forever, so would like to get healthy and make use of this freedom at home while I can, before any situations change.

During these middle adult years, I was pretty active chasing my kids around, playing my horns in an alumni high school band, doing the mom thing of practices and shuttling children. I thought i was eating healthy and feeding my kids healthy. I developed a taste for pasta, we had a bread machine, loved pizza. The more i forced the bad foods, the more desensitized my system became and stopped sending me pain signals. Second husband grew up on a farm in Iowa, was handy, and kind of down homey. He was thin, and for a while I maintained my weight without too much effort. Until the second child. My highest weight for him was 139. Not too bad for a second pregnancy. After pregnancy I returned to about 115-120. I remember spouse not being happy with that size. He would ride his bike to work, sun himself (he was a sun bathing person), and I recall him asking me why didn't i just go to the gym. By this time I had a full-time job, 3 kids, church duties. I felt attacked and really unwanted. No woman wants to hear that from a spouse. We didn't last too long after that. More stress. Fatigue. Depression. Don't get me wrong, some of those early years were really beautiful. I married into a wonderful family and thought we would be all together forever, but it wasn't to be so. And that took a huge toll. He was 9 years older than me, and I figured he had it together; bad assumption. Never assume the age of a person shows their maturity.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-13T19:02:40+0300

### Daina — 2015-06-14T05:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-168036>

.

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=167985)
>
> You write very well. Feels like I know you...

Thank you Fitness! I have read a little CS Lewis while trying to find myself; recommended by 2nd husband. I think the Screw Tape letters were first, and then the Chronicles of Narnia, circa read during the 90's. Honestly, sometimes it was over my head, but now as I am older and more mature, I should go back and read those again, as the Screw tape letters are quite funny and relevant, and the chronicles of Narnia make more sense to me. I am sure he has other writings, however I am not an explorer. My tastes vary per year or decade. Because I wanted to find out true application, for a while I was a Joyce Meyers fan; she gives practical application of faith and I really needed that early in my walk. I could probably stand to go back and listen to her.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T15:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-168087>

I read this last night and had to ruminate upon it. It is clear your maternal line is the issue..........but the irony for me is that it appears from what you wrote that the mitonuclear connection may not be bad. It appears that the 0-6 yrs when you emerged from the vagina might have seriously altered that coaptation and set the tone. You siblings out come really are the big clue. Your mom's slow descent is also instructive. If you're not well versed in maternal mitochondrial DNA inheritance there is a quick overview of it at the end of Ubi 17. If you want to know more about it Read Nick Lane's book Power sex and suicide. If there are AI's in mom and in some of your siblings that would be a big clue that your early home environment (stressed to be kind) was a big factor on altering all of your trajectories.

Your Mom sounds eerily like my Mom. I think this is why I had to ruminate on it. Your story really parallels my own. You with early on set DJD in joints and me being a fat ass. Your Dad being a boozer also has a massive epigenetic effect as we are now finding out in epigenetic research. This could be the reason your coaptation was looser than we might have expected.......but since you have been in Fla I am struck how all your lives have been ground to a nub. You know better than me Orlando has changed massively in the last 50 yrs. It is now a mini NYC with tons of population density and tons of technology advancement.

Your music training will help you recover.......you need to play the piano a lot. I won't say more here but I have a whole series on music coming in future years when I get to them........you're mom intuition about music was spot on to help you all during your rearing. She may not have known why but her advice here was really really good. Mom's bad picker seems to have been instilled in you too. Remember who are you good for until you are good enough for yourself. Me personally I think this is why people relationships fail. They are looking for an outside savior when really a relationships is an inside job. When you get you right the universe bring another soul to you to compliment your transformation. Ask Caroline a bit about this..........she may not want to dive in deep but I think she knows a lot about this subject. Jury on you is not out........I know why you're here now. This place is the first step in your personal renovation to get you back on track. There won't be any saviors here but we will fill you with a lot of info and tools to help you undergo a successful optimization and not a sustained renovation over decades. You've been there and done that. Relationships for me, are a lot like mitonuclear coaptation........when things are bad within your mitochondria and nucleus and I see relationship problems beginning because the person is not capable of fully connecting with themselves much less someone else. Your floating issue is psychological. You need to get to a local YMCA and take a class on swimming and floating. This is the first homework I am giving you. Why? Girl you need CT more than me. It is going to be one of the keys for you. In Orlando there is an ice bar. You will be able to use it to help you cold adapt. You don't have to drink in the bar. Just ask for Pellegrino......use their bar to cold adapt fast. Then it will be to the water for you. Orlando is also close to Vero Beach where it is quiet.......great place to go into the ocean and walk on the beach. You need that. And this won't be just a summer time affair you're going to develop either. Yes.......I might force you to embrace discomfort in the beginning.

The Iowa guy.........like your mom might have been trying to instinctively tell you something your brain was not capable of hearing and instead your heart heard something else that caused emotions to rise cortisol to explode and things to disintegrate. When you restart you must be willing to erase everything that you are. Here is the interesting part I rarely talk about. Not all of you will extinguish itself. You have to be willing to release all that you are right now, to become all that you might be. The old must be torn down for the new to be raised. There is much that remains in us that was once true, right, and good; and that should get woven into the new; in fact, "our new" couldn't be what it is without the old. They real key is having the wisdom to know what to subtract to refurbish the soul. I think you might find some of that wisdom here.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T16:15:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-168092>

For the arthritis......you need to find out is it really an AI, inflammatory or just plain old OA. Second bit of homework is learning to breath during CT. [http://www.pnas.org/content/111/20/7379.full.pdf](http://www.pnas.org/content/111/20/7379.full.pdf)

[http://www.medicaldaily.com/natural...ercises-and-ice-baths-may-quell-immune-337890](http://www.medicaldaily.com/natural-inflammation-treatment-breathing-exercises-and-ice-baths-may-quell-immune-337890)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T17:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-168103>

Daina one last thing about CT and fear........wisdom destroys fears quick.

What is not esoteric in the deep discussions of biology? Natural truth bombs. FFA's release what during cold? Protons from fat, is that answer. CT controls CO2. Did you know that? CO2 is another signaling compound that gets electrons to the place where they need to go. CO2 has no protons in it, but yet is it acid. Why? Because its presence controls what protons (pH) can and can’t do by controlling electrons within your atomic lattice. Science is astounding when you get it.

Fascinating stuff science can be.....real science usually is. The stuff we used to think is true just need to be refurbished. The examples are in journals books and video’s, but the key is making sure the ideas in you mind also keep up with that pace of discovery. More often than not beliefs lag behind the leading edge of science and in that lag is where paradigms become established. Most people beliefs about their world chain them to paradigms.

> _**I don't know where to start in my mind. Thank you for taking the time to comment in my journal. I must digest and it may take a while. I must study the meaning of coaptation. Ubi 17 is tonights reading. Trying to remember the possibility of AI in my siblings and will have to think on that. I was considered the goody two shoes of the family, trying to be good, do the right thing; not a popular stance. One more clue, mother's mother died of alzheimers at 83. My mom is now 76. Mother's father passed away at 95.**_
>
> Your Mom sounds eerily like my Mom. I think this is why I had to ruminate on it. Your story really parallels my own. You with early on set DJD in joints and me being a fat ass. Your Dad being a boozer also has a massive epigenetic effect as we are now finding out in epigenetic research. This could be the reason your coaptation was looser than we might have expected.......but since you have been in Fla I am struck how all your lives have been ground to a nub. You know better than me Orlando has changed massively in the last 50 yrs. It is now a mini NYC with tons of population density and tons of technology advancement.
>
> _**My mama has been a huge influence(good) and cautionary tale(bad) in my life. It is extremely hard to unlearn behaviors and attitudes that are deeply ingrained. It feels like you are fighting against yourself. I experienced a lot of this during my 30s. And learning to be strong enough to tell her that I am not her, do not wish to think like her, but still love her dearly. And being willing through various stages to accept the possibility that at any time I express these things that she could opt to not be there for me. She has never walked away, but she too can't figure out why I am so different from my siblings. The joints are fine, the knees are fine. But whenever I treat myself to a massage, which isn't often, I can feel the knots in my muscles and in my back. Perpetual stress. I don't like what has become of Orlando, either. I grew up in a small town that just happened to have Disney World. It was small town and community oriented. Now not so much. If I had to drive in that everyday, I feel I would have probably been over the edge by now.**_
>
> Your music training will help you recover.......you need to play the piano a lot. I won't say more here but I have a whole series on music coming in future years when I get to them........you're mom intuition about music was spot on to help you all during your rearing. She may not have known why but her advice here was really really good. Mom's bad picker seems to have been instilled in you too. Remember who are you good for until you are good enough for yourself. Me personally I think this is why people relationships fail. They are looking for an outside savior when really a relationships is an inside job. When you get you right the universe bring another soul to you to compliment your transformation. Ask Caroline a bit about this..........she may not want to dive in deep but I think she knows a lot about this subject. Jury on you is not out........I know why you're here now. This place is the first step in your personal renovation to get you back on track. There won't be any saviors here but we will fill you with a lot of info and tools to help you undergo a successful optimization and not a sustained renovation over decades. You've been there and done that. Relationships for me, are a lot like mitonuclear coaptation........when things are bad within your mitochondria and nucleus and I see relationship problems beginning because the person is not capable of fully connecting with themselves much less someone else. Your floating issue is psychological. You need to get to a local YMCA and take a class on swimming and floating. This is the first homework I am giving you. Why? Girl you need CT more than me. It is going to be one of the keys for you. In Orlando there is an ice bar. You will be able to use it to help you cold adapt. You don't have to drink in the bar. Just ask for Pellegrino......use their bar to cold adapt fast. Then it will be to the water for you. Orlando is also close to Vero Beach where it is quiet.......great place to go into the ocean and walk on the beach. You need that. And this won't be just a summer time affair you're going to develop either. Yes.......I might force you to embrace discomfort in the beginning.
>
> _**I agree. It took me a long time to recognize that for many years I was disconnected from myself. I just wasn't connected. I was me, but being connected to the real me was very painful. Vulnerable was painful. Accessible was painful. If life happened to the 'me' that had the body but wasn't in the mind and heart of 'me', then 'me' could be protected. Took a while to get all the parts on the same page, and sometimes 'me' can/will disconnect, but getting better. I have heard of the ice bar and will start looking into it. I realized after reading about CT that I needed it. I am starting very slow. I slowly turn the shower to cold most days before getting out.(no frigid water here in Orlando, lol and i still go slow) I have gotten two ice packs that i use on my shoulders, neck, chest, stomach, thighs and back at various times of the day while working. I will get more. I sleep with them. Got the AC repaired; i had been having it 85 degrees during the daytime, but now keep it at least at 78 or lower.**_
>
> The Iowa guy.........like your mom might have been trying to instinctively tell you something your brain was not capable of hearing and instead your heart heard something else that caused emotions to rise cortisol to explode and things to disintegrate. When you restart you must be willing to erase everything that you are. Here is the interesting part I rarely talk about. Not all of you will extinguish itself. You have to be willing to release all that you are right now, to become all that you might be. The old must be torn down for the new to be raised. There is much that remains in us that was once true, right, and good; and that should get woven into the new; in fact, "our new" couldn't be what it is without the old. They real key is having the wisdom to know what to subtract to refurbish the soul. I think you might find some of that wisdom here.

_**Not sure I understand this first thought and sentence, but I will digest and think it through. I am not where I want to be, but I thank God I am not where I was. I keep pressing toward the mark. But the interesting thing is, if I am pressing spiritually, but not taking care of the temple that was given to me, then I cannot be optimal in my faith, nor optimal in my marriage, nor optimal as a parent or child. The cares of the world and the expectations of others as well as the scales of pain must be chipped away and melted from the mind to return to the essence of the person that my God foreknew and foresaw. Everything I have done and been through has led me to where I am right now. I got here by looking up a book of Suzanne Sommers that was recommended by my OB/GYN about bio identical hormones. I think you may have popped up in my search or in the amazon 'people are also looking at' and landed on your page. I have been browsing and ingesting blog articles for about 2 and half weeks now. I was a wheatbellyer, but boomeranged and then stalled. I have been questing for a long time. I am already thought of as weird, but that no longer bothers me. I can learn to embrace my differentness. My uniqueness. I had stopped playing music at home now for about a year on my piano. You are right that I need to restart. Working my hands and fingers and shoulders and engaging my mind and spirit is a uniquely fulfilling experience. And now it will be because I want to, not because I have to prepare music for the choir, as I stepped down as director last year. Again, thank you. I am amazed that we have parallels. Writing down my history and thoughts is both scary and freeing.**_

as for me ..... Jack told me a profound truth a year ago that took me to ground zero ............ and now, at my age, I am living the most incredible love affair.

The spot light needs to be uncomfortable. You are going to be tested in life........and I will test your endurance too. Ask anyone who has been here. What is contained in this site is fundamental to your health. We don't deal with nonsense here. In fact, if I sense someone is dealing in nonsense they are shown the door quick. People are down on what they’re not up on. You must endure a lot of ignorance when you swim upstream to build wellness. Life is not longer something to endure, but to live well. There's just not a better or shorter way. Sometimes there's only the hard way. Life really is a test. Our will gets tested, our courage gets tested, our strength gets tested. And our patience and endurance are tested daily to see if we will persist on the correct road or opt to stop for the idea of "the big easy". No journey has merit if it cannot test us. Endurance is the spirit which can bear things, not simply with resignation, but with a blazing hope. Get some.

I understand this. Thank you Caroline for your input and I agree. This is a real struggle and part of me that is unpacking right now. I once told a friend of mine that the most important word I learned was 'no'. Not to be mean, but I can't and won't be all things to all people because it isn't good for me.

But it seems that the next step in that area is to not just say no to things that I cannot or should not do, but to say yes to things that ultimately are for my good and taking care of myself. As we talk about my maternal environment, I definitely see these actions in my mom to martyr and sacrifice herself. Not healthy at all. And there is a constant pull in me, to do the same. It is tiring and exhausting to rewire those desires, with no way to replenish my mind and body. Until now. Thank you for sharing your experience and the outcome; I know that ground zero must be extremely difficult, because it sounds to me that you had an incredible outcome!

> What is not esoteric in the deep discussions of biology? Natural truth bombs. FFA's release what during cold? Protons from fat, is that answer. CT controls CO2. Did you know that? CO2 is another signaling compound that gets electrons to the place where they need to go. CO2 has no protons in it, but yet is it acid. Why? Because its presence controls what protons (pH) can and can’t do by controlling electrons within your atomic lattice. Science is astounding when you get it.
>
