### Daina — 2015-06-16T15:03:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dainas-new-life-journal.15421/post-168382>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=168092)
>
> For the arthritis......you need to find out is it really an AI, inflammatory or just plain old OA. Second bit of homework is learning to breath during CT. [http://www.pnas.org/content/111/20/7379.full.pdf](http://www.pnas.org/content/111/20/7379.full.pdf)
>
> [http://www.medicaldaily.com/natural...ercises-and-ice-baths-may-quell-immune-337890](http://www.medicaldaily.com/natural-inflammation-treatment-breathing-exercises-and-ice-baths-may-quell-immune-337890)

Hi Jack. Honestly, when I looked at the first link, my eyes crossed over with all the data and figures and I was intimidated so much, that I didn't even go to the second link. This morning, I put on my big girl pants and dove in, only to discover that low and behold, the data also had movies attached! minilink movies to the rescue! I was able to read the data and also view the movies contained within, which helped to tie the data together for me. So increasing and training in breathing techniques releases excess CO2 from the blood stream and seems to also release lactic acid, which builds up in the body, ie, the muscles? (seemed to remember reading long ago that those stress points in my back muscles are concentrations of lactic acid?) Anyway, I will be coming back to review this as i embark on my CT journey.

Since I survived the first article, (feeling kind of cheeky and brave, lol) I went to the second article about Wim Hof.
At first, i was thinking well, why didn't he put this one first? But I feel or believe that there is a method to the madness, such as wanting to know the info so that a person wades through to get what they need to know. It prepares the mind, even if you don't really understand everything that you are reading. Or maybe it just happened that way just cause. lol. Thank you so much.

> Your music training will help you recover.......you need to play the piano a lot. I won't say more here but I have a whole series on music coming in future years when I get to them........you're mom intuition about music was spot on to help you all during your rearing. She may not have known why but her advice here was really really good. Mom's bad picker seems to have been instilled in you too. Remember who are you good for until you are good enough for yourself. Me personally I think this is why people relationships fail. They are looking for an outside savior when really a relationships is an inside job. When you get you right the universe bring another soul to you to compliment your transformation. Ask Caroline a bit about this..........she may not want to dive in deep but I think she knows a lot about this subject. Jury on you is not out........I know why you're here now. This place is the first step in your personal renovation to get you back on track. There won't be any saviors here but we will fill you with a lot of info and tools to help you undergo a successful optimization and not a sustained renovation over decades. You've been there and done that. Relationships for me, are a lot like mitonuclear coaptation........when things are bad within your mitochondria and nucleus and I see relationship problems beginning because the person is not capable of fully connecting with themselves much less someone else. Your floating issue is psychological. You need to get to a local YMCA and take a class on swimming and floating. This is the first homework I am giving you. Why? Girl you need CT more than me. It is going to be one of the keys for you. In Orlando there is an ice bar. You will be able to use it to help you cold adapt. You don't have to drink in the bar. Just ask for Pellegrino......use their bar to cold adapt fast. Then it will be to the water for you. Orlando is also close to Vero Beach where it is quiet.......great place to go into the ocean and walk on the beach. You need that. And this won't be just a summer time affair you're going to develop either. Yes.......I might force you to embrace discomfort in the beginning.
>
> The Iowa guy.........like your mom might have been trying to instinctively tell you something your brain was not capable of hearing and instead your heart heard something else that caused emotions to rise cortisol to explode and things to disintegrate. When you restart you must be willing to erase everything that you are. Here is the interesting part I rarely talk about. Not all of you will extinguish itself. You have to be willing to release all that you are right now, to become all that you might be. The old must be torn down for the new to be raised. There is much that remains in us that was once true, right, and good; and that should get woven into the new; in fact, "our new" couldn't be what it is without the old. They real key is having the wisdom to know what to subtract to refurbish the soul. I think you might find some of that wisdom here.

Hi Jack, so for my first homework assignment, it was 'get thyself to a YMCA for floating and swimming'. I checked the local Y schedule, and the next set of classes begin sometime in July. This gives me an opportunity to arrange the life schedule and probably take the 16 yo dancer with me, before she goes off to dance classes on saturday. Modeling behavior. Or maybe i will just go by myself as the thing to do strictly for me. (putting my oxygen on first).

It is going to be one of the keys for you. In Orlando there is an ice bar. You will be able to use it to help you cold adapt. You don't have to drink in the bar. When you restart you must be willing to erase everything that you are. Here is the interesting part I rarely talk about. Not all of you will extinguish itself. You have to be willing to release all that you are right now, to become all that you might be. The old must be torn down for the new to be raised. They real key is having the wisdom to know what to subtract to refurbish the soul. I think you might find some of that wisdom here.

So I thought very hard about the first 0-6 years and what I know about my life during that time. My mama is from a large family; there were four girls and three boys. My mother was the oldest and helped to raise her siblings. My grandpa was the patriarch, my grandmother was strong but quiet. My mother became pregnant with my sister at 17. Both of them crossed the stage for graduation 1957 (sis was a June baby). Shotgun wedding for my mom and dad, pretty much. My mom went to college, and my great- grandmother and grandmother kept my sister, as my mom went off to college for music. My brother was born May 1959. They kept him and helped out as well. Not sure of my mom and dad's living arrangement during this time, as they were married but she was in college; maybe got pregnant home on break? Anyway that was it, college time over. There was a miscarriage for them between 1960 and my birth in 1962. My great-grandmother always knew when her daughter and her granddaughter were pregnant long before they knew, and foretold the sex of each of my mother's children except the last one, as she had passed. My mom came home, got a job in a restaurant downtown. She helped to start a nursery program at our church. This was not the home church of her mother, and I am a little foggy on how she ended up at the Lutheran church. My guess is that they needed an organist. I will have to ask her to refresh that part of my history. She moved into assisted housing. The relationship between my parents was volatile, as she fell for a troubled guy, her husband. At this point I think is where I come in.

The nursery she worked first may have been at our Lutheran Church. She then was employed by the nazarene church. There is picture at one of the churches which has me in a play pen off to the side while she is teaching. Not sure how long she worked there. She ran a daycare and school from her home after that. Replete with graduations from kindergarten, and kids remembering her way after they graduated high school. She has photos of kids hitting a pinata as part of play time, and playing games in the yard, and I can see me there, about 2-3 years old. She has pictures taken of her graduating classes and year end program. The plays had scenery which she painted or designed, and she played music and the kids sang, and acted and danced and showed their parents what they had learned. If I was too young to go somewhere, my grandma saw to me and cared for me. Her house was very quiet and I have vague happy memories of her house. We moved to a family owned house after an older relative passed away, and we have a picture of my grandma holding plump, baldheaded me in her lap and my grandma is beaming. I have always liked that picture. It was the last picture we have of my grandma with gray hair; she began dying it red! The pic is black and white, so you can't tell how much gray, but apparently she could!
I think my grandma must have heavily influenced my early life while my mom got her feet under her. But she also kept my brother and my sister before me. Once we moved into the family owned house and i guess my Dad moved with us too, there were four children.

My brother under me was born in February 1964. He was the last baby in the family whose sex was foretold by my great-grandma. Back then, when you had a baby, you were put into the hospital for at least a week. It was like you were packing to go on vacation. Magazines, hair stuff, night gowns. My mama often said that for some moms this was the only time that they could be assured of getting any rest, sleep or pampering. The medical community also had kind of marginalized the birth process, as my mom can remember being 'knocked out' to give birth. This caused a bit of a problem, because when she awoke in her room, the nurses were remarking about her beautiful baby girl that she had just delivered! And just how cute and everything her baby girl was. My mom was so sure of what HER grandma had told her, that she told the nurses that they needed to go and make sure they had the right baby, because her baby was a BOY! Sure enough, they came back from the nursery, having undressed my brother, and found indeed that he was a boy! Have to go work now. More later.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T05:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-your-reality-begin-in-mitochondria.15452/post-179044>

Light alone creates is capable of creating matter and that happens in mitochondria.

Free radicals code for energy and information release from mitochondria to alter size and shape of all things within a cell. The smaller or more compact something is the less energy it needs to become un-condensed or open. Light is capable of un-condensing things with short wavelength light to cause a stress response. We can condense proteins with certain frequencies of light like red. Red light acts on cytochrome c oxidase to improve electron flow in ECT. The more open or uncondensed something is the more energy is needed to condense it. The irony of these natural laws is when an animal evolves in an environment that is energy poor (low quantum yield), for any reason the animal will become larger…..to become more energy efficient. This is similar to the ‘economy of scale’ idea in business mergers. In biology, it is has another name, called Kleiber’s law. This is why elephants are huge and why mice are small and mice have faster metabolic rates. Their metabolic rates reflect these difference in thermodynamics, and this change is tied to how their cytochromes in their mitochondria also have different magnetic and electric oscillating forces to make free radicals to work differently at controlling how they handle electrons from foods they eat and photons from the light their surfaces assimilate. ROS and RNS are linked in us for a similar reasons like the nitrogen/oxygen ratio is fixed at 3.727 in the atmosphere.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T05:57:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-your-reality-begin-in-mitochondria.15452/post-179045>

Spintronics is an emerging technology exploiting both the intrinsic spin of the electron and its associated magnetic moment, in addition to its fundamental electronic charge, in solid-state devices. Solid-state electronics are those circuits or devices built entirely from solid materials and in which the electrons, or other charge carriers, are confined entirely within the solid material. In a solid-state component, the current is confined to solid elements and compounds engineered specifically to switch and amplify it. Current flow can be understood in two forms: as negatively charged electrons (the DC current) and as positively charged electron deficiencies called holes. They get a more positive charge because the electron is no longer where it was previously, leaving a more positively charged environment behind. The DC current is capable of imprinting a cell tensegrity system.........so can the holes in the cell membranes.......they both creates paths for light to follow or divert from.............
DHA in cell membranes has a lot of these + holes in it because of the alternating double bonds between its 22 carbons in its molecular structure. This molecular arrangement “draws” electrons to it with incredible force. Light is drawn to pi electron clouds. UV light is exceptional drawn to electrons but does not always hit the electrons. It is a totally random event. This is the basis of the photoelectric effect. This is essentially how light controls the redox potential in all cells. It works as electrons move from place to place in proteins. As they collect electrons proteins become more hydrophilic and as they do, the hydration shell around all proteins forms a larger EZ. This is why DHA has specifically selected for other proteins to work with in the lipid rafts of cell membranes. DHA and UV light have a special quantum relationship that usually is only seen in UV light and metals. If you look at the construction or human cell membranes you will see massive differences within the eukaryotic and prokaryotic kingdom. Prokaryotes find UV light toxic but euklaryotes use it well because of the massive amount of CM we have filled with DHA and all the pi electrons. These changes are actually tied to why humans lost their hair on the skin surface compared to the their primate ancestors. The skin in humans is fundamentally a solar panel back up system to run your brain and heart in quantum fashion. This is also the reason the glands in your skin are designed to sweat a lot to cool the surface temperature to allow for better assimilation of sunlight to your plasma. This facilitates rapid conduction of electrons from your cell membrane in skin to your RBC's in your blood plasma below the skin surface at a depth of 1/10th of a mm. UV light only penetrates a short distance. IR light penetrates deeply. Sunlight induces 60% increase blood flow to the surface to capture UV light best. Cooling your surface allows for more light absorbtion. Sunlight induces biochemical and photochemical changes in the skin. Full spectrum sun produces calcitriol, histamine, and sulfhydryl-groups. Sulfur helps dissipate the many frequencies in sunlight over a larger surface area to limit scattering of light so our blood can capture most of light's power and momentum in our plasma and move it away from the surface to make the skin surface even cooler so we can get more UV light in to porphyrins in RBC's. IR light can penetrate quite deeply easily for use or storage in cells, tissues and in cytochrome c. Sulfhydryl groups also helps select out and direct specific light frequencies in sunlight that life like best to maintain metastability between the thermodynamic and quantum edges in our body. The cooler things become on the surface and below our skin, the more energy efficient the system becomes in cells at deeper levels because the magnetic fields created in our mitochondria control the release of either singlet or triplet free radicals and the release of IR light from protons released from the matrix that are colliding into an Fe/S target that causes liquid water to shrink in size in the MINOS just outside the cytochrome mouth. Water condenses/shrinks when it is heated by IR light. This tightens the respiratory chain to make ECT faster and more efficient. When water freezes, it expands so fish can swim beneath it. People have forgotten how this little aspect of water chemistry is the foundation of how the Cold Thermogenesis protocol really works to help us absorb more light when UV light is not plentiful. The flow of infrared energy is carried in protons, electrons, photons, in mitochondria for signaling and for tissue creation. Light alone can make matter.
Singlet versus triplet oxygen combined with bad environment and artificial antioxidants give wrong cellular information. That's when antioxidants can screw you up. Proper free radical signaling is necessary for cell differentiation. No proper radicals=no cell differentiation= cancerous cells This is also why when in pseudohypoxia the metabolism is shifted away from mitochondrial respiration to cytosolic glycolysis there is a problem. It's not just about the quantitatively less ATP production (2 vs 36), also qualitatively the cytosolic ATP differs. The lack of proper ROS/RNS decreases cell differentiation leading to mito-nuclear coaptation problems and the cancer needs to seek out new mitochondria to survive. That is what metastatic cancer is at its core. A cell looking for a new quantum heat engine that works better. Mitochondria use free radicals (ROS and RNS) to code information and energy thermodynamically using the same quantum effects. Mitochondrial cytochrome protein clusters ‘shoots’ the radicals into water and oxygen to send to all parts of cell increasing its coherence as time develops.

> _The hydrogen bonding network in water is a quantum measuring device because its motions and action provides a record or a memory of the state of entangled pair of electrons in free radicals made in mitochondria._ This is why mitochondria use free radicals to signal. They also release _monochromatic infrared light_ because you can only**entangle electrons** and protons with light at **one specific frequency**. The double slit experiment proving quantum entanglement only works if light is monochromatic. This is why your skin has to begin the filtering process of sunlight a long time before your mitochondria can process the electromagnetic wave of light. You should be reminded that water absorbs electromagnetic radiation best at 270 nm directly in the ultaviolet range. Everything is coupled by quantum design when you understand her plan.

> What should be becoming clear to you is how a mitochondria fundamentally works now, is not what is currently in your biochemistry book. I told you earlier in the series that mitochondria are small nano-electromagnets. The magnet is important for entangling electrons by using their “spin state” and the frequency of light released. Fundamentally, mitochondria are creators of entangled subatomic waves/particles from the unpaired electrons of free radicals.
> Free radicals are formed by single-electron oxidation or reduction of an atom (Carbon) or molecule: an example is the production of superoxide by the electron transport chain in mitochondria. _If a mitochondria cannot form free radicals to signal, diseases result._ The best example is T2D. In this disease, mitochondria can not make superoxide free radicals to signal properly from cytochrome one. Acute hyperglycemia in normal non diabetic normally generates superoxide too. This shows you carbs are not always bad. It also shows you why LCHF diets might present problems chronically. Superoxide comes from the ECT and is formed by single-electron oxidation or reduction of an atom or molecule. Normally, we expect to see superoxide creation from cytochrome complex I in the region of the FAD moiety, by the Fe-S cluster N1-a (iron sulfur cluster alert). This should make you ask a question, why can’t a diabetic do this?

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-24T00:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-your-reality-begin-in-mitochondria.15452/post-193332>

> when an animal evolves in an environment that is energy poor (low quantum yield), for any reason the animal will become larger…..to become more energy efficient

I have a nice little story to tell for this one.

When I was playing around with Reiki in the late 1990's, a friend brought home a stray kitten diagnosed with feline leukemia. I did Reiki for the little black kitty for about 2-3 weeks, twice a week. We retested her, and the test came back positive. So the friend gave up. About half a year later, we went to her home. The cat was doing extremely well, no sign of disease. But it got immensely HUGE. It's body wasn't fat, but kind of 'lean and mean'. I think it must have weighed at least 25 lbs. It looked like a small black panther. Don't know if the Reiki did anything for it, but if it was Kleiber's law, it adapted very well.

### seanb4 — 2015-06-14T13:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/penis-problems.15456/post-168065>

Bit of a weird one this, so bare with me.

If I masturbate after my final meal of the day (generally 5pm) I have terrible nights sleep. I wake up with an uncomfortable erection multiple times throughout the night and I feel like I maybe slightly need to pee even though most of the time I don't or if I do it doesn't bring relief. This doesn't happen if I masturbate earlier in the day.

I personally think it's a combination of dehydration (I can't drink any water between tea time and bed time) and maybe some semen still stuck in the shaft???

I have had this ever since my health has gone in decline (4yrs) and google doesn't seem to help much. Any ideas?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T15:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/penis-problems.15456/post-168077>

the effect should be opposite so I would say it is not biological but environmental. It maybe a subtle way your environment is try to speak to you through your behavior.

### Bill1 — 2015-06-14T17:52:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/penis-problems.15456/post-168102>

.
more common would over masturbation -

Over-masturbation/over-ejaculation/excessive-orgasm can castrate your hypothalamus-pituitary-testicular(ovarian) axis by arterial constriction and inflammatory narrowness in your brain and pelvic organs (testicles, prostate, seminal vesicles, penis, ovaries, uterus, vagina and clitoris) due to excessive release of prolactin, norepinephrine, and epinephrine, excessive binding of norepeinephrine/epinephrine on the alpha-adrenergic receptors, the norepinephrine/epinephrine induced excessive prostaglandin E2, and the post-sex deficiency of nitric oxide and prostaglandins E1/E3 production.

Also, excessive prolactin, norepinephrine, epinephrine and/or prostaglandin E2 increase the risk of autoimmune disorders.

Ejaculation/orgasm induced sleeping disorder is a result of excessive norepinephrine-induced prostaglandin E2 in the preoptic area of the hypothalamus via the ventral norepinephrine noradrenergicbundle, that elevate the core temperature for sleeping disorder. It is like your body on fire. The persistent sexual arousal and ejaculation urgency are due to excessive prostaglandin E2 stimulation on your seminal vesicles, prostate, bulbourethral glands, penis, kidneys and bladder. This means the norepinephrine-induced prostaglandin E2 over-excited your pineal gland and retinal and cut down your melatonin production, inflamed your eye balls, and over-dilated your eye pupils.

don't masturbate or have sex for about 7 days & you should start to feel better. no arousal either.

# Thread: Stroke Prevention and Recovery.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-06-14T15:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/post-168082>

Hi people.
Both my grandmothers recently got strokes. No fun. I wish people to never get one.
Do I tell them the standard Jack Kruse protocols? Or should I fine tune it a bit?
Man it's easy to do something for myself, even hardcore CT, but convincing someone to do something is much harder.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-14T16:07:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/post-168089>

DHA and sunlight and water.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-14T16:09:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/post-168090>

Absolutely no fun. My aunt at the end of her life the last 5-7 years had weekly ones or so it seemed. She rattled from the pills the doctors chucked into her but she believed that they were her salvation. She lived an independent life but was anguished by not living up to what mentally she perceived to be her potential.
My father had a stroke age 47 which left his left side paralysed . He went into the mountains to wade hip deep in icecold streams . Doctors told him that he would never get mobility back . He did.....

Maybe just focus on one protocol that should help. Maybe suggest a vacation at the seaside if your grandma is mobile with the stroke and cold plunges "for teh minerals ya know" You know your Grandmas . If they are openminded do tell them about the protocols if they are not focus on a "harmless" one ,like eating fish ( too much mercury), getting out in the sun (cancer)cold therapy ( you want to give me another stroke) I am just preparing you for the objections so that you can get your ducks in a row .

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-14T16:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/post-168093>

When I was a kid, my father suffered multiple strokes with some years between. He too was half paralysed and had aphasia. I remember the doctor telling me to take his hand to the things he wanted and not to give him the things. Also he ordered me to practice my school work with him (I thought this doctor was crazy, I didn't want to do school work). We as a family helped him to get his movement back and to get him talking again, but of course my father was a young man back then. But maybe with a lot of attention and figuring out some inventive ways to stimulate/relearn, you could help your grandmothers face whatever difficulty they now have to conquer too.

With concern to Jack stuff... when I visit my grandma I always bring fish for her. We eat fish and drink water in the garden . I don't tell her why.

### Penny — 2015-06-19T03:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-prevention-and-recovery.15457/post-168854>

Yeah, never tell them why - it only makes them suspicious... tell her you felt like eating seafood tonight - cook it, bring it over - or visit her in the morning for the morning sun - I have even mixed in beef heart with the regular burger meat to get some CO q10 into them - not that it's a lot, but it's better than nothing -

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-19T04:09:58+0300

> Man it's easy to do something for myself, even hardcore CT, but convincing someone to do something is much harder.

I feel for ya Dude. My dad has parkinson's and then to add insult to injury he got a stroke.
He doesn't trust anything that I tell him. He says "You can't even get yourself well!" He has a point. So now I'm just concentrating on myself so I can lead by example. I think Dr. Kruse would tell you the same thing.

# Thread: Connecting some SCN and CT dots?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connecting-some-scn-and-ct-dots.15459/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jnewman21 — 2015-06-14T22:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connecting-some-scn-and-ct-dots.15459/post-168133>

I recently posted to the fb group to ask Why I have an urge to yawn when deep breathing or even sighing. I was told that yawning cools the SCN. I remembered that and decided to see if the same thing would happen while doing CT. That was my n=1 experiment for the week. CT being the variable controlled for, I began my CT and deep breathed away...no yawning....tried over and over taking a break between bouts of deep breathing.... End result: no yawning while deep breathing during CT.
This leads me to wonder this: Could it be that many addictions somehow serve to ultimately cool the SCN? Many of us certainly seem to be developing an "addiction" to CT. Seems to me other addictions could derive the same benefit to the SCN as CT does but maybe to a lesser or even minute scale?

This is just the ramblings of a newbie, feeling their way around. I have a lot of blogs to read... A TON of them.

P.S. Jack Kruse, if you are reading this...I've already googled "dopamine Kruse" and got a few hits...give me nuggets please...just nuggets here...they make it fun like a scavenger hunt for this former girlscout...I love the critical thinking aspect of this and LOVE surprises. Thank you Jack for sharing all your knowledge

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connecting-some-scn-and-ct-dots.15459/post-168148>

try Jack kruse and yawning..........

# Thread: INSANE Fatigue + Lethargy After Morning Protein
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-fatigue-lethargy-after-morning-protein.15460/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LiveElevated — 2015-06-14T22:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-fatigue-lethargy-after-morning-protein.15460/post-168135>

Hello All!

Today was day-4 of Leptin reset for me.

Does anyone else experience intense fatigue and lethargy after eating their morning protein? I literally CANNOT function for a few hours after eating. It's like a giant foggy veil has been placed over my eyes, and I go easily go back to bed and sleep for another 4-hours.... and this is NOT like me at all. I nearly always wake up feeling great, and then even better after my usual Bulletproof Coffee with only 11g of added collagen protein.

Since adding so much protein into my morning eating, it feels very similar to the feeling I've had after carb re-feed, when I've been following a ketogenic diet... except the protein effects seem to be WAY more extreme than the carb fatigue!

I am otherwise very healthy 31F, little stress, rarely ever sick, love to lift weights, hike, and do intervals,... but limit these activities to no more than once or twice per week, as I fear they may hinder my weight loss efforts if leptin IS my issue.

I am eating clean, grass-fed/organic sources of protein, and even tried supplementing a bit to include organic/grass-fed whey protein, although I am aware that whey protein affects BG for many people. Basically.. I was mixing it up to see if HOW much food I ate was the culprit. It didn't matter.

A little backstory: I am new to the forum, but am not new to health, nutrition, or a "paleo-esque" approach to nutrition. After a substantial weight loss in 2011, I later went grain, sugar, wheat, gluten, and processed food free... and have been so for about a year and a half now. I eat "bulletproof" and low carb and usually feel great. Eliminated a million toxins and such from my life. My husband also eats the same way that I do.

Early last year, I experienced odd fluctuations in my weight (mostly creeping UP) despite being on a relatively low-carb / paleo diet, I obtained a wellness fx blood panel and later a cortisol test which indicated that I was displaying some signs of adrenal fatigue. (Everything else was GREAT, and my triglycerides were insanely optimal... enough to where the doctor commented that I had surpassed anything he personally had ever seen) I was advised to take a number of supplements to support mitochondrial health, and to go into deeper ketosis and keep myself there as long as possible. I also obtained hormone panels and I was told it all looked good.. but recalled seeing something that did look too high or low.

As of mid-January of this year, my diet has been **extremely** low-carb (below 15 total per day or less), and I had been maintaining VERY high ketone levels with only a few lbs of losses. I was really only eating two high-fat meals per day, and still little to no losses. I would eat moderate protein+fat if I got hungry in the afternoon - but for the most part, it would be some type of grass-fed meat and veggies for dinner. I had figured I would have lost the 15-ish lbs that I needed to by now... instead it just fluctuates back and forth... and it is now slowly creeping up again. I am not obese, but I look "thick". It's annoying when I eat so clean and have been so disciplined with my eating for so long.

After reading about leptin resistance, I suspected this may be why I am not losing. After all, I _did_ lose my weight using traditional "low-fat, low calorie" diet methods, and I had a terrible relationship with food in my teens... often not eating for very long periods of time, and then later carb loading because I was a "vegetarian" that rarely got to eat vegetables. It was a recipe for disaster.

Other than this, sleep is good and light exposure is great. I do not work in a traditional office, and this helps greatly.

I am thinking about cutting some of the protein back, and/or trying another route. My profession requires me to be mentally at 100%, and I need my morning brainpower.

Any input is greatly appreciated!

The more you read the less rational you will become and the more ripe you will be for transformation. Why do we think all discoveries must manifest by rational thinking? The divergent mind sees new things in concepts no one has thought yet. _My members on my site read what I write because they know I dare to breach the surface and sink to find the treasures that biology has missed._ **_The key is will you be ready for what awaits you? If you read, you will.......that choice is now yours. How you proceed is up to you._**

I concur - your website clearly has an abundance of information! I have had a long journey of weight loss, and healing and am always looking to take it to the next level. Hopefully I can also be of assistance to those in your forums who may be struggling in areas that I have overcome.

And for every piece of great information that I have read on your website, I have also stumbled upon countering information by naysayers on other websites. To me, this is a wonderful sign. In my own life, I have achieved my biggest successes by doing what others simply will not do... because they are afraid, lazy, or skeptical. I love the saying: "Do what others do, to have what others have.". What do MOST people have? Terrible health, debt, unrest, stress, unfulfilling careers, and _closed_ minds. This is certainly not a coincidence.

I understand that when you go against the grain in any manner, you will acquire a healthy amount of people who will tell you that you are wrong. It is unfortunate that many may not hear about things such as leptin resistance... simply because others said it was irrelevant.

In any event, it is troubling to hear that my lack of seafood is directly correlated with my primary question. I will do some digging on your website, as I am sure there are answers everywhere. I know that supplements can only do so much (if anything).

### LiveElevated — 2015-06-15T06:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-fatigue-lethargy-after-morning-protein.15460/post-168188>

_"We are designed to eat a marine diet year round to lower the oxidative state in our cells to support the metabolic rate of our nervous system. Oxidation creates inflammation. Inflammation causes leptin resistance. When we do not eat a marine diet, leptin resistance is the result. Neolithic disease follows."_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-15T14:23:06+0300

and the current blog Ubi 17 tells you exactly why.

[http://spectrum.ieee.org/biomedical...al&utm\_source=twitter.com&utm\_campaign=buffer](http://spectrum.ieee.org/biomedical/devices/the-vagus-nerve-a-back-door-for-brain-hacking?utm_content=buffere25bb&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter.com&utm_campaign=buffer)

### Scompy — 2015-07-27T05:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insane-fatigue-lethargy-after-morning-protein.15460/post-173180>

This is a great forum thread!

I've been doing Keto for 3+ years now. From my personal experiences, I used to get foggy and lethargic after any meal with 20-25+ grams of protein. But in the past month and 4 weeks of CT, I've been able to bring my breakfast up to 60 grams of protein without any lethargy whatsoever, and breakfast lasts me over for full for 5 hours. I've been doing a pattern of around 60 grams of protein in the morning, 30ish at lunch and 15 at dinner. I'm typically eating seafood over other protein-sources, but that has been MAGIC for the first meal of the day. I typically get a slice of salmon with two eggs, or some shrimp since they are so high in protein content. No lethargy whatsoever. I've also tested my Blood-glucose and Ketones and they've also been way-better this past month, again, most likely great thanks to the CT, improved DHA levels, better water sources and blue-light blocking.

For these reasons, I believe now I've been under the wrong assumptions for years on Keto that 20+ grams of ANY protein at any meal could cause the lethargy. That was one-sided biochemistry-only thinking rather than considering the biophysics.

Also as a side issue with coffee..., I also used to get adrenal strain in my lower-back after any coffee and chocolate, and avoided it like the plague until I found high-elevation, high-quality coffee beans from south america. In thinking back to the years I also used to get it drinking iced-tea, I'm like 99% convinced it's the molds in them. This south-american coffee so far has been the only type of coffee that doesn't invoke this reaction in my lower back, which is probably inflammation over-straining the kidneys along with pounding the adrenals that sit on top of the kidneys. Now it's time to find an 85% or higher dark chocolate that also doesn't invoke that response. Any clues here?

# Thread: Making a connection; DHA and Electron Chain Transport

### billy — 2015-06-15T00:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-connection-dha-and-electron-chain-transport.15461/post-168142>

Trying to make a connection with what I've been learning...

from Dr K's blog;
UBIQUITINATION 17: GEARS OF THE “EYE CLOCK”
[http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/](http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/)

"DHA is critical in all these eukaryotic cell membranes for one specific quantum reason. In DHA, electrons in the pi cloud are at 6 Angstrom distance. This allows DHA laden membranes to work even faster at tunneling electrons over membranes compared to the mitochondrial respiratory proteins."

and prior to that...

"Recall, that 1 Angstrom increase distance in the mitochondrial ECT is equivalent to a ten fold reduction of the amount of ATP capable of being made in a mitochondria."

1.  Does DHA increase glycolisis, producing more ATP?
2.  Does DHA affect fermentation - the citric acid (krebs) cycle as well?
3.  Is the increase in DHA speeding up the Redox Reaction... is that the key here?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-15T00:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-connection-dha-and-electron-chain-transport.15461/post-168143>

1\. You missed point. DHA is on eukaryotic CM. Mitochondria are prokaryotic membranes. They work slower that ours cause they are filled with transition metals and MO. Read this:[http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-8-dha-come/](http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-8-dha-come/) Prokaryotes have slower conduction of electrons than eukaryotes therefore eukaryotes who by their nature are designed to have CM LOADED with DHA can tunnel faster.......why is this BIG......not FREAKING HUGE? Because of what is in Ubi 2. To be in control using GPS physics you need to run faster than what you are aiming to control. The eukaryotic CM controls the action of what happens in mito. This is why DHA is an amazing antenna. Read this:[http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-14-electrosensitivity-is-antenna-failure/](http://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-14-electrosensitivity-is-antenna-failure/)

2\. Yes.

3\. Yes/ No. It is keeping the ability of the the SCN running faster than the eukaryotic CM which must run faster than the mitochondrial inner membrane where food electrons are tunneled. Everything is about signaling. The main redox sensor is the NAD+/NADH ratio in cytochrome 1 which is altered when DHA is absent.

### PaulG — 2015-06-17T04:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-ct-vs-creek-ct.15501/post-168505>

Sea Water Benefits:

-   The medicinal use of sea water dates all the way back to 4th century B.C. when Hippocrates first used the word “thalassotherapy”.

-   This word comes from the Greek thalassa, meaning water, and therapia, meaning to cure. Known also as the father of modern medicine, Hippocrates was the first to use sea water for healing purposes.

-   Seawater contains the same 84 vital elements found also in the human body. These elements include vitamins, mineral salts, trace elements and amino acids. But sea water is also rich in living microorganisms that produce beneficial antibiotic, antimicrobial and antibacterial substances.

-   Human beings have the natural ability to easily assimilate all these elements, for our bodies’ mineral constitution, as mentioned above, is very similar to that of the sea water. In fact, sea water contains almost the same concentration of minerals and trace elements as the human blood plasma.

-   The salt contained in the sea water helps remove toxins from the skin and acts as a natural exfoliator – it removes dead cells and encourages the production of the new ones.

-   Sea water replenishes our skin with minerals. Magnesium in particular improves its hydration, as well as its overall appearance.

-   One of the most important sea water health benefits lies in its ability to heal damaged and irritated skin.

-   Sea water can reduce inflammation and help cure many skin disorders such as atopic dermatitis, rosacea, psoriasis and eczema.

-   It has great antiseptic properties, very useful in healing minor wounds, cuts, rashes and abrasions – salt and potassium chloride seem to be majorly responsible for these beneficial, mending effects.

Swimming in the sea water can greatly contribute to our overall health.

A study has shown that sea water-grown cherry tomatoes have higher amounts of antioxidants than those grown using fresh water. The study has further found that these tomatoes can improve the body’s defenses against heart disease and cancer. But this is not all!

-   Being so rich in many vital elements, sea water has the ability to activate the body’s healing mechanism and boost the immune system. It opens skin pores, allowing them to absorb sea minerals, while helping them expel harmful toxins, responsible for numerous diseases.

-   It also increases the level of oxygen in our bloodstream, and thereby the amount of beneficial elements needed by the blood to fight off free radicals.

-   Sea air and sea mist are full of negatively charged hydrogen ions, which are powerful antioxidants. Inhaling sea mist can therefore improve the immune system and enhance the overall health of every cell and organ in our body.

-   The iodine contained in sea water supports our immune system function and boosts the thyroid activity. Being a great natural antiseptic, it also considerably increases our body’s ability to fight infections and kill parasites, bacteria and fungus.

_Two more benefits:_

-   Sea water improves blood circulation, due to its high concentration of minerals.
    In fact, thalassotherapy increases blood circulation by replenishing our body with essential minerals, which have been depleted due to different internal and external factors.

-   Sea water has also the ability to relieve cold and flu symptoms, as well as to cure bronchitis and sinusitis. Inhaling sodium chloride, or salt from sea water, helps loosen mucus and treat pulmonary problems.

Reducing stress and healing the mind is however one the greatest sea water health benefits.

-   Sea water and sun work in synergy to treat many emotional disorders, such as depression, nervousness, anxiety, apathy and many others.

-   Magnesium, lithium and bromine contained in sea water are exceptional calming elements, which have the ability to stabilize and maintain proper serotonin, melatonin and tryptamine levels in the brain.

-   Lithium, for example, is a medication that is often used to treat many psychological problems, and especially the bipolar disorder.

-   The high content of magnesium in the sea water can efficiently help reduce stress, relax muscles and nerves, and induce calmness. In fact, minerals contained in the sea water are in some way natural hypnotics that promote good quality sleep and help cure insomnia.

In conclusion, sea water is an excellent treatment for many nervous problems and it can considerably increase the sense of well-being and relaxation.

Cite : [Sea Water Benefits](http://youqueen.com/life/health/sea-water-health-benefits/)

### PaulG — 2015-06-17T04:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ocean-ct-vs-creek-ct.15501/post-168506>

UBI17 alert..

[Article:](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0129972) Stimulatory Effects of Balanced Deep Sea Water on Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Function

**BDSW enhances mitochondrial enzyme activity by increasing AMPK and SIRT1 phosphorylation and PGC-1α deacetylation**

**BDSW stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis dependent on AMPK and SIRT1 signaling pathways**

> **Abstract:** The worldwide prevalence of metabolic diseases, including obesity and diabetes, is increasing. Mitochondrial dysfunction is recognized as a core feature of these diseases. Emerging evidence also suggests that defects in mitochondrial biogenesis, number, morphology, fusion, and fission, contribute to the development and progression of metabolic diseases. Our previous studies revealed that balanced deep-sea water (BDSW) has potential as a treatment for diabetes and obesity. In this study, we aimed to investigate the mechanism by which BDSW regulates diabetes and obesity by studying its effects on mitochondrial metabolism. To determine whether BDSW regulates mitochondrial biogenesis and function, we investigated its effects on mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) content, mitochondrial enzyme activity, and the expression of transcription factors and mitochondria specific genes, as well as on the phosphorylation of signaling molecules associated with mitochondria biogenesis and its function in C2C12 myotubes. BDSW increased mitochondrial biogenesis in a time and dose-dependent manner. Quantitative real-time PCR revealed that BDSW enhances gene expression of PGC-1α, NRF1, and TFAM for mitochondrial transcription; MFN1/2 and DRP1 for mitochondrial fusion; OPA1 for mitochondrial fission; TOMM40 and TIMM44 for mitochondrial protein import; CPT-1α and MCAD for fatty acid oxidation; CYTC for oxidative phosphorylation. Upregulation of these genes was validated by increased mitochondria staining, CS activity, CytC oxidase activity, NAD+ to NADH ratio, and the phosphorylation of signaling molecules such as AMPK and SIRT1. Moreover, drinking BDSW remarkably improved mtDNA content in the muscles of HFD-induced obese mice. Taken together, these results suggest that the stimulatory effect of BDSW on mitochondrial biogenesis and function may provide further insights into the regulatory mechanism of BDSW-induced anti-diabetic and anti-obesity action.

> For now, we speculate that the stimulatory effect of BDSW on mitochondria-associated gene expression and enzyme activity affects the synthesis of ATP or NAD+, which are known to be substrates of AMPK and SIRT1, respectively, contributing to an increase in AMPK and SIRT1 activity. As shown by Nathan et al. \[[51](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0129972#pone.0129972.ref051)\], SIRT1 plays an essential role in resveratrol-induced stimulation of AMPK and improvement of mitochondrial function, both in vitro and in vivo. We also speculate that SIRT1 activity is required for AMPK activation as well as the stimulatory effects of BDSW on mitochondrial biogenesis and function, although our present findings indicate only some indirect evidence for the association between SIRT1 and AMPK in the regulatory mechanism of BDSW on mitochondrial function. As shown in [Fig 5B, NA](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0129972#pone-0129972-g005) completely inhibited the phosphorylation of both AMPK and Sirt1. In contrast, CC completely inhibited the phosphorylation of AMPK but not Sirt1. Therefore, further studies are needed to investigate what is essential for the stimulatory effect of BDSW on AMPK and SIRT1 activity as well as mitochondrial biogenesis and function, and clarify the regulatory mechanism by BDSW on AMPK and SIRT1 activity. In conclusion, our findings suggest that BDSW effectively stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis and function, and may provide a possible therapeutic approach for preventing or treating diabetes and obesity.

"You should also be aware that Earth's geomagnetic field is a source of low-intensity radiation. reference: [http://www.emfsafehome.com/protectyourself.html](http://www.emfsafehome.com/protectyourself.html)

### Joseph Thia — 2015-06-16T09:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-singapore.15520/post-168353>

Hi! I am Joseph from Singapore. I was wondering how many of you are currently living in Singapore? Would love to learn about how you implement the various protocols in Singapore!

I am currently on low carb high protein diet (slowly moving towards ketogenic diet) and I have been eating more baked salmon and lightly boiled oysters (the raw oysters that I found were a bit pricey) since I started reading this forum. I have been biohacking my workspace (using the TriField Meter) to reduce my nnEMF exposure and I am waiting eagerly for the Cool Fat Burner to be shipped to me for CT.

That's all for now!

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-16T11:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-singapore.15520/post-168363>

Well, I lived mostly in Singapore for 17 years (1991 to 2008), so I guess that counts for something

It's tough to do this stuff in Singapore. Lots of people, hence lots of non-native EMF, lots of bright lights at night. If you're not living up in a highrise Condo or HDB flat, that is even worse because of the elevation, and lots of people simultaneously using WiFi.

High-DHA seafood in Singapore is expensive as hell . Only thing you can do for seafood is take advantage of whatever international shipping discounts the big supermarkets get. We used to snap up lots of Norwegian Salmon at Giant whenever there was a sale. Any big Sheng Shiong branch used to be pretty good with getting fresh shellfish back in the day, not sure what's the case now since I haven't been back in more than 2 years.

If you're stuck having to eat out, I honestly can't think of anything that is very healthy. Probably the only option is something like Sushi. But TBH, I don't know how big a factor diet is when you're living in a crazy urban jungle like Singapore. Food is still going to be a stressor of course. As much as I like my bakchang, it will definitely stress out the system in a blue-light ridden place like Singapore. Same goes for basically anything else (even something like Orh Jian takes oysters and makes it a fried gluten-full mess ).

Location in Singapore is going to be the most important factor, and if you've been reading this forum, then you know you want to be as far from population centres as possible, with good access to the beach if possible. Back when I was a kid, I spent a lot of time in the Hillview / Bukit Batok area, which at least still had a lot of forests (and a haunted mansion, and a SAF training ground nearby ), but all of that is developed into private Condos now. West Coast used to be good, but today's it's all built-up too. If I had to make a choice of living somewhere in Singapore today, maybe a house in Pasir Ris next to the seaside.

Focus on the places that you spend most of your time in, and try to fix those in terms of excess nn-EMF. Other people who work in office settings will probably help you more in that regard (I'm clueless since I work from home).

The usual recommendations apply -- lots of sun (easy in Singapore, even in Nov-Dec), lots of grounding, don't be a typical Singaporean who can't fall asleep until 12am, etc ...

Oh, and the Conrad Hotel buffet is awesome . 6 kinds of raw oysters and sashimi to the max!!!!

....

Paskovanaryba - We live in a highly dense urban EMF hellhole in Singapore so doing CT is one way to offset this risk, regardless of seasons. ### JJ sg — 2016-02-15T10:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-singapore.15520/post-189074>

Hey Guys,
I am from SG too. Getting fresh oyster is not cheap at all, Instead I would go buffets and eat as much oysters as I can ~50.
I am using a water filter from Australia, which removes fluoride, chlorine, chloramine all those nasty stuffs with dissolve hydrogen in it.
So far so good, at least it removes chlorine, fluoride and have hydrogen in it. Managed to convinced my boss to have 1 at the office pantry too.

Its hard to do CT in singapore, the water is always around 30degrees celcius. What i do is i fill up the water in a pail instead and let it "cool" overnight, so the water can get pretty cold, ~ 27degrees, but thats all. Have just ordered cool far burner and waiting for it to arrive.

If you guys are looking for wild caught seafood, can look for the alaskan guys, they do stock some wild caught salmons, cods, halibut, but its not cheap, $42-82/kg
Would be great if you guys can share any deals in SG, eg. oysters/seafoods etc. I am contemplating to buy seafood from JB instead, as seafood is much cheaper compared to SG.

JJ

### Lite Nomad — 2019-10-20T13:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-singapore.15520/post-280821>

Hi everyone, glad I stumbled on this thread

Another Singaporean here learning and adopting Dr Kruse's way of thinking and living for about a year now. I'm a newbie and would love to learn more on how mitochondriacs and residents are coping here. It's definitely not an easy place for optimal living.

Drinking Water: Icelandic Glacial Water seems to be the lowest in Fluoride
**Avoid San Benedetto** - the label claimed F to be <0.1 but the lab results received was 2mg/L
**Avoid Zaiho Japanese Spring Water** - 3mg/L fluoride
Water Testing: [https://www.alsglobal.com/](https://www.alsglobal.com/) ($30/test for fluoride)
I pretty much stopped drinking juices and hot soups from hawkers and restaurants. It was a tough decision but a necessary one.

Fresh oysters/cod fish/prawns/scallops: [https://www.evergreenseafood.com.sg/](https://www.evergreenseafood.com.sg/)
Wild caught salmon/sea bass/crabs: [https://www.facebook.com/thealaskaguys/](https://www.facebook.com/thealaskaguys/)

Bloodwork: [https://www.reinhealth.com.sg](https://www.reinhealth.com.sg/) (cheapest I could find without having to see a doctor, order the test online, visit Quest lab at Paragon, takes <20 mins, minimum waiting required if go early, lab report is emailed back 3-7 days, depending on type of tests done)
Hormone / Gut Test: [https://www.verisana.co.uk/](https://www.verisana.co.uk/) (they accept samples to be shipped from Singapore to their labs in Germany for analysis)

CT: bought an industrial grade plastic container just big enough to fit myself, filled half my freezer with ice blocks made from take away containers.. good thing there's a 7/eleven just around the corner. Anyone interested in group CT under the sun? Worried I might get arrested or sent to IMH if I have a go alone

Recently signed up Transcendental Meditation for stress management & better mental performance, have seen massive sleep improvement when paired with Leptin protocol (deeper, undisturbed sleep and dreaming more): [http://sg.tm.org/](http://sg.tm.org/)

Grounding: picked a small portion of a beach at East Coast with the lowest EMF. Walk barefoot 2 times a week (30 mins) and finish off with 20 mins body dunk in the sea. The water quality ain't great and I get occasional patches of sticky fuel (discarded from the ships) stuck on my feet. Hoping the benefits of grounding outweighs the risk of pollution and EMF but to be honest, I have no idea. Anyone with better suggestion for grounding in Singapore?

Sunlight: probably the only thing I love about this place, no shortage of it, so long areas with strong EMF are avoided.

ALAN: minimum lighting after sundown, blue blockers. Right now looking into UVA setup to compliment a small IR light - anyone with experience on how to set this up?

EMF: Cleaned up EMF in our bedroom and got an electrician to install a kill switch. Wifi is switched off before bed. DIY RF shielding in sleeping area seems to work well for 4G. I have no idea how 5G can be managed when launched next year. Wife and I are already making plans to move in 2021.. seems like the only solution for long term health.

Happy to link up and learn if you are here in Singapore.

# Thread: Lost a tooth filling . . . now what?

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-06-16T15:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lost-a-tooth-filling-now-what.15532/post-168392>

A huge filling I had in between two teeth, so essentially covering two cavities - 1 in each tooth, has just fallen out. When I had this put in 5 years ago shortly after getting sick it was to replace my mercury fillings. They told me then that it was a HUGE filling and may not "take." I'm glad it lasted this long, but now I have no idea what to do next.

With my poor health, I'm really concerned about a root canal. At the same time I'm also concerned about extractions and I'm pretty sure I would need both of them pulled! I've read so much negative stuff about root canals. And I don't do well at the dentist as is. I also have reactions to the anasthesia/numbing stuff they use. This is absolutely not what I need to be dealing with right now  I was finally starting to feel like I was making a few improvements here and there and now this!

Any suggestions/insight on how to make this decision? I'm pretty sure they won't just fill it again since it is so large. Thanks so much for any input at all. I just have no idea what to do.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-16T15:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lost-a-tooth-filling-now-what.15532/post-168394>

### lilreddgirl — 2015-06-16T19:29:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-dependent-on-5-htp.15534/post-168437>

Hello all, first I am doing excellent with my recovery from EMF sensitivity and accompanying issues  yay

Second, I went back and reread old blogs and realized I have been eating Epi-Paleo but not quite correctly - I was zero carbing for over a year after a year or so of anorexia and the past months was having frequent wheat-sugar-chocolate binges, sometimes at night!

Well I read about serotonin and using 5-HTP for 2 weeks. I started taking only 100mg 5-HTP upon waking right before breakfast. The first two to three times I got cramps... but then... I started feeling AMAZING!

That feeling of loneliness reduced immediately upon taking the 5-htp, and especially my tendency to snap at people when feeling stressed was gone. I don't know that I've EVER felt this emotionally stable (I think I zero carbed many days throughout my life in the past).

The couple days I didn't take the 5-HTP when I first started I noticed the difference in my mood immediately. And then if I took the 5-HTP, even later in the day, IMMEDIATELY I started to feel differently. No longer upset. Just strong and stable.

How do I keep feeling like this? It's been 3 weeks of 5-HTP steadyness, and the past two days - bec I read about zero carb and low serotonin - I've upped my carbs from zero to some carbs - I used raspberries, dried dates, or sugared chocolate - at all meals.

I feel some calming from the carb ingestion but not as stable and long lasting as the 5-htp... that was keeping me feeling steady all day.

What do I do?!!!

I don't want to become dependent on a supplement, I don't even know what 5-HTP ingestion might do long term.

But right now I feel kinda bummed and moody and that feeling I'm familiar with already... though I just ate some raspberries and dates at breakfast. I'm thinking about taking the 5-HTP and how smily and comfortable I will feel if I go take one right now, without even using my brain energy to fighting with my obessive sad thoughts.

Jack PLEASE help!

So I realized WHY to include carbs (previously they were making me feel awful and giving me huge crabings - my thoughts due to my liver issues but as I've Leptin timed and CT'd my way out of that it's not a problem, and they're not giving me cravings now).

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-16T20:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-dependent-on-5-htp.15534/post-168450>

I would not and have posted about its use before.
5 HTP initially features a "comfortably numb" interim phase before frank atypical depression symptoms can manifest in a large section of people. This make the initiation of its use euphoric.........but there is the latent effect where the problems arise. 5 HTP makes melatonin and serotonin at the same time.........Most dont realize this. Most people are using it for the short term melatonin effect on mitochondria. That melatonin effect can ruin melanopsin signaling in chronic fashion. 5 HTP, is therefore initially better tolerated but since 5 HTP creates melatonin just as melatonin pills obviously do, chronically used melatonin tablet can be more deletarious to mood and other psychiatric issues because of its effect on the eye clock. 5HTP is even more worrisome because it can be more depressant like because it also creates serotonin at the same time as melatonin. Again, this is why I said in CPC 9.......if youre designed to make it.........do not take it exogenously.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-16T20:22:29+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-16T20:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-dependent-on-5-htp.15534/post-168452>

Human patients now live in a world that steals their redox potential constantly.......Humans are not broken, but the environment they built is and is ruining them. Supplements often gives us a half truth to suit their marketing needs. The first casualty in supplement marketing is usually the truth. No human being itself should be considered impaired innately, instead there are environmental short comings that cause the impairment. Thus, it is incumbent on the on the clinician to recommend treatment of the environment their patient is in. People react to an inferior environment, way before their genome is altered. That is what the science of epigenetics and ubiquitin marking are telegraphing us, but the modern paradigm is not listening. You must begin to understand this by reading.
People are down on what they’re not up on. You must endure a lot of ignorance when you swim upstream to build wellness. Life is no longer something to endure, but to live well. There's just not a better or shorter way. Sometimes there's only the hard way. Life really is a test. Our will gets tested, our courage gets tested, our strength gets tested. And our patience and endurance are tested daily to see if we will persist on the correct road or opt to stop for the idea of "the big easy". No journey has merit if it cannot test us. Endurance is the spirit which can bear things, not simply with resignation, but with a blazing hope. Supplements are a short cut that could hurt you longer term.

### lilreddgirl — 2015-06-16T21:43:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-dependent-on-5-htp.15534/post-168461>

What ACTUALLY helps me tolerate CT is having a friend in the bathroom to talk to while I'm in the tub for the 20 min. Then the time flies by for me.

Nothing else comes close to helping my tolerance... though trying to read Jack's printed blog helps when my hand is not shaking so much I can't hold the paper. At first when I shivered too much to read I'd count up and down to 200 slowly aout 3 times. This made the cold worse, but also made me be able to stay in there "come on lets count to 200 one more time." Can't say much about getting back in there the next day, I'd procrastinate for hours. This went on for 4 months but I was only actually doing the tub once or twice a week while obsessing about it everyday!

But having the tub full now with cold water and just getting my legs in, or dunking my arms and face, then I can 'dip' in it and stay in ther for even just a count of 50 or 100 or a bit longer still seems to help me a lot!! And I'm actually getting in that tub everyday - usually starting just by stepping in it up to my calves multiple times a day (whenever I enter the bathroom pretty much) without procrastination or fear or getting sad about 'making myself' do something that feels not good and then rebelling by not doing it another day, which was what was happening to me before when trying to do the 20 min plus.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - Can you give us more info on waterfall therapy? All the googling I'm doing is just turning up soundtracks. Is it beneficial at all just to meditate and zone out to a waterfall sound? How about using a waterfall showerhead?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-17T17:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-dependent-on-5-htp.15534/post-168592>

The separation of electric charges accompanying the aerodynamic breakup of water drops, first studied systematically by the German physicist Phil Lenard. Experiments have shown that the degree of charge separation in spray processes depends upon the drop temperature, presence of dissolved impurities, speed of the impinging air blast, and contact with foreign surfaces. The largest fragments of the broken drops are observed to carry positive charges (or cations) and the fine spray of drops carried off in the impinging air current carries net negative charges (anions).

### JoeBranca — 2015-06-17T00:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-168473>

some about my context and environment...

Born in Santa Barbara 1975, my parents did not name me for the first 6 months of my life. With my feet extremely inwardly bent pigeon toed and never straightening out as they ought to, first doctor Rx was braces and a warning I probably wouldn't walk normal. Asking around more another doc got casts on my legs for months as an infant to restore joint alignment. I likely missed some critical motor development because of that. I also had hydrocele, which I had heard at one time is related to the feet problem pathologically, but I never got more details.

Over recent years my family is priority and movement is my passion. I need to check myself in the mirror more closely, while also trapping lightening for my loved ones.

We currently live in a suboptimal environment but some mitigations are in place. Plenty of untouched nature, hills, trails around to escape these smart metered suburbs. My 25-30 minute commute takes me to my work environment which is a research university in an EMF hellhole called Irvine, CA. Often I escape mid day and climb, crawl, swim, ground. Things are obviously on the table for major revision in terms of environment.

The Alpha UHS gaussmeter tests out .15-.25 mG average around the home. Circuit breakers in bedrooms are off almost 100% of the time, everyone sleeps on magneticos. Natural light, salt lamps, amber bulbs, and red head lamps are the main lighting. I opted out of the smart meter early last year, and before our second child was born I covered our nearest neighbors' smart meter with grounded metal screening (its only about 25-40 feet away from kids' bedrooms). Nearest cell tower is 1/2 mile away (with plenty of pine trees in between), nearest HV power lines are 1 mile away.

### JoeBranca — 2015-07-08T21:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-171409>

^-- i'm glad to get that BUN/Cr ratio down to a manageable level finally, but still needs improvement.

As far as the low T, like Jack says its got to be something about the environment. I have made a lot of improvements in local home environment, and also this year I've managed to reduce my time in the suboptimal work environment by 20% by arranging to work from home 1x/week. Also I doubt I'm lacking in UV light generally, but I may be making assumptions about how much I can get at this latitude?

So I'm thinking my action plan for the next month is getting more \*full body\* UV exposure just short of getting arrested for indecent exposure (tanning beds? hm... not sure), more CT, and even more oysters. I'm suspecting that CT and oysters in the evenings as the _standard habit_ rather than occasional, should contribute to increased T production at night. Depending if something else isn't chronically mucking up the production of sex steroid hormones (which I'm not understanding how if my HS-CRP and Vit D are decent). I think an ASI w melatonin and another serum T / free T test in a month will give me some insight. There are probably other things I allow in my environment that need mitigation, but I'll have to think about that some more.

### JoeBranca — 2015-09-01T07:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-176151>

August has been a memorable month in more ways than one, a turning of the page as I turned the age of 40. I lost my dad. He had been "not living" for quite a while, and ultimately died from what I would call severe nature deficiency, manifest in slow failures of cardiopulmonary and renal functions.. I was pretty much the lone voice in the wilderness, a role I don't much enjoy having to be.

I'm finally acting more on things Jack have been saying in so many ways since Santa Fe, that light is more fundamental than food. More than ever I have put light front and center in my "diet", further dialing in the right frequencies at the right times. And I can sense this is only the end of the beginning. Food appetite has adapted pretty quickly and effortlessly to a sort of periodic fasting.

The wife has been experiencing a noticeable downshift in breastmilk production lately, and our boy is only 8.5 months old. Told her she needs to cut out the post dinner snacking, and get her boobs out in the morning sun regularly, and grounding. She gets a fair amount of DHA regularly, I see to that, but suspecting it may not even be enough in this context. Introducing solids to the baby I suppose could be a contributor, though at least his first foods are the likes of lamb heart and liver, salmon roe, sea urchin, pork belly, etc.

Fenugreek for milk production, so I've heard? I suppose that woudn't hurt if everything else is in place, but I'm clued in enough to know premature drops in breastmilk output is not fundamentally caused by a lack of fenugreek.

In the next couple of weeks I'll retake the T hormone panel and see where things are at.

and Ubi 23-24 is melting my face off

### JoeBranca — 2015-09-09T20:47:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-176869>

Little adjustments in daily decisions to harvest more of the full spectrum sunlight in the AM. The increased light diet has downshifted food appetite, eating 1-2 meals over 2 or 3 days during the week is no big deal. Funnest way to break a fast is with my new obsession lamb brains.

This morning I was up before 5, at 6 took the 4 year old and the dogs out for a sunrise hike. I told her a story about an Aussie kangaroo assassin for hire with a broken leg who hopped along a long floating strip of sea kelp to a small island for healing... as we hiked aside the ridge of pine trees between the long shadows and long, strong strips of AM sun.

Had an interesting conversation with my wife about her highly sensitive personality and the ways in which it is not evolutionarily adaptable for the overstimulations of the modern environment.

I think its wise to also check my Vit D level as a quick and dirty way to determine whether the environment is hindering me from making use of UV light as I should be.

### JoeBranca — 2016-06-14T07:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-196536>

The power of encounters, encounters that could snowball real change beyond what could be preplanned or foreseen.

A political candidate, running for Irvine mayor, attented our dinky little church service last week. He naturally stuck aroud after stumping, kissing babies and all that. Fine.

When he approached me and I asked about his existing experience as a public official he talked about being an outsider candidate, an architect by trade living in Irvine for 18 years, an immigrant success story, concerned about overtaxation and overdevelopment in an area that cannot sustain what is now being demanded of it, etc. I asked if he would hear me out on an issue with immense public health ramifications but not commonly addressed by public officials. So I laid it all out about the potential biological health effects of EMFs and wireless tech and development, epsecially in a region like Irvine, Orange County, where you have a master planned city meant to combine suburban paradise with a world class tech sector.

Amazingly he listened with interest and some expressed concern. I asked him to check into the rising autism rates in Irvine, that he'd be shocked. I passionately raised concern about the planting of cell towers basically on school campuses and on the edges of dense neighborhoods. He asked me to point him in the direction of existing research. I told him its out there but there are industry and market forces that are involved with the direction research has went over the decades, so that the health effects remain inconclusive and the technologies deemed safe by governing bodies. He heard what I was saying with that point. He also said he has kept his cell phone away from his head since hearing about brain tumors back in the 90's.

We talked about the unhappy task of policy makers trying to balance the expectations of communities wanting all the conveniences of technology with the need to look more closely at the safety of the technology bringing these conveniences. I basically channeled Martin Blank with the precautionary principle approach. He told me I should make a presentation to city hall. I said that probably won't fly if I don't have scientific credentials or residence in Irvine. But it was an interesting idea nevertheless.

I appreciated him genuinely hearing out an unpopular issue; hopefully the links I sent him on the bioinitiative reports and martin blank and devra davis videos will plant some seeds if he ends up in office.

The same morning I discussed with my friend who's in UCI med school the Doug Wallace video I told him to watch. One of those days you sense the power of change in the air. all around. I'm not a doctor, I'm not a policy maker, but who knows when something sparks in conversation. Gotta love it.
