### JoeBranca — 2017-05-22T01:03:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-214390>

Last night I returned from one week in NOLA. This was ostensibly for a tech industry conference. There was so much more and the week could easily be described as dichotomous but in a way that moves things forward in my journey.

Beyond the training and deep dive educationals germane to my line of work, if you could boil down the overarching theme of the conference that would be

**Digital transformation of the world**

Or (my wording) the joys and wonders of creating economic opportunity in disconnecting the human species from nature

Two note worthy examples given. In the 1990's there were two rules of thumb for kids:

**1) Don't get in the cars of strangers

2) Don't meet strangers off of the Internet**​

These have been turned upside down:

**1) Literally summon strangers through the Internet to get into their car (Uber, Lyft)

2) Literally prioritize the online self promotion of strangers as relationship potential, over face to face encounters (match.com, tinder)**​

What struck me is that the atmosphere of this event was that the rapid transformation of the dynamics of human interactions because of the digital revolution at every process and control level for social and economic relationships, was not touted as either good or evil. The mood was amoral, in terms of consequences and gain vs loss in the human experience. In another sense the mood was both optimistic and opportunistic where new risks = new opportunities, more and faster change = more offloading of human labors into efficient automaton => better living, better return in life and business; in all of this the unspoken subtext is that this is to be embraced _as long as this change_ is supported by premier corporate technological entities and their profitable relationships with all the vertical markets that continue to redefine our new world.

The irony? The case studies touted were all about minimizing _risk,_ but only in terms of business continuity and immediate short term quality of life if the vertical market is somehow about public health. No surprise there.

So I came prepared for these sessions inside rooms of densely packed IT personnel under blue light and RF city:

And I spent my time wisely in this ragtag city outside of my conference commitments:

### JoeBranca — 2017-05-22T01:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-214392>

Getting down to brass tacks, this short trip reinforced the directional guidelines of my journey, that I need to be and stay all in with light, water and magnetism. It really just works and there's no joy like experiencing the simplicity of nonlinear effects of seemingly small-ish actions in the light direction resulting in compounded returns.

I could use words to convey how deeply better I feel; happier more connected and glowing inside and out even after miserable air travel and LA traffic shuttling home, sleeping like a baby with vivid dreams, waking up refreshed ready to take on the world and share that flame that so many voices try to convince me is not there to share.

I was at the beach today with my family relaxing in the sun with the "Doheny Blues Festival" performing loud enough for us to hear right up the coastline in Dana Point; and Jack Alert I was inspired to bring some hot Italian sausages, peppers onions garlic and rosemary to cook up on my camping stove right there on the sand

Sitting in the sun enjoying the warmth of the time creating heat and the warmth of family, my wife looking at me says, "You look significantly more muscular and toned in the week since you've been gone." I said all I did all week was sit in sessions, drink, eat oysters and a TON of walking around the French quarter in tank tops and shorts. Then I pointed up at the sun and smiled and nodded.

I'm driven to remain opportunistic, and what I mean is looking more more opportunities to get into the healing sun of the optimal environments. This is the real deal **N=1**

I smile at the glow and the sharing of the flame. I smile at the uncertainites ahead with the change that must come for me, and for my family. I smile at the fact that I can't control when and how the perfect time is for things to happen. ### JoeBranca — 2017-05-26T22:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-214784>

Lunch break here under the Newport Beach sun I'm reflecting on one of the many cool conversations I had with Jack when I was in NOLA last week. One topic that stuck out is that bit of a shift in emphasis in his interactions with long term members. A lot to do with relationships this last Mexico member event, compared to before when there's a lot of us figuring out to biohack our lives in tech and other related careers.

It seems like the natural evolution that the more we align with nature's principles the more we want to have coherent relationships and direct more energy to bring our loved ones to their very best.

As I spend more time healing and thinking, I see some fine tuning of my observational skills happening... not only of those around me but of myself and who I am. My wife knows the journey I am on and knows she and the kids must be part of it. But thankfully she will not allow me to unnecessarily be an ass in the process. We are not here to shove anything down the throats of our loved ones that we think is best. We really must be patient and listen, not just to words but to other forms of language in these bonds, and calibrate our own channels of energy correctly to build up rather than tear down.

A big part of my journey right now is developing the skill of when to speak and when to just listen, when to recommend and when to just do. And as we are in relationship with someone with we're together to always learn from each other, it can't be a one sided deal. We support each other and what we do and say should help set each other's stage for each own's greatest performances. For my money, nothing compares to a professional and meaningful live musical or theatrical performance; well in a relationship we are employed stage hands for each other's best productions, but we're our own composer and producer, nature the conductor. With the right crew you can make masterpieces of magic happen.

Remember to sustain my thinking along these lines, through constant change and personal evolution, life epochs, migrations, every pivotal crux...

### JoeBranca — 2017-12-06T00:28:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/far-from-the-maddening-crowds.15536/post-229592>

Jack wrote in the "Music for Mitochondriacs" thread:

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=224494)
>
> Music with a strong beat can stimulate brain waves on EEG which improve concentration and cognition, even after you've stopped listening. This is the power of sound waves because their energy can last in a fluid cell for extended periods of time because of their physics.

I distinctly remember a period around 97/98 at the end of my college years where I became a junkie for BT's music (classically trained electronica composer). I would put his beats on the headphones when plowing through studying for math finals, or preparing for job interviews after graduation, or road tripping through epic scenes. It worked for all of that, and I couldn't figure out why because I was never into techno or electronic dance before. I even ended putting up his first fan site on the web.

Well during the late 90's he had a brief contribution in a documentary about the dance / rave scene at the time - turns out he was really trying to get sound waves right:

He lives a crazy electrified life. He completely misses the breakdown comparing his experimental work in the studio with indigenous tribal rituals using acoustic waves and natural light (IR) pulsing. I still wonder how much he can offset risk by the actual resultant sound waves.

The other day on FB he said this:

I’ve made a conscious effort to push and try to force evolve the refresh rate of my listeners brains and try to induce and grow the capacity of data per second in my work (ala all the molecular attention to small bursts of nested compositional elements like micro and fractal rhythmic figures).

I think it’s also why creative people enjoy my music (people that do lots of high level abstract thinking) because they’re actually able to get more out of it and all that granularity stimulates creative thinking. It does for me at least and I’ve heard same from lots of super creative fans!
​

I replied to that comment sending him a Kruse post about acoustic phonons and told him to read Pollack.

# Thread: Question about lead and cadmium in shellfish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lead-and-cadmium-in-shellfish.15537/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eugenia — 2015-06-17T00:42:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lead-and-cadmium-in-shellfish.15537/post-168474>

So, I've been reading a number of papers and articles (and... lawsuit papers), and apparently a lot of oysters and other shellfish are accumulating lead and/or cadmium (clams seem to be the least accumulating). And this doesn't only include shellfish from China's or Korea's coasts, but also from Italy and Vancouver's, and who knows where else from too! Unfortunately, no one knows how much cadmium each oyster has, because it varies even by a few kilometers in the same coast! Please note that the amount of cadmium found in some of these oysters (e.g. these in BC, Canada) was as much as three times as the FDA daily limit! And that was the rule, not the exception in some of these parts of the same coast!

So what's the deal with that? Is it safe to eat 3-4 cans of oysters per week? Cadmium doesn't seem to have a natural detoxifying agent like Mercury does with Selenium. It accumulates, and accumulates in the body. So what to do? Just ignore it, or limit their consumption to once a week?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-17T01:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lead-and-cadmium-in-shellfish.15537/post-168479>

Cadmium in its ionic form (which is bioavailable to oysters) is complexed by chloride ions and rendered unavailable for uptake. Cadmium bioaccumulation decreases as water salinity increases. My oysters are always saltwater so I don't see it as a problem.

Cadmium poison in humans is rare and almost always pulmonary from gas. If you did get it from oysters it would have to be the renal form which is crazy rare.

-   Chronic cadmium inhalation may result in impairment of pulmonary function with obstructive changes.
-   Cadmium toxicity may cause renal dysfunction with both tubular and glomerular damage with resultant proteinuria.
-   Bone changes appear to be secondary to renal tubular dysfunction.
-   **No evidence of teratogenic effects in cadmium-exposed humans has ever been reported.**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-17T01:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lead-and-cadmium-in-shellfish.15537/post-168480>

Only series I know of with seafood etiology is in Japan. “Itai-itai” or ouch-ouch disease was first described in post-menopausal Japanese women exposed to excessive levels of cadmium over their entire lifetimes. The women were exposed through their diet because the region of Japan in which they resided was contaminated with cadmium (Ikeda _et al._ 2000; Watanabe _et al._ 2000).

# Thread: Two Autoimmune Diseases Correlated with Solar Cycles?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-autoimmune-diseases-correlated-with-solar-cycles.15538/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2015-06-17T06:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-autoimmune-diseases-correlated-with-solar-cycles.15538/post-168517>

**Interesting possible correlation between AI disease and solar flares/magnetism. But, of course, they are barking up just one leg of the three-legged stool. And how might the Maunder Minimum affect incidence of new AI case diagnoses?

Researchers correlate rheumatoid arthritis and giant cell arteritis with solar cycles**

[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-06-rheumatoid-arthritis-giant-cell-arteritis.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-06-rheumatoid-arthritis-giant-cell-arteritis.html)

"The results, published in May in _BMJ_ (formerly _British Medical Journal_) _Open_, show a "highly significant" correlation between periodic solar storms and incidences of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and giant cell arteritis (GCA), two potentially debilitating autoimmune diseases. The findings by a rare collaboration of physicists and medical researchers suggest a relationship between the solar outbursts and the incidence of these diseases . . .

"Although the authors make no claim to a causal explanation for their findings, they identify five characteristics of the disease occurrence that are not obviously explained by any of the currently leading hypotheses. These include the east-west asymmetries of the RA and GCA outbreaks and the periodicities of the incidences in concert with the [solar cycle](http://medicalxpress.com/tags/solar+cycle/). Among the possible causal pathways the authors consider are reduced production of the hormone melatonin, an anti-inflammatory mediator with immune-enhancing effects, and increased formation of free radicals in susceptible individuals. A study of 142 electrical power workers found that excretion of melatonin—a proxy used to estimate production of the hormone—was reduced by 21 percent on days with increased geomagnetic activity.

Confirming a causal link between outbreaks of RA and GCA and geomagnetic activity would be an important step towards developing strategies for mitigating the impact of the activity on susceptible individuals. These strategies could include relocating to lower latitudes and developing methods to counteract direct causal agents that may be controlled by geomagnetic activity. For now, say the authors, their findings warrant further investigations covering longer time periods, additional locations and other [autoimmune diseases](http://medicalxpress.com/tags/autoimmune+diseases/)."

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-17T13:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-autoimmune-diseases-correlated-with-solar-cycles.15538/post-168572>

I added this to the news today blog..........I think when the series is done people will understand that solar radiation and ubiquitin rates are yoked and in many diseases the relationship is very linked but transparent to medicine because they do not understand light and how our bodies interact with it.

At work I was doing some quick readings with my meter for a co-worker who works all day with a wifi transmitter just above his head!! I know that they don't take it very seriously but I was surprised when my boss actually showed a bit of concern. He asked if I had any good studies or evidence that he could look at and I said that I could find some.
I'm hoping for something that just knocks him on his ass right away or else he might overlook it and think its no big deal.
If he gets something that is really concerning I'm sure that the company will pay to do a full shop assessment from a qualified EMF technician. That would be nice!

Thanks in advance.

### Penny — 2015-06-18T07:09:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-some-real-eye-opening-easy-to-understand-emf-data-for-my-boss.15571/post-168684>

Here ya go

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9587915](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9587915)

Everything you could ever want to know about emf and calcium efflux:

[http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html)

look up: calcium efflux emf

Here's how it breaks DNA: Columbia University:
[https://www.defendershield.com/damaging-effects-emf-exposure-cell/](https://www.defendershield.com/damaging-effects-emf-exposure-cell/)

More genetic effects complements of Henry Lai:
[http://www.buergerwelle.de/assets/files/genetic\_effects\_of\_nonionizing\_electromagnetic\_fields.pdf](http://www.buergerwelle.de/assets/files/genetic_effects_of_nonionizing_electromagnetic_fields.pdf)

and Magda Havas on youtube always has some fun blood clot videos after spending 20 minutes on a cordless phone

[http://www.magdahavas.com/category/havas-video-presentations/](http://www.magdahavas.com/category/havas-video-presentations/)

Then there are the 190 scientists from around 20 different countries making a plea to the United Nations to do something about the wireless:
[http://www.businesswire.com/news/ho...cientists-Appeal-U.N.-Protect-Humans-Wildlife](http://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20150511005200/en/International-Scientists-Appeal-U.N.-Protect-Humans-Wildlife)

There's a better site Dr. K posted, but can't find it at the moment - it reads like a who's who in the molecular biology world

The Greeks at Athen University proteomics has some really interesting effects of EMF on the brain:
[http://electromagnetichealth.org/electromagnetic-health-blog/mice-proteome/](http://electromagnetichealth.org/electromagnetic-health-blog/mice-proteome/)

Let us know how you fare I find that everyone is so hung up on the thermal effects, they have trouble getting past that - but "calcium efflux" explains lots of effects -

# Thread: low carb, intermitent fasting energy crash
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2015-06-18T18:54:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/post-168771>

Up until two months ago I was always eating junk food at night.....much of which was high carb.....and I would eat quite a lot.... 8 krispy crèmes was no big deal for me. I would eat super healthy throughout the earlier parts of the day according the the epi paleo diet recommendations.....but I just kept giving in at night. Anyway, I managed not give into the cravings for a few weeks and that's about how long it took to lose the habit of eating junk at night....and so for the past 8 weeks I have been eating perfectly and intermittent fasting (16 hours without food each day). At first I felt much better as I wasn't eating so close to bed time and I also wasn't eating all the shitty ingredients that were in the gas station junk food. However, about a week ago, I noticed myself starting to get headaches and I have never been one to get headaches. Also, on the day the headaches started, I noticed my energy go down quite a bit. Fast forward one week to today and my energy is getting close to rock bottom. I have really struggled to get through my shifts at work the past two days. I have a feeling that because I haven't taken on board many recommended lifestyle changes eg. not doing CT (I do short cold showers though as don't have a bath), too much blue light exposure, and I work shift work.....I have a feeling those things have caused me not do well on my low carb epi paleo diet and intermittent fasting. Is there a good chance that my guess is correct? It would make sense why I had no energy crashes when I was pumping the carb-loaded junk foods and no headaches either....even though I didn't feel great eating the junk food I still had energy. Nothing else in my life changed that I am aware of during the time, or leading upto the time, that my energy crashed and headaches started. Also, I remember about a year and half ago when I was eating super clean epi paleo and intermittent fasting that my energy started to crash then too.....back then I thought it was ironic that my energy got worse as my eating got better....but now, as stated above, I think it is due to lifestyle factors coupled with the healthy low carb eating. I am going to increase my carbs to see if the energy comes back and headaches go away. I will do this at least until I am able to make the necessary lifestyle changes that fit in well with low carb epi paleo eating. If you have any other thoughts or suggestions please let me know. By the way, I am in Sydney, Australia and it is coming into winter here now.

### Ben1983 — 2015-06-18T19:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/post-168773>

Also, I'll mention that I haven't been eating any coconut oil as it makes my stomach feel upset every time I do. My primary fat has been ghee.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-18T19:53:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/post-168783>

you live in Southern hemisphere? Why CO now at winter solstice?

### Ben1983 — 2015-06-18T20:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/post-168791>

### WereBear — 2015-07-08T18:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-intermitent-fasting-energy-crash.15611/post-171396>

I can sympathize. I was desperate and ate carbs, but now that I'm further along the protocol, with greatly improved sleep, I can IF without problems.

I cannot do much in the way of CT because it makes me too fatigued, but I'm confident I can work up to it. And sleep seems to be a real problem for you; which is preventing your body from working as it should.

# Thread: Fractals in space and time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fractals-in-space-and-time.15612/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2015-06-18T18:59:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fractals-in-space-and-time.15612/post-168772>

Jack says he's a virus. That's on the cosmological scale. But he's also a planet, maybe even a galaxy on the microscopic and quantum levels. We all are. Completely depends on your scale of focus. One's purposes and goals depend primarily on the scale one chooses to focus on. Some of us have acquired the ability to adjust our mental focus to zoom in and zoom out of these different fractals more effortlessly than others, in both spacial and temporal domains.

So, do you want to tend to your galaxy or do you want to infect the universe? I can't tell you the answer, but I do believe that it is the most fundamental ethical question a person has to answer.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-18T19:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fractals-in-space-and-time.15612/post-168782>

epistemology will determine response.

### NeilBB — 2015-06-19T01:19:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fractals-in-space-and-time.15612/post-168820>

I have long thought that ethics followed directly from epistemology, Jack. Rand certainly thought that. Most philosophers strongly disagree and maintain that there is no way to scientifically derive an "ought" from an "is." But the whole truth is much deeper than any of those philosophers, Rand included, ever imagined. And the full answer has not been clearly demonstrated... Yet...

[http://www.shaolindisciplesunion.com/DAMO.html](http://www.shaolindisciplesunion.com/DAMO.html)

^^^^ a case of sacrificing the micro for the macro when really the large is built from the small and the small is influenced by the large. In this case the monks had grown to disregard the needs of the body, even to despise this mortal coil, in pursuit of higher goals.

Jack has often said there is no mind/body effect - they are inseperable - just like the sub atomic is inseperable from its environment.

Jack calls us starfish. HE's picking each one of us, and teaching us how to change our current universe, then tossing us back into the sea, to change others through our personal evolution's. He's not just fishing for us, but teaching us how to fish, as well as teaching us how to teach others.

we come from the stars and will returns to the stars. I am but a fractal of a larger, more complex system of fractals that are working in harmony. ### WereBear — 2015-06-20T18:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169052>

I've never understood why they fight so hard against things that _do no harm_. They will claim people will "rely on that" instead of what they have to offer, but when what they have to offer _doesn't work_, their argument falls apart.

I encountered this myself when I had a cancer scare and they pressured me into signing away permission to do a complete removal of some important hormone making machinery based on "surgeon's assessment and frozen section." They were utterly floored when I went away, did my homework, and showed back up the day before the surgery to retract the permission, allowing them only to take the one, damaged, organ, and I fortunately had a spare. This still plunged me into sudden menopause and started the cascade of events that landed me here.

It would have been worse if I'd just listened to them. I'm glad I did not.

### Daina — 2015-06-23T20:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169395>

> [WereBear said:](/goto/post?id=169052)
>
> I've never understood why they fight so hard against things that _do no harm_. They will claim people will "rely on that" instead of what they have to offer, but when what they have to offer _doesn't work_, their argument falls apart.
>
> I encountered this myself when I had a cancer scare and they pressured me into signing away permission to do a complete removal of some important hormone making machinery based on "surgeon's assessment and frozen section." They were utterly floored when I went away, did my homework, and showed back up the day before the surgery to retract the permission, allowing them only to take the one, damaged, organ, and I fortunately had a spare. This still plunged me into sudden menopause and started the cascade of events that landed me here.
>
> It would have been worse if I'd just listened to them. I'm glad I did not.

Nearly the same thing happened to me WereBear. A few years ago, I think maybe 7 years, I started bleeding during a cycle profusely, and my body was producing bloody tissue, that contained liver-like thick tissue. I had a history of fibroid cysts, but they were not active mostly. My sister passed from ovarian cancer in 2004. My ob/gyn sent me to get sonograms and labs and referred me to an ob/oncologist. They took more blood work and told me my CA125 was elevated. During this time of my life I was doing a natural herbal cleanse once a year, sometimes twice a year, which kept me feeling almost normal. Until this. With the elevated CA125 and the history of ovarian cancer, even though my numbers were higher, but not that high, the oncologists recommended a full hysterectomy. I heard him out, then politely asked what were my other options. I was told that I could wait it out and see if the numbers came down, but that my best bet would be to have the surgery to 'eliminate' the chance of ovarian cancer. I told him that after my sisters ovaries were removed, her 'cancer' progressed to other organs in her body. My question for him was, isn't it true that the cancer is really at the cellular level and moving in the blood? And therefore could he 'guarantee' me that by removing my 'ovaries' I wouldn't get ovarian cancer? And if he couldn't guarantee that, why would I get my ovaries removed when I knew that my ovaries worked in conjunction with my thyroid and endocrine system? And if a part was removed, how would my system work properly? He said he could guarantee nothing. I thanked him for his honesty.

At this point he asked me if I were a nurse; I said no, just a patient who tries to do her research before deciding to do anything drastic. I then proceeded to tell him my choice. I opted for what he called 'Conservative Treatment'. I told him that I was going to Italy on vacation in 2 weeks for a walking tour with my daughter. I was due for my herbal cleanse that would last a month. I needed to clean up what I was eating that may have been causing and contributing to my cysts being aggravated, and that I would see him in 6 months for a retest of my CA125(which I knew would come down. Once he explained that the marker is an indicator of inflammation in the body and that it could be ANY part of the body contributing to the number, not just the ovaries. It just happened that there were external signs for the ovaries like the bleeding, abdominal pain, and so forth). We ordered the BRAC(?) to make sure I didn't have any genetic predispositions. Went back in 6 months. CA125 down. To this day I still have my ovaries. I visited that oncologist every 6 months for about 3-4 years, and then once a year for a couple of years. They kept expecting my numbers to change and go up. They never did; always the same or down some more . Each appointment I would ask questions about food, health, and the connection of his 'specialty organ' to my other organs on that system. He knew some stuff, but not enough. Certainly not enough for me to sanction any type of operation. He was probably a brilliant surgeon, but going to the oncologist was also very depressing, as many women I saw in the waiting room were very sick, taking treatment, and didn't seem to me to get better. Nobody was pushing healthy eating and vitamins and supplements. They couldn't believe at my age I wasn't on a slew of medications.

I applaud you for reversing your permission and saving your organ.

I mourn with you the effect of the many problems that operation caused, as it happened that way for my sister. We just didn't know enough and were swimming truly upstream for a cure. And I was the baby sister, not a doctor. I knew something was wrong with her 'system', but could not articulate to her what needed to be done on a consistent basis to correct her system. You are tough and brave, a true warrior!

### Gracelind — 2015-06-23T22:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169397>

Hi Daina,

My condolences on the loss of your sister. 2004 was the year my mother was diagnosed with OVCA at age 80. I did all the reading I could at that time, joined several forums. (It's sobering when you see the reason women leave the forum is because they die.) My mother said she would follow the oncologist's advice and so didn't heed the wisdom of eliminating sugar, dairy and gluten. She rarely went out into the sun. Stayed up late at night. Many classic activities that we now know are so counter-productive. She lasted through 3 chemo rounds and 2 1/2 years.

Many of your symptoms are the same as I had, too. With the elevated CA-125 my uterus was enlarged and the doc suggested hysterectomy. I resisted. With menopause CA-125 is fine, uterus normal size, everything still intact. Routine TV-US have all been normal. Before finding this website I'd already been on an anti-inflammatory diet, now refined to Epi-Paleo. It makes such a difference! Even when I was put on 60mg prednisone then tapered down to 50 then eventually down to 15 I didn't gain any weight, had minimal bloating.

How I wish we had that time back to help the ones we've lost to that horrible disease.

### WereBear — 2015-06-24T02:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169403>

I'm so sorry for your losses, Daina and Gracelind.

> [Daina said:](/goto/post?id=169395)
>
> You are tough and brave, a true warrior!

Thank you, though I didn't have the added burden of losing a blood family member. It probably helped me be a bit more clear-headed.

Their way, they had a 1-2% cure rate. (I found this out by reading the papers doctors wrote for each other.) So my plan was to skip them entirely and do a ketogenic diet. Fortunately, it didn't come to that. But I had discussed it with my husband and we were totally serious about going that route.

### smart cat — 2015-06-24T23:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169493>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=168784)
>
> [http://complete-health-and-happiness.com/burzynski-cancer-cure-finally-released-by-the-feds/](http://complete-health-and-happiness.com/burzynski-cancer-cure-finally-released-by-the-feds/)

Burzynski? Really? This is an infomercial. They don't even get the facts right. If you check the study the Burzynski Research Institute talks about in the press release it is not very impressive. Others aren’t either. Burzynski's own 13 pubmed papers are only hypothesis and case reports (cherry picking anecdotical evidence. )
[http://www.prnewswire.com/news-rele...on-adenocarcinoma-of-the-liver-300056848.html](http://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/burzynski-research-institute-inc-announces-publication-of-phase-2-clinical-study-in-metastatic-colon-adenocarcinoma-of-the-liver-300056848.html)
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Stanislaw Burzynski\[Author\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Stanislaw%20Burzynski[Author])
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4366171/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4366171/)
[http://www.mayoclinicproceedings.org/article/S0025-6196(11)63835-4/abstract](http://www.mayoclinicproceedings.org/article/S0025-6196%2811%2963835-4/abstract)

There is the dubious practice in Big Pharma and cancer industry. But there is also weird stuff in the "Alternative Medicine Business". I smelled something fishy with all the hype about this polish "hero". If something sounds too good to be true, maybe it isn't true. My BS radar warned me.

I dont't like the sceptic's agenda at all (Mainstream Medicine is good, everything else is BS), but sometimes the guys are helpful.
It's one thing to waste money with useless but harmless therapies. It's a completely different animal to delude desperate cancer patients. This is not only unethical but unbelievable. First thing: Burzynski's narrative - one brave doctor fights against the cancer industry- is just a story. Made up by people who need such things. Like the pop culture narratives. What he does is the usual thing: chemotherapy with nasty side effects. His wonder peptide derivate is not approved by the FDA to treat anything because none of Burzynski's trials were ever completed. And it has nasty side effects. Most patients get the approved cancer drugs and other chemicals.

### smart cat — 2015-06-24T23:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169494>

**Food for thought is all over the internet.**

"Once patients arrived at Burzynski's office, the board says, **he misled them in several ways:**

• By making patients pay a retainer **before receiving any diagnosis or treatment.**
• By imposing "exorbitant charges" for drugs and lab tests, without telling patients that he also owned the pharmacy and lab being used.
• By **allowing unlicensed staff to treat patients, while describing the staff as doctors.**
Burzynski also prescribed **unapproved combinations of highly toxic chemotherapy** in ways that caused harm to several patients, the board says."

[http://www.usatoday.com/story/news/nation/2014/07/24/new-charges-for-burzynski/13111483/](http://www.usatoday.com/story/news/nation/2014/07/24/new-charges-for-burzynski/13111483/)

“A family who sent their terminally ill daughter to the US for experimental treatment said the clinic **misled them about her chances of survival.**
The parents of four-year-old Amelia Saunders who died in January, say they were told she had a 54 per cent chance of survival with the clinical trial in Houston, Texas.

A **BBC Panorama investigation** shown on Monday questioned whether the Burzynski Clinic was “selling hope” to families.

"I think the figures Panorama were given show there was **700-odd patients they treated and something like 15 per cent survived over five years.** If you look at all brain tumours **that’s almost identical to any other treatment.**
In November last year Dr Burzynski told the family a new scan showed Amelia’s tumour was breaking down. UK doctors at Great Ormond Street Hospital in London said it actually showed **the tumour was continuing to grow.**
We asked repeatedly throughout the time Amelia was under treatment for **evidence to back up his claims, and he at no point produced this."**
[http://www.getreading.co.uk/news/amelia-saunders-family-mislead-burzynski-4051287](http://www.getreading.co.uk/news/amelia-saunders-family-mislead-burzynski-4051287)

"The FDA report also observed that patients were treated **despite having dangerous side effects that would require removing them from the study**. The FDA observed that several patients were not properly terminated from the study despite **life threatening electrolyte problems** The Clinic also failed to **properly report severe adverse events,** such as swelling in the brain and pneumonia.

Finally, to add insult to injury, the Clinic **failed to properly inform patients of costs before enrolling them, potentially leaving patients with huge, unanticipated medical bills.**

The Clinic, “…failed to comply with protocol requirements related to the primary outcome, therapeutic response” This included **incorrectly listing patient as being in remission (having a “complete response” to treatment); incorrectly listing patients as having a “partial response” to treatment.** This is terrifying. Incorrectly assessing a response to treatment gives patients false hopes and makes data look more favorable than they really are."
[http://www.forbes.com/sites/peterli...isturbing-picture-of-burzynski-cancer-clinic/](http://www.forbes.com/sites/peterlipson/2013/11/11/fda-documents-paint-disturbing-picture-of-burzynski-cancer-clinic/)

### smart cat — 2015-06-24T23:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169495>

**The OTHER Burzynski patient group**
[https://theotherburzynskipatientgroup.wordpress.com/](https://theotherburzynskipatientgroup.wordpress.com/)

"because a powerful Representative, **Rep. Joe Barton** (R-TX), was putting **near constant pressure on the FDA** for “persecuting” Burzynski, he was given a golden opportunity by the FDA to “legitimize” his quackery. Basically, **the FDA caved and agreed to let Burzynski do clinical trials.** Here’s what **Burzynski’s lawyer, Richard Jaffe**, [later wrote about those clinical trials](http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2013/07/22/how-stanislaw-burzynski-became-burzynski-the-brave-maverick-doctor-part-1/):

_So we decided to hit the FDA with everything at the same time. All of his current patients would be covered in a single clinical trial which Burzynski called “CAN-1.” **As far as clinical trials go, it was a joke.** Clinical trials are supposed to be designed to test the safety or efficacy of a drug for a disease. It is almost always the case that clinical trials treat one disease.
The CAN-1 protocol had almost two hundred patients in it and there were at least a dozen different types of cancers being treated. And since all the patients were already on treatment, **there could not be any possibility of meaningful data coming out of the so-called clinical trial.** It was all an artifice, a vehicle_ we and the FDA created to legally give the patients Burzynski’s treatment_. The FDA wanted all of Burzynski’s patients to be on an IND, so that’s what we did._
So what Burzynski did was to [completely abuse the clinical trial process](http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2013/12/12/the-drip-drip-drip-drip-of-fda-findings-against-stanislaw-burzynski-continues/), **charging enormous sums of money** to enroll desperate patients in his clinical trials. Burzynski had just suffered the PR hit of a [huge article in USA TODAY](http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2013/11/15/stanislaw-burzynski-in-usa-today-abuse-of-clinical-trials-and-patients-versus-the-ineffectiveness-of-the-fda-and-texas-medical-board/) documenting how the death of a child as a complication of his antineoplaston therapy had resulted in an **FDA site visit and investigation, as well as a partial clinical hold,** first on all his pediatric clinical trials, then on all his adult clinical trials as well.
[http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2014/10/30/r-i-p-mckenzie-lowe-stanislaw-burzynski-failed-you/](http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2014/10/30/r-i-p-mckenzie-lowe-stanislaw-burzynski-failed-you/)

"Dr. Burzynski was claiming that **he could identify who would benefit from specific targeted therapies simply from blood tests.** If he could do this for real, Burzynski could easily publish in high impact journals like [Clinical Cancer Research](http://clincancerres.aacrjournals.org/), the [Journal of Clinical Oncology](http://jco.ascopubs.org), or another high impact clinical cancer journal. Heck, a result like that could probably make it into general medical journals, such as the [New England Journal of Medicine](http://www.nejm.org) or [The Lancet](http://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/issue/current), which have an even higher impact factor. If he were able to demonstrate that his method of testing tumors and picking targeted therapy could result in a complete response rate anywhere near 85% for breast cancer, even more so. If, as he claims later in the chapter, Dr. Burzynski has patients with pancreatic cancer and advanced liver cancer whose **tumors have disappeared within two months after he began treatment**, the same would be true. If, as Burzynski claims, he achieves a **50% complete response rate in advanced brain tumors,** again, the same would be true. He doesn’t submit his results to these journals. Why not? "
[http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2011/12/05/personalized-gene-targeted-cancer-therapy/](http://scienceblogs.com/insolence/2011/12/05/personalized-gene-targeted-cancer-therapy/)

### smart cat — 2015-06-25T00:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-medicine-works-when-you-step-out-of-line.15614/post-169501>

PS: I was diagnosed with melanoma at the age of 28. The literature said: 5 years survival rate. The doc at university hospital who did the MRI didn't believe I would survive. I knew for sure that I would never do crippling surgery, toxic chemo or harmful radiation if things got worse. And the literature said it probably will get worse (metastases). It didn't. A tiny little dose of ruthenium applied on a small part of my choroid did the trick. Side effects: a bit of "skin damage" and a bruised optic nerve. I can't see much any more with my left eye but without radiation the poor thing would have been more damaged so the decision was easy.

I lived with the possibility that I may be dead in 3 or 5 years. This was good. I did what I always wanted to do. As much as I could. I knew that nobody can "heal" cancer. So "Alternative Medicine" would be out too. And the last thing I would do is forking out 30,000 or 100,000 bucks which I don't have anyway for toxic stuff pumped into my body to live 1 or 3 years longer (miserable years because of damage from treatment).
But if someone believes in mainstream medicine and has business to finish, living 3 years longer with surgery and chemo makes total sense. At least if he can decide when it is time to go. For Wolfgang Herrndorf it was the point where he couldn't think, write and talk normally. He had a pistol as exit strategy. He could't have paid Burzynski anyway. No melodramatic media hype for a modestly successful author.
[http://berlinbooks.org/brb/2014/09/work-and-structure/](http://berlinbooks.org/brb/2014/09/work-and-structure/)

# Thread: Raw oysters- The more the better?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-19T02:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168836>

This may be a dumb question but I find chucking back the raw oysters very fast, easy and convenient.
Plus if I eat enough of them they seem to improve my mood.

I tried the raw shrimp smoothie thing and I had a fairly uncomfortable reaction to it.

The oysters on the other hand I could eat a ton of them!

Could "the dose make the toxin" when it comes to oysters, or is " more better"?

### Lahelada — 2015-06-19T03:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168842>

More is better in this case. Oysters beat shrimp hands down.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-19T03:54:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168855>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=168842)
>
> More is better in this case. Oysters beat shrimp hands down.

That's kind of what I thought. And that makes it really easy for me. It looks like I've been cleaning out the local grocery stores though (LOL!).

When you're chronically tired, a meal that takes no preparation is always great as long as its healthy. And in this case it looks like nothing beats it.

Thanks for the reply!

### nicld — 2015-06-19T17:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168941>

Lucky, I can't find fresh raw oysters here. "People may get sick from them" is what I was told at a local grocery store

### lilreddgirl — 2015-06-19T21:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168964>

I do get 'sick' from them if they're raw - even raw ones in a jar - and basically it's the awesomest 'sick' ever. I wind up purging bile stones out 3-4 times a day for the next couple days and then my tummy even looks concave lol.

I'm going to get more right now!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-20T02:23:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-168979>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=168941)
>
> Lucky, I can't find fresh raw oysters here. "People may get sick from them" is what I was told at a local grocery store

I noticed when I first started eating them earlier this year I didn't feel very good afterwards.
I wasn't 'sick' but my mood and energy level was kind of crappy. Interestingly I felt the same after my first few sessions of CT. But now it seems they actually improve my mood but I have to eat quite a bit of them.
I'm sure in time I won't have to eat much of them at all. (knock on wood...)

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-26T22:36:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-oysters-the-more-the-better.15616/post-207401>

Ok so I like raw oysters but now I seem to be having some issues with them. I got sick about amonth ago was puking my guts out. Every time i ate them after i would have upset stomach or loose stools. I took a break for a month and tried again the other night and still im getting noxious and loose stools. I have symptoms for 6 hours afterwards. Is there anyway to hack these symptoms ive tried some digestive aids like Now super enzymes, ox bile and HCL and doesnt seem to do anything. I dont wanna stop eating them but man its getting rough. ### Rocky — 2017-01-30T16:26:50+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-30T17:07:52+0200

# Thread: Another from down under
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-19T08:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168903>

It has taken me a few monthes but I need to stop lurking as I have almost caught up to the current blog series and would love to introduce myself and share a little of my story.
My name is Josh and I live on the Gold Coast in Australia. To say the last 6 months has been a huge game changer to myself and family would be an understantment. I grew up in Melbourne and in 1998 at the age of 23 got diagnosed with Ankylosing spondylitis (rheumatoid arthritis). Being young this turned my world up side down and only serious meds could control the pain. The side effect somehow outweighed the symptoms and without the knowledge I have gained since I still somehow intuitivly knew there were better paths to follow. I begain changing what I thought at the time was a healthy enviroment, specifically diet and within 6 months was off all meds. I was by no means cured but was making big strides in the right direction. This is where my passion for finding true health began. I continued self studying alternative health but alot of what I learnt was based on the body being a sum of parts and not a system. It wasn't completly resenating with me.
The next significant change was 10 years ago when I moved to queensland, not because of a better enviroment but both my parents were diagnosed with cancer and needed support. They both chose the convential route and sadly passed away within 2 years of each other. This was hugly emotional time for myself and my health declined. I strenthened my resolve to know better and studied intergrative health. Things steadily improved until I came across Dr Kruse about 8 monthes ago and since then my health has reached another level. My energy levels are great, I sleep well and am very excited about the future. The pure scale of the which the body is looked at is something I feel I have long been searching for.
An average week now consists of daily ocean swims, regular seafood binges and walking around in a homemade ice vest from compression material. Lights are off at dusk and switchboard goes off by 10pm. I find myself reading Dr K's blogs at every opportunity. I think it's an addiction!
I also have a wonderful wife who supports our changes and is managing her own thunderstorm and 2 amazing little epi paleo toddlers who embrace the cold and eat raw oysters!. My kids are extra special after being told my auto immune meds would keep my sterile.
Now just turned 40 my biggest issue is that I fly twice a week for work but plan to change that in the near future. I still have alot of work to do and better choices to make.
I am hoping to join as a member soon and also start a journal.
I have many questions relating to my personal context and will post them in the relevent sections.
Thanks again to all the great people here and hope to meet you one day and to Dr Kruse you are a true visionary and thank you.

### caroline — 2015-06-19T10:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168907>

Welcome Josh ...lots of Aussies here!

### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-19T12:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168914>

Hi guys,

Thanks for the greetings. I have noticed a few aussies here. It's been interesting to read about the challanges of living down this way. So much to take in.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-19T13:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168920>

Welcome. Good to have another Josh on board! (We have a couple already )

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-19T14:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168926>

Welcome........History shows that troublemakers are the innovators and provocateurs. If you want new you must stop doing old. Innovation favors the connected mind. Innovation is one's reflection, a coherent plan for action, while being the only spectacle to see the reality of our dreams. Dreams are where new facts meet new values to bear creative fruit. Creativity must be shared with the world to fully manifest. Why should you risk sharing your precious ideas? Because you know a better idea might surface after the initial plunge. An idea, like a wave, when thrown into a bigger body of water creates a powerful wave of momentum that might change the world. Our success rises and falls on human connections, not on wisdom alone.

### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-19T15:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168931>

Thank you Jack. This is so true. I have learnt the hard way that to get a different result, you need to look at things differently. I think your ideas are manifesting and changing many lives. I must say you hurt my head on a regular but I am very grateful for it.

### nonchalant — 2015-06-19T16:38:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168934>

Welcome, JoshT! Sounds like you've made some great changes. Nice to hear your family is benefiting also!

### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-20T00:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-168969>

Thanks nonchalant. I still have a long way to go but definately feel I'm at the right place to fill in the missing pieces. I always felt better living here as apposed to where I was better never knew why until recently.

### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-20T06:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-169005>

Jack, your comments have given me alot to consider which goes beyond my own health. There are aeras that I need to grow and you have thrown a stone into my pond.
Thank you.

### DavidL — 2015-06-20T08:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-169015>

Welcome. I love that u confess lurking. Lol. J has a way with words that find resonance deep within the soul.

### Josh Thomas — 2015-06-21T02:12:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-from-down-under.15639/post-169099>

Dave....lol I think the first step in being a reformed lurker is admitting it in the first place. Congrats on your journey with Vigr. Your products sound great. Elk antler is something forgein to us down here but looks to provide a lot of benefits. I am in the process of launching a range of Kombuchca infused with sea minerals. Just in the early stages but getting great feedback. Great to touch base with you.

### cyberkyoobun — 2015-06-29T04:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sunny-and-hot-south-florida.15653/post-169807>

> [JenRN said:](/goto/post?id=169790)
>
> Welcome! Lowering blood pressure is part of my goal as well. I'm just getting started but relying more on vitamin D supplements because I'd need over an hour daily of full body sun exposure to hit goal with my skin color. I am originally from Florida and love the way the sun feels there. I miss the gentle breezes and light summer rains, not so much the hurricanes though! Anyway, I will be posting the lab results periodically, hopefully you can too?

Good evening JenRN,
Hypertension is only one of my symptoms. My challenges also include obesity and Hashimotos. At present, I have been studying a lot of material. I read Jack's book and have ordered other books he has recommended. I've eliminated gluten and nightshades and greatly reduced my intake of carbohydrates. I am reviewing Jack's book to determine which supplements I will begin taking. I've been taking Vit D3 and K2 for a year. The bottom line is that the acquisition of knowledge is always an ongoing work in progress. One of my areas of doubt is bio-identical hormone replacement. I was seeing a physician who prescribed hormones and Naturethroid. Then . . . I succumbed to fear as my PCP and my Gynecologist vehemently recommended that I cease the hormone replacement. I was feeling wonderful with the hormones and my fear based thinking lead me to stop taking the hormones. Since I stopped taking the hormones I feel that I have aged 20 years. I'm back to feeling poorly. The topic of hormones or no hormones is a big issue for me as there is very little clear information available on this subject. However, I will share my weight loss, lab results when I am tested again. I am happy to have found Jack and this site as I no longer feel alone in my quest to achieve optimal health. Knowledge does indeed protect and ignorance endangers.

Are you a Registered Nurse?

Best,

### cyberkyoobun — 2015-06-29T04:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sunny-and-hot-south-florida.15653/post-169808>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=169804)
>
> welcome.........just got home from cruise........

Thank you for the warm welcome! Kruse does the Cruise? Good for you!

# Thread: Lupus and Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Esa — 2015-06-20T12:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169021>

Hi all,

Recently I found out about dr Kruse and his work. And all he said resonated strongly for me.

I have lupus for almost 22 years and in that time frame I had some horrible thing happening to me. And to make long story short my situation right now is:
\-I don't have a hair on two big areas on the back of my head
\-My antinuclear and anti DNA antibodies are sky high for 7 years
\-I'm off grains and off diary (except butter) for 10 years (paleo)
\-And I have scars on my hands (healed vasculitis) and on my face.

OK, scars are treated, dissapearring slowly. Hair is not growing and I feel bad. This winter I was swollen, especially my fingers and I said -Aha, that's normal in the winter. Water element in Chinese traditional medicine, everything will be fine in spring.
But spring was late here in southeast Europe so I could hardly wait for Sun to come. Then I got out to get some tan...

In summer 2000. in four days my fingers swell and deformed because I had to bring home my child from kindergarten. Sun was so strong and temperature was 38 C. For next 7 years my fingers will remain deformed. And in 2 months after I stopped eating wheat (and grains) they've returned to normal. And it was electricity experience, in just few seconds impuls in fingers was terrible and day after that I've noticed my fingers are finally normal, and vasculitis (blood vessels inflamation) healed.
(Sorry, English isn't my native language.)

In 2010. my tested levels of vitamin D3 were 10 times below normal.

This summer for two weeks I went out on the Sun, I was on the granite bench, 5 minutes front side, 5 minutes back, and again (20 minutes total), started showering with cold water and usually I have 1 raw egg and lots of butter for breakfast, 60gr of dark chocolate, and 1 big piece of beef liver (cooked in boiling water for one minute) with few pieces of tomato and cucumber and half of an onion for lunch. Or my other lunch was sea food, or sea fish with seaweeds.
My goal was to eat 500gr of butter in one week, but I was close.
And I'm on 5mg of prednisone and the only supplement I use these days is plain powdered sulfur (not MSM).

I have low cholesterol so I thought butter+sulfur+Sun=human photosynthesis=cholesterol

And what happened?

I've started gaining weight so I stopped with so much butter. And my fingers swell again. I think it's the Sun. Few nights ago in my sleep I could sense (I could see) something like star explosion in my finger joints (knuckles?). Tomorrow pain was horrible.

What am I dong wrong?
Can dr Kruse or anyone else help me with answers - what to do to stop swelling and what to do not to gain weight?
And what to do to heal my body so my immune system doesn't have to work so hard?
Thank you

### Dia — 2015-06-20T16:15:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169039>

Hi Esa! Dr Kruse and some of the other more knowledgeable members here may chime in, but I noticed you haven't mentioned anything about DHA, or fish above... Last summer something similar was going on with my daughter (18). Couldn't tolerate the sun at all. It would cause her eyes to water , dizziness, burning skin, migraines, joint pain.... she was having panic attacks, fainting spells, and a whole lot of other issues. Took her to six doctors, all wanted to put her on meds for depression, bipolar, and psych meds (she was suicidal). Suffice it to say it was bad! I have been following Dr K since 2012, so have read his blogs, and practice what he teaches. Of course my family thought I was mad! I knew I was sane, so I never listened to all their berating. Anyway, I got her to do the things I was doing.... giving her lots of fish, raw oyster, smoked salmon, two to three times per day. Got her to do CT, got her a magnetico, blue blockers, get her to bed by ten (she use to stay up till midnight). Turned off wifi, limit her phone use. Got her to ground. Got her to do the redox protocol, lots of epsom salt with CT. It was a chore and a half. It took a while... but within five months, she was a new person! Now, she continues to do it! She loves the way she feels! And my family doesn't thing I am a mad person anymore. I am so happy for her, and so happy I had the knowledge that Dr K provided here so I could help her. She gets her morning sun and evening sun everyday, and CT almost daily. She is back to school now. Wants to be a ND.

Moral of the story, read all the blogs here. You are at the right place! UBI blogs are priceless! Eat lot of DHA, and do CT. You seem to be doing the sulfur, but you will not be able to turn the sunlight into a DC current for the body, if the DHA isn't present. The body cannot heal itself without that DC current. It is that important. And good water too btw. The answers are all here. Take it slow, implement, and give it time. All the best!

### Dia — 2015-06-20T16:38:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169040>

I noticed you DID mention seafood above.... I'm guessing you may need to eat more than you are right now. We have always eaten seafood at home (two or three time a week), and yet my daughter still got ill. It wasn't until I started giving her seafood everyday, some days two or three time a day, that I noticed a change in her. Of course we did a lot of other things that were beneficial to her as well. It's possible you don't have enough DHA in the cells.... , and you also need to mitigate nnEMF

### Lahelada — 2015-06-20T18:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169046>

Welcome . You do not mention water. Sun exposure without hydration is difficult. I used to burn in the sun,stay redand then go pale again. This summer I managed my first tan since being a teenager I think. I swear that it was the water and the spry of Epsom salt water I subjected myself too. In the UBiquination 17 blog the doc talks about epsom salt /magnesium sulfate being an important helper.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-20T18:13:31+0300

Ah ,I mean water combined with DHA,oysters by preference. I just saw on your profile that you are a girl. Iodine and lack thereof plays havoc with us girls. Don't supplement though. Again the answer is fish and if you can swing it oysters.

### Esa — 2015-06-21T18:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169173>

Dia, thank you. I could only wish my daughter would go to bed earlier  How I wish she'd listen to me as your child. Often I find her sleeping with smartphone by her head. And she knows all do and don't of health and EM fields, but... nothing.
My problem is Prednisone therapy. It's corticosteroid medicine and I've been using it for such a long time. My doctor wants me on higher doses, but I already have cataracts and I have to strictly pay attention to food. So two meals a day with one (chocolate) snack and that is all.
I could do sea foods and fish for breakfast too, but that would mean no eggs or nuts or fruits sometimes. And if it works ok, I'll do it. But, is there anything else I'm missing? ..Ok, I'll try just sea foods (including fish, oysters, those small things we call here frutti di mare and seaweeds).

Lahelada, thank you too. The only spring water here is sold in plastic bottles, so my other solutions are distilled and plain water. My country (Serbia) is not doing fluorine, but water is chlorinated. I drink herbal infusions where I pour 1l of boiling water over 30 gr of dry nettle leaves (30gr dandelion roots, 15gr linden flowers, few other herbs) and put the lead on the jar for next 4 hours. Then I strain it and drink. It is amazing elixir full of minerals, great for rejuvenation, cheaper than any vitamin and mineral complex. And I've learned about those nourishing herbal infusions from well-known American lady herbalist.
You both girls can try those (nettle and linden especially).
I use Dead sea salt. It has magnesium also. And I use magnesium chloride as lotion. And for the last 20 years I've used sooo many supplements and the only thing I wish now is to never see them again. And Chinese doctor gave me black ants... and homeophaty... and so many thing.
But thank you anyway, any help is appreciated

### Esa — 2015-06-22T14:27:49+0300

Thank you and sorry, I've just put my pic
Started to read about redox. So, hake fish is pouring from my ears right now, but I'll manage.
Next questions is grilled, cooked, baked or raw - does it make difference?, but I'll go trough website, blog and forum, maybe someone already asked the same.

And I'll post later how things are going.
Thank you all

### Lahelada — 2015-06-22T14:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169251>

How you cook your fish makes a difference. Raw is best as it keeps its DHA intact. Cooked is fine but try not to add too much plant derived oil. A splash of extra vrgin olive oil is fine . Bsicqlly the idea is to buil up you r body's DHA levels which is an omega 3 fat .If you add too much omega 6 you keep the gap open so to speak.

Great breakfast and I know about the fish translatuion problem. I find ome really lurid fish names in english sometimes only to find out that they avw quite common names in English as they are an export item from here. Wonder if your hake hails from our shores.

### kovita — 2015-06-22T15:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169252>

Hi Esa, my brother died of lupus aged 12 and my father is in remission since 1979 when he had discoid form. There are at least two suspicious deaths in my father's family 2 generations before me. I can understand your pain. I have chronic lyme and lupus as diagnosis always hangs above me. I was pretty sure I am alrady there last spring. Jusy be firm and change your life and take it fast where it is possible (location, DHA, light...) amd slow where you have to start slowly. It will pay off and you will get well!

### Esa — 2015-06-23T17:02:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-and-sun.15681/post-169387>

Lahelada, no, no oil. Just cooked it in little water with few pinches of oregano as spice. Olive oil is good, but it costs a lot. Small bottle is more than 10 euros here, and if it is cheaper it's not olive oil but some kind of fraud. So... to spare myself of thinking I'm not using it. Next time I'll try fish with butter or maybe coconut oil.
Yesterday was good, I lost almost 1kg of my weight just losing liquid (going to toilet) and my fingers are thinner. But after cold shower in the evening I got slightly higher temperature for a while. I'll watch how things are going and I'll post in month or two is there anything different.

Hi Kovita, location is dictated by financial situation. So, I won't be moving soon
I'm sorry about your brother... My brother was always big, healthy man and in less than 6 months he died from lung cancer. We can't choose the date, but I'd like to live a real life till I'm alive.
