# Thread: Journal for Chris
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-chris.15694/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daedalus351 — 2015-06-21T13:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-chris.15694/post-169130>

I'm going to try and cover as much here as I can think of right now but I'm bound to forget something so I'll add anything else when I think of it. I'm not sure if there is a template for the journal, but it's something that might be useful.

Some of my history:

\* Cesarean birth, breastfeed till I was 1 where I weaned myself. Was given goats milk for 6 months and then on to cows milk.

\* Always been fairly thin until my 20s. Never had a huge amount of muscle mass.

\* At about 10 or 11 years of age my parents and teachers noticed I became less happy and more introverted. I've always been a nervous and shy person.

\* At 15 I noticed that my memory was getting worse and my energy and motivation reduced. I struggled to get out of bed in the morning.

\* My late 20s were quite good. I was working and had a decent amount of energy, although still had low grade depression that started in my teens. I think the energy and feeling good was probably due to starting to drink coffee. I put on weight though. At my highest I was 93Kg. I'm 183cm tall.

\* I am a software developer in an office building in the Brisbane CBD.

\* In 2013 I started getting anxious for no reason and was feeling a lot more stressed than usual. In August 2013 I had a panic attack that lasted several hours and had me curled up on the bed.

\* After the panic attack I would wake up every morning and after a couple of seconds would get bad anxiety that would last until the evening. After a week of that I went to the Dr and was put on antidepressants (Venlafaxine XR). I had previously been on the same one from late 2008 to April 2013. The morning anxiety has gotten better and it's only there slightly now and not all the time.

\* In early 2014 I cut out all processed food and things high in sugar like fruit. Since then, me weight dropped from 93Kg and has stabilised at 68Kg. Since my teens I used to sweat a lot but since losing weight I hardly sweat at all anymore. I don't think my weight is too bad but my cheeks are a bit hollow.

\* My sleep seems pretty good. I fall asleep pretty quickly and usually don't wake up during the night. I dream every night and after increasing sea food I think they are even more vivid. Most mornings I don't have too much trouble getting out of bed for work. I feel like I could quite easily fall asleep during the day though (I don't though). I go to bed at 10.30pm and wake up for work at 6.30am. There are no electronics in the bedroom except for an old mobile phone (on flight mode) and an old alarm clock. I have ordered some blue blocking glasses but they haven't arrived yet.

\* My maternal Grandmother had scoliosis of the spine, asthma, angina, digestive issues (reflux, cycling diarrhea and constipation). She developed dementia (which I realise now is probably due to the gut issues she had most of her life) and passed away in November last year. Hearing the symptoms of her "dementia" sounded a lot like what I was/am going through although a lot worse. For as long as I can remember she was always forgetful and would use words that sounded like the word she wanted to use but wasn't correct.

\* My Mother has had some gut issues herself although not as bad as her Mum. She's also had anxiety, depression, insomnia, poor stress tolerance, joint pain, muscle pain, etc. My Mum's first pregnancy was two weeks away from full term when the baby kicked and burst a blood vessel on the outside of her uterus and she started bleeding internally. She was rushed to hospital where she had an emergency cesarean and the baby didn't survive.

\* One of my sisters had a baby last year and had issues with her pregnancy that required her to have a cesarean.

\* My other sister has gut issues and like me is shy and nervous.

Right now, the signs and symptoms that I'm living with are:
\- Feeling anxious and irritable from time to time (often if I've been standing still for a period of time) for no reason, usually accompanied by the urge to go to the toilet
\- Anxiety/irritability, low energy, headaches, the urge to go to the toilet, not being able to think straight shortly after breakfast which lasts for up to 2 1/2 hours after eating (regardless of what I eat). I get it sometimes after eating lunch and dinner as well but if I do it's not as bad as breakfast. I wake up feeling not too bad but eating usually sends me downhill.
\- Upper abdominal pain in the middle and to the right, not all the time but sometimes
\- Some abdominal bloating and distension
\- Gastric reflux
\- Strange dizzy sensation in my head occasionally
\- Poor memory and can't think straight a lot of the time
\- Joint pain, especially knees, fingers wrists and elbows
\- Generally low mood and energy levels
\- Blue/purple nail beds on my fingers
\- Sore/stiff jaw from time to time
\- Bad dandruff
\- Slightly dry skin
\- Dry eyes
\- The tip of my tongue is red
\- My hands and feet are often cold (feet always, hands most of the time)
\- Slight hair loss on the outside of my lower legs
\- Muscle twitches
\- Tender calf muscles
\- Itchy skin a lot of the time. Nowhere specific.
\- Ringing/hissing in my ears when ambient noise is low
\- Grainy vision and a strange problem where the position I'm focusing on looks ok but things around it shimmer and wave
\- What feels like poor blood sugar control
\- Light sensitivity sometimes
\- Almost non-existent libido. In fact orgasms make me feel flat and depressed instead of happy and relaxed.

I've had a host of tests since late 2013.

\- My AST and ALT liver enzymes were elevated. December 2013 AST was 50 U/L and ALT was 103 U/L. With a diet change and supplements they have been coming down. One is still slightly elevated and one is ok now. March 2015 AST was 26 U/L and ALT was 54 U/L.

\- My ferritin level was high for several years prior to 2013. On 22 May 2010 it was 349ug/L. I have one of the haemochromatosis gene mutations but it's not the one that usually results in development of the disease. My ferritin level dropped dramatically in 2014 and last it was checked was normal.

\- In January 2014 my testosterone was at the low end of the range at 187 pmol/L as was my DHEAS at 6.7 nmol/L. My Estrone was high at 27 pg/mL. I've not had it retested since but don't have any reason to believe it's changed.

\- In late 2014 I had a HTMA done which showed pretty well all mineral levels were low and many mineral ratios were bad. No significant levels of toxic metals showed up though.

\- January 2014 my intestinal permeability was high.

\- January 2014 my urinary pyrrole level was 32.9 (elevated).

\- January 2014 my copper level was good 18 umol/L, although because of the high pyrrole level I was put on zinc supplements and late 2014 my zinc was ridiculously high and my copper was low. I stopped taking zinc and started taking copper and they were balanced, although both slightly low, so I'm taking both.

\- January 2014 my histamine was high 61 ug/L and I was told I was an undermethylator and prescribed supplements to help reduce histamine

\- December 2013 my thyroid function was tested: Free T4 was 14.1 pmol/L, Free T3 was 4.7 pmol/L and TSH was 0.8 mIU/L. Unfortunately the test didn't include rT3.

\- March 2013 my cholesterol was high at 5.7 nmol/L and in December 2013 it had plummeted to 3.8 nmol/L. March 2015 it was 4.8 nmol/L.

\- Late 2014 a stool test showed dientamoeba fragilis which I was prescribed metronidazole for. After 4 days I started vomiting and was given tinidazole. A follow up test showed the parasite still present. Some people seem to find it causes symptoms while others never have any so I don't know if it's causing any of mine.

\- Early 2015 a stool test showed an overgrowth of Enterococcus, Streptococcus and Bifidobacterium and low numbers of Eubacterium and Lactobacillus. I was given antibiotics for the overgrowth and probiotics for the low ones.

\- October 2014 morning cortisol was 10.9 nmol/L, midday 1.7 nmol/L, afternoon was 1.2 nmol/L and midnight was 0.3 nmol/L. My DHEAS in the morning was 7.8 nmol/L.

\- I had a food intolerance test which show a high IgG response to dairy protein, lactose and eggs so I stopped eating those.

My diet consists of:

Breakfast: approx. 250g of steak and 100g rice, with about 3 - 4 tablespoons of ghee.

Lunch: A dozen fresh oysters and 100g - 200g of roast pork

Dinner: steak/pork/chicken/fish with lettuce, carrot, a potato, pumpkin, lightly cooked garlic and sometimes
cauliflower. I've started having cooked onion as well in the last couple of days.

I only drink water (filtered) and try to drink at least 1.5L a day.

Since adding in the oysters at lunch and switching to steak, rice and ghee in the morning I've noticed that my memory and cognitive function has improved slightly and I can go longer without eating.

Supplements/Medication:

Breakfast: slippery elm, compounded supplements (Vit C 1500mg, B6 150mg, P5P 100mg, biotin 500ug, Methyl B12 100ug, chromium picolinate 100ug, methionine 1000mg, Vit A 2500IU, B2 10mg, B5 100mg, B1 50mg), Venlafaxine XR 37.5mg, 8 drops Lugols soln (10% potassium iodide and 5% iodine), Vit E, Vit B3, hydrocortisone 12mg, copper 2mg, digestive enzymes, rhodiola, DHEA 24mg.

Mid morning: Magnesium glycinate 930mg, Mutaflor

Lunch: slippery elm, digestive enzymes, hydrocortisone 8mg, rhodiola

Mid afternoon: Magnesium glycinate 930mg, Mutaflor

Dinner: slippery elm, digestive enzymes, compounded supplements (Vit C 1500mg, zinc 50mg, biotin 500ug, chromium 100ug, methionine 1000mg, selenium 150ug)

Bed time: Magnesium glycinate 930mg, Mutaflor

I don't know if I'm leptin resistant at the moment so some thoughts there would be great.

In summer I was having cold showers but now that it's winter I just can't do it anymore. I can't stand the cold, although I only wear shorts and a t-shirt at night despite feeling cold. I would like to dry face dunks but need more information on how to do it properly/effectively.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-21T14:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-chris.15694/post-169132>

Good morning Chris. Happy winter !  Now that brings me right to my first point .
Here is how the doc explains the proceedings and rationale behind facedunking.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/)
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-is-the-goal-of-face-dunking.8631/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-is-the-goal-of-face-dunking.8631/)

I can't help you with the leptin issue but maybe this blog excerpt helps you to decide.

**"1. First make sure you really are Leptin resistant (LR) to begin with.**
The easiest way to do this if you are heavy is to look in the mirror. If you’re overweight you definitely are Leptin resistant. If you still have a large appetite and crave carbohydrates, especially at night, these are also signs that you are likely Leptin resistant. If you are fit or in decent shape and not sure based upon the above symptoms, I would tell you to go get a blood test and check your reverse T3. It will be elevated. I also recommend simultaneously checking a salivary cortisol level. With LR, you will always see higher cortisol levels later in the day."
(Excerpt from [http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/))

### Lahelada — 2015-06-22T13:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-chris.15694/post-169248>

Daedalus,it pays to focus on the bigger picture . Docs treat you by symptom as in a consult few have the time to piece the puzzle together . Acid reflux points to a leaky gut,which points to blue light overload in light and food terms. As you gain more experience here you will understand the reference to food. Do not treat the symptom of acid reflux only though ,the aches you suffer are probably also related to your gut .

However,rather than putting ideas in your head google your main symptoms here.
The best way to do this is by using google . the search query should read like this
jack kruse acid reflux or jack kruse forum acid reflux . Or whichever you want to know.

As far as leptin resistance is concerned it does get a bit involved. How is your weight,your sleep your appetite? Your blue light exposure?

I also see a lot of supplements. You are whipping the tired horse to my mind. It sounds like a listing of all the things good I ever read in Cosmopolitan magazine  Do not dump them though over night.Have a read and see what you might keep . I would start adding fish , the best fresh you can find. If you cannot find any financially or otherwise canned sardines are great. Oysters are the ferrari of seafood . Make a beeline for them.

### JanSz — 2015-06-22T23:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-chris.15694/post-169280>

Daedalus351
Chris
software developer in Brisbane CBD, Australia
on antidepressants (Venlafaxine XR)
weight dropped from 93Kg and has stabilised at 68Kg
sleep seems pretty good
could quite easily fall asleep during the day though

Gastric reflux
Almost non-existent libido. In fact orgasms make me feel flat and depressed instead of happy and relaxed.
have one of the haemochromatosis gene mutations

\- October 2014 morning cortisol was 10.9 nmol/L, midday 1.7 nmol/L, afternoon was 1.2 nmol/L and midnight was 0.3 nmol/L. My DHEAS in the morning was 7.8 nmol/L.

long list of supplements

\====================================================================

Please convert your cortisol and DHEAs units.

cortisol from nmol/L to ug/dL (microgram/deciliter)
DHEAs from nmol/L to ug/dL

You should aim at getting top range (serum) values
cortisol(AM) (6.2-19.4)ug/dL
cortisol(PM) (2.3-11.9)ug/dL
DHEAs (520-640) ug/dL

To get there use DHEA pills and
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology 150mg tabs

I am eating 300mg/day DHEA, you may need more.
Start with one pregnenolone tab, increase as need.

Additionally measure progesterone serum.
Slow down pregnenolone increases when your progesterone goes ove 1.4 ng/mL, you may go to 2 or 3 on progesterone but treat it as temporary.
If at that point you are still missing a lot of cortisol, we will have to add hydrocortisone o Medrol to mix.

With such a low testosterone I am wondering that you are able to ejaculate at all.
Eventually you may have to use supplemental testosterone.
Problem is that you are talking hemochromatosis.
Also fertility will have to be adressed.
Monitor your hematocrit, hemoglobin, RBC.
You do not want any of the three to go over its laboratory top range.
Supplemental testosterone at certain higher level will cause increase of HCT, Hbg and RBC.
Watch out foods containing heme.

\=======================================
You are eating lots of supplements.
How did you figured which one to eat and how much?
There is company in USA, Spectracell.
It
have a Micronutrient test, that is answering this question.
If you are ever in USA or Geneva Switzerland, get that test.
It is worth all the money you would spend on that test.

Hi Chris and welcome

You might try drinking apple cider vinegar with water for the GERD/gut issues - I'm having fabulous results with that - I'm also having fabulous results with "the berberines" (Oregon grape root) for blood sugar control - one after every meal - personally, I would skip the betaine HCL and go straight for digestive enzymes with ox bile to break down the fat - the cortisone => U R blue light toxic - instead of the cortisone, I would do progesterone and rhodiola and you \*MUST\* avoid blue light/EMF - this is going to be interesting as you are a software engineer - maybe you could stick a lead plate by your desk which would absorb some of the EMF? At least wear blue blocker glasses every time you look at a screen to avoid cratering DHA further -

As you hate the cold, you might go with ice packs on your carotid artery and where your legs meet your torso - this is where the most blood flow it -

Put Epsom Salts with water in a spray bottle or make a lotion out of it, spray it/rub it on your skin and get our into the morning sun (between 9:00-11:00) - or you could buy a grow light like this - shine in on your belly button:
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00IYSQIPG?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s01](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00IYSQIPG?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s01)

You just screw it into any lamp socket and point it at your belly button - not at your eyes - there is a major artery behind the belly button which will enable the light to heal you -

I would add ubiquinol to your list of supplements -

The parasite might be sucking the life out of you or it could be benign:
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2851273/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2851273/)

But, if you want to try the "natural" parasite method my go to list includes the following: cats claw, diatomaceous earth, cloves, pumpkin seeds, garlic garlic and more garlic - chased with a molybdenum -

You also might want to try to find someone who does energy testing - my chiropractor basically sticks a supplement in the energy pathway that's clogged and he can tell what I lack - gee, I needed calcium - oh, for the efflux we are all experiencing these days...

This is an interesting article because it lets you see "energetically" what remedy might be more effective for your particular parasite:
[http://www.wellsphere.com/lyme-dise...sting-accelerates-the-healing-process/1216691](http://www.wellsphere.com/lyme-disease-article/how-bioenergetic-testing-accelerates-the-healing-process/1216691)

Dr. Dietricht Klinghardt is big into this form of "muscle testing" - naturally, this also means you are low in electrons...

Another thing about these lovely bugs - taking anything orally just feeds the bugs, not you - this is why transdermal is probably the better way to go -

The pyroluria just means you have an infection - I didn't notice molybdenum in your supplement list - I would definitely be taking 1000 mg or that per day in 3 divided doses - and add pantethine - pantethine is also needed to make hormones -
You also might want to supplement vit k2 from Life Extension - read this it is you:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/](http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/)

[http://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/London2014/SeneffHeartDisease2014.pdf](http://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/London2014/SeneffHeartDisease2014.pdf)

You can get all wrapped up in the minutia, but truly, the etiology of it all is: calcium is effluxing because of EMF...

# Thread: Nicotine gum biohack - UBI 17
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 102
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2015-06-24T14:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169463>

Good morning,

a lot of people are trying this on themselves after the idea was seeded in the UBI17 blog.
A lot of people are reporting but in a disjointed fashion in diaries ,other post etc. How about if we pool our experiences here ?
Looking at @[Martin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/950/) ,@[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/) ,@[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/) ,@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) ,@[KetoKatie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12617/) , @[colt](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5700/) , @[Valerie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10247/) ,@[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)

Fancy sharing here ?
\* For readers who have not read the UBI17 blog yet it is here .
[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/)

### caroline — 2015-06-24T14:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169465>

great idea Lah! loved talking to you this morning! You are very special woman

my experience ..... I have been using the coolmints -2mg. I feel hyper I think - I don't think I would describe it as more energy or more brain power.
I have only used it in the morning. My sleep has been lousy so I wondered if it was a contributing factor???

But in another thread we have mentioned lousy, hot/sweaty sleep and changing sweating patterns - I haven't had a BO problem for years. Now that I think about it - when we first came to Australia I all of a sudden had body odour and used to quiz everyone on what kind of deodorant to use ....

### Lahelada — 2015-06-24T14:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169466>

I tried the nicotine gum in a disjointed fashion . The first two days I notice a lot more mental clarity but also more cold sensitivity. No energy burst . My results are skewed though as I have NOT tapered off carbs as I should have done going to none/very little on winter solstice day . I will restart this next week.

My nicotine gum: Nicotine 2mg equivalent to 10mg polacryline nicotine,gum base contan hydroxtoluene butylate,calcium carbonate,sorbitol,xylitol,anhydrous sodium carbonate,bicarbonate of soda,polacryline,glycerol,mint millcap,purified water,natural mint flavour,levomenthol,saccharine,aceulfame,manitol,gelatine,titanium dioxide,carnauba wax , lol more natural mint flavour.

Something tells me that this - being the only nicotine gum available here- is not really doing me any favours . Hmm..

### nicld — 2015-06-24T15:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169470>

Great idea La.

I am very curious about this but will not even think of trying it until I get the house rewired. Having one place where people can share their experience will really help others.

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-24T17:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169475>

My 2mg gum ingredients: Chewing gum base, sorbitol, polacrilin, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, flavourings, glycerol, talc and E321. Really boring taste so I'm not tempted by a minty sweet flavour, I can keep it medicinal. Some days I have 4 x 2mg, some days less, today none so far .....I'm near the coast for a few days, and together with a BAB what they seem to be doing is taking away the temptation to eat the local pasties, ice cream and clotted cream scones ....
I'm not sure if I can give all the credit to the gum because I've also struck lucky with the accommodation - one wifi signal and having removed the bedside clock, the emf readings on my meter are less than at home, good blackout curtains, sleep really good. Add some barefoot walking on the moors and at the beach plus some time in the sea, lots of seafood, and I can feel the difference.  But I do think the gum has given me a mental edge, and gives me just enough of a boost when i'm getting tired or my energy is dipping to avoid self sabotage ....

### Martin — 2015-06-24T18:11:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169476>

I like this. Prompted me to take the time to re-read UBI 17.

I started with 2 mg Nicorette gum a couple of weeks ago and got sick as a dog for about an hour. Figured it wasn't for me, but something told me to keep the pack for later.

Oh yeah.

I started back a week ago with .5 mg (I quartered each piece) and what a difference. NO quesy, sick, spinning feeling. Experienced a bit more mental clarity and a decent amount of extra energy, but not speedy and hyper. A few hours later chewed another quarter and did well. Finished another quarter about the middle of the afternoon and stopped for the day. Repeated same for the next week and today I'm upping to 1 mg first thing in AM and then at noon to see any changes. If I don't climb the walls or crash I'm doing another 1 mg in the afternoon.

DIL is always interested in the crazy shit I've been doing for the last four years and is put off by the reputation of nicotine as being addictive. I'm sending her UBI 17 paragraph highlighting nicotine gum to show that there is SOLID science behind everything I do and this is not some dipshit reckless tactic to rebuild my body.

I seem to remember that nicotine and resveratrol (or ubiquinol?) taken together is beneficial. Got to go research that more.

Who'da thought nicotine has a place in bio-hacking?!

We all know who.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-24T19:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169478>

Thank you @Martin.I just did the same and overdosed by accident. I took 2 mg at 10 am ,felt good enough to go for skipping lunch.Took a second one at 12.30 and almost immediately brain fog,pressure headache and felt woozy.Drank a litre of water,a bit better.Bought lunch,ate lunch slightly better.But HUNGRY still.
I will back off and restart with a much lower dose. Edit:add feeling really cold and tired to the equatjon now.

It is really important to take this slowly.

E

### Lahelada — 2015-06-24T19:13:34+0300

### fitness@home — 2015-06-24T20:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169481>

I've only experimented a bit with it and prefer the lozenges over gum.

I find that using 2mg between 6:15-6:45 a.m. when outside and watching the sun come up is best for me. Good energy, doesn't leave me edgy, no ill effects thus far. Last Saturday I also did another dose at 4:00 p.m. and my energy/stamina remained strong late into the night (you want that in New Orleans!) and had no detrimental affect on my sleep.

\*\*Editing to add: I also find it makes me very "regular". This is a good thing.\*\*

### seanb4 — 2015-06-24T20:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169482>

Cool thread.

Main thing I have noticed is light headedness and weakness.

I take 4mg first thing in the morning, cold shower, then breakfast. The 0-8mg spread throughout the day.

On the odd occasion I have gotten very weak, sickly and dizzy where I cannot stand up for fear what will happen. I also burn up at this time. My interpretation of this is it is up regulating uncoupling hence the heat but my body isn't coping with it well thanks to poor redox/hormones/etc.

I should probably get round to reading UB17...

### lilly antic — 2015-06-24T21:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169484>

Excellent idea, Lahelada.
2 mg for me is definitely too much. It left me dizzy and light headed . Now I break it into halves of 1 mg, which is lots better. No dizziness.

But what i experienced, even with 1 mg is like a globus sensation in the throat, like a lump when swallowing. Has anybody had that so far?
It stays for an hour or so and then disappears .
I'm not sure if it's due to the nicotine or the other ingredients.
I use the gum twice a day, 1 mg every time. So far, other than the globus I have not noticed a big difference. Maybe a little more energy, but it can be my imagination. Maybe 2 x 1 mg is too little???
What I did feel yesterday, is that CT was A LOT easier..I spent the week in the Austrian mountains with freezing mountain lakes and alpine streams.
So we seized the opportunity to do some deep CT.
First I thought I got the temperature wrong, because it felt so warm! But the temp was freezing...
Have to retest that tomorrow !!!

### Valerie — 2015-06-25T04:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169518>

The nicotine gum continues to provide energy and takes away the need for food (breakfast/lunch); been on it about 3.5 weeks. I am a consistent coffee drinker and found it hard to give up. With the gum I continue to drink coffee with no problem. Use 2 mg first thing in morning and 2 mg near lunch and shifting to just eating dinner (possible late afternoon snack) with no real issue. Previously, I had to eat 2-3 meals a day. Guess now I am activating the PPP (finally).

Periodically I take 1-3 day break. My rule is no gum if I am going to eat a meal (like breakfast). Hopefully use of food in place of gum might prevent addiction.

Interesting observation is that in the last week several dark floaters in my eyes have shown substantial improvement (less dark and more diffuse, and dropping to the bottom of my eye out of field of view). So I am going to continue for awhile more to see if the nicotine might be helping maybe my DC current? Back in March I did start eating raw fish, and I continue to expose my closed eyes to the early morning sun on the weekends which also could be contributing to the improvements.

No significant impact to body temperature, maybe a little warmer.

### Shijin13 — 2015-06-25T04:51:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169522>

I've been experimenting with it in 2mg longezes Initially I wasn't going to for a while... but I had the start of a migraine, and went out and picked up 2mg, after reading several journal articles the day prior about nicotine, and APQ4 gates and migraines. so I popped 2mg and stood outside grounding. w/in 15min the migraine was gone. I felt awesome all day.

Though some days I get a bit light headed... w/the 2mg... so I'm going to try a week of 1mg. and see how that goes. Ive been waking right before the sunrise since taking it. When I was down in NOLA there were days I had it, and others that I skiped it w/no desire either way for it...

I think I'll be using nicotine as a way to help me couple during times of stress, and seasonal changes... and see how that goes...

@[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/) out to give his n-1 with this as well.... so should @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/)

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-25T07:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169547>

I've been using 2mg gum every morning for 12 days. I find it is a disgusting habit to chew gum in the morning lol. Since I've been a nicotine addict for 17 years, I notice no dramatic or immediate effect, even after I haven't smoked for some years now. I didn't feel different or at least not something that I can notice right now. But something happened... my body temperature dropped consistently after 2 days of chewing by 0,4 degrees C. Today was the last gum and I will quit now out of fear for addiction. It will be interesting to see if my temperature will go back to usual. Wondering what drop in temp means? Does anybody know?

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-25T08:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169552>

It was different but I can't put my finger on why, but it might be worth a try. I also experienced the first chewing of the gum to get it going as a slight fizz, almost a burning but not quite, on my tongue. So I've also experimented with holding it on and under my tongue, paying attention to the fizz .... that seems much quicker, I get rid of the gum earlier, possibly because the tongue as a sense organ probably concentrates DHA? I was wondering whether having conditioned myself with the physiological effects of nicotine first, perhaps I can now use nicotine and the tongue as a quantum signal. Yesterday I didn't use it, but thought about it a few times, remembering the sensation on my tongue .... Virtual nicotine anyone?

### Sue-UK — 2015-06-28T11:15:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-169761>

I've had a 2 day break but am now doing a 7 day cycle. Reason is that I think the nicotine helps keep the clock in my head running slightly faster, it also seems to give my gut a bit of extra current (so needing less fibre). My working theory is that if I can keep the clock in my head running at the right speed, with my gut having some battery powered peristalsis,  excess fat will sort itself out naturally, providing a steady supply of stem cells for other ongoing repairs, particularly repairs to my solar battery ......(I'm concentrating my light therapy on the damaged collagen of my belly stretch marks, using a cream that contains garlic extract).

I think I can tolerate 2mg doses without light-headedness, nausea etc because my mitochondria can handle the charge because of months of more water, DHA and CT. I'm finding now though that I don't want to keep the gum in for long, so I might halve the dose and increase the frequency, just to keep the clock consistently running slightly ahead.

### lilly antic — 2015-06-30T16:09:04+0300

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-07-01T04:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-170153>

I started on nicotine on the Cruise and seemed to do really 'well' on it. 2mg a day just in the morning no food or cravings of any kind all day. Ate in the evening only. Yesterday (Monday) day after I got back again took 2mg and felt kind of strongly 'affected' all day. Did not eat at all yesterday very unusual for me..............like as in never happened before.

But felt 'flushed' and hot, warm face and hands and feet a bit too. So today did not take ate once today.

Anyway this in not really important except for me to work out but I spoke to someone today and he said 'the problem with taking nicotine is your body will stop making it and then if you stop you will feel depleted or really craving it' Road to addiction?. So my question is simple enough how about the principle of 'if you make it don't take it'. How does that fit into the nicotine story?

Also the only effect I sort of understand is it stimulated the para-sympathetic nervous system..............I can kind of feel that and I 'like' it. I am a bit of a stress case so anything that encourages rest and recreation etc is most welcome to me. Again though am I getting dependent on a substance to alter my mood. Downside - addiction? or dependence whatever you want to call it.

I felt very encouraged the first 4 days am I am definitely interested in pursuing it more but I have those questions. It's like the argument against taking testosterone or is nicotine not really analogous to hormones? Any replies welcome.

Hi Nikita

Ubi 10 has a nugget about hiccoughs.

" Hiccoughs don’t occur because you’re scared or stressed. It is due to altered nitrogen and oxygen coupling in your diaphragm, causing myoclonus of the the muscle. The more stress you have from your environment (positive charge) the longer the effect sticks around. It is a deep sign you are “light mismatched” on the nitrogen side of the equation."

When I started with nicotine gum I would sometimes hiccough, but it would only be one usually, sometimes 2. So it was a sign that I am still "light mismatched" but not as badly as if I was hiccoughing for longer. Now I don't usually hiccough when I chew the gum, (so I think I'm moving forward) but if I do its now a sign to change the environment my mitochondria are sensing _at this moment in time_. ### freesia — 2015-07-11T07:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-171651>

^^ Woops ^^ I meant to write:
[I have a little bit of experience to share on the nicotine front.
\*I've never smoked or used nicotine before.

I started using nicotine gum four days ago. 2 mg first thing, with am sunlight. It's mid winter here.
At first I thought it was having no effect but later I realised afternoon/evening brain fog was improved slightly.
Days 2 and 3 exact same thing - oh and by now I've realised it might have also had an effect on improving gut function.
Day 4 (today) I used the same 2 mg and immediately got the lump feeling in my throat followed by onset of nausea. I guess I reached my saturation point?
I'll leave it a couple of days and try 2 mg again.

I'm intrigued by the effects! Less brain fog in the evenings has been put to good use. I feel like floodlights have come on via the blogs (and recommended reading)....never mind a flashlight.  I dreamed about Gerald Pollack last night. He had nine daughters in my dream lol.

Will update again in due course...
Thx to whoever suggested this might be worth a trial.
Anne.](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/)

### Richelle Jones — 2015-08-28T02:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-175956>

I have been using 4mg uncoated sugar free gum in the morning and half an hour before yoga in the afternoons. I put the gum under my tongue so the nicotine soaks into the little blood vessels under there. The gum improves my mental clarity and energy levels and decreases appetite. I do ashtanga yoga and the gum definitely improves my flexibility and energy throughout my practice. I have been epi-paleo for a couple of yrs so my DHA levels should be good. I am an ex-smoker so 4mg is no problem for me. Years ago when I used nicotine patches to try to give up smoking I found they made me dizzy and caused ringing in the ears but I would have had very low DHA levels back then.

### nonchalant — 2015-08-28T22:12:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-175991>

> [Jude said:](/goto/post?id=175971)
>
> Hey Sally, what are you noticing with your gums?
> How long does it take before you have chewed the nicotine out?
> Please elaborate

Hi Jude, I think I was chewing for too long. I would think the gum is all used up, but I would park it, and I'd notice it still tingled. So I'd keep it in my mouth.
I'd say my gums are receding, but that usually means away from the teeth. The gums are fine around my teeth. Further away from my teeth, near where the cheek attaches, the gum appears sunken a bit, and darker pink than usual. I used to move the n. gum around to various parts of the gum. Inside near the tongue, however, I don't see any problems, though I would keep the n. gum under my tongue for long periods.

Now I am using less gum, and not chewing it for as long. I never park it now. I've been spraying my gums with silver solution, exposing them to sunshine (not easy for the area near the cheek), and I have increased my vitamin k mk4. I wonder if exposing the teeth to sunshine benefits the gums. I suspect it does. I've used the red led light on the area a couple times.

### CoolSwede — 2016-05-27T15:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-195459>

Finally, I took a leap of faith biohacking nicotine use. I know that it is not the proper season here up in the north, but I still wanted to see if there were any effects from using it. Dose has been 0.5 mg (2 mg gum cut in quarters), once or twice daily.

\-Increase in mental clarity. Notable improvement in verbal speed and processing. Easier to find words.
\-Mood boost. I'm mostly happy but it made me more witty and joking.
\-A notable increase in morning wood occurence and libido! This was especially interesting since I have been wondering why my libido has been lowish/average despite having high salivary Testosterone.
\-Dopamine simply rocks!

### Valerie — 2016-06-13T14:57:28+0300

Light needs to be balanced, blue light produces ROS.

### Jenelle — 2016-06-19T17:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-196916>

Thanks for bringing up this older thread with lots of great info. I discovered it this morning and had to read all 5 pages, outside grounding in the sun with my nicotine lozenge.

I'll just add my experience here. I am an ex-smoker. I smoked off and on for ~20 years, from the ages of 14-34. I was \*very\* hesitant to try nicotine in any form. I read John Kiefer's e-book about it over a year ago, and just could not bring myself to do it.

After reading about it here, I experimented with the drops from Blue Brain Boost. That did not go well. One drop was too much all at once. It caused stomach upset and overall just did NOT feel right. I threw the bottle in the trash so I would not be tempted to continue something that was clearly wrong for me at the time.

When I decided to experiment again, I have been using the 2mg mini lozenges ~ parking and spitting. This, combined with everything else I am doing since coming here, has been no less than magical for me. I feel like myself again. I can look back and see where I was over this past winter, and it seems like a different person.

I don't feel addicted to it like I did with smoking. It's not the same for me. I know there is a time and a place for it, and I am able to use it only when needed.

I don't fully understand how it all works, but the words are starting to make more sense to me. For now I will just say I am eternally grateful.

### Jenelle — 2016-06-25T18:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-197266>

> [Toby King said:](/goto/post?id=197218)
>
> Wow thats an amazing experience Jenelle!
>
> .
> .
> .
>
> I have been considering biohacking nicotine myself, but given Im still 17 I dont want to risk what I believe is an increased likelihood of addiction for my age range. Would love input on this.

Toby, I believe my mother may have smoked while pregnant with me (it was the 70's, after all  ). I know she was at least exposed to second-hand -- I would say constantly, as my father smoked in the car and home. Then I was of course exposed to it growing up, then of course I began smoking myself.

What I can tell you is that quitting was always a MAJOR struggle... and one that I would not wish upon anyone. BUT. I can look back to those years now and see that I was not eating right, I was not sleeping right, my stress level was high with little kids, and I was not exercising. Certainly not eating DHA, getting lots of sunshine, fresh air, and grounding. Certainly no knowledge of blue light, EMFs, eye clocks, etc.

In other words -- I think most smokers do not take very good care of themselves, like we try to do here.

I have been thinking and thinking about your question. I have a son who is 15, and who has just recently started consuming caffeine for the first time in his life (mostly in the form of green tea... also occasional diet soda). It gives him a boost, he feels good and happy, and I'd say he's probably addicted. Would I let him experiment with the nicotine lozenges at this age? ...Man... that's a tough one. I'm gonna have to sit back for awhile and see what other responses you get.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I went as far as google scholar "effect of nicotine on the adolescent brain" and got as much as a study that says this : "While upregulation also occurs during prenatal, neonatal and postnatal developmental periods, as well as adolescence, there have been conflicting results regarding a change in corresponding gene expression. However, there is strong evidence that nicotine exposure during these influential periods of development contributes to later changes in the cholinergic system." [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/gbb.12251/full](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/gbb.12251/full)

This second study has more info and from the little "clear text"  I can decipher I would say **NO** to nicotine in adolescence.. Toby , I think your gut feeling of staying away from this is correct.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2746456/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2746456/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-07-06T01:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-197918>

Luis's bio-hack points out the key with nicotine posted July 5, 2016: You got to get UV/IR light to make it unleash.

"Last fall I experimented with nicotine lozenges for a few weeks to test for cognitive boost. (I've never smoked cigarettes) other than burning sensation in my mouth, I didnt notice much and would even forget to use it....most addictive substance....meh.

Figured the losenges were weak so got some nicotine drops at the end of winter. Three or four drops transdermally didnt do much. So I tried sublingual, got a bit of a buzz with 2 drops. Stopped using it again. A coupleweeks ago after much increasing my solar quantum yield (as much as is possible in Chicago, I figured I would use it again for an upcoming meeting

I quickly put a few drops under my tongue and drove off to the meeting. Almost immediately I became very dizzy like I was high. I figured I must have over dosed. So I pulled back to 2 drops, still dizzy.

I just pulled back to one drop and I'm still slightly loopy.

UV light increases dopamine production, IR light induces dopamine receptor regeneration. So I am guessing I am now more sensitive to exogenous dopamine.

Light is one helluva drug!"

Previously, I had to eat 2-3 meals a day. Guess now I am activating the PPP (finally).

So I am going to continue for awhile more to see if the nicotine might be helping maybe my DC current? Back in March I did start eating raw fish, and I continue to expose my closed eyes to the early morning sun on the weekends which also could be contributing to the improvements.
>

### kris90 — 2019-01-03T22:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/post-259394>

I recently started using nicotine to help biohack SAD. For the most part, I can use CT entirely to treat SAD symptoms experienced in winter, but I thought why not experiment.

I learned about MAO genes, so I'm curious to get my SNPs done, because I feel like I lose dopamine and other neurotransmitters ridiculously fast, and with nicotine being a MAO inhibitor, I thought it would be a good experiment.

So far I've definitely noticed a dopamine boost. The boost is significant when I use the gum while outside walking in the cold or doing CT. I've also noticed quite the boost when using it with Sperti sunlamp or tanning bed. I usually take 2mg in gum form while watching sunrise, and again around noon while outside.
